com:Annotation.com:AnnotationText: presentation_title=Balance of Payments: Aggregated Standard Presentation ; presentation_description=Balance of Payments: Aggregated Standard Presentation
presentation_title=Balance of Payments: Aggregated Analytic Presentation ; presentation_description=Balance of Payments: Aggregated Analytic Presentation

str:Dataflow.com:Name: Balance of Payments (BOP)
str:Dataflow.com:Description: The Balance of Payments (BOP) is a statistical statement that summarizes transactions between residents and nonresidents during a period. It consists of the goods and services account, the primary income account, the secondary income account, the capital account, and the financial account.

str:Category.com:Name: Economic forecasts and analysis
str:Category.com:Description: Economic forecasts, policy analysis, and other economic research.

str:Category.com:Name: Economy and prices
str:Category.com:Description: Economic activity, prices, and national accounts.

str:Category.com:Name: Financial and monetary sector
str:Category.com:Description: Financial institutions, monetary aggregates, and interest rates.

str:Category.com:Name: External sector
str:Category.com:Description: International trade, balance of payments, and external debt.

str:Category.com:Name: Demographic and social
str:Category.com:Description: Population demographics, social indicators, and labor market statistics.

str:Category.com:Name: Environment and climate
str:Category.com:Description: Environmental conditions, climate change, and natural resource use.

str:Category.com:Name: Working Papers
str:Category.com:Description: Datasets relating to working papers, which are typically preliminary research papers that present new findings, methodologies, or analysis for academic or policy-related discussion.

str:Category.com:Name: IMF Operations
str:Category.com:Description: IMF institutional data like Fund Accounts.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Balance of Payments (BOP) BOP Accounting Entry

str:Code.com:Name: Credit/Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit).

str:Code.com:Name: Debit/Expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Outflows or payments made, representing expenditures.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets, Net acquisition of financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Assets are a store of value representing a benefit or series of benefits accruing to the economic owner by holding or using the entity over a period of time. It is a means of carrying forward value from one accounting period to another. Net acquisition of financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (credits less debits)
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of total credits minus total debits.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities, Net incurrence of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations. Net incurrence of liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets
str:Code.com:Description: Assets are a store of value representing a benefit or series of benefits accruing to the economic owner by holding or using the entity over a period of time. It is a means of carrying forward value from one accounting period to another.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (net acquisition of financial assets less net incurrence of liabilities), Transactions
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between net acquisition of financial assets and the net incurrence of liabilities in an accounting period. Economic flows that result from interactions between institutional units by mutual agreement, involving an exchange of value.

str:Code.com:Name: Negative Credit/Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: A situation where credits or revenues are negative, indicating a deficit or loss.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets, Positions
str:Code.com:Description: Assets are a store of value representing a benefit or series of benefits accruing to the economic owner by holding or using the entity over a period of time. It is a means of carrying forward value from one accounting period to another. The financial holdings of assets, liabilities, and equity at a specific point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities, Positions
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations. The financial holdings of assets, liabilities, and equity at a specific point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Net incurrence of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations. Net incurrence of liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (assets less liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: Net refers to the value of assets less the value of liabilities

str:Code.com:Name: Net (credits less debits), Transactions
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of total credits minus total debits. Economic flows that result from interactions between institutional units by mutual agreement, involving an exchange of value.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (net acquisition of financial assets less net incurrence of liabilities), Positions
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between net acquisition of financial assets and the net incurrence of liabilities in an accounting period. The financial holdings of assets, liabilities, and equity at a specific point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all relevant components or categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Not allocated/unspecified
str:Code.com:Description: Not specified or detailed.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Not applicable or relevant in the given context.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets
str:Code.com:Description: Assets is store of value, over which ownership rights are enforced and from which their owners may derive economic benefits by holding them over a period of time (2016 MFSMCG glossary).

str:Code.com:Name: Assets, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Assets, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Assets, Claims on, Credit to
str:Code.com:Description: Assets, Claims on, Credit to

str:Code.com:Name: Assets, Claims on, Other claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Assets, Claims on, Other claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Gross sales of assets (decrease)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of asset sales, representing a decrease in asset holdings.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross assets refer to the total value of all assets owned or held by an individual, organization, or entity before accounting for any deductions, such as depreciation or liabilities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross acquisition of assets (increase)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of assets acquired, representing an increase in asset holdings.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets (net)
str:Code.com:Description: Assets is store of value, over which ownership rights are enforced and from which their owners may derive economic benefits by holding them over a period of time (2016 MFSMCG glossary).

str:Code.com:Name: Assets - short position
str:Code.com:Description: Store of value representing a benefit or series of benefits accruing to the economic owner by holding or using the entity over a period of time. It is a means of carrying forward value from one accounting period to another.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets (short or negative positions)
str:Code.com:Description: Assets that are either shorted or have negative positions due to borrowing or other financial obligations.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance (credits minus debits)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the total credits and debits in an account, representing net credit position.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit (Resources)
str:Code.com:Description: Credits related to resource inflows, including financial and non-financial resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit (Environment + Economy)
str:Code.com:Description: Credits accounted for within the context of both environmental and economic transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit/Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit).

str:Code.com:Name: Contingent liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities that may occur depending on the outcome of a future event, as defined in the European System of Accounts (ESA) and Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Claims
str:Code.com:Description: Financial claims on other entities

str:Code.com:Name: Credit to
str:Code.com:Description: Credit to

str:Code.com:Name: Credit (excluding exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Credits that exclude any exceptional financing measures such as bailouts or special financial assistance.

str:Code.com:Name: Debit (Uses)
str:Code.com:Description: Debit entries relate to imports of goods and services, earned income payable, transfer income payable and acquisitions of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit).

str:Code.com:Name: Debit (Environment + Economy)
str:Code.com:Description: Debits accounted for within the context of both environmental and economic transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Domestic (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to domestic accounts to reflect accurate financial positions.

str:Code.com:Name: Debit/Expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Outflows or payments made, representing expenditures.

str:Code.com:Name: End use
str:Code.com:Description: The final consumption or utilization of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: End use (Environment + Economy)
str:Code.com:Description: The final consumption or utilization of goods and services within the context of both environmental and economic considerations.

str:Code.com:Name: End use, of which emissions relevant
str:Code.com:Description: End use of goods and services where emissions are a significant factor.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (domestic)
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Transformation use
str:Code.com:Description: Use of resources or funds for transformation activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Transformation use (Environment + Economy)
str:Code.com:Description: Use of resources or funds for transformation activities within the context of environmental and economic considerations.

str:Code.com:Name: Economy to environment
str:Code.com:Description: Financial flows or impacts from economic activities to environmental resources or impacts.

str:Code.com:Name: Environment to economy
str:Code.com:Description: Financial flows or impacts from environmental resources or activities to the economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Assets that are in the form of cash, stocks, bonds, or other financial instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to the value or reporting of financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets (domestic)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assets held within the domestic economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to foreign accounts to reflect accurate financial positions.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets (foreign)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assets held in foreign entities or markets.

str:Code.com:Name: Inflows (reserves template)
str:Code.com:Description: Incoming funds or resources within the context of a reserves template.

str:Code.com:Name: Net flows (reserves template)
str:Code.com:Description: Net inflows or outflows within the context of a reserves template.

str:Code.com:Name: Outflows (reserves template)
str:Code.com:Description: Outgoing funds or resources within the context of a reserves template.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross
str:Code.com:Description: The total value before any deductions.

str:Code.com:Name: Net income on inward FDI
str:Code.com:Description: The net income generated from inward foreign direct investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Inflows
str:Code.com:Description: The incoming funds or resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Net income on outward FDI
str:Code.com:Description: The net income generated from outward foreign direct investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross decrease in liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total reduction in liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (foreign)
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross liabilities refer to the total value of all obligations or debts owed by an individual, organization, or entity before accounting for any adjustments or offsets

str:Code.com:Name: Gross incurrence of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of liabilities incurred before any deductions.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities to, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities to, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities: Unofficial
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.. Liabilities that are not necessarily recognized by official records or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (excluding exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (assets minus liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value obtained by subtracting total liabilities from total assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net acquisition of financial assets other than cash
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of acquiring financial assets excluding cash.

str:Code.com:Name: Net Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Financial claims on other entities less liabilities to the same entity

str:Code.com:Name: Net Claims on, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Net Claims on, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Net Claims on, Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Net Claims on, Liabilities to

str:Code.com:Name: Net Credit to
str:Code.com:Description: Net Credit to

str:Code.com:Name: Net Credit to, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Net Credit to, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Net Credit, Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Net Credit, Liabilities to

str:Code.com:Name: Net Liabilities (liabilities minus assets)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value obtained by subtracting total assets from total liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Negative Credit/Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: A situation where credits or revenues are negative, indicating a deficit or loss.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (acquisitions less disposals)
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of total acquisitions minus total disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (assets less liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: Net refers to the value of assets less the value of liabilities

str:Code.com:Name: Net (credits less debits)
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of total credits minus total debits.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (liabilities less assets)
str:Code.com:Description: Net refers to the value of liabilities less the value of assets

str:Code.com:Name: Net (receivables less payables)
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of total receivables minus total payables.

str:Code.com:Name: Net foreign assets
str:Code.com:Description: Net foreign assets

str:Code.com:Name: Net Foreign Assets, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Net Foreign Assets, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Net Foreign Assets, Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Net Foreign Assets, Liabilities to

str:Code.com:Name: Balance on goods, services, and income
str:Code.com:Description: The net balance of trade in goods, services, and income flows between countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Net FDI inward
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of foreign direct investment inflows.

str:Code.com:Name: Net FDI outward
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of foreign direct investment outflows.

str:Code.com:Name: Other Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Other Liabilities to

str:Code.com:Name: Provided by
str:Code.com:Description: Resources or funds provided by an entity.

str:Code.com:Name: Private liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.. Liabilities with private entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Provided to
str:Code.com:Description: Resources or funds provided to an entity.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities: Official
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations. Liabilities that are not necessarily recognized by official records or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (net acquisition of financial assets less net incurrence of liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between net acquisition of financial assets and the net incurrence of liabilities in an accounting period.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Standard Classification Type
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist identifies the standardized classification systems used for categorizing and organizing data within a resource. Examples include industry classifications, product classifications, or occupational classifications.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Country
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist identifies the geographical, economic, or aggregate entities for which statistical data is collected and shared. It includes countries, territories (not necessarily recognized as states under international law), economic entities, and UN and IMF country groups used for statistical calculations.

str:Code.com:Name: الدول الهشة والمتأثرة بالنزاعات
Нестабильные и затронутые конфликтом государства

Fragile and Conflict-affected States (FCS)
États fragiles et touchés par un conflit
Estados Frágiles y Afectados por Conflictos
脆弱和受冲突影响的国家
str:Code.com:Description: This is a country grouping established by the World Bank's classification of countries in fragile and conflict-affected situations. The list consists of (i) countries with high levels of institutional and social fragility and (ii) countries affected by violent conflict, using indicators and thresholds assessed by the World Bank. It is updated annually and available publicly. Under the new IMF strategy for Fragile and Conflicted States (FCS), the Fund has adopted the methodology, thresholds, and criteria of the World Bank's Fragility and Conflict Situations for the IMF Fragile and Conflicted States. This is explained in the FCS Strategy Board paper: The IMF Strategy for Fragile and Conflict-Affected States (March 14, 2022, p. 21-22).

str:Code.com:Description: The European Currency Unit (ECU) was a basket of European currencies used as the unit of account of the European Monetary System (EMS) from 1979 to 1998, prior to the introduction of the euro in 1999. The ECU was a composite currency, meaning its value was derived from a weighted average of the currencies of the participating countries.

str:Codelist.com:Name: COFOG
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents the Classification of the Functions of Government (COFOG) 1999, a standardized system for classifying government expenditures by their purpose.

str:Code.com:Name: Economic affairs n.e.c. (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Economic affairs expenditures that do not fit into other specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: R&D General economic, commercial and labour affairs (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on research and development related to general economic issues, commerce, and labor markets.

str:Code.com:Name: R&D Agriculture, forestry, fishing and hunting (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on research and development related to agriculture, forestry, fishing, and hunting.

str:Code.com:Name: R&D Fuel and energy (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on research and development related to energy production, distribution, and efficiency.

str:Code.com:Name: R&D Mining, manufacturing and construction (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on research and development related to the mining, manufacturing, and construction industries.

str:Code.com:Name: R&D Transport (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on research and development related to transportation technologies and infrastructure.

str:Code.com:Name: R&D Communication (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on research and development related to communication technologies and infrastructure.

str:Code.com:Name: R&D Other industries (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on research and development related to industries not categorized elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Distributive trades, storage and warehousing (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government support for wholesale and retail trade, logistics, and warehousing, including infrastructure development and regulation.

str:Code.com:Name: Hotels and restaurants (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government support for the hospitality industry, including hotels, restaurants, and tourism.

str:Code.com:Name: Tourism (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government support for the tourism industry, including marketing campaigns, infrastructure development, and tourism promotion.

str:Code.com:Name: Multi-purpose development projects (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on large-scale development projects that have multiple objectives, such as economic development, social welfare, and environmental protection.

str:Code.com:Name: Communication (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses related to telecommunications infrastructure, regulation, and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Road transport (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on road infrastructure, including highways, streets, and bridges.

str:Code.com:Name: Water transport (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on water transportation infrastructure, including ports, waterways, and support for shipping.

str:Code.com:Name: Railway transport (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on railway infrastructure, including tracks, trains, and stations.

str:Code.com:Name: Air transport (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on air transportation infrastructure, including airports, air traffic control, and subsidies for airlines.

str:Code.com:Name: Pipeline and other transport (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on pipeline transport and other modes of transportation not categorized elsewhere, such as cable cars and ferries.

str:Code.com:Name: Mining of mineral resources other than mineral fuels (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on the mining of minerals other than fossil fuels, such as metals and industrial minerals.

str:Code.com:Name: Manufacturing (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government support for the manufacturing sector, including subsidies, tax incentives, and infrastructure development.

str:Code.com:Name: Construction (CS)
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on public construction projects, such as roads, bridges, buildings, and other infrastructure.

str:Code.com:Name: External affairs (CS)

str:Code.com:Name: Executive and legislative organs, financial and fiscal affairs, external affairs
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures related to the core functions of government administration, financial management, and foreign relations.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign economic aid
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on aid programs aimed at supporting economic development in other countries.

str:Code.com:Name: General services
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures on administrative and support services that are not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Description: General public services that do not fit into other specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Public debt transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses related to managing and servicing government debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Transfers of a general character between different levels of government
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made between different levels of government (e.g., central to local) for general purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: General public services
str:Code.com:Description: Broad category for expenditures related to the general functioning of government, such as executive and legislative functions, financial administration, and external affairs.

str:Code.com:Name: Defence
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on military personnel, operations, equipment, research, and foreign military aid.

str:Code.com:Name: Public order and safety
str:Code.com:Description: Includes expenditures on law enforcement (police), fire protection, judicial systems, and prisons.

str:Code.com:Name: Economic affairs
str:Code.com:Description: A broad category encompassing government spending on activities that promote economic growth and development, such as general economic, commercial and labour affairs, agriculture, forestry, fishing and hunting, fuel and energy, mining, manufacturing and construction, transport, communication, other industries, R&D, and other economic affairs not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Environmental protection
str:Code.com:Description: Spending on environmental protection measures, such as pollution abatement, waste management, biodiversity conservation, and environmental research.

str:Code.com:Name: Housing and community amenities
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on the development and maintenance of housing, as well as community facilities like parks, recreation centers, and cultural venues.

str:Code.com:Name: Health
str:Code.com:Description: Encompasses all government expenditures on healthcare services, including medical products, appliances, outpatient services, hospital care, public health initiatives, and medical research.

str:Code.com:Name: Recreation, culture and religion
str:Code.com:Description: Spending on recreational facilities, cultural institutions (e.g., museums, theaters), religious organizations, and related services.

str:Code.com:Name: Education
str:Code.com:Description: All levels of education, from pre-primary to tertiary, as well as ancillary services like school transportation and meals.

str:Code.com:Name: Social protection
str:Code.com:Description: Covers government programs aimed at providing social security and welfare, including pensions, unemployment benefits, family support, and social exclusion initiatives.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: This code is used when a specific COFOG category does not apply to a particular expenditure.

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all government current and capital expenditures across all COFOG categories.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Topic
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist provides a high-level categorization of the resource's subject matter, indicating the main area or domain to which the data pertains.

str:Code.com:Name: مخاطر المناخ

気候リスク
Climate risks
Risques climatiques
Riesgos climáticos
气候风险
str:Code.com:Description: The potential adverse consequences of climate change for various sectors and systems, including physical risks (e.g., extreme weather events, sea-level rise), transition risks (e.g., policy changes, technological shifts), and liability risks (e.g., legal claims, reputational damage).

str:Code.com:Name: انبعاثات غازات الاحتباس الحراري

Emisiones de gases de efecto invernadero

str:Code.com:Name: Climate and weather
str:Code.com:Description: The long-term patterns and average conditions of temperature, humidity, precipitation, wind, etc. in a specific region (climate), as well as the day-to-day state of the atmosphere (weather).

str:Code.com:Name: Transition to a low carbon economy
str:Code.com:Description: The global shift away from economic activities that rely heavily on fossil fuels (like coal, oil, and natural gas) and towards systems that generate minimal greenhouse gas emissions. This includes data on renewable energy adoption, energy efficiency improvements, and sustainable technology development.

str:Code.com:Name: Physical and transition risk indicators
str:Code.com:Description: Physical and transition risks related to climate change. Physical risks refer to the potential impact of climate change on physical assets, while transition risks are related to the shift to a low-carbon economy and include factors such as policy changes and technological innovations.

str:Code.com:Name: البيئة
Окружающая среда
環境
Environment
Environnement
Medio ambiente

证券
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments that represent ownership in a company (stocks) or debt obligations (bonds). This includes information on issuance, trading volumes, prices, and returns.

str:Code.com:Name: الودائع
Депозиты
預金
Deposits
Dépôts
Depósitos
存款
str:Code.com:Description: The funds held by individuals and businesses in bank accounts, including demand deposits, time deposits, and savings accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: المشتقات المالية
Финансовые деривативы
金融派生商品
Financial derivatives
Produits dérivés financiers
Derivados financieros
金融衍生品
str:Code.com:Description: Financial contracts whose value is derived from an underlying asset, such as stocks, bonds, commodities, or currencies.

str:Code.com:Name: المؤسسات المالية
Финансовые учреждения
金融機関
Financial institutions
Institutions financières
Instituciones financieras
金融机构
str:Code.com:Description: The financial activities and performance of banks, insurance companies, pension funds, investment funds, and other financial intermediaries, including balance sheets, income statements, and risk exposures.

str:Code.com:Name: البنوك التجارية
Коммерческие банки
商業銀行
Commercial banks
Banques commerciales
Bancos comerciales
商业银行
str:Code.com:Description: The financial activities and performance of commercial banks, which are the primary providers of credit and deposit services to households and businesses. This includes balance sheets, income statements, and risk exposures.

str:Code.com:Name: المؤسسات المالية غير المصرفية
Небанковские финансовые учреждения
ノンバンク金融機関
Nonbank financial institutions
Institutions financières non bancaires
Instituciones financieras no bancarias
非银行金融机构
str:Code.com:Description: The activities and performance of financial institutions that are not banks, such as insurance companies, pension funds, investment funds, and other financial intermediaries.

str:Code.com:Name: السياسة النقدية
Монетарная политика
金融政策
Monetary policy
Politique monétaire
Política monetaria
货币政策
str:Code.com:Description: The actions taken by central banks to influence the availability and cost of money in the economy, such as interest rate changes, quantitative easing, and other monetary policy tools.

str:Code.com:Name: متطلبات الاحتياطي
Требования к резервам
預金準備率
Reserve requirements
Réserves obligatoires
Requisitos de reserva
法定存款准备金
str:Code.com:Description: The minimum amount of reserves that banks are required to hold against their deposits, as mandated by the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: النقود
Деньги
マネー
Money
Monnaie
Dinero
货币
str:Code.com:Description: The various forms of money in circulation, including currency, demand deposits, and other liquid assets.

str:Code.com:Name: المجاميع النقدية
Денежные агрегаты
マネタリー・アグリゲーツ
Monetary aggregates
Agrégats monétaires
Agregados monetarios
货币总量
str:Code.com:Description: The total stock of money in circulation, including currency in circulation, demand deposits, and other liquid assets. Monetary aggregates are used as indicators of economic activity and to gauge the impact of monetary policy.

str:Code.com:Name: القاعدة النقدية
Денежная база
マネタリーベース
Monetary base
Base monétaire
Base monetaria
货币基础
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of currency in circulation and reserves held by commercial banks at the central bank. The monetary base is a key component of the money supply and is directly controlled by the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: إجماليات الائتمان
Кредитные агрегаты
信用総量
Credit aggregates
Agrégats de crédit
Agregados crediticios
信贷总量
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of credit outstanding in the economy, including loans to households, businesses, and the government.

str:Code.com:Name: التحليل المالي الكلي
Макрофинансовый анализ
マクロ金融分析
Macrofinancial analysis
Analyse macrofinancière
Análisis macrofinanciero
宏观金融分析
str:Code.com:Description: The assessment of the overall health and stability of a country's financial system, including its interactions with the broader economy. This encompasses indicators of credit growth, asset prices, capital flows, and other financial variables that can affect economic stability.

str:Code.com:Name: الدورات المالية
Финансовые циклы
金融サイクル
Financial cycles
Cycles financiers
Ciclos financieros
金融周期
str:Code.com:Description: The fluctuations in financial variables over time, such as credit growth, asset prices, and leverage.

str:Code.com:Name: الروابط المالية الكلية
Макрофинансовые связи
マクロ金融リンケージ
Macrofinancial linkages
Liens macrofinanciers
Vínculos macrofinancieros
宏观金融联系
str:Code.com:Description: The interconnections between the financial sector and the broader economy, including the transmission channels through which financial shocks can affect economic activity and vice versa.

str:Code.com:Name: السياسة الاحترازية الكلية
Макропруденциальная политика
マクロプルーデンス政策
Macroprudential policy
Politique macroprudentielle
Política macroprudencial
宏观审慎政策
str:Code.com:Description: Policies and regulations designed to mitigate systemic risks in the financial system, such as capital requirements, leverage ratios, and borrower-based measures.

str:Code.com:Name: أدوات السياسة الاحترازية الكلية
Макропруденциальные инструменты политики
マクロプルーデンス政策手段
Macroprudential policy instruments
Instruments de politique macroprudentielle
Instrumentos de política macroprudencial
宏观审慎政策工具
str:Code.com:Description: The specific tools used by policymakers to mitigate systemic risks in the financial system, such as capital buffers, leverage ratios, and borrower-based measures.

str:Code.com:Name: التحليل الاحترازي الكلي
Макропруденциальный анализ
マクロプルーデンス分析
Macroprudential analysis
Analyse macroprudentielle
Análisis macroprudencial
宏观审慎分析
str:Code.com:Description: The assessment of systemic risks and vulnerabilities in the financial system, using both quantitative and qualitative methods.

str:Code.com:Name: الرقابة والتنظيم الماليين
Финансовый надзор и регулирование
金融監督と規制
Financial supervision and regulation
Supervision et réglementation financières
Supervisión y regulación financieras
金融监管
str:Code.com:Description: The oversight and enforcement activities of regulatory bodies overseeing financial institutions and markets. This includes information on regulatory frameworks, supervisory practices, and enforcement actions.

str:Code.com:Name: تنظيم البنوك
Банковское регулирование
銀行規制
Bank regulation
Réglementation bancaire
Regulación bancaria
银行监管
str:Code.com:Description: The rules and regulations governing the operations of banks, including capital requirements, liquidity requirements, and risk management standards.

str:Code.com:Name: إدارة مخاطر البنوك
Управление банковскими рисками
銀行リスク管理
Bank risk management
Gestion des risques bancaires
Gestión de riesgos bancarios
银行风险管理
str:Code.com:Description: The practices and procedures used by banks to identify, assess, and mitigate risks, such as credit risk, market risk, operational risk, and liquidity risk.

str:Code.com:Name: الإشراف على المؤسسات المالية غير المصرفية
Надзор за небанковскими финансовыми учреждениями
ノンバンク金融機関監督
Nonbank financial institution supervision
Supervision des institutions financières non bancaires
Supervisión de instituciones financieras no bancarias
非银行金融机构监管
str:Code.com:Description: The oversight and regulation of nonbank financial institutions, such as insurance companies, pension funds, and investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: إدارة الأصول والخصوم
Управление активами и пассивами (ALM)
資産負債管理
Asset and liability management (ALM)
Gestion actif-passif
Gestión de activos y pasivos
资产负债管理
str:Code.com:Description: The strategies and practices employed by financial institutions to manage their assets and liabilities, including interest rate risk, liquidity risk, and credit risk. This is used to assess the effectiveness of ALM in maintaining financial soundness.

str:Code.com:Name: مقاييس مخاطر الخصوم
Меры риска обязательств
負債リスク指標
Liability risk measures
Mesures du risque de passif
Medidas de riesgo de pasivo
负债风险度量
str:Code.com:Description: Various measures used to assess the riskiness of an institution's liabilities, such as the maturity mismatch between assets and liabilities, the volatility of funding sources, and the potential for runs on deposits.

str:Code.com:Name: التوقعات والتحليلات الاقتصادية
Экономические прогнозы и анализ
経済予測と分析
Economic forecasts and analysis
Prévisions et analyses économiques
Previsiones y análisis económicos
经济预测与分析
str:Code.com:Description: Economic forecasts, policy analysis, and other economic research.

str:Code.com:Name: التنبؤ الاقتصادي
Экономическое прогнозирование
経済予測
Economic forecasting
Prévisions économiques
Previsiones económicas
经济预测
str:Code.com:Description: Economic forecasts for various countries and regions.

str:Code.com:Name: حسابات الصندوق
Счета фонда
基金勘定
Fund accounts
Comptes de fonds
Cuentas de fondos
基金账户
str:Code.com:Description: The IMF's financial accounts and resources.

str:Code.com:Name: البيانات المالية لصندوق النقد الدولي
Финансовые данные МВФ
IMF金融データ
IMF Financial data
Données financières du FMI
Datos financieros del FMI
国际货币基金组织金融数据
str:Code.com:Description: The IMF's financial operations and lending activities.

str:Code.com:Name: تقارير مراقبة صندوق النقد الدولي
Отчеты МВФ о мониторинге
IMFサーベイランスレポート
IMF Surveillance reports
Rapports de surveillance du FMI
Informes de vigilancia del FMI
国际货币基金组织监督报告
str:Code.com:Description: Reports on the IMF's surveillance of member countries' economic and financial policies.

str:Code.com:Name: Working papers datasets
str:Code.com:Description: Datasets relating to working papers, which are typically preliminary research papers that present new findings, methodologies, or analysis for academic or policy-related discussion.

str:Code.com:Name: International Financial Statistics (IFS)
str:Code.com:Description: Datasets underlying the International Financial Statistics, one of the Fund’s principal statistical compilations. Available since 1948, it has broad geographic and conceptual coverage.

str:Code.com:Name: World Economic Outlook (WEO)
str:Code.com:Description: Datasets related to the World Economic Outlook and Regional Economic Outlooks

str:Code.com:Name: Third-Party Data
str:Code.com:Description: Licensed economic and financial data from commercial and non-profit providers, subject to strict copyright terms. External data.

str:Codelist.com:Name: GFS Stocks, Transactions, Other Flows
str:Codelist.com:Description: A classification of data items used in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS) framework, distinguishing between stocks (levels at a point in time), transactions (changes over a period), and other flows.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: A term used to indicate that a specific item or category does not apply in the given context.

str:Code.com:Name: Amortization of total public debt
str:Code.com:Description: The process of gradually paying off the total public debt through scheduled payments over time. This includes both principal repayments and interest payments on government debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Budget balance
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between a government's total revenue and total expenditure over a specific period. A positive balance indicates a surplus, while a negative balance indicates a deficit.

str:Code.com:Name: Cyclically adjusted balance
str:Code.com:Description: The cyclically adjusted balance is a measure of a government's budget balance that takes into account the effects of the economic cycle. It adjusts the actual budget balance for the impact of cyclical fluctuations in economic activity, such as changes in output, employment, and tax revenues, to provide a clearer picture of the underlying fiscal position. This adjustment helps policymakers assess the structural or underlying fiscal position of the government, independent of temporary fluctuations caused by the economic cycle. A positive cyclically adjusted balance indicates a structural surplus, while a negative cyclically adjusted balance indicates a structural deficit.

str:Code.com:Name: Cyclically adjusted primary balance
str:Code.com:Description: The cyclically adjusted primary balance is similar to the cyclically adjusted balance but focuses specifically on the primary balance, which excludes interest payments on government debt. It adjusts the primary balance for the effects of the economic cycle to provide a measure of the structural fiscal position of the government, independent of cyclical fluctuations. Like the cyclically adjusted balance, a positive cyclically adjusted primary balance indicates a structural surplus, while a negative cyclically adjusted primary balance indicates a structural deficit.

str:Code.com:Name: Consumption of fixed capital (CFC): major improvements, nonproduced assets
str:Code.com:Description: The decline in value of major improvements to nonproduced assets, such as land reclamation or infrastructure development, over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Net cash inflow from operating activities
str:Code.com:Description: The net amount of cash generated from the government's regular operational activities, including tax collections and fees, minus operational expenses.

str:Code.com:Name: Cash inflow from operating activities
str:Code.com:Description: The cash generated by a government's regular operational activities, including tax collections, fees, and other income.

str:Code.com:Name: Corporate income tax (CIT)
str:Code.com:Description: A?corporate tax, also called?corporation tax?or?company tax, is a type of direct tax?levied on the income or capital of?corporations?and other similar legal entities. The tax is usually imposed at the national level, but it may also be imposed at state or local levels in some countries. Corporate taxes may be referred to as income tax or capital tax, depending on the nature of the tax.

str:Code.com:Name: Total changes in net worth, stock-flow discrepancy
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between total changes in net worth and the stock-flow discrepancy, indicating potential errors or inconsistencies.

str:Code.com:Name: Cash receipts from operating activities
str:Code.com:Description: The total cash received from a government's regular operational activities, including all forms of income and revenue.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital spending
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures by the government on acquiring, constructing, or improving physical assets such as infrastructure, buildings, and equipment.

str:Code.com:Name: Cash surplus/deficit
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between a government's cash inflows and outflows over a specific period. A surplus indicates more inflows than outflows, while a deficit indicates more outflows than inflows.

str:Code.com:Name: Cash surplus/deficit (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to the reported cash surplus or deficit to account for corrections, reallocations, or other changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Deficit
str:Code.com:Description: The amount by which a government's total expenditures exceed its total revenues in a specific period, leading to a shortfall that must be financed through borrowing.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt service charges
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of interest payments and repayments of principal on government debt that are due within a specific period.

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical discrepency
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between statistical data collected from different sources or methods, indicating potential errors or inconsistencies.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal end day
str:Code.com:Description: The final day of the government's fiscal period, marking the end of the fiscal year or quarter.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal impulse
str:Code.com:Description: A measure of the change in the fiscal stance of the government, indicating the impact of fiscal policy on economic activity.

str:Code.com:Name: Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue refers to the total inflow of resources, typically in the form of taxes, fees, charges, grants, and other income, received by a government entity during a specific period. It represents the funds generated by the government through its various activities and sources, including taxation, sales of goods or services, investment income, and transfers from other entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Revenue from extractive industries
str:Code.com:Description: Government revenue generated from the extraction of natural resources, such as minerals, oil, and gas.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Compulsory financial charges levied by the government on individuals, businesses, and transactions to fund public services and programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Unallocated tax revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Tax revenue that cannot be allocated to specific categories and is classified as miscellaneous.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income, profits, and capital gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the income, profits, and capital gains of individuals and businesses.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income, profits, and capital gains from extractive industries
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the income, profits, and capital gains generated by extractive industries, such as mining, oil, and gas.

str:Code.com:Name: Unallocated taxes on income, profits, and capital gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on income, profits, and capital gains that cannot be allocated to specific categories and are classified as miscellaneous.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income, profits, and capital gains, payable by individuals
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the income, profits, and capital gains of individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income, profits, and capital gains, payable by corporations and other enterprises
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the income, profits, and capital gains of corporations and other business enterprises.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on income, profits, and capital gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on income, profits, and capital gains that do not fit into specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on payroll and workforce
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on payroll and workforce expenses, such as social security contributions and payroll taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on property
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the ownership, use, or transfer of property, including real estate and personal property.

str:Code.com:Name: Recurrent taxes on immovable property
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on immovable property, such as land and buildings, on a regular basis.

str:Code.com:Name: Recurrent taxes on net wealth
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the net wealth of individuals or entities on a regular basis.

str:Code.com:Name: Tax, property, estate, inheritance, and gift taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on property ownership, estates, inheritances, and gifts.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital levies
str:Code.com:Description: One-time charges or taxes imposed by the government on capital assets or wealth to raise revenue for specific purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Other recurrent taxes on property
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on property on a regular basis that do not fit into specific property tax categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the sale or consumption of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on goods and services not classified elsewhere in general taxes on goods and services or excises.

str:Code.com:Name: General taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: A category of taxes that includes all taxes levied on the sale or consumption of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Value added tax, goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: A consumption tax levied on the value added at each stage of production and distribution

str:Code.com:Name: Sales taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the sale of goods and services, typically collected at the point of sale.

str:Code.com:Name: Turnover and other general taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the total revenue generated by the sale of goods and services, including turnover taxes and general sales taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on financial and capital transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on financial and capital transactions, such as stock trades and real estate sales.

str:Code.com:Name: Excises
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes imposed on specific goods and services, such as alcohol, tobacco, and fuel, typically included in the price of the product.

str:Code.com:Name: Profits of fiscal monopolies
str:Code.com:Description: Profits generated by government-owned monopolies involved in fiscal activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Specific services tax: goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: A tax levied on specific goods and services, such as telecommunications or tourism.

str:Code.com:Name: Use of goods tax, goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the use of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Motor vehicles taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes imposed on the ownership, registration, or use of motor vehicles.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on use of goods and on permission to use goods or perform activities
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the use of goods and services and on permissions to use goods or perform activities that do not fit into specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Business and professional licenses
str:Code.com:Description: Permits issued by government authorities that allow individuals or businesses to engage in specific professional activities or operate certain types of businesses.

str:Code.com:Name: Pollution taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on activities that cause environmental pollution, intended to reduce harmful emissions and encourage cleaner practices.

str:Code.com:Name: Radio and television licenses
str:Code.com:Description: Fees charged by the government for issuing licenses to operate radio and television stations.

str:Code.com:Name: Licenses and permits for households
str:Code.com:Description: Fees charged by the government for issuing licenses and permits to households, allowing them to engage in specific activities or use certain goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on use of goods and on permission to use goods or perform activities not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the use of goods and services and on permissions to use goods or perform activities that do not fit into any specific tax category.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the sale or consumption of goods and services that do not fit into specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on international trade and transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on international trade and transactions, including customs duties and import/export taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Customs and other import duties
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes and fees imposed by the government on imported goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on exports
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on goods and services exported from the country.

str:Code.com:Name: Profits of export or import monopolies
str:Code.com:Description: Profits generated by government-owned export or import monopolies.

str:Code.com:Name: Exchange profits
str:Code.com:Description: Profits generated from the exchange of goods and services, including gains from favorable exchange rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Exchange taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the exchange of goods and services, including customs duties and other import/export taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on international trade and transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on international trade and transactions that do not fit into specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes
str:Code.com:Description: A category of taxes that includes all taxes levied by the government that do not fit into specific tax categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes payable solely by business
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes that are exclusively levied on businesses.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes payable by other than business or unidentifiable
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes collected from entities other than businesses or from sources that cannot be identified.

str:Code.com:Name: Tax (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to the recorded tax revenue to account for corrections, reallocations, or other changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes not classified elsewhere in taxes on income, profits, and capital gains, taxes on property, taxes on goods and services, or taxes on international trade and transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other tax
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes collected by the government that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as miscellaneous.

str:Code.com:Name: Tax revenue [WEO]
str:Code.com:Description: Tax revenue data sourced from the World Economic Outlook (WEO) reports.

str:Code.com:Name: Social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Compulsory payments made to social insurance schemes by employees, employers, and the self-employed to fund future social benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Compulsory payments made to social security schemes by employees, employers, and the self-employed.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions: employee contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by employees to social security schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions: employer contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by employers to social security schemes on behalf of their employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions: self-employed or nonemployed contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by self-employed individuals or those not employed to social security schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions: unallocable contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions to social security schemes that cannot be allocated to specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions to social security schemes and other social benefits that do not fit into specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other social contributions: employee contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by employees to social security schemes and other social benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Other social contributions: employer contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by employers to social security schemes and other social benefits on behalf of their employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Other social contributions: imputed contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions that are assumed to have been made to social security schemes and other social benefits, even if no actual transaction took place.

str:Code.com:Name: Social contributions (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to the recorded social contributions to account for corrections, reallocations, or other changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that the government expects to receive from various entities for various purposes, including operational support and capital projects.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants adjustment
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to recorded grants to account for corrections, reallocations, or other changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Current grants receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that the government expects to receive from other entities for current purposes, such as operational support or social benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants receivable from foreign governments: current grants
str:Code.com:Description: Current grants that the government expects to receive from foreign governments for operational support or social programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital grants receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that the government expects to receive from other entities for capital purposes, such as infrastructure development or major equipment purchases.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants receivable from foreign governments: capital grants
str:Code.com:Description: Capital grants that the government expects to receive from foreign governments for infrastructure development or major equipment purchases.

str:Code.com:Name: Other revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue collected by the government from various sources that do not fit into specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Property income
str:Code.com:Description: Income generated from government-owned property, including rent, leases, and sales.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest (before FISIM allocation), receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Interest income that the government is entitled to receive before the allocation of Financial Intermediation Services Indirectly Measured (FISIM).

str:Code.com:Name: Dividends receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Payments that the government expects to receive from its investments in state-owned enterprises or other equity holdings.

str:Code.com:Name: Withdrawals from income of quasi-corporations, receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Payments that the government expects to receive from quasi-corporations from their income, typically representing the government's share of profits or other earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Property income from investment income disbursements
str:Code.com:Description: Income generated from investments in property assets, including dividends, interest, and rent.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance income receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Income that the government expects to receive from insurance policies, such as premium payments or claims reimbursements.

str:Code.com:Name: Rent, receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Rent payments that the government expects to receive for the use of its property or land.

str:Code.com:Name: Reinvested earnings on foreign direct investment
str:Code.com:Description: Earnings from foreign direct investments that are reinvested in the same foreign enterprises.

str:Code.com:Name: Sales of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue generated from the sale of goods and services by the government.

str:Code.com:Name: Sales by market establishments
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue generated from the sale of goods and services by government or nonprofit establishments operating in the market.

str:Code.com:Name: Administrative fees
str:Code.com:Description: Charges levied by government entities or organizations for the provision of administrative services. These fees cover the costs associated with processing applications, permits, and other administrative activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Incidental sales by nonmarket establishments
str:Code.com:Description: Sales of goods and services by government or nonprofit establishments that primarily do not operate in the market but occasionally sell products as a by-product of their main activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Imputed sales of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Sales that are assumed to have occurred, even if no actual transaction took place, based on

str:Code.com:Name: Fines, penalties, and forfeits
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue collected by the government from fines, penalties, and forfeits imposed for legal or regulatory violations.

str:Code.com:Name: Transfers, receivable not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Transfer payments expected to be received that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as miscellaneous.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as miscellaneous current transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Current tansfers not elsewhere classified, Subsidies
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assistance provided by the government to support specific sectors, activities, or entities, not classified under other categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers not elsewhere classified: other
str:Code.com:Description: Other financial transfers that do not fit into specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers of capital that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as miscellaneous capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, fees, and claims related to nonlife insurance and standardized guarantee schemes, receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts that the government expects to receive from nonlife insurance policies and standardized guarantee schemes, including premiums, fees, and claims.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, fees, and current claims, receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts that the government expects to receive for premiums, fees, and current claims related to nonlife insurance policies and standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums receivable
str:Code.com:Description: The amount of insurance premiums that the government expects to receive from policyholders.

str:Code.com:Name: Fees for standardized guarantee schemes, receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Fees that the government expects to receive from entities participating in standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Current claims receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts that the government expects to receive from other entities for current transactions, such as taxes, fees, or transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital claims receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts that the government expects to receive from other entities for capital transactions, such as sales of nonfinancial assets or repayments of loans.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous and unidentified revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue that cannot be classified under specific categories due to its irregular or unidentified nature.

str:Code.com:Name: Revenue (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to the recorded revenue to account for corrections, reallocations, or other changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity related revenues
str:Code.com:Description: Income generated from the production and sale of commodities such as oil, gas, minerals, and agricultural products.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants in cash receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Cash grants that the government expects to receive from other entities, such as local governments or international organizations.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants in kind receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Non-cash grants that the government expects to receive from other entities, such as goods or services provided by local governments or international organizations.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary revenue
str:Code.com:Description: The government's total revenue excluding any interest income.

str:Code.com:Name: Tax revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue generated from taxes levied by the government on individuals, businesses, and transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Revenue, excluding grants
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue refers to the total inflow of resources, typically in the form of taxes, fees, charges, grants, and other income, received by a government entity during a specific period. It represents the funds generated by the government through its various activities and sources, including taxation, sales of goods or services, investment income, and transfers from other entities. This indicator excludes grants receivable. Grants are transfers receivable (payable) by government units, from (to) other resident or non-resident government units or international organisations, that do not meet the definition of a tax, subsidy, or social contribution.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-oil revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Government revenue generated from sources other than oil production and related activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Expense
str:Code.com:Description: The total outflow of resources, including payments, purchases, and transfers, made by a government entity during a specific period to fulfill its various functions and obligations.

str:Code.com:Name: Compensation of employees
str:Code.com:Description: The total remuneration, including wages, salaries, and benefits, paid to employees by the government for their work.

str:Code.com:Name: Wages and salaries
str:Code.com:Description: The total remuneration paid to employees by the government, including regular pay, bonuses, and other compensation.

str:Code.com:Name: Wages and salaries in cash [GFS]
str:Code.com:Description: Cash payments made by the government to employees as part of their wages and salaries, recorded in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Wages and salaries in kind [GFS]
str:Code.com:Description: Non-cash benefits provided by the government to employees as part of their wages and salaries, such as housing or transportation, recorded in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers to social security schemes or other social insurance programs on behalf of their employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Actual employers' social contributions [GFS]
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers to social security schemes, recorded in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS). These contributions are intended to provide future social benefits for employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Imputed employers' social contributions [GFS]
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions that are assumed to have been made by employers on behalf of their employees, recorded in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Use of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: The consumption of goods and services by the government for its operational activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Consumption of fixed capital (CFC)
str:Code.com:Description: The systematic allocation of the cost of fixed assets over their useful lives, reflecting the reduction in their value over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest payable
str:Code.com:Description: The amount of interest that the government is obligated to pay on its borrowed funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies payable
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts that the government owes as subsidies to various sectors, activities, or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants payable
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that the government provides to other entities for various purposes, including operational support and capital projects.

str:Code.com:Name: Current grants payable
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that the government provides to other entities for current purposes, such as operational support or social benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants payable to foreign governments: current grants
str:Code.com:Description: Current grants that the government provides to foreign governments for operational support or social programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants payable to international organizations: current grants
str:Code.com:Description: Current grants that the government provides to international organizations for operational support or social programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants payable to foreign governments
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that the government provides to foreign governments for various purposes, including development assistance and disaster relief.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants receivable from foreign governments
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that the government expects to receive from foreign governments for various purposes, including development assistance and disaster relief.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital grants payable
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that the government provides to other entities for capital purposes, such as infrastructure development or major equipment purchases.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants payable to foreign governments: capital grants
str:Code.com:Description: Capital grants that the government provides to foreign governments for infrastructure development or major equipment purchases.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants payable to international organizations: capital grants
str:Code.com:Description: Capital grants that the government provides to international organizations for infrastructure development or major equipment purchases.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Financial or in-kind benefits provided by the government to individuals or households to support their well-being, including social assistance, pensions, and unemployment benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Benefits provided by social security schemes, including pensions, unemployment benefits, and disability payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security benefits in cash
str:Code.com:Description: Cash payments provided by social security schemes to beneficiaries.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security benefits in kind
str:Code.com:Description: Non-cash benefits provided by social security schemes, such as healthcare or housing assistance.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits other than social transfers in kind (nonmarket production)
str:Code.com:Description: Benefits provided by the government through nonmarket production, excluding social transfers in kind.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits other than social transfers in kind
str:Code.com:Description: Benefits provided by the government that do not fit into the category of social transfers in kind, such as direct financial assistance.

str:Code.com:Name: Social assistance benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Government-provided financial assistance to individuals or households in need, aimed at improving their economic well-being.

str:Code.com:Name: Social assistance benefits in cash [GFS]
str:Code.com:Description: Cash payments provided by the government to individuals or households as social assistance benefits, recorded in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Social assistance benefits in kind [GFS]
str:Code.com:Description: Non-cash benefits provided by the government to individuals or households, such as food, housing, or medical services, recorded in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Employer social benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Benefits provided by employers to their employees, including health insurance, retirement benefits, and other welfare benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Employer social benefits in cash [GFS]
str:Code.com:Description: Cash payments made by employers to employees as part of social benefits, recorded in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Employer social benefits in kind [GFS]
str:Code.com:Description: Non-cash benefits provided by employers to employees, such as health care or housing, recorded in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Other expense
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses incurred by the government that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as miscellaneous expenses.

str:Code.com:Name: Property expense other than interest
str:Code.com:Description: Property-related expenses that do not include interest payments, such as maintenance, insurance, and taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Dividends payable
str:Code.com:Description: Payments that the government owes to shareholders of state-owned enterprises as a distribution of profits.

str:Code.com:Name: Withdrawals from income of quasi-corporations, payable
str:Code.com:Description: Payments that quasi-corporations are required to make to the government from their income, typically representing the government's share of profits or other earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Property expense for investment income disbursements
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses related to the disbursement of investment income from property assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Rent payable
str:Code.com:Description: Rent payments that the government owes for the use of property or land.

str:Code.com:Name: Reinvested earnings on foreign direct investment, payable
str:Code.com:Description: Earnings from foreign direct investments that are reinvested in the same foreign enterprises, representing amounts owed to foreign investors.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous non-interest property expense
str:Code.com:Description: Property-related expenses that do not involve interest payments, such as repairs and operational costs.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous current property expense other than interest
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses related to property that do not include interest payments, such as maintenance, insurance, and taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous non-interest property expense: capital
str:Code.com:Description: Capital expenses related to property that do not involve interest, such as major repairs and improvements.

str:Code.com:Name: Transfers payable not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Transfer payments that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as miscellaneous.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers n.e.c.: other current expense
str:Code.com:Description: Financial transfers that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as other current expenses.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers not elsewhere classified: other capital expense
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers of capital that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as other capital expenses. These transfers may include various types of capital transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, fees, and claims related to nonlife insurance and standardized guarantee schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Payments related to nonlife insurance policies and standardized guarantee schemes, including premiums, fees, and claims.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, fees, and current claims, payable
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts that the government owes for premiums, fees, and current claims related to nonlife insurance policies and standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, payable (premiums, fees, and claims related to nonlife insurance and standardized guarantee schemes)
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts that the government owes for premiums related to nonlife insurance policies and standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fees for standardized guarantee schemes, payable
str:Code.com:Description: Fees that the government owes for participation in standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital claims payable (premiums, fees, and claims related to nonlife insurance and standardized guarantee schemes)
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts owed by the government for premiums, fees, and claims related to nonlife insurance policies and standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, fees, and claims related to nonlife insurance and standardized guarantee schemes, capital claims
str:Code.com:Description: Capital claims related to nonlife insurance policies and standardized guarantee schemes, including premiums and fees.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, fees, and claims related to nonlife insurance and standardized guarantee schemes: current claims payable
str:Code.com:Description: Current claims related to nonlife insurance policies and standardized guarantee schemes, including premiums and fees.

str:Code.com:Name: Expense not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses that do not fit into specific categories and are classified as miscellaneous expenses.

str:Code.com:Name: Expense (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to recorded expenses to account for corrections, reallocations, or other changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Expense excluding consumption of fixed capital
str:Code.com:Description: The total outflow of resources for operating expenses, excluding the depreciation of fixed assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Expense and investment in nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: The total outflow of resources for operating expenses and the acquisition of nonfinancial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Expenditure
