str:Codelist.com:Name: Macroeconomic and financial accounts
str:Codelist.com:Description: A framework for organizing macroeconomic and financial data into a set of integrated accounts and tables that provide a comprehensive overview of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital account
str:Code.com:Description: The capital account records acquisitions and disposals of non-financial assets as a result of transactions with other units, internal bookkeeping transactions linked to production (such as changes in inventories and depreciation) and the redistribution of wealth by means of capital transfers. In the context of the external accounts, the capital account shows (a) capital transfers receivable and payable between residents and nonresidents and (b) the acquisition and disposal of nonproduced, nonfinancial assets between residents and nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital and financial account
str:Code.com:Description: The capital account records acquisitions and disposals of non-financial assets as a result of transactions with other units, internal bookkeeping transactions linked to production (such as changes in inventories and depreciation) and the redistribution of wealth by means of capital transfers. In the context of the external accounts, the capital account shows (a) capital transfers receivable and payable between residents and nonresidents and (b) the acquisition and disposal of nonproduced, nonfinancial assets between residents and nonresidents. The financial account records all transactions in financial assets and liabilities. In the external accounts, only transactions between residents and non-residents are included.

str:Code.com:Name: Current account
str:Code.com:Description: The current account covers the transactions in goods, services, earned income and transfer income for institutional sectors and between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Current, capital and financial account
str:Code.com:Description: The current account covers the transactions in goods, services, earned income and transfer income for institutional sectors and between residents and non-residents. The capital account records acquisitions and disposals of non-financial assets as a result of transactions with other units, internal bookkeeping transactions linked to production (such as changes in inventories and depreciation) and the redistribution of wealth by means of capital transfers. In the context of the external accounts, the capital account shows (a) capital transfers receivable and payable between residents and nonresidents and (b) the acquisition and disposal of nonproduced, nonfinancial assets between residents and nonresidents. The financial account records all transactions in financial assets and liabilities. In the external accounts, only transactions between residents and non-residents are included.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial account
str:Code.com:Description: The financial account records all transactions in financial assets and liabilities. In the external accounts, only transactions between residents and non-residents are included.

str:Code.com:Name: Currency composition of International Investment Position
str:Code.com:Description: The breakdown of a country's international investment position by the currency in which the assets and liabilities are denominated.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary survey
str:Code.com:Description: An aggregate financial statement that consolidates the accounts of the central bank and other depository corporations, showing the sources and uses of funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Fund Accounts
str:Code.com:Description: A system of accounting used by non-profit organizations and governments to track the use of funds restricted for specific purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: International Investment Position (IIP)
str:Code.com:Description: Statement drawn up at a particular point in time of the value of financial assets of residents of an economy that are claims on non-residents or are gold bullion held as reserve assets and of the liabilities of residents of an economy to non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Allocated shares
str:Code.com:Description: Represents shares of ownership or equity that have been formally assigned or distributed to specific individuals or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Statement of operations
str:Code.com:Description: A statement summarizing the revenues, expenses, and results of operations of government over a specific period.

str:Code.com:Name: International Investment Position (IIP) - Assets
str:Code.com:Description: Statement drawn up at a particular point in time of the value of financial assets of residents of an economy that are claims on non-residents or are gold bullion held as reserve assets and of the liabilities of residents of an economy to non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: International Investment Position (IIP) - Liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Statement drawn up at a particular point in time of the value of financial assets of residents of an economy that are claims on non-residents or are gold bullion held as reserve assets and of the liabilities of residents of an economy to non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Statement of other economic flows
str:Code.com:Description: Captures changes in assets and liabilities that are not due to transactions, such as revaluations, write-offs, and other adjustments.

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all relevant components or categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance Sheet Account
str:Code.com:Description: The balance sheet account records stocks of nonfinancial assets, financial assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: GDP by income approach
str:Code.com:Description: Total income earned through the production of goods and services in the economy, consisting of remuneration of employees plus gross operating surplus plus gross mixed incomes plus taxes less subsidies on both production and imports

str:Code.com:Name: GDP by expenditure approach
str:Code.com:Description: The value of total output less total intermediate consumption plus any taxes less subsidies on products not already included in the value of output.

str:Code.com:Name: GDP by production approach
str:Code.com:Description: Total value added (total output less total intermediate goods used to produce the output) from the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Concentration and distribution measures
str:Code.com:Description: Statistical measures used to assess the concentration of financial assets or economic activities and their distribution across different sectors or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Additional Financial Soundness Indicators
str:Code.com:Description: Supplementary indicators used to assess the stability and soundness of the financial system, beyond the core set of financial soundness indicators.

str:Code.com:Name: Additional series - Financial Soundness Indicators
str:Code.com:Description: Additional data series that provide further insights into the financial soundness of institutions or the financial system as a whole.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance sheets and income statements
str:Code.com:Description: Financial statements that provide a snapshot of an entity's financial position (balance sheet) and its performance over a period (income statement).

str:Code.com:Name: Core Financial Soundness Indicators
str:Code.com:Description: Key indicators used to assess the financial health and stability of financial institutions, such as capital adequacy, asset quality, earnings, and liquidity.

str:Code.com:Name: Distribution measures
str:Code.com:Description: Measures used to analyze the distribution of income, wealth, or other economic variables within a population or sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Encouraged Financial Soundness Indicators
str:Code.com:Description: Indicators recommended for use in addition to the core financial soundness indicators to provide a more comprehensive assessment of financial stability.

str:Code.com:Name: Income and expense statement (FSI)
str:Code.com:Description: A financial statement that shows the revenues, expenses, and profits or losses over a specific period, also known as an income statement or profit and loss statement.

str:Code.com:Name: Memorandum series: additional series
str:Code.com:Description: Supplementary data series included in financial or economic reports to provide additional context or insights.

str:Code.com:Name: Memorandum series: balance sheet-related series
str:Code.com:Description: Supplementary data series related to balance sheet items, providing additional detail or context.

str:Code.com:Name: Memorandum series: income statement-related series
str:Code.com:Description: Supplementary data series related to income statement items, providing additional detail or context.

str:Code.com:Name: Memorandum series
str:Code.com:Description: A set of supplementary data series included in financial or economic reports to enhance understanding and analysis.

str:Code.com:Name: Memorandum series: supervisory-based series
str:Code.com:Description: Supplementary data series based on information collected by supervisory authorities, often related to regulatory compliance and oversight.

str:Code.com:Name: Government expenditures by function
str:Code.com:Description: Government expenditures by function.

str:Code.com:Name: Statement of sources and uses of cash
str:Code.com:Description: A summary of the government’s actual cash inflows (receipts) and outflows (payments) over a given period, showing how cash is generated, spent, and how the residual affects the government’s cash balance.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance Sheet (GFS)
str:Code.com:Description: A snapshot of the government’s stock of assets and liabilities at a particular point in time, including the resulting net worth (assets minus liabilities).

str:Code.com:Name: Balance Sheet (FSI)
str:Code.com:Description: Balance sheet records stocks of nonfinancial assets, financial assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Stocks and flows by counterparty
str:Code.com:Description: Stocks and Flows of assets and liabilities by counterparty.

str:Code.com:Name: Central Bank Balance Sheet
str:Code.com:Description: Central Bank Balance Sheet.

str:Code.com:Name: Depository Corporations' Balance Sheet
str:Code.com:Description: Depository Corporations' Balance Sheet.

str:Code.com:Name: Other Depository Corporations' Balance Sheet
str:Code.com:Description: Other Depository Corporations' Balance Sheet.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Sex
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents a set of codes and definitions that classify an individual's sex based on assigned sex at birth and/or biological attributes.

str:Code.com:Name: Female
str:Code.com:Description: Female sex assigned at birth.

str:Code.com:Name: Male
str:Code.com:Description: Male sex assigned at birth.

str:Code.com:Name: Intersex
str:Code.com:Description: An individual having innate different sex traits, such as chromosomes, gonads, or genitals, that fall outside of the typical male/female binary and/or have male and female sex traits.

str:Code.com:Name: X
str:Code.com:Description: A category for non-binary individuals, gender non_x0002_conforming individuals, and other persons who do not wish to define themselves in terms of binary sex categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Used in response to a question or a request for information that does not apply to the circumstances of the unit being surveyed. This concept is to be understood as meaning "statistically not applicable"; i.e. _Z is to be used only for statistical purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Unknown
str:Code.com:Description: Failure to obtain a measurement (e.g. non response, no data available, information not known by the respondent unit, etc.).

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: Used for expressing totals.

str:Code.com:Name: Other
str:Code.com:Description: Used to cover residual information not contained in other categories of the code list (in some contexts, e.g. classifications, referred to as n.e.s., not elsewhere specified, n.e.c., not elsewhere classified, etc.)

str:Code.com:Name: Non response
str:Code.com:Description: Failure to obtain a measurement on one or more study variables for one or more elements in a survey.

str:Codelist.com:Name: IMF Data Overlap
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist indicates if there is a potential overlap in the coverage of a particular indicator with another indicator in other datasets published by the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Overlapping Indicator
str:Code.com:Description: This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across datasets, differences can occur due to variations in sources and methods, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and timing differences in the collection, processing and publication of the indicator.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Valuation
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist defines refers to the rules and conventions used for recording monetary flows and stocks in macroeconomic statistics. Standard national accounts valuations include the basic price (what the seller receives) and the purchaser's price (what the purchaser pays). The purchaser's price is the basic price, plus taxes less subsidies on products, plus invoiced transportation and insurance services, plus distribution margin. International trade in goods considers the free on board (fob) price and cost-insurance-freight price, among others. Other valuation types include prices used in financial markets (e.g., buy and sell prices).

str:Code.com:Name: Nominal value
str:Code.com:Description: Outstanding amount that at any moment in time the debtor owes to the creditor. It reflects the funds originally advanced, plus any subsequent advances, plus interest that has accrued, less any repayments (2016 MFSMCG definition).

str:Code.com:Name: Market value
str:Code.com:Description: The price at which an asset would change hands if it sold on the open market. Market prices for transactions are defined as amounts of money that willing buyers pay to acquire something from willing sellers; the exchanges are made between independent parties and on the basis of commercial considerations only, sometimes called “at arm’s length.”

str:Code.com:Name: Face value
str:Code.com:Description: The amount of principal to be repaid (for example, the redemption amount of a bond). Sometimes called initial contractual value, for loans, the face value is the original amount of the loan as stated in the loan contract. If the loan is not fully disbursed, then the face value will include future disbursements, just as the face value of a zero-coupon bond includes interest that has not yet accrued.

str:Code.com:Name: Book value
str:Code.com:Description: Valuation used in business accounts, generally referring to the value recorded in the enterprise records. Book values may have different meanings because their values are influenced by the timing of acquisition, company takeovers, frequency of revaluations and tax and other regulations.

str:Code.com:Name: Fair value
str:Code.com:Description: Valuation that reflects the market-equivalent value of an asset or liability. It is defined as the price at which an asset could be exchanged, or a liability settled, between knowledgeable, willing parties in an arm's-length transaction.

str:Code.com:Name: Factor cost
str:Code.com:Description: Gross value added at factor cost is derived by subtracting "other taxes, less subsidies, on production" from gross value added at basic prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Cost insurance freight (CIF)
str:Code.com:Description: The c.i.f. price (i.e. cost, insurance and freight price) is the price of a good delivered at the frontier of the importing country, including any insurance and freight charges incurred to that point, or the price of a service delivered to a resident, before the payment of any import duties or other taxes on imports or trade and transport margins within the country.

str:Code.com:Name: Free on board (FOB)
str:Code.com:Description: The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country.

str:Code.com:Name: Fixed rate, face value
str:Code.com:Description: A debt instrument with a fixed interest rate and valued at its face value (the amount to be repaid).

str:Code.com:Name: Fixed rate, nominal value
str:Code.com:Description: A debt instrument with a fixed interest rate and valued at its nominal value (the original issue price).

str:Code.com:Name: Variable rate, face value
str:Code.com:Description: A debt instrument with a variable interest rate and valued at its face value.

str:Code.com:Name: Variable rate, nominal value
str:Code.com:Description: A debt instrument with a variable interest rate and valued at its nominal value.

str:Code.com:Name: Cash basis
str:Code.com:Description: Recording of flows at the time the cash is received or disbursed.

str:Code.com:Name: Previous year's replacement costs
str:Code.com:Description: The cost to replace an asset in the previous year, used for valuation purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Current replacement cost
str:Code.com:Description: Valuation method also known as current cost accounting, representing the cost to replace an asset at current prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Gold at market price
str:Code.com:Description: Gold at market price

str:Code.com:Name: National valuation
str:Code.com:Description: National valuation

str:Code.com:Name: Official gold price
str:Code.com:Description: Official gold price

str:Code.com:Name: Accrual basis
str:Code.com:Description: Recording of flows and changes in positions at the time economic value is created, transformed, exchanged, transferred or extinguished (which is not necessarily when cash or its equivalent is received or paid).

str:Code.com:Name: Net market to market value
str:Code.com:Description: Net mark-to-market value is the current value of an asset or portfolio after accounting for gains and losses based on market prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Not Applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Not Applicable

str:Code.com:Name: Not Available
str:Code.com:Description: Not Available

str:Code.com:Name: Market rate
str:Code.com:Description: Market rate is the prevailing interest rate or price at which financial instruments, goods, or services are traded in the open market based on supply and demand

str:Code.com:Name: Commitments Basis
str:Code.com:Description: In the commitments basis of recording, flows are recorded when an institutional unit has committed itself to a transaction.

str:Code.com:Name: Due-for-Payments Basis
str:Code.com:Description: In the due-for-payment basis of recording, flows that give rise to cash payments are recorded at the latest times they can be paid without incurring additional charges or penalties or, if sooner, when the cash payment is made.

str:Code.com:Name: Mixed Basis
str:Code.com:Description: Multiple basis of recording.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Security Classification
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist designates the level of sensitivity or confidentiality associated with data, dictating the protective measures required for its handling, storage, and transmission.

str:Code.com:Name: Public or Unrestricted
str:Code.com:Description: Information that has been published in full by the Fund and has been approved to be made available inside and outside the Fund without restriction is “Public” or “Unclassified.”

str:Code.com:Name: For official use only
str:Code.com:Description: Information that is intended for all staff, without restriction, and in some cases, may be shared with national officials or other authorized individuals or institutions must be classified “For Official Use Only.” This may be information not authorized for public use but to which staff members may have access without demonstrating a particular direct interest in the matter in question. Such information must be marked accordingly when necessary to avoid being mistaken for unclassified information.

str:Code.com:Name: Confidential
str:Code.com:Description: Information that is sensitive and to which access needs to be confined to those with a need to know about the matter in question must be classified “Confidential.” Given the desirability of broad dissemination of information within the Fund and of simplicity in the design of information handling systems, this classification should be used sparingly.

str:Code.com:Name: Strictly Confidential
str:Code.com:Description: Information to which access is to be limited to those having a strict need to know and which would not be adequately protected by the Confidential classification shall be classified “Strictly Confidential.” The classification “Strictly Confidential” should be used only exceptionally for information of the highest sensitivity. For example, Crown Jewels information must be classified as Strictly Confidential.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal and Confidential
str:Code.com:Description: Information that is both "Personal" and "Confidential." Information that is sensitive and to which access needs to be confined to those with a need to know about the matter in question must be classified “Confidential.” Given the desirability of broad dissemination of information within the Fund and of simplicity in the design of information handling systems, this classification should be used sparingly.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal and Strictly Confidential
str:Code.com:Description: Information that is both "Personal" and "Strictly Confidential." Information to which access is to be limited to those having a strict need to know and which would not be adequately protected by the Confidential classification shall be classified “Strictly Confidential.” The classification “Strictly Confidential” should be used only exceptionally for information of the highest sensitivity.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Observation Status
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist indicates the status of an observation within a dataset, such as whether it is a provisional value, an estimated value, a final figure, or a value where a time series break occurs. (Source: SDMX)

str:Code.com:Name: Normal value
str:Code.com:Description: To be used as default value if no value is provided or when no special coded qualification is assumed. Usually, it can be assumed that the source agency assigns sufficient confidence to the provided observation and/or the value is not expected to be dramatically revised.

str:Code.com:Name: Time series break
str:Code.com:Description: Observations are characterised as such when different content exists or a different methodology has been applied to this observation as compared with the preceding one (the one given for the previous period).

str:Code.com:Name: Definition differs
str:Code.com:Description: Used to indicate slight deviations from the established methodology (footnote-type information); these divergences do not imply a break in time series.

str:Code.com:Name: Estimated value
str:Code.com:Description: Observation obtained through an estimation methodology (e.g. to produce back-casts) or based on the use of a limited amount of data or ad hoc sampling and through additional calculations (e.g. to produce a value at an early stage of the production stage while not all data are available). It may also be used in case of experimental data (e.g. in the context of a pilot ahead of a full scale production process) or in case of data of (anticipated/assessed) low quality. If needed, additional information can be provided through free text using the COMMENT_OBS attribute at the observation level or at a higher level. This code is to be used when the estimation is done by a sender agency. When the imputation is carried out by a receiver agency in order to replace or fill gaps in reported data series, the flag to use is I "Value imputed by a receiving agency".

str:Code.com:Name: Forecast value
str:Code.com:Description: Value deemed to assess the magnitude which a quantity will assume at some future point of time (as distinct from "estimated value" which attempts to assess the magnitude of an already existent quantity).

str:Code.com:Name: Experimental value
str:Code.com:Description: Data collected on the basis of definitions or (alternative) collection methods under development. Data not of guaranteed quality as normally expected from provider.

str:Code.com:Name: Data included in another category
str:Code.com:Description: This code is used when data for a given category are missing and are included in another category, sub-total or total. Generally where code "K" is used there should be a corresponding code "W - Includes data from another category" assigned to the over-covered category. Implementers and data reporters should use the COMMENT_OBS observation-level attribute to specify under which category the data are included.

str:Code.com:Name: Includes data from another category
str:Code.com:Description: This code is used when data include another category, or go beyond the scope of the data collection and are therefore over-covered. Generally, where code "W" is used there should be a corresponding code "K - Data included in another category" assigned to the category which is under-covered. Implementers and data reporters should use the COMMENT_OBS observation-level attribute to specify which additional data are included.

str:Code.com:Name: Missing value
str:Code.com:Description: This code is to be used when no breakdown is made between the reasons why data are missing. Data can be missing due to many reasons: data cannot exist, data exist but are not collected (e.g. because they are below a certain threshold or subject to a derogation clause), data are unreliable, etc.

str:Code.com:Name: Missing value; data cannot exist
str:Code.com:Description: Used to denote empty cells resulting from the impossibility to collect a statistical value (e.g. a particular education level or type of institution may be not applicable to a given country's education system).

str:Code.com:Name: Provisional value
str:Code.com:Description: An observation is characterised as "provisional" when the source agency - while it bases its calculations on its standard production methodology - considers that the data, almost certainly, are expected to be revised.

str:Code.com:Name: Strike and other special events
str:Code.com:Description: Special circumstances (e.g. strike) affecting the observation or causing a missing value.

str:Code.com:Name: Missing value; data exist but were not collected
str:Code.com:Description: Used, for example, when some data are not reported/disseminated because they are below a certain threshold.

str:Code.com:Name: Missing value; holiday or weekend
str:Code.com:Description: Used in some daily data flows.

str:Code.com:Name: Missing value; suppressed
str:Code.com:Description: Used, for example, when data are suppressed due to statistical confidentiality considerations.

str:Code.com:Name: Derogation
str:Code.com:Description: Clause in an agreement (e.g. legal act, gentlemen's agreement) stating that some provisions in the agreement are not to be implemented by designated parties; these derogations may affect the observation or cause a missing value. In general, derogations are limited in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Not significant
str:Code.com:Description: Used to indicate a value which is not a "real" zero (e.g. a result of 0.0004 rounded to zero).

str:Code.com:Name: Low reliability
str:Code.com:Description: This indicates existing observations, but for which the user should also be aware of the low quality assigned.

str:Code.com:Name: Unvalidated value
str:Code.com:Description: Observation as received from the respondent without further evaluation of data quality.

str:Code.com:Name: Derived value
str:Code.com:Description: A value that is calculated or estimated, rather than directly observed or measured.

str:Code.com:Name: Value imputed
str:Code.com:Description: A value that is estimated or assigned when it cannot be directly measured or observed. Observation imputed by a receiving agency to replace or fill gaps in reported data series. This code is intended to cover all cases where a receiving agency publishes data about a sending agency that do not come from an official source in the sender agency's reporting framework. When the estimation is done by the sender agency, the flag to use is E "Estimated value".

str:Code.com:Name: Derived from counterpart country data
str:Code.com:Description: Data that is estimated or calculated based on information obtained from counterpart countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Unknown
str:Code.com:Description: This code is used when the observation status (rather than the value) is not available.

str:Code.com:Name: Observation status unavailable from external organisation
str:Code.com:Description: This code is used when the observation is from an external organization and the observation status (rather than the value) is not available. The observation may be from an external organization that does not use any system of flags when sharing data. Implementers should use attributes at the observation-level or at a higher-level such as: COMMENT_OBS, DATA_SOURCE or NATURE, to provide information on the source or nature.

str:Code.com:Name: Excludes one or more subcategories
str:Code.com:Description: This code is used when data exclude one or several subcategories that it should normally include, resulting in under-coverage of the category. This situation typically arises when the category is an aggregate and code ‘K’ or ‘O’ applies to one (or several) of its subcategories.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Functional category
str:Codelist.com:Description: A classification used to categorize investments based on their purpose or function, such as equity investments, debt investments, or other investments.

str:Code.com:Name: Allocated foreign exchange reserves
str:Code.com:Description: Those foreign exchange reserves for which the currency of denomination has been identified and reported in the Currency Composition of Official Foreign Exchange Reserves (COFER).

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment is a category of cross-border investment associated with an investor (or its affiliated enterprise) in one economy having control or significant degree of influence over the management of an investee enterprise resident in another economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). They can include monetary gold, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), reserve positions in the IMF, and other reserve assets. Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options
str:Code.com:Description: Financial derivatives are negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Employee stock options are options to buy the equity of a company, offered to employees of the company as a form of remuneration and often at a discount to the market value. If the financial derivative is linked to a reserve asset it is excluded from the value of this indicator.

str:Code.com:Name: Total foreign exchange reserves
str:Code.com:Description: Total of Reserve assets as reported in the International Liquidity database (formerly International Financial Statistics)

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Unallocated foreign exchange reserves
str:Code.com:Description: In the context of the Currency Composition of Official Foreign Exchange Reserves (COFER), Unallocated Foreign Exchange Reserves (FXR) is a residual category consisting of the difference between Total FXR sourced from the IMF's International Liquidity database and the total allocated reserves reported in COFER. It has two components: (i) the total FXR of nonreporting countries; and (ii) the discrepancy (if any) between the Total FXR of COFER-reporting countries and the total Allocated FXR reported in COFER.

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories
str:Code.com:Description: All categories of investment including direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives, employee stock options, reserve assets, and other investments.

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories (total) - sum of (direct investment, portfolio investment)
str:Code.com:Description: The combined total of direct investment and portfolio investment, representing the sum of these two categories.

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories (total) excluding direct investment
str:Code.com:Description: The combined total of all investment categories except for direct investment.

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories (total) excluding direct investment and reserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: The combined total of all investment categories except for direct investment and reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories (total) excluding financial derivatives and employee stock options
str:Code.com:Description: The combined total of all investment categories except for financial derivatives and employee stock options.

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories (total) excluding financial derivatives, employee stock options and reserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: The combined total of all investment categories except for financial derivatives, employee stock options, and reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories (total) excluding reserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: The combined total of all investment categories except for reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Change in reserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: The difference in the value of reserve assets between two points in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment + portfolio investment
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of direct investment and portfolio investment, indicating the total investment in these two categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment and equity security
str:Code.com:Description: Cross-border investment where the investor has control or significant influence, including ownership of equity securities in the enterprise.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment between two enterprises that are under control or influence of the same direct investor but where neither of the enterprises controls or influences the other (directly or indirectly).

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is nonresident
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is nonresident

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises; if ultimate controlling parent is resident in another EU country outside the euro area
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment between fellow enterprises; if ultimate controlling parent is resident in another EU country outside the euro area

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises; if ultimate controlling parent is resident in another euro area country
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment between fellow enterprises; if ultimate controlling parent is resident in another euro area country

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises; if ultimate controlling parent is resident outside the EU
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment between fellow enterprises; if ultimate controlling parent is resident outside the EU

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises; if ultimate controlling parent is resident outside the euro area
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment between fellow enterprises; if ultimate controlling parent is resident outside the euro area

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment enterprises in direct investor
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment by direct investment enterprises in direct investors (reverse investment)

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment excluding direct investment between fellow enterprises
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment, excluding those between fellow enterprises

str:Code.com:Name: Direct Investment, net acquisition of financial assets less net incurrence of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The net change in financial assets and liabilities resulting from direct investment transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investor in direct investment enterprises
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment by direct investors in direct investment enterprises.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Official financial instruments linked to other specific instruments, indicators, or commodities, and employee stock options provided as a form of remuneration.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options (unofficial)
str:Code.com:Description: Unofficial financial instruments linked to other specific instruments, indicators, or commodities, and employee stock options provided as a form of remuneration.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives and employee stock options
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities, through which specific risks can be traded in their own right.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign direct investment and equity security liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities arising from foreign direct investment and cross-border transactions involving equity securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Inward direct investment
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment where the entity controlling or influencing the investment decision is resident abroad.

str:Code.com:Name: Inward direct investment: debt-creating liabilities to foreign direct investors
str:Code.com:Description: Inward direct investment that results in debt obligations to foreign direct investors.

str:Code.com:Name: Inward direct investment, excluding debt-creating liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Inward direct investment that does not result in debt obligations to foreign direct investors.

str:Code.com:Name: Net investment in foreign portfolio assets
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of investments in foreign portfolio assets, after accounting for acquisitions and disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Not allocated/unspecified
str:Code.com:Description: Investment or assets that are not specifically categorized or detailed.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Functional category is not applicable in the context.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Other investments made by official institutions, including loans, deposits, and trade credits.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment (private)
str:Code.com:Description: Other investments made by private entities, including loans, deposits, and trade credits.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment (unofficial)
str:Code.com:Description: Other investments made by unofficial entities, including loans, deposits, and trade credits.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment: loans, deposits, and trade credits
str:Code.com:Description: A residual investment category that includes positions and transactions in loans, deposits, and trade credits.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment: loans, deposits, and trade credits (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Official positions and transactions in loans, deposits, and trade credits categorized as other investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment: loans, deposits, and trade credits (unofficial)
str:Code.com:Description: Unofficial positions and transactions in loans, deposits, and trade credits categorized as other investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment: liabilities to foreign banks
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities arising from other investment transactions owed to foreign banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Outward direct investment
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment where the entity controlling or influencing the investment decision is resident in the domestic economy

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio Investment (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Cross-border transactions and positions in debt or equity securities made by official institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio Investment (private)
str:Code.com:Description: Cross-border transactions and positions in debt or equity securities made by private entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio Investment (unofficial)
str:Code.com:Description: Cross-border transactions and positions in debt or equity securities made by unofficial entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment by type of creditor
str:Code.com:Description: Classification of portfolio investment based on the type of creditor involved in the transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment liabilities to foreign banks
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities arising from portfolio investment transactions owed to foreign banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets including the country's position in the IMF, which can be used to meet balance of payments financing needs.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, excluding monetary gold
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets excluding holdings of monetary gold.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets in SDR basket
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets that are part of the Special Drawing Rights (SDR) basket.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs)
str:Code.com:Description: International reserve assets created by the IMF to supplement member countries' official reserves.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves data template
str:Code.com:Description: A standardized format for reporting and presenting reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency reserves in convertible foreign currencies
str:Code.com:Description: Foreign currency reserves in convertible foreign currencies

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment liabilities to official creditors
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities arising from portfolio investment transactions owed to official creditors.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment liabilities to other creditors
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities arising from portfolio investment transactions owed to creditors other than foreign banks or official creditors.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment: liabilities to other sectors
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities arising from other investment transactions owed to other sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), adjusted with IMF records
str:Code.com:Description: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) included in reserve assets, reflecting adjustments based on alignment with International Monetary Fund (IMF) records.

str:Code.com:Name: Other Investment: liabilities to official creditors
str:Code.com:Description: Other Investment: liabilities to official creditors

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment adjusted for liabilities constituting foreign authorities' reserves (LCFAR)
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment adjusted for liabilities constituting foreign authorities' reserves (LCFAR).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment adjusted for liabilities constituting foreign authorities' reserves (LCFAR)
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment adjusted for liabilities constituting foreign authorities' reserves (LCFAR).

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves minus liabilities constituting foreign authorities' reserves (LCFAR)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves minus liabilities constituting foreign authorities' reserves (LCFAR).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment, excluding exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment, excluding exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Direct investment, excluding exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) adjusted using IMF accounting records.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives and employee stock options (other than reserves)
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Excludes reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign exchange reserves that have been imputed
str:Code.com:Description: Foreign Exchange Reserves (FXR) are reserve assets other than monetary gold, SDRs, and reserve position in the IMF. Reserve assets are external assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. This represents the foreign exchange reserves that have been imputed by IMF staff.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Decimals
str:Codelist.com:Description: This code list provides a list of values showing the number of decimal digits used in the data. This code list was released in 2009. More information about this code list and SDMX code lists in general (e.g. list of generic codes for expressing general concepts like "Total", "Unknown", etc.; syntaxes for the creation of further codes; general guidelines for the creation of SDMX code lists) can be found at this address: https://sdmx.org/?page_id=4345.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Unit Multiplier
str:Codelist.com:Description: This code list provides code values for indicating the magnitude in the units of measurement. More information about this code list and SDMX code lists in general (e.g. list of generic codes for expressing general concepts like "Total", "Unknown", etc.; syntaxes for the creation of further codes; general guidelines for the creation of SDMX code lists) can be found at this address: https://sdmx.org/?page_id=4345.

str:Code.com:Description: يُعبر عنها بالترميز العلمي بوصفها 10 أس صفر (10^صفر)
В математических терминах представляется как десять в нулевой степени (10^0)
Representado em notação científica como dez elevado à potência de zero (10^0)
科学的表記では、10の0乗 (10^0)と表記
In scientific notation, expressed as ten raised to the power of zero (10^0)
En notation scientifique, exprimé comme dix élevé à la puissance zéro (10^0)
En notación científica, expresado como diez a la potencia de cero (10^0)
在科学记数法中，表示为10的零次方（10^0）

Dezenas de biliões
十兆
Tens of trillions
Dizaines de milliers de milliards
Decenas de billones
十万亿
str:Code.com:Description: يُعبر عنها بالترميز العلمي بوصفها 10 أس 13 (10^13)
В математических терминах представляется как десять в тринадцатой степени (10^13)
Representado em notação científica como dez elevado à potência de treze (10^13)
科学的表記では、10の13乗 (10^13)と表記
In scientific notation, expressed as ten raised to the power of thirteen (10^13)
En notation scientifique, exprimé comme dix élevé à la puissance treize (10^13)
En notación científica, expresado como diez a la potencia de trece (10^13)
在科学记数法中，表示为10的13次方（10^13）

str:Codelist.com:Name: Prices
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents different types of prices or price adjustments, including those that have been adjusted for inflation (real or constant prices) and those that have not (nominal or current prices). It also includes various methods for adjusting prices for inflation, such as using deflators or converting to a constant base period.

str:Code.com:Name: Constant prices
str:Code.com:Description: Values that have had the effect of price changes removed, expressed as time series in constant prices (fixed base) or chain-linked volume terms, or as non-time series in previous year's prices

str:Code.com:Name: Closing price
str:Code.com:Description: An adjusted deflator index that has been recalibrated to a new base period.

str:Code.com:Name: Current prices
str:Code.com:Description: Values expressed without any adjustment for price changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Price index
str:Code.com:Description: A measure that examines the weighted average of prices of a basket of consumer goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Breakeven external oil price
str:Code.com:Description: The oil price at which a country's external balances are neither in surplus nor deficit.

str:Code.com:Name: Breakeven fiscal oil price
str:Code.com:Description: The oil price at which a country's fiscal balance is neither in surplus nor deficit.

str:Code.com:Name: Volume
str:Code.com:Description: The quantity of goods or services produced or traded, often measured in terms of physical units or monetary value.

str:Code.com:Name: Value
str:Code.com:Description: The value of a product reflects its price multipled by its quantity.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Terms or data that do not apply to the current context or situation.

str:Code.com:Name: Unit prices
str:Code.com:Description: The unitary price of an item or commodity.

str:Code.com:Name: Unit value
str:Code.com:Description: The unit value (value / quantity) of an item or commodity.

str:Code.com:Name: Export price
str:Code.com:Description: The export price of a commodity.

str:Code.com:Name: Import price
str:Code.com:Description: The import price of a commodity.

str:Code.com:Name: Current prices in US Dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Measured in current prices denominated in US Dollar.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Organization
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist identifies and categorizes institutions relevant to economic, financial, and statistical data, providing standardized codes and corresponding names. It includes international organizations, central banks, and government institutions involved in data collection and dissemination.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Maturity of Financial Instrument
str:Codelist.com:Description: A classification of financial instruments based on their remaining time to maturity or expiration.

str:Code.com:Name: Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: The original contract specifies a maturity of one year or less, or repayment on demand (short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Short-term, remaining maturity basis
str:Code.com:Description: Short term on a remaining maturity basis. Indicates residual maturity of one year or less at reference date.

str:Code.com:Name: Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: The original contract specifies a maturity of more than one year, or has no stated maturity (long-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Bid yield to maturity
str:Code.com:Description: The bid yield is the yield to maturity corresponding to the current bid price (the price at which a bond can be purchased). YTM is calculated implicitly from the current market price, the remaining term to maturity, and the amount and frequency of coupon payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Yield to maturity
str:Code.com:Description: A bond’s yield to maturity (YTM) is the internal rate of return (IRR) that equates the present value of all future cash flows from the bond, including coupon payments and principal, to its current market price

str:Code.com:Name: All original maturities
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments with their initial maturity at issuance.

str:Code.com:Name: All original maturities with long-term residual maturity over 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments initially issued for any term, with more than one year remaining until maturity.

str:Code.com:Name: All original maturities with short-term residual maturity up to 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Instruments initially issued for any term, with up to one year remaining until maturity.

str:Code.com:Name: Long-term original maturity over 1 year with long-term residual maturity over 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Instruments initially issued for more than one year, with more than one year remaining until maturity.

str:Code.com:Name: Long-term by original maturity with payment due in one year or less
str:Code.com:Description: Instruments initially issued for more than one year, with one year or less remaining until maturity.

str:Code.com:Name: Not allocated/unspecified
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments or data that have not been categorized or specified.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Not applicable

str:Code.com:Name: Total residual maturity
str:Code.com:Description: Remaining (residual) maturity aggregated across all instruments in the category.

str:Code.com:Name: Other long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other long-term

str:Code.com:Name: Other short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other short-term

str:Code.com:Name: Over 3 months and up to 1 Year
str:Code.com:Description: Under the original contract, payment falls due in more than three months and within one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Up to 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Under the original contract, payment falls due within one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Agreed maturity
str:Code.com:Description: Maturity agreed between the parties to the transaction.

str:Code.com:Name: Up to 1 month
str:Code.com:Description: Under the original contract, payment falls due within one month.

str:Code.com:Name: More than 3 months and up to 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Under the original contract, payment falls due in more than three months and within one year.

str:Code.com:Name: More than 1 and up to 3 months
str:Code.com:Description: Under the original contract, payment falls due in more than one month and within three months.

str:Code.com:Name: Falling due within 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Under the original contract, payment falls due within one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Residual maturity more than 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Remaining or residual maturity (payment due) specified on the original contract falls due within 1 year

str:Code.com:Name: Over 5 years
str:Code.com:Description: Indicates a financial instrument with an original or remaining maturity exceeding 5 years.

str:Code.com:Name: Short to medium term
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to instruments with a maturity period that spans from short-term to medium-term duration.

str:Code.com:Name: Short-term by original maturity
str:Code.com:Description: The original contract specifies a maturity of one year or less, or repayment on demand (short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Overnight
str:Code.com:Description: Instruments with a maturity of one day, repayable on the next business day.

str:Code.com:Name: Up to 2 years
str:Code.com:Description: Long-term instruments with maturity of up to two years.

str:Code.com:Name: 1 Year
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term instruments with maturity of up to one year.

str:Code.com:Name: 1 Month
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term instruments with maturity of up to one month.

str:Code.com:Name: 6 Months
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term instruments with maturity of up to six months.

str:Code.com:Name: 3 Months
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term instruments with maturity of up to three months.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Commodity
str:Codelist.com:Description: Comprehensive list of all the different commodity codes used in IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Agricultural raw materials
str:Code.com:Description: Agricultural Raw Materials Index, 2016 = 100, includes Timber, Cotton, Wool, Rubber, and Hides Price Indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. str:Code.com:Name: Other food and beverage
str:Code.com:Description: Other Food and Beverage Price Index, 2016 = 100. This indicator reflects price movements in food and beverages not included in the main Food and Beverage indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Food and beverage, excluding others
str:Code.com:Description: Food and Beverage Price Index, 2016 = 100, excluding the Other Food and Beverage Index. This indicator includes the main Food and Beverage indices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Other fertilizers
str:Code.com:Description: Other Fertilizers Index, 2016 = 100. This indicator reflects price movements in fertilizers not included in the main Fertilizer index (DAP, Potash, Urea). This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Fertilizers, excluding others
str:Code.com:Description: Fertilizers Price Index, 2016 = 100, excluding the Other Fertilizers Index. This indicator includes DAP, Potash, and Urea prices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Other raw materials
str:Code.com:Description: Other Raw Materials Index, 2016 = 100. This indicator reflects price movements in agricultural raw materials not included in the main Agricultural Raw Materials Index (Timber, Cotton, Wool, Rubber, Hides). This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Raw materials, excluding others
str:Code.com:Description: Raw Materials Price Index, 2016 = 100, excluding the Other Raw Materials Index. This indicator includes Timber, Cotton, Wool, Rubber, and Hides prices. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Fishmeal: Peru Fish
str:Code.com:Description: Fishmeal, Peru Fish meal/pellets 65% protein, CIF, Price Index, 2016 = 100

str:Code.com:Name: Barley Mpls Terminal prices
str:Code.com:Description: Barley Mpls Terminal prices U$/Bushel.

str:Code.com:Name: Coffee, Average of OFECPAR and COFECPRB
str:Code.com:Description: Coffee, Average of Arabica, Other Milds, and Robusta.

str:Code.com:Name: Leg of lamb
str:Code.com:Description: Leg of lamb.

str:Code.com:Name: Apple French PPI
str:Code.com:Description: Apple backward index.

str:Code.com:Name: Milk US PPI
str:Code.com:Description: Milk backward index.

str:Code.com:Name: PEA French PPI
str:Code.com:Description: PEA backward index.

str:Code.com:Name: Tomato French PPI
str:Code.com:Description: Tomato backward index.

str:Code.com:Name: Plywood, Malaysian
str:Code.com:Description: Plywood; Tokyo, Lauan, type 1, 12mm.

str:Code.com:Name: Wood Pulp
str:Code.com:Description: Wood Pulp($/MT).

str:Codelist.com:Name: Instrument and assets classification
str:Codelist.com:Description: Because of the symmetry of financial assets and liabilities, the term ‘instrument’ is used to relate to both the asset and the liability aspect of financial transactions, other flows and financial balance sheets. (Source: Handbook of National Accounting, 2013)

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Not relevant or not suitable to the context.

str:Code.com:Name: Net financial worth
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between total financial assets and total financial liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Net financial worth: excluding pension liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between total financial assets and total financial liabilities, excluding pension liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Economic resources owned by an entity that can provide future benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisition of foreign assets
str:Code.com:Description: The purchase or acquisition of financial or non-financial assets located in foreign countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Total assets (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of all economic resources owned by an entity, before deducting any liabilities or depreciation. This includes both financial and non-financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets: short position
str:Code.com:Description: A position in financial markets where the value of assets sold exceeds the value of assets owned.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets excluding land and natural resources
str:Code.com:Description: Economic resources owned by an entity, excluding land and natural resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities or debt to banks
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed to banking institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock of reserves
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of reserve assets held by a central bank or monetary authority. These assets typically include monetary gold, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), foreign currency holdings, and reserve positions in the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Use of IMF Credit and Loans
str:Code.com:Description: Use of IMF Credit and Loans comprises of a member country's purchases as well as borrowings under the various lMF's lending instruments to meet the different needs and specific circumstances of its members. IMF members have access to the General Resources Account (GRA) on non-concessional terms (market-based interest rates), but the IMF also provides concessional financial support (currently at zero interest rates) through the Poverty Reduction and Growth Trust (PRGT) and the recently established Resilience and Sustainability Trust (RST).

str:Code.com:Name: Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Financial claims or entitlements held by an entity.

str:Code.com:Name: Contingent Liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Potential obligations that may arise based on the outcome of future events.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, inter-company lending
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents arising from inter-company lending.

str:Code.com:Name: Total debt claims on nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of debt claims held against nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt
str:Code.com:Description: Money borrowed by one party from another, typically evidenced by a note which specifies, among other things, the principal amount, interest rate, and date of repayment.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents: inter-company lending
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents arising from inter-company lending.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt (at market value)
str:Code.com:Description: The value of debt calculated based on the current market price of the debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Total financial assets/liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of financial assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary gold and SDRs
str:Code.com:Description: Gold held as a reserve asset and Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) allocated by the International Monetary Fund (IMF).

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary gold
str:Code.com:Description: Gold held as a reserve asset.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary gold: unallocated gold accounts
str:Code.com:Description: Gold accounts where the holder owns a share of a pool of gold rather than specific gold bars.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary gold: of which gold under swap for cash collateral
str:Code.com:Description: Portion of monetary gold that is held under swap agreements for cash collateral.

str:Code.com:Name: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund and allocated to its members to supplement existing reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs): allocations: accumulation of arrears
str:Code.com:Description: Accumulated arrears related to allocations of Special Drawing Rights by the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs): allocations
str:Code.com:Description: SDR allocations are reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and allocated to its member countries to supplement existing reserve assets. Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) are international reserve assets that the IMF allocates to its members as a form of supplementary liquidity. These allocations are based on each member country's IMF quota and are intended to provide additional liquidity and support to the global financial system.

str:Code.com:Name: Gold (including gold deposits and, if appropriate, gold swapped)
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of gold held by an entity, including physical gold, gold deposits held with other institutions, and any gold held under swap agreements. This includes gold held as a reserve asset and other forms of gold holdings.

str:Code.com:Name: SDRs holdings
str:Code.com:Description: SDR Holdings refer to an interest-bearing reserve asset created by the IMF to supplement members’ reserve assets. It is a purely official asset and can only be held and used by members in the SDR department, the IMF, and certain designated official entities. SDR holdings can be exchanged with other members for freely usable currency.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary gold
str:Code.com:Description: Gold held as a reserve asset.

str:Code.com:Name: Currency and deposits
str:Code.com:Description: Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: IMF holdings of currency
str:Code.com:Description: IMF’s Holdings of Currencies represent members’ paid-in quota subscription in local currencies that are held in designated depository agencies (e.g., central banks). Currency holdings are held in three accounts: IMF No. 1, IMF No. 2, and IMF Securities Accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Inter-bank positions
str:Code.com:Description: Financial positions held by banks with other banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Currency and deposits: fixed rate
str:Code.com:Description: Currency and deposits that earn interest at a fixed rate.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposits
str:Code.com:Description: Non-negotiable contracts that represent the placements of funds available for later withdrawal. They are claims on the central bank, other deposit-taking corporations, other financial corporations, and, in some cases, other institutional units.

str:Code.com:Name: Currency and deposits: variable rate
str:Code.com:Description: Currency and deposits that earn interest at a variable rate.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities
str:Code.com:Description: Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: bonds and notes
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities that are long-term obligations issued by a borrower to raise capital, typically with a fixed interest rate, such as bonds and notes.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: financial derivatives
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments whose value is derived from the performance of underlying debt securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: accumulation of arrears
str:Code.com:Description: The buildup of unpaid interest or principal on debt securities that has become overdue.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: accumulation of arrears, interest / coupon
str:Code.com:Description: The buildup of unpaid interest or coupon payments on debt securities that has become overdue.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: accumulation of arrears, original interest / coupon
str:Code.com:Description: The buildup of unpaid original interest or coupon payments on debt securities that has become overdue.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: accumulation of arrears, principal
str:Code.com:Description: The buildup of unpaid principal on debt securities that has become overdue.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: accumulation of arrears, penalty interest
str:Code.com:Description: The buildup of unpaid penalty interest on debt securities that has become overdue.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: fixed rate
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities that pay a fixed rate of interest.

str:Code.com:Name: High quality tradable securities
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments that are highly rated and can be easily traded in financial markets.

str:Code.com:Name: Issues of new debt securities
str:Code.com:Description: The process of offering new debt instruments for sale to investors.

str:Code.com:Name: Issues of new debt securities on behalf of authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Issuing new debt instruments on behalf of governmental or regulatory authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: (unofficial)
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities that are not issued by official entities or private entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities issued by official entities, such as governments or central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: (private)
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities issued by private entities, such as corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: short or negative positions
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities positions where the holder has sold securities they do not own, betting on a decline in the price, or positions that result in a liability greater than the value of the securities held.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities and loans
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments representing a debt obligation, such as bonds and notes, and loans which involve borrowing a sum of money that is expected to be paid back typically with interest.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: variable rate
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities that pay interest at a variable rate, which can change over time based on a benchmark rate or index.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities that exclude those related to exceptional financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans
str:Code.com:Description: Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back typically with interest.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit lines
str:Code.com:Description: Pre-approved lines of credit that can be drawn upon as needed by the borrower, and in some cases when conditions are met.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans, credit, and loans with the IMF (other than reserves)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments including loans, credit, and loans with the International Monetary Fund (IMF), excluding those held as reserves.

str:Code.com:Name: Net credit and loans from the IMF (excluding reserve position)
str:Code.com:Description: The net amount of credit and loans received from the International Monetary Fund (IMF), excluding those held in reserve positions.

str:Code.com:Name: Concessional loans
str:Code.com:Description: Loans provided on terms more generous than market conditions, often with lower (or zero) interest rates or longer repayment periods.

str:Code.com:Name: Implicit transfers resulting from loans at concessional interest rates
str:Code.com:Description: Financial benefits given to borrowers through loans offered at below-market interest rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans: fixed rate
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that have a fixed interest rate for the entire term of the loan.

str:Code.com:Name: Lending to the IMF (GRA)
str:Code.com:Description: Lending to the IMF involves IMF member countries providing financial support to the IMF to enable the IMF to fulfill its mandate of providing loans and assistance to member countries facing balance of payments problems or other economic challenges.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonperforming loan assets
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are in default or close to being in default because the borrower has not made scheduled payments for a specified period.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans: variable rate
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that have an interest rate that can fluctuate over the term of the loan.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Residual claims on the assets of the institutional units that issued the relevant instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity
str:Code.com:Description: All instruments and records acknowledging ownership rights and claims on the residual value of a corporation or quasi-corporation after the claims of all creditors have been met. Ownership of equity is usually evidenced by shares, stocks, participations, depository receipts or similar documents.

str:Code.com:Name: Other equity
str:Code.com:Description: Equity holdings that do not fall under the main categories of shares or stocks.

str:Code.com:Name: Shares and other equity
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments representing ownership in a corporation, including shares and other forms of equity.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity securities: excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Equity securities that exclude those related to exceptional financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment fund shares/units
str:Code.com:Description: Shares or units in a fund that pools investor money to purchase securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Money market fund shares/units
str:Code.com:Description: Shares or units in funds that invest in short-term, high-liquidity financial instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Reinvestment of earnings: of which money market fund shares or units
str:Code.com:Description: Reinvested earnings specifically in money market fund shares or units.

str:Code.com:Name: Money market fund shares/units: debt securities
str:Code.com:Description: Shares or units in money market funds that primarily invest in debt securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Reinvestment of earnings: of which special purpose entities
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of reinvested earnings from investment fund shares or units that is specifically attributed to special purpose entities. Special purpose entities are legal entities created to fulfill specific, narrow, or temporary objectives.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment fund shares/units: reinvestment of earnings
str:Code.com:Description: Shares or units in investment funds acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares/units: other than reinvestment of earnings
str:Code.com:Description: Equity and investment fund shares/units excluding those acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares/units: reinvestment of earnings
str:Code.com:Description: Equity and investment fund shares/units acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares/units (unofficial)
str:Code.com:Description: Equity and investment fund shares/units of private sector

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares/units (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Equity and investment fund shares/units of public sector

str:Code.com:Name: Equity: short or negative positions
str:Code.com:Description: Positions where the holder has sold equity they do not own, betting on a decline in the price, or positions that result in a liability greater than the value of the equity held.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares/units: excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Equity and investment fund shares/units that exclude those related to exceptional financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares equity: securities other than investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Equity securities that do not include investment fund shares.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares equity: securities other than investment fund shares, listed
str:Code.com:Description: Listed equity securities that do not include investment fund shares.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and investment fund shares equity: securities other than investment fund shares, unlisted
