str:Code.com:Description: Unlisted equity securities that do not include investment fund shares.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Financial asset category, grouping together nonlife insurance technical reserves; life insurance and annuity entitlements; pension entitlements; claims of pension funds on pension sponsor; entitlements to nonpension benefits, and provisions for calls under standardized guarantees.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonlife insurance technical reserves
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves held by insurance companies to cover claims related to nonlife insurance policies.

str:Code.com:Name: Life insurance and annuities entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: Financial claims of policyholders against an institutional unit offering life insurance or providing annuities.

str:Code.com:Name: Pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: The obligations of pension plans to provide benefits to their members.

str:Code.com:Name: Claims of pension funds on pension managers
str:Code.com:Description: The financial claims held by pension funds on pension managers.

str:Code.com:Name: Entitlements to nonpension benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Rights to receive benefits other than pensions, such as healthcare or unemployment benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Provisions for calls under standardized guarantee schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Funds set aside to cover potential calls under standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes: fixed rate
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance, pension, and standardized guarantee schemes that offer fixed interest rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Life insurance and annuity entitlements, pension entitlements, claims of pension funds on pension managers and entitlements to non-pension benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Combined financial obligations including life insurance and annuity entitlements, pension entitlements, and claims of pension funds on pension managers and entitlements to non-pension benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Claims of pension funds on pension managers and entitlements to non-pension benefits
str:Code.com:Description: The financial claims held by pension funds against pension managers, including entitlements to non-pension benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance technical reserves
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves that insurance companies set aside to cover future claims and policyholder benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes: variable rate
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance, pension, and standardized guarantee schemes that offer variable interest rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Financial arrangements that include insurance policies, pension plans, and standardized guarantees.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and including employee stock options.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial instruments whose value is derived from the performance of underlying assets, indexes, or interest rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) options
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Options contracts that are financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) forward type contracts
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Forward type contracts that are financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves)
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves.

str:Code.com:Name: Employee stock options
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments granted to employees as part of their compensation package, giving them the right to buy company stock at a predetermined price.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents total
str:Code.com:Description: The aggregate notional value of all foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options: official
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options: unofficial
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options.

str:Code.com:Name: Other accounts receivable/payable
str:Code.com:Description: Accounts that are either receivable or payable but not classified under specific categories like trade receivables/payables or loans.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade credits and advances
str:Code.com:Description: Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress.

str:Code.com:Name: Other accounts receivable/payable: excluding trade credits and advances
str:Code.com:Description: Miscellaneous other items receivable or payable such as liabilities for taxes, emissions permits, cash collateral received by nondeposit-taking corporations (except those for reverse transactions), purchase and sale of securities, securities lending fees, gold loan fees, crypto lending fees, wages and salaries, dividends, and social contributions that have accrued but have not yet been paid. It also includes prepayments of those items.

str:Code.com:Name: Other accounts payable
str:Code.com:Description: Accounts that are payable but not classified under specific categories like trade payables or loans.

str:Code.com:Name: Other accounts receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Accounts that are receivable but not classified under specific categories like trade receivables or loans.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Financial claims and gold bullion held by monetary authorities as a reserve asset.

str:Code.com:Name: All financial instruments
str:Code.com:Description: The full range of financial contracts made between institutional units. They may be recorded on the balance sheet or off the balance sheet (e.g., constructive and contingent assets and liabilities).

str:Code.com:Name: Overdue obligations to the IMF
str:Code.com:Description: 1 fund shares, insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes, financial derivatives, 2 and other accounts receivable/payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Net implicit obligations for social security benefits
str:Code.com:Description: The net present value of future obligations for social security benefits that are not explicitly recorded as liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt Instruments: of which debt securities
str:Code.com:Description: A specific category of debt instruments that are classified as debt securities.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt a country owes to foreign creditors.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: arrears
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of external debt that is overdue and unpaid.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: debtor based
str:Code.com:Description: External debt classified based on the identity of the debtor.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: debtor based, official
str:Code.com:Description: External debt classified based on the identity of the official debtor, such as the government.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: official
str:Code.com:Description: External debt that is owed by official entities, such as governments or central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross external debt
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt that a country owes to foreign creditors.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial investment
str:Code.com:Description: Allocation of money with the expectation of a financial return.

str:Code.com:Name: Arrears
str:Code.com:Description: Unpaid overdue amounts, such as interest or principal on loans or debt securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears on original interest/coupon from debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: The accumulation of unpaid original interest or coupon payments on debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears on penalty interest from debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: The accumulation of unpaid penalty interest on debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears on principal from debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: The accumulation of unpaid principal amounts on debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears on other debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: The accumulation of unpaid amounts on various types of debt instruments, excluding the original interest or coupon payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Arrears on original interest
str:Code.com:Description: Unpaid overdue original interest on loans or debt securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Arrears on penalty interest
str:Code.com:Description: Unpaid overdue penalty interest on loans or debt securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Arrears on principal
str:Code.com:Description: Unpaid overdue principal amounts on loans or debt securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve position in the IMF
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs. The amount is as reported by the reporting economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve position in the IMF (adjusted using IMF records)
str:Code.com:Description: The reserve position in the IMF, adjusted according to IMF records, reflecting a member country's financial commitment to the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. In the external accounts, trade credit and advances are in a separate category from other accounts receivable/payable and are also included in debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. In the external accounts, trade credit and advances are in a separate category from other accounts receivable/payable and are also included in debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt instruments (excluding exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Debt instruments that exclude those related to exceptional financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Other debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Debt instruments not classified under any other specific category.

str:Code.com:Name: Other debt instruments: new drawings / deposits
str:Code.com:Description: New drawings or deposits under other debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other debt instruments: new drawings / deposits on behalf of authorities
str:Code.com:Description: New drawings or deposits under other debt instruments made on behalf of authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other debt instruments: prepayment
str:Code.com:Description: Debt instruments that include prepayments.

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears on debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: The process of accumulating unpaid interest or principal on debt instruments that are past due.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assets that are in the form of money or can be easily converted to money.

str:Code.com:Name: Other liabilities/assets (GFS concept)
str:Code.com:Description: Other liabilities or assets according to the Government Finance Statistics (GFS) concept.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt
str:Code.com:Description: Amount of debt held by an institutional unit after subtracting the value of financial assets it holds corresponding to debt liabilities of other institutional units

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt: creditor based
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt categorized based on the creditor.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt: commercial banks, creditor based
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt owed by commercial banks, categorized based on the creditor.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt: commercial banks
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt owed by commercial banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt: commercial banks, debtor based
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt owed by commercial banks, categorized based on the debtor.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt (official): debtor based
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt owed by official entities, categorized based on the debtor.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt: arrears
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of net external debt that is overdue and unpaid.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt (official)
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt owed by official entities, such as governments or central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt (official): creditor based
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt owed by official entities, categorized based on the creditor.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt: private debt
str:Code.com:Description: Other categories of net external debt owed by private sector entities.

str:Code.com:Name: WEO External Debt Indicators
str:Code.com:Description: WEO External Debt Indicators.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt: debt to official creditors
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt owed to official creditors, such as governments or international organizations.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external debt: debt to private creditors
str:Code.com:Description: The net external debt owed to private sector creditors.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity and debt instruments (BOP methodology)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments representing ownership interest (equity) and debt obligations as defined in the latest balance of payments manual.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment fund shares/units: life insurance, and pension schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Shares or units in investment funds that are part of life insurance and pension schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt (non-standard definition)
str:Code.com:Description: Debt defined in a manner that does not conform to standard definitions, potentially including additional or alternative criteria.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets in remaining net flows of households deprecated
str:Code.com:Description: The value of household assets, excluding any depreciated amounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Securities (equity, investment fund shares/units and debt securities)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments representing ownership in a company (equity), pooled investments (investment fund shares/units), and borrowings (debt securities) that provide returns to the holder.

str:Code.com:Name: Securities other than shares
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments representing ownership that do not constitute shares, such as bonds and notes.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets (currency, deposits, securities, financial derivatives and other claims)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets that include currency, deposits, securities, financial derivatives, and other claims.

str:Code.com:Name: Securities; currency and deposits
str:Code.com:Description: Financial instruments that include both securities and currency and deposits.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets (financial derivatives, loans to non-banks and other)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets that include financial derivatives, loans to non-bank entities, and other assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets other than gold, SDRs, IMF reserve position, currency, deposits, securities, financial derivatives and loans (to non-banks)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets held by a country that exclude gold, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), IMF reserve positions, currency, deposits, securities, financial derivatives, and loans made to non-bank entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities related to reserve assets owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit and loans from the IMF: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents arising from credit and loans provided by the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposits: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Deposits that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans, Other (reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents)
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans, Repo Loans (reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents)
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents, specifically those involved in repurchase agreements (repo loans).

str:Code.com:Name: Other short-term foreign currency liabilities to nonresidents: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term foreign currency liabilities to nonresidents that are classified as reserve-related liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets: currency and deposits
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets in the form of currency and deposits.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets: currency and deposits claims on monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets in the form of currency and deposits with claims on monetary authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets: currency and deposits claims on other entities
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets in the form of currency and deposits with claims on entities other than monetary authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets: securities
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets in the form of securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets: equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets in the form of equity and investment fund shares.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets: equity and investment fund shares, of which securities under repo for cash collateral
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets in the form of equity and investment fund shares, specifically those under repurchase agreements (repos) for cash collateral.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets: financial derivatives
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets in the form of financial derivatives.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets: other claims
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets that include other types of claims not classified under specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other reserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve assets not classified under specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other foreign currency assets not included in reserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: Foreign currency assets, including securities, deposits, loans, financial derivatives, and gold, that are not part of official reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Currency and deposits: loans and securities
str:Code.com:Description: Currency and deposits that are invested in loans and securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other, mainly inter-company loans
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are primarily inter-company but not classified under specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign exchange
str:Code.com:Description: The global marketplace for buying and selling national currencies.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities or debt to official creditors
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed to official entities such as governments or international organizations.

str:Code.com:Name: Net worth
str:Code.com:Description: The total assets minus total liabilities of an entity.

str:Code.com:Name: Net worth excluding land, natural resources and pension liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total assets minus total liabilities, excluding land, natural resources, and pension liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Maastricht debt
str:Code.com:Description: The total public sector debt of European Union member states, as defined by the Maastricht Treaty.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D4)
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities, including various economic flows and transactions, as categorized under classification D4.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D1) debt liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification D1, specifically including D1 debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Holding gains and losses gross debt (D1) holding gains and losses (D1) debt liabilities holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: The increase or decrease in the value of gross debt (D1) and (D1) debt liabilities due to changes in market prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D4) other economic flows, (D1) debt liabilities other economic flows
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D1), including other economic flows and (D1) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D1) other volume changes, (D1) debt liabilities other volume changes
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D1), including other volume changes and D1 debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D1) transactions, (D1) debt liabilities transactions
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D1), including transactions and D1 debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D2), debt liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D2), specifically including (D2) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Holding gains and losses gross debt (D2) holding gains and losses (D2) debt liabilities holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: The increase or decrease in the value of gross debt (D2) and (D2) debt liabilities due to changes in market prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D2) other economic flows, (D2) debt liabilities other economic flows
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D2), including other economic flows and (D2) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D2) other volume changes, (D2) debt liabilities other volume changes
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D2), including other volume changes and (D2) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D2) transactions, (D2) debt liabilities transactions
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D2), including transactions and (D2) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D3) debt liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification D3, specifically including D3 debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Holding gains and losses gross debt (D3) holding gains and losses (D3) debt liabilities holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: The increase or decrease in the value of gross debt (D3) and (D3) debt liabilities due to changes in market prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D3) other economic flows, (D3) debt liabilities other economic flows
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D3), including other economic flows and (D)3 debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D3) other volume changes, (D3) debt liabilities other volume changes
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D3), including other volume changes and (D3) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D3) transactions, (D3) debt liabilities transactions
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D3), including transactions and (D)3 debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D4), net of currency and deposits assets
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification D4, net of currency and deposit assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D1), net of currency and deposits assets, (D1) debt liabilities net of currency and deposits assets
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D1), net of currency and deposit assets, specifically including (D1) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D2), net of currency and deposits assets, (D2) debt liabilities net of currency and deposits assets
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D2), net of currency and deposit assets, specifically including (D2) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D3), net of currency and deposits assets, (D3) debt liabilities net of currency and deposits assets
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification (D3), net of currency and deposit assets, specifically including (D3) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross debt (D4) other economic flows
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt liabilities categorized under classification D4, including other economic flows.

str:Code.com:Name: Guaranteed Debt (at Market Value)
str:Code.com:Description: Debt that is backed by a guarantee and valued at its current market price.

str:Code.com:Name: Memo: Privatization proceeds allocated to redemption of debt (GFS concept)
str:Code.com:Description: Proceeds from privatization allocated to reduce debt, as recorded in Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Memo: Universal Mobile Telecommunications System proceeds allocated to redemption of debt (GFS concept)
str:Code.com:Description: Proceeds from the Universal Mobile Telecommunications System allocated to reduce debt, as recorded in Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Government gross debt per national definition
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of government debt calculated according to the national definition.

str:Code.com:Name: Gold
str:Code.com:Description: A precious metal held as a store of value and often used as a reserve asset by central banks. This includes physical gold in the form of bars, coins, and other forms.

str:Code.com:Name: Gold holdings
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of gold held by an entity, including both monetary and non-monetary gold. This may include physical gold, gold deposits, and other forms of gold ownership.

str:Code.com:Name: International Investment Position
str:Code.com:Description: A financial statement that shows the value of a country's external financial assets and liabilities at a specific point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: SDR Allocation, escrow account
str:Code.com:Description: SDRs allocated as part of a special allocation to participants with overdue obligations to the IMF are deposited and held in an escrow account within the SDR Department and released to the participants on settlement of all overdue obligations.

str:Code.com:Name: Outstanding Purchase (GRA)
str:Code.com:Description: IMF member’s outstanding purchases of Fund resources through the General Resources Agreement (GRA), the counterparts of which are increases in the member’s domestic currency liabilities to the Fund.

str:Code.com:Name: IMF Quota
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to the financial contribution made by a member country to the IMF as a part of its membership in the organization. It consists of two components. i) foreign exchange component (25 percent) which a member is required to pay in SDRs or in foreign currencies acceptable to the IMF; and ii) domestic currency component (75 percent) which is payable in the member’s own currency at a designated depository, normally the member’s central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve tranche position
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve tranche position in the IMF represents the member’s unconditional drawing right on the IMF, created by the foreign exchange portion of the quota subscription, plus increases (decreases) through the IMF’s sale (repurchase) of the members’ currency to meet the demand for use of IMF resources by other members in need of balance of payments financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations that an entity owes to others.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities excluding pension liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations excluding those related to pension benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Buildings other than dwellings (net)
str:Code.com:Description: The value of non-residential buildings after accounting for disposals or depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Machinery and equipment
str:Code.com:Description: Physical assets used in the operation of a business, including tools, machinery, and equipment.

str:Code.com:Name: Machinery and equipment (net)
str:Code.com:Description: The value of machinery and equipment after accounting for depreciation or disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Weapons systems (net)
str:Code.com:Description: The value of weapons systems after accounting for depreciation or consumption.

str:Code.com:Name: Fixed assets, acquisitions
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of fixed assets acquired during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Fixed assets, disposals
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of fixed assets disposed of during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Fixed assets (acquisitions less disposals)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of fixed assets acquired minus those disposed of during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Other fixed assets (acquisitions less disposals)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of other fixed assets after accounting for acquisitions and disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Inventories by type of inventory (net)
str:Code.com:Description: The value of inventories categorized by type after accounting for depreciation or disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Valuables, acquisitions (net of consumption of fixed capital)
str:Code.com:Description: The acquisitions of valuable items after accounting for the consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Valuables, disposals
str:Code.com:Description: The disposals of valuable items.

str:Code.com:Name: Valuables (acquisitions less disposals)
str:Code.com:Description: The net acquisitions of valuable items, such as precious metals, stones, and art, after accounting for disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Land (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of land before accounting for any depreciation or disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Land (acquisitions less disposals)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of land acquired minus land disposed of during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Mineral and energy reserves (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of mineral and energy reserves before accounting for extraction or usage.

str:Code.com:Name: Other naturally occurring assets (acquisitions less disposals)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of other naturally occurring assets after accounting for acquisitions and disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsoil Assets
str:Code.com:Description: Natural resources found below the earth's surface, such as minerals and fossil fuels.

str:Code.com:Name: Intangible non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Non-physical assets that have not been produced, such as patents, trademarks, and goodwill.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-produced non-financial assets (acquisitions less disposals)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of acquisitions of non-produced non-financial assets after accounting for disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Net debt
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt minus financial assets that can be used to repay that debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Net debt (D4)
str:Code.com:Description: The total debt classified under category D4, net of financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net incurrence of domestic liabilities (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: The net increase in domestic liabilities after adjustments for accounting discrepancies.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonfinancial assets other than inventories, disposals
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of disposals of nonfinancial assets other than inventories.

str:Code.com:Name: All non-financial assets (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of all non-financial assets before accounting for disposals or depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Net incurrence liabilities (adjusted using Fund records)
str:Code.com:Description: The net incurrence of liabilities represents the difference between taking on new liabilities less the retirement of liabilities. Country data adjusted using Fund records.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonproduced assets other than inventories, acquisitions
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of acquisitions of nonproduced assets other than inventories.

str:Code.com:Name: All non-financial assets, acquisitions less disposals
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of acquisitions minus disposals of non-financial assets during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions/Disposals (net) of nonproduced nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of purchases minus disposals of nonproduced assets, such as land and natural resources, during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions (net): nonproduced assets
str:Code.com:Description: The purchases of nonproduced assets, such as land and natural resources, during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Disposals, nonproduced nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: The sale or disposal of nonproduced assets, such as land or natural resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross investment in nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: The total investment in assets that are not financial in nature, such as property, plant, and equipment.

str:Code.com:Name: Sales of non-financial assets (net)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of sales of non-financial assets after accounting for purchases and disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve Assets, Not in SDR basket
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve Assets, not included in the basket of currencies used to value Special Drawing Rights (SDRs).

str:Code.com:Name: Net (excluding exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The net amount, excluding any transactions related to exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-financial assets, acquisitions less disposals (includes total produced fixed assets and land underlying dwellings)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of acquisitions of nonfinancial assets, including produced fixed assets and land underlying dwellings, after accounting for disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonfinancial assets other than inventories, acquisitions (net of CFC)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of acquisitions of nonfinancial assets other than inventories, after accounting for consumption of fixed capital (CFC).

str:Code.com:Name: Buildings and Structures
str:Code.com:Description: Physical structures, including residential and non-residential buildings and infrastructure.

str:Code.com:Name: Other Fixed Assets
str:Code.com:Description: Fixed assets not classified under specific categories like buildings, machinery, or equipment.

str:Code.com:Name: Other liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total external liabilities under the functional category “Other Investment,” for which data reported by economies have been adjusted using IMF accounting records (e.g., for SDR holdings and allocations, reserve position in the Fund, and IMF loans).

str:Code.com:Name: Other non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Non-produced assets not classified under specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Produced assets not classified under specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Permits to use natural resources
str:Code.com:Description: Official permissions granted to use natural resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Publicly guaranteed debt
str:Code.com:Description: Debt that is guaranteed by a public entity, such as a government.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities or debt to private institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed to private sector entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock-flow discrepancy: D1 debt liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Discrepancies between stock and flow data related to (D1) debt liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock-flow discrepancy: financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Discrepancies between stock and flow data related to financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock-flow discrepancy: liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Discrepancies between stock and flow data related to liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock-flow discrepancy: nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Discrepancies between stock and flow data related to nonfinancial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Total assets
str:Code.com:Description: Total assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit and loans with the IMF: other than reserves
str:Code.com:Description: Credit and loans provided by the IMF that are not related to reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Use of IMF credit (GRA)
str:Code.com:Description: Total value of credit and loans outstanding with the IMF by IMF member countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Outstanding loans from IMF (PRGT)
str:Code.com:Description: Outstanding loans from the IMF represent the aggregate sum of financial assistance provided by the International Monetary Fund to member countries, which have not yet been fully reimbursed. These loans are extended to countries experiencing economic challenges, aiming to stabilize their economies and restore sustainable growth. The repayment of these loans is typically subject to negotiated terms and conditions, including interest rates and repayment schedules

str:Code.com:Name: Money market instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term financial instruments that are highly liquid and low-risk, such as treasury bills and commercial paper.

str:Code.com:Name: Total outlays (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to total outlays to account for specific factors or discrepancies.

str:Code.com:Name: Total investment
str:Code.com:Description: The total external asset position in portfolio investment, covering all categories of financial instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Use of IMF Credit and Loans
str:Code.com:Description: The Use of Fund Credit and Loans (UFC) refers to the allocation and utilization of financial assistance (loans and credit lines) extended by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) to its member countries. This encompasses the deployment of IMF funds by member countries to address diverse economic needs, including balance of payments deficits, currency stabilization efforts, and implementation of structural reforms

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all relevant components or categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities to foreign banks
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed to banking institutions located in foreign countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities to official creditors
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed to official entities such as foreign governments, central banks, or international organizations.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities to other sectors
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed to entities in sectors other than banks or official creditors, such as private corporations, households, or other non-financial institutions located in foreign countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Official liabilities (including use of Fund credit)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed to official creditors, including liabilities arising from the use of credit provided by the International Monetary Fund (IMF).

str:Code.com:Name: Private liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed to private sector entities, such as corporations, households, or other non-official institutions located in foreign countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans, trade credit and deposits
str:Code.com:Description: The combined value of loans, trade credits and advances, and deposits held by an entity. Loans represent borrowed funds, trade credits represent short-term financing from suppliers, and deposits are funds placed with financial institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets, external
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assets held by an entity that represent claims on non-residents. These assets can include foreign currency holdings, deposits in foreign banks, holdings of foreign securities, and other claims on foreign entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign assets of the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assets held by the central bank of a country that represent claims on non-residents. These assets are typically part of the country's official reserves and may include foreign currency holdings, gold, and SDRs.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign liabilities of the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Financial obligations owed by the central bank of a country to non-residents. These liabilities may include deposits from foreign central banks, borrowings from international organizations, or other obligations to foreign entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Bank debt
str:Code.com:Description: Loans or other debt instruments issued by a bank that are held as assets by another entity.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: Government (official)
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of a country's external debt that is owed by the government or other official entities, such as central banks or government agencies.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: Government (official)
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of a country's external debt that is owed by the government or other official entities, such as central banks or government agencies.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Tangible and intangible economic resources owned by an entity that are not financial assets. These include fixed assets like buildings, machinery, and land, as well as inventories, valuables, and non-produced assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Total liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of all financial obligations owed by an entity to other entities. These obligations represent claims held by others against the entity and may include loans, debt securities, accounts payable, and other forms of debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Total liabilities, external
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of all financial obligations owed by an entity to non-residents. These obligations represent claims held by foreign entities against the reporting entity and may include loans, debt securities, accounts payable, and other forms of debt owed to foreign creditors.

str:Code.com:Name: Lending to the IMF (GRA)
str:Code.com:Description: Lending by a member country to the International Monetary Fund (IMF) through the General Resources Account (GRA). This lending contributes to the IMF's resources for providing financial assistance to member countries facing balance of payments problems.

str:Code.com:Name: Outstanding IMF loans (PRGT)
str:Code.com:Description: The outstanding balance of loans provided by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) through the Poverty Reduction and Growth Trust (PRGT). The PRGT provides concessional loans to low-income countries to support their poverty reduction and economic growth efforts.

str:Code.com:Name: SDR Allocation
str:Code.com:Description: SDR Allocations are a distribution of SDRs to members by decision of the IMF. Allocations of SDRs are made to participants in the SDR department (currently, all IMF members are participants) in proportion to their quotas in the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Outstanding Purchase (GRA)
str:Code.com:Description: IMF member’s outstanding purchases of Fund resources through the General Resources Agreement (GRA), the counterparts of which are increases in the member’s domestic currency liabilities to the Fund.

str:Code.com:Name: Outstanding IMF Loans (RST)
str:Code.com:Description: The outstanding balance of loans provided by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) through the Resilience and Sustainability Trust (RST). The RST provides loans to IMF members to support their efforts to adapt to and mitigate climate change.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary gold: gold bullion
str:Code.com:Description: Gold in the form of bars or ingots held as a reserve asset.

str:Code.com:Name: Forward type
str:Code.com:Description: Forwards are unconditional contracts by which two counterparties agree to buy or sell a specified quantity of an underlying item (financial or nonfinancial) at an agreed-upon contract price (the strike price) on a specified date. Forward contracts are traded over-the-counter.

str:Code.com:Name: Unallocated gold accounts
str:Code.com:Description: Unallocated gold accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt instruments other than insurance, pension, and standardized schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Debt instruments other than insurance, pension, and standardized schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Listed shares
str:Code.com:Description: Equity securities listed on an exchange (also known as quoted shares).

str:Code.com:Name: Unlisted shares
str:Code.com:Description: Equity securities that are not listed on an exchange (also known as unquoted shares).

str:Code.com:Name: Other claims (other reserve assets)
str:Code.com:Description: Other claims (other reserve assets).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt instruments other than SDRs, insurance, pension, and standardized schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Debt instruments other than SDRs, insurance, pension, and standardized schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio Investment Assets by Economy of Holder and Issuer
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio Investment Assets by Economy of Holder and Issuer.

str:Code.com:Name: Total, short or negative positions
str:Code.com:Description: Short position is a position when an institutional unit sells securities for which it is not the economic owner.

str:Code.com:Name: Outward Direct Investment
str:Code.com:Description: Outward investment position relates to direct investment assets less liabilities between resident direct investors and their direct investment enterprises. It also covers direct investment assets less liabilities between resident and nonresident fellow enterprises if the ultimate controlling parent is resident.

str:Code.com:Name: Outward less Inward Direct Investment Position
str:Code.com:Description: Outward less Inward Direct Investment position for all instruments (used in CDIS)

str:Code.com:Name: Outward Direct investment Between Fellow Enterprises
str:Code.com:Description: Outward investment position between fellow enterprises relates to direct investment assets less liabilities between resident and nonresident fellow enterprise if the ultimate controlling parent is resident. Fellow enterprises are enterprises under the control or influence, either directly or indirectly, of the same direct investor, but neither of the enterprises controls or influences the other enterprise.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities, adjustment
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities, adjustment.

str:Code.com:Name: Bitcoin
str:Code.com:Description: Bitcoin (BTC) is a decentralized digital currency, without a central bank or single administrator, that can be sent from user to user on the peer-to-peer bitcoin network without the need for intermediaries. 1

str:Code.com:Name: Securities under repo for cash collateral
str:Code.com:Description: Securities under repo for cash collateral.

str:Code.com:Name: Net International Investment Position
str:Code.com:Description: A financial statement that shows the value of a country's external financial assets and liabilities at a specific point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Currency and deposits, Claims on other entities
str:Code.com:Description: Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions, claims on other entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Currency and deposits, Claims on monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions, claims on monetary authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Options
str:Code.com:Description: Options

str:Code.com:Name: Gold at 35 SDRs per ounce
str:Code.com:Description: Gold at 35 SDRs per ounce.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonfinancial assets excluding land and natural resources
str:Code.com:Description: Economic resources owned by an entity, excluding land and natural resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Gold, volume
str:Code.com:Description: Gold, volume.

str:Code.com:Name: Gold at national valuation
str:Code.com:Description: Gold at national valuation.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives and employee stock options
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives and employee stock options.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assets (adjustment).

str:Code.com:Name: Major improvements non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Major improvements non-produced assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve Assets included in SDR basket
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve Assets included in SDR basket.

str:Code.com:Name: Land
str:Code.com:Description: Land.

str:Code.com:Name: Fixed assets
str:Code.com:Description: Fixed assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-produced non-financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Non-produced non-financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Valuables
str:Code.com:Description: Valuables.

str:Code.com:Name: Weapons systems
str:Code.com:Description: Weapons systems.

str:Code.com:Name: Other fixed assets
str:Code.com:Description: Other fixed assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Inventories by type of inventory
str:Code.com:Description: Inventories by type of inventory.

str:Code.com:Name: Mineral and energy reserves
str:Code.com:Description: Mineral and energy reserves.

str:Code.com:Name: Other naturally occurring assets
str:Code.com:Description: Other naturally occurring assets.

str:Codelist.com:Name: NA Stocks, Transactions, Other Flows
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents the different classifications of data items used in the System of National Accounts (SNA) framework, distinguishing between stocks (levels at a point in time), transactions (changes over a period), and other flows.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants in cash
str:Code.com:Description: Grants provided in monetary form.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants in kind
str:Code.com:Description: Grants provided as goods or services rather than cash.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on property
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on ownership or transfer of property (land, buildings, etc.).

str:Code.com:Name: Recurrent taxes on wealth: taxes on property
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied regularly on the value of wealth, including property.

str:Code.com:Name: Other recurrent taxes on property: taxes on property
str:Code.com:Description: Recurrent property taxes not classified as wealth taxes, such as annual property taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Total employment
str:Code.com:Description: The total number of employed persons.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions in financial assets and liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: All transactions involving changes in financial assets (e.g., stocks, bonds) and liabilities (e.g., loans).

str:Code.com:Name: Factor Income
str:Code.com:Description: Income earned from factors of production (labor, capital).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross operating balance
str:Code.com:Description: The surplus or deficit from production activities before deducting interest and other property income.

str:Code.com:Name: Net operating balance
str:Code.com:Description: The gross operating balance less consumption of fixed capital (depreciation).

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / net borrowing (-) [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between total revenue and total expenditure. A positive value indicates net lending; a negative value indicates net borrowing.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary net lending (+) / net borrowing (-) [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Net lending/borrowing excluding interest payments and receipts.

str:Code.com:Name: Implicit transfers resulting from concessional interest rates
str:Code.com:Description: The benefit received by a borrower when receiving a below-market interest rate loan.

str:Code.com:Name: Net cash inflow from financial activities
str:Code.com:Description: The net change in cash resulting from financing activities (e.g., borrowing, issuing equity).

str:Code.com:Name: Compensation of employees excluding for own-account capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Payments to employees (wages, salaries, social contributions) excluding those related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Wages and salaries excluding for own-account capital formation [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Payments to employees in cash and in kind, excluding those related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Wages and salaries in cash excluding for own-account capital formation [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Payments to employees in monetary form, excluding those related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Wages and salaries in kind excluding for own-account capital formation [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Payments to employees in the form of goods or services, excluding those related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' social contributions, excluding for own-account capital formation [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers to social security schemes on behalf of their employees, excluding those related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual social contributions, excluding for own-account capital formation [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions employers are legally obliged to pay, excluding those related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' imputed social contributions, excluding for own-account capital formation [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: The value of social benefits provided directly by employers to their employees, considered as contributions, excluding those related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies other than for own-account capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by governments to producers to lower their production costs, excluding those related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Property income before FISIM allocation, excluding investment income payable on pension entitlements [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Income received from ownership of assets (interest, dividends, rent), before adjusting for FISIM (Financial Intermediation Services Indirectly Measured), excluding investment income payable on pension entitlements.

str:Code.com:Name: Property income other than interest, excluding investment income payable on pension entitlements [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Property income excluding interest (e.g. dividends, rent), excluding investment income payable on pension entitlements.

str:Code.com:Name: Social contributions, excluding employment related schemes' pension contribution [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by individuals and employers to social security schemes, excluding pension contributions related to employment schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions: employee contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions paid by employees to social security systems.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions: employer contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions paid by employers to social security systems.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions: self-employed or nonemployed contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions paid by self-employed or non-employed individuals to social security systems.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions: unallocable contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Social security contributions that cannot be attributed to specific groups (employees, employers, etc.).

str:Code.com:Name: Other social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions outside of standard social security arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Other social contributions, employee contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Employee contributions to social schemes outside regular social security.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits in cash, excluding other social insurance pension benefits [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Cash payments from social security schemes to individuals, excluding pensions from other social insurance schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits, excluding other social insurance pension benefits and social transfers in kind - non-market production [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: All social benefits excluding pensions from other social insurance schemes and social transfers in kind provided by non-market producers.

str:Code.com:Name: Employer social benefits, excluding pension benefits [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Social benefits provided directly by employers to employees, excluding pension benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums of nonlife insurance schemes other than standardized guarantees
str:Code.com:Description: Premiums paid for non-life insurance policies, excluding those related to standardized guarantees.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous current transfers: other than fines, penalties, and forfeits
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers not elsewhere classified, excluding fines, penalties, and forfeits.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, fees, and current claims
str:Code.com:Description: Payments related to insurance and guarantee schemes, including premiums, fees, and claims payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Premiums, fees, and claims related to nonlife insurance and standardized guarantee schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Payments related to non-life insurance and standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers that don't fit into other defined categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes not classified as taxes on income, profits and capital gains, social security contributions, payroll taxes, taxes on property, taxes on goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes payable solely by business
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes paid exclusively by businesses.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes payable by other than business or unidentifiable
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes paid by entities other than businesses or where the payer is not identifiable.

str:Code.com:Name: Deficit-debt adjustment
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to reconcile the government deficit with the change in government debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Time of recording and other differences in DDA analysis (ECB concept)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments for differences in the timing of recording transactions and other discrepancies in deficit-debt adjustment analysis.

str:Code.com:Name: Other expense [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Government expenses not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Other revenue [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Government revenue not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Total government expenditure [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: The total spending by government units.

str:Code.com:Name: Total expense
str:Code.com:Description: Total spending by government units, including consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Expense, excluding consumption of fixed capital
str:Code.com:Description: Total spending by government units, excluding depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Total government revenue [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: The total receipts of government units.

str:Code.com:Name: Sales of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue from sales of goods and services by government units.

str:Code.com:Name: Sales by market establishments
str:Code.com:Description: Sales by government units operating as market producers.

str:Code.com:Name: Use of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: The use of goods and services in production or final consumption.

str:Code.com:Name: Consumption of fixed capital (use of fixed assets) [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: The decline in the value of fixed assets due to wear and tear, obsolescence, or accidental damage.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary and secondary income
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of primary income (from factors of production) and secondary income (current transfers).

str:Code.com:Name: Primary income
str:Code.com:Description: Income derived directly from the factors of production (labor, capital).

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income
str:Code.com:Description: Income received through current transfers (e.g., social benefits, taxes).

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Redistribution of income through current transfers, such as taxes, social benefits, and other unrequited payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: other current transfers excluding personal transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers excluding transfers between households (e.g., remittances), encompassing transfers between governments, businesses, and non-profit institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Inventories
str:Code.com:Description: Stocks of goods held by producers for sale or further processing.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in positions other than transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in assets and liabilities not due to transactions, such as revaluations or other changes in volume.

str:Code.com:Name: Economic disappearances of non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Reductions in the value or quantity of non-produced assets due to economic factors.

str:Code.com:Name: Depletion of natural resources
str:Code.com:Description: Reduction in the stock of natural resources due to extraction or use.

str:Code.com:Name: Other economic disappearances of non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Other decreases in non-produced assets not due to depletion, such as write-offs.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in sector classification and structure
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the classification of institutional units to different sectors or changes in the structure of the economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in classification of assets and liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the classification of assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Revaluations / Nominal holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the monetary value of assets and liabilities due to changes in prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Neutral holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in value due to general inflation.

str:Code.com:Name: Revaluations due to exchange rate changes
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in value due to fluctuations in exchange rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Revaluations due to other price changes
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in value due to changes in specific asset prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Other valuation effects of which due to issuance and redemption value
str:Code.com:Description: Valuation effects specifically relating to the issuance and redemption values of financial instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other changes excluding revaluations
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in assets and liabilities not due to transactions or revaluations.

str:Code.com:Name: Other volume changes in debt liabilities (K.3, K.4, K.5) (-)
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in debt liabilities due to factors other than transactions, revaluations, or classification changes (e.g., debt write-offs).

str:Code.com:Name: Closing balance sheet / positions / stocks
str:Code.com:Description: The value of assets and liabilities at the end of a period.

str:Code.com:Name: Labour productivity (per persons)
str:Code.com:Description: Output per employed person.

str:Code.com:Name: Opening balance sheet / positions / stocks
str:Code.com:Description: The value of assets and liabilities at the beginning of a period.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in balance sheet / positions / stocks
str:Code.com:Description: The net change in assets and liabilities over a period.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets: residential structures
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in residential buildings and dwellings.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets non-residential structures
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in non-residential buildings and structures (e.g., factories, offices).

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in non-produced assets (e.g., land, mineral rights).

str:Code.com:Name: Nominal Pre-tax profits
str:Code.com:Description: Profits before taxes are deducted.

str:Code.com:Name: Household saving ratio
str:Code.com:Description: The proportion of household disposable income that is saved.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjustment product taxes less subsidies
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to product taxes less subsidies to align with specific accounting principles.

str:Code.com:Name: Total current expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on current operations (e.g., wages, goods and services).

str:Code.com:Name: Primary current expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Current expenditure excluding interest payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Total current revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Government receipts from current sources (e.g., taxes, social contributions).

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits other than social transfers in kind + social transfers in kind, purchased market production + subsidies payable + current taxes on income, wealth, etc. + other taxes on production + property income other than interest + other current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: A combination of various current transactions including social benefits, subsidies, taxes, and property income.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest, including flows on swaps and FRA's
str:Code.com:Description: Interest payments and receipts, including those related to financial derivatives.

str:Code.com:Name: Total tax receipts
str:Code.com:Description: Total revenue collected from taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Total receipts from taxes and social contributions after deduction of amounts assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Total tax and social contribution revenue adjusted for uncollectible amounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Total receipts from taxes and social contributions (including imputed social contributions) after deduction of amounts assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Total tax and social contribution revenue including imputed contributions and adjusted for uncollectible amounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Tax burden = total receipts from taxes and compulsory social contributions after deduction of amounts assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: The proportion of the economy represented by tax and social contribution revenue, adjusted for uncollectible amounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Memo: Difference between deliveries and corresponding cash payment (EDP concept)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the value of goods/services delivered and the actual cash payments made.

str:Code.com:Name: Intermediate consumption, other taxes on production, current taxes on income and wealth, etc., and adjustment for the change in net equity of households in pension funds reserves
str:Code.com:Description: A combination of intermediate consumption, taxes, and adjustments related to pension funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production, current taxes on income and wealth, etc. and adjustment for the change in net equity of households in pension funds reserves
str:Code.com:Description: Similar to OEA, but potentially with a slightly different scope.

str:Code.com:Name: Intermediate consumption + Other taxes on production + Current taxes on income, wealth, etc. + Adjustment for the change in pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: A combination of intermediate consumption, taxes, and adjustments related to pension entitlements.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production + Current taxes on income, wealth, etc. + Adjustment for the change in pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: Similar to OEC, but potentially with a slightly different scope.

str:Code.com:Name: Total impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI
str:Code.com:Description: The overall impact on Gross National Income (GNI) due to changes in statistical standards between ESA2010 and ESA95.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: R&D created by a market producer
str:Code.com:Description: Specific impact on GNI due to the treatment of R&D produced by market producers.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: R&D created by a non-market producer
str:Code.com:Description: Specific impact on GNI due to the treatment of R&D produced by non-market producers.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Valuation of output for own final use for market producers
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI from changes in how market producers value output used for their own purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Non-life insurance - Output, claims due to catastrophes, and reinsurance
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI related to non-life insurance accounting, particularly concerning catastrophes and reinsurance.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Weapon systems in government recognized as capital assets
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI due to the classification of weapon systems as capital assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Decommissioning costs for large capital assets
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI related to the accounting of decommissioning costs.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Government, public and private sector classification
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI from changes in sector classification rules.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Small tools
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI due to the treatment of small tools as assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: VAT-based third EU own resource
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI related to the calculation of the VAT-based EU own resource.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Index-linked debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI from changes in the accounting for index-linked debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Central Bank - allocation of output
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI related to the allocation of central bank output.

str:Code.com:Name: Impact of differences in definitions between ESA2010 and ESA95 on GNI: Land improvements recognized as a separate asset
str:Code.com:Description: Impact on GNI from recognizing land improvements as a separate asset class.

str:Code.com:Name: Guarantee fees receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Fees received for providing guarantees.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross disposable income minus final consumption expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of disposable income remaining after households have spent on final consumption. This represents household saving.

str:Code.com:Name: Total capital expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending on investment in fixed assets (e.g., infrastructure, buildings).

str:Code.com:Name: Member State's net revenue from pre-acceding programmes
str:Code.com:Description: Net revenue received by a Member State from EU pre-accession programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on income, wealth, etc. plus other taxes on production plus property income other than interest plus other current transfers and adjustment for the change in pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: A comprehensive measure of current government revenue, including taxes, property income (excluding interest), other transfers, and pension adjustments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Government revenue from sources other than taxes on income/wealth, production, and property income.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on income, wealth, etc. plus other taxes on production plus property income other than interest plus other current transfers excluding current transfers to NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Similar to OOCE, but excluding current transfers to Non-Profit Institutions Serving Households (NPISH).

str:Code.com:Name: Total government expenditure excluding: interest, calls on guarantees, capital injections recorded as capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending excluding debt interest payments, guarantee payments, and capital injections classified as capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Total government expenditure excluding: interest, calls on guarantees, capital injections recorded as capital transfers, capital transfers recorded in the context of asset purchases
str:Code.com:Description: Similar to OOE, but further excluding capital transfers related to asset purchases.

str:Code.com:Name: Other revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Purchases
str:Code.com:Description: Acquisitions of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross disposable income - gross operating surplus and mixed income - compensation of employees - social contribution and social benefits, other than social transfer in kind - current taxes on income, wealth, etc.
str:Code.com:Description: A calculation to isolate a specific portion of income by subtracting various components from gross disposable income.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjustments for scope and valuation of Maastricht debt
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to government debt figures to comply with Maastricht Treaty criteria.

str:Code.com:Name: Difference between interest paid (+) and accrued (EDP D.41)(-)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between cash interest payments and the interest expense recognized in accrual accounting.

str:Code.com:Name: Difference between interest (EDP D.41) accrued(-) and paid (+) of which: interest flows attributable to swaps and FRAs
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of the difference between accrued and paid interest that relates to interest rate swaps and forward rate agreements (FRAs).

str:Code.com:Name: Appreciation(+)/depreciation(-) of foreign-currency debt
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the value of foreign-currency denominated debt due to exchange rate movements.

str:Code.com:Name: Issuances above(-)/below(+) nominal value
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the cash received from issuing debt and the face value of the debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-financial transactions not included in the working balance
