str:Code.com:Description: Transactions related to non-financial assets and liabilities that are not part of the government's working balance.

str:Code.com:Name: Other adjustments (+/-) in EDP Table 2
str:Code.com:Description: Various other adjustments made in the context of Excessive Deficit Procedure (EDP) reporting.

str:Code.com:Name: Redemptions of debt above (+) / below (-) nominal value
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the cash paid to redeem debt and the face value of the debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Working balance
str:Code.com:Description: A measure of the government's cash flow from operating activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Working balance (+/-) of entities not part of the subsector
str:Code.com:Description: The working balance of entities excluded from the government subsector.

str:Code.com:Name: Savings, gross plus net capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of gross saving and net capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits other than social transfers in kind and social transfers in kind - purchased market production + current transfers to NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Social benefits excluding in-kind transfers from non-market producers, plus in-kind transfers from market producers, plus transfers to NPISHs.

str:Code.com:Name: Total government expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Total spending by government.

str:Code.com:Name: Total expenditure other than Compensation of employees, Social benefits and social transfers in kind for products supplied to HH via market producers, Intermediate consumption and Gross fixed capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Government spending excluding wages, social benefits, in-kind transfers from market producers, intermediate consumption, and gross fixed capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Total government expenditure excluding interest payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Trading gain or loss
str:Code.com:Description: Profit or loss from trading activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Total government revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Total receipts by the government.

str:Code.com:Name: Net revenue / cost for general government (only including transactions as defined for EDP financial crisis tables and to be used with custom breakdown FC)
str:Code.com:Description: Government net revenue/cost as defined specifically for EDP financial crisis reporting.

str:Code.com:Name: Total receipts from taxes and social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Combined revenue from taxes and social contributions.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade and transport margins
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the price paid by the final purchaser and the price received by the producer, covering trade and transport costs.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade margins
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of OTTM related to trade activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Transport margins
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of OTTM related to transport activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions in products
str:Code.com:Description: Transactions involving goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Implicit service fees in nonlife insurance premiums and standardized guarantees
str:Code.com:Description: The service charge component embedded within non-life insurance premiums and standardized guarantee fees.

str:Code.com:Name: Output for own final use, cost attributable to compensation of employees (GFSM purpose)
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of output used for own final consumption that is attributable to employee compensation.

str:Code.com:Name: Output for own final use, cost attributable to intermediate consumption (GFSM purpose)
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of output used for own final consumption that is attributable to intermediate consumption.

str:Code.com:Name: Output for own final use, cost attributable to consumption of fixed capital (GFSM purpose)
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of output used for own final consumption that is attributable to the consumption of fixed capital (depreciation).

str:Code.com:Name: Output for own final use, Own-account capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services produced by an entity for its own capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Production of goods and services used as compensation of employees in kind
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services produced by an entity and given to employees as part of their compensation.

str:Code.com:Name: Payments for other non-market output
str:Code.com:Description: Payments for non-market output other than those covered by taxes or social contributions.

str:Code.com:Name: Administrative fees
str:Code.com:Description: Fees charged for administrative services.

str:Code.com:Name: Incidental sales by nonmarket establishments
str:Code.com:Description: Minor sales of goods or services by non-market producers.

str:Code.com:Name: Other non-market output, n.e.c.
str:Code.com:Description: Non-market output not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Output gap (old)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between actual and potential output, calculated using an older methodology.

str:Code.com:Name: Market output, output for own final use and payments for other non-market output
str:Code.com:Description: A combination of market output, output for own final use, and payments for other non-market output.

str:Code.com:Name: Potential output
str:Code.com:Description: The level of output an economy can produce at full capacity.

str:Code.com:Name: Potential output: excluding oil related transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Potential output excluding the impact of oil-related activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Service charges payable to providers of nonlife insurance
str:Code.com:Description: Fees paid to non-life insurance providers for their services.

str:Code.com:Name: Intermediate consumption resulting from FISIM allocation
str:Code.com:Description: Intermediate consumption that is attributed due to the allocation of FISIM (Financial Intermediation Services Indirectly Measured).

str:Code.com:Name: Final domestic demand excluding inventories
str:Code.com:Description: Total spending on final goods and services within the domestic economy, excluding changes in inventories.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of services
str:Code.com:Description: Spending by households on services.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of semi-durable and non-durable goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Spending by households on semi-durable and non-durable goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Final domestic demand
str:Code.com:Description: Total spending on final goods and services within the domestic economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Total domestic demand
str:Code.com:Description: Final domestic demand including changes in inventories.

str:Code.com:Name: Total demand
str:Code.com:Description: Total demand for goods and services in the economy, including both domestic and foreign demand.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in fixed assets (acquisitions minus disposals).

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions of new fixed assets
str:Code.com:Description: Purchases of newly produced fixed assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions of existing fixed assets
str:Code.com:Description: Purchases of fixed assets that have already been used.

str:Code.com:Name: Disposals of existing fixed assets
str:Code.com:Description: Sales or other disposals of fixed assets that have already been used.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets: other structures
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in structures other than residential buildings.

str:Code.com:Name: Costs of ownership transfer on non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Costs associated with transferring ownership of non-produced assets (e.g., land, patents).

str:Code.com:Name: Consumption of fixed capital
str:Code.com:Description: The decline in the value of fixed assets due to wear and tear, obsolescence, or accidental damage (depreciation).

str:Code.com:Name: Consumption of fixed capital on gross operating surplus
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of consumption of fixed capital allocated to gross operating surplus.

str:Code.com:Name: Consumption of fixed capital on gross mixed income
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of consumption of fixed capital allocated to gross mixed income.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross fixed capital formation: machinery and equipment and other
str:Code.com:Description: Investment in machinery, equipment, and other fixed assets not classified as structures.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross fixed capital formation: non-residential structures
str:Code.com:Description: Investment in non-residential buildings and structures (e.g., factories, offices, infrastructure).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross fixed capital formation: residential structures
str:Code.com:Description: Investment in residential buildings and dwellings.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross fixed capital formation: structures
str:Code.com:Description: Total investment in all types of structures (residential and non-residential).

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of valuables
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in products of considerable value that are not used primarily for purposes of production or consumption but are held as a stores of value over time. Examples are precious metals and stones, antiques, paintings, sculptures or other art objects

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in inventories, acquisition less disposals of valuables and acquisition less disposals of non-financial non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: The combined changes in inventories, valuables, and non-financial non-produced assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross capital formation and acquisitions less disposals of non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Total investment in fixed assets and non-produced assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net capital formation and acquisitions less disposals of non-produced assets
str:Code.com:Description: Gross capital formation less consumption of fixed capital, plus net acquisition of non-produced assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in inventories and acquisition less disposals of valuables
str:Code.com:Description: The combined change in inventories and valuables.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Capital formation by an entity using its own resources and labor.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: compensation of employees
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of own-account capital formation attributable to employee compensation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: wages and salaries in cash
str:Code.com:Description: Cash payments to employees for own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: wages and salaries in kind
str:Code.com:Description: Payments in goods or services to employees for own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: actual employers' social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Employer social security contributions for own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: imputed employers' social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Imputed social contributions for own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: intermediate consumption
str:Code.com:Description: The value of intermediate goods and services used in own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: consumption of fixed capital
str:Code.com:Description: The consumption of fixed capital used in own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: other taxes on production minus other subsidies on production
str:Code.com:Description: The net effect of other taxes and subsidies on production related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: other taxes on production
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on production incurred during own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Own-account capital formation: other subsidies on production
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies received for own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Re-export of goods
str:Code.com:Description: Goods imported and then exported without further processing.

str:Code.com:Name: Export on second hand goods
str:Code.com:Description: Export of used or previously owned goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods sent abroad before processing (outward processing, i.e. your country is the principal)
str:Code.com:Description: Goods sent to another country for processing and then returned.

str:Code.com:Name: Processing fees
str:Code.com:Description: Fees received for processing goods belonging to another entity.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of FISIM
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of Financial Intermediation Services Indirectly Measured.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the economic ownership of goods from nonresidents to residents of the compiling economy, as well as services provided by non-residents to residents of the compiling economy

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of FISIM
str:Code.com:Description: Imports of Financial Intermediation Services Indirectly Measured.

str:Code.com:Name: Production
str:Code.com:Description: The output of goods and services by resident units.

str:Code.com:Name: Total payable tax credits
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of tax credits that can be claimed by taxpayers.

str:Code.com:Name: Depreciation rate (PIM)
str:Code.com:Description: The rate at which fixed assets are assumed to depreciate according to the Perpetual Inventory Method (PIM).

str:Code.com:Name: Retirement rate (PIM)
str:Code.com:Description: The rate at which fixed assets are assumed to be retired from use according to the PIM.

str:Code.com:Name: Revaluation rate (PIM)
str:Code.com:Description: The rate used to revalue fixed assets in the PIM.

str:Code.com:Name: Total rate (PIM)
str:Code.com:Description: A combined rate used in the PIM, possibly encompassing depreciation, retirement, and revaluation.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock building
str:Code.com:Description: The change in inventories, which contributes to GDP growth.

str:Code.com:Name: Tax credits payable that exceed the taxpayer's liability
str:Code.com:Description: Tax credits that are greater than the amount of tax owed, resulting in a refund.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers and investment grants, excluding nonlife insurance capital claims
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers and investment grants, excluding nonlife insurance capital claims: Capital transfers other than those related to non-life insurance claims, specifically including investment grants (one-off transfers for investment purposes).

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers, excluding nonlife insurance capital claims
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers, excluding nonlife insurance capital claims: All capital transfers except those related to non-life insurance claims (e.g., major damage payments).

str:Code.com:Name: Grants to / from governments and international organizations
str:Code.com:Description: Grants to / from governments and international organizations: Unrequited transfers (payments without direct return) between governments and international bodies (e.g., foreign aid, international organization contributions).

str:Code.com:Name: European Union (EU) own resources and miscellaneous contributions
str:Code.com:Description: European Union (EU) own resources and miscellaneous contributions: Funds contributed to the EU budget, including those based on member state contributions and other sources.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in net worth due to nominal holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the value of assets and liabilities due to price changes over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in net worth due to other economic flows
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the value of assets and liabilities NOT due to price changes, such as transactions or other economic events.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross value added (GVA)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services produced by a producer, industry, or sector, minus the cost of goods and services used in production during an accounting period

str:Code.com:Name: Value added, net
str:Code.com:Description: GVA minus depreciation of assets used in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Operating surplus, gross
str:Code.com:Description: Profits generated by corporations from their production activities before deducting consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Operating surplus, net
str:Code.com:Description: Operating surplus gross minus depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Mixed income, gross
str:Code.com:Description: Income earned by unincorporated businesses (like sole proprietorships) from both labor and capital, before deducting consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Mixed income, net
str:Code.com:Description: Mixed income gross minus depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Entrepreneurial income (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: Income earned by corporations from production, plus property income received, minus property income paid. Excludes dividends, withdrawals from quasi-corporations, and reinvested earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Entrepreneurial income (net)
str:Code.com:Description: Entrepreneurial income gross minus depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross national income (GNI)
str:Code.com:Description: Total income earned by all residents within an economic territory during an accounting period. It is equal to gross domestic product plus earned income receivable from abroad minus earned income payable abroad.

str:Code.com:Name: Net national income (NNI)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross national income, less depreciation and depletion.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers to social security and other social insurance programs on behalf of their employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' imputed pension contributions (primary income)
str:Code.com:Description: The estimated value of pension benefits accrued by employees during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' imputed non-pension contributions (primary income)
str:Code.com:Description: The estimated value of non-pension social benefits (like healthcare) earned by employees during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on production and imports
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the production or import of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on imports excluding value added tax and duties
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on imports other than VAT and import duties.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on imports excluding value added tax (VAT) and duties and excluding taxes on specific services
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on imports other than VAT, import duties, and taxes on specific services.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on lotteries, gambling and betting
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on lottery, gambling, and betting activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on production that are not directly tied to specific products.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production n.e.c.
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on production other than taxes on products that enterprises incur as a result of engaging in production, not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: [This is a bit vague. More context is needed to provide a specific description. It likely refers to taxes on goods and services not captured in other categories.]

str:Code.com:Name: Reinvested earnings on FDI (Excluding IF)
str:Code.com:Description: Foreign direct investors' share of retained earnings in their foreign investments, treated as distributed and then reinvested, excluding investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Reinvested earnings on FDI (Including IF)
str:Code.com:Description: Same as D43, but including investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on winnings from lottery or gambling
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on lottery or gambling winnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on winnings from lottery or gambling n.e.c.
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on lottery or gambling winnings not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on income, wealth, etc., excluding taxes on income, profits, and capital gains.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on capital
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on the property or net wealth of institutional units, excluding taxes on assets used in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current taxes n.e.c.
str:Code.com:Description: Current taxes not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Actual payments made by employers for social security and other social insurance programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' imputed pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: The estimated value of pension benefits accrued by employees during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' imputed non-pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: The estimated value of non-pension social benefits earned by employees during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits other than social transfers in kind
str:Code.com:Description: Cash benefits provided by social security and social assistance programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits and social transfers in kind
str:Code.com:Description: All social benefits, including cash benefits and benefits in kind (like healthcare and education).

str:Code.com:Name: Social transfers, other than social transfers in kind minus net social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: [This needs further clarification. It likely refers to a specific calculation within the system of national accounts.]

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers financed by the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF)
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers specifically funded by the European Union's Recovery and Resilience Facility.

str:Code.com:Name: Recurrent taxes on immovable property
str:Code.com:Description: Recurring taxes on property (like real estate), excluding taxes on assets used in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Output for own final use
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services produced and used by the producer for their own consumption or investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Market output and output for own final use
str:Code.com:Description: Total output, including goods and services sold in the market and those used by the producer.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on goods and services by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of the needs or wants of individual households. Individual final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of durable goods
str:Code.com:Description: Spending on durable goods (lasting 3+ years), like cars and appliances.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of semi-durable goods
str:Code.com:Description: Spending on semi-durable goods (lasting 1-3 years), like clothing and footwear.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of non-durable goods
str:Code.com:Description: Spending on non-durable goods (lasting less than 1 year), like food and fuel.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, as well as services provided by residents of the compiling economy to non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical discrepancy (production approach)
str:Code.com:Description: Differences that arise due to data limitations when calculating GDP using the production approach.

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical discrepancy (income approach)
str:Code.com:Description: Differences that arise due to data limitations when calculating GDP using the income approach.

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all relevant components or categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Not allocated/unspecified
str:Code.com:Description: Not specified or detailed.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Not applicable or relevant in the given context.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjusted net national savings
str:Code.com:Description: Net national savings adjusted for the impact of certain factors, such as depletion of natural resources or other exceptional events.

str:Code.com:Name: Economically active population
str:Code.com:Description: All persons supplying, or willing to supply, labour to undertake an activity included within the SNA production boundary.

str:Code.com:Name: Balancing and net worth items
str:Code.com:Description: Accounting constructs obtained residually as the difference between the total value of revenues, or (changes in) assets, on one side of an account and the total value of expenditures, or (changes in) liabilities, on the other side. It cannot be measured independently of the entries in the accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in net worth
str:Code.com:Description: Balance of changes in the value of stocks and positions on the balance sheet, due to (i) savings and capital transfers; (ii) nominal holding gains and losses; and (iii) other changes in the volume of assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in net worth due to saving and capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Balance of changes in the value of stocks and positions on the balance sheet, due to savings and capital transfers. It also reflects the positive or negative amount available to the unit or sector for the acquisition of non-financial and financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net transactions in assets and liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The net change in a unit's or sector's financial position due to transactions in assets and liabilities. This reflects the impact of buying and selling assets and incurring or repaying liabilities on net worth.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in net worth due to other changes in volume of assets
str:Code.com:Description: Balance of changes in the value of stocks and positions on the balance sheet due to factors other than transactions and holding gains and losses (e.g., catastrophic losses, uncompensated seizures, and appearance and disappearance of assets and liabilities other than by transactions).

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in net worth due to neutral holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in net worth resulting from changes in the general price level applied to assets and liabilities. These gains/losses reflect inflation's impact on value.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in net worth due to real holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in net worth due to changes in the relative prices of assets and liabilities, excluding the effect of general inflation. This reflects changes in market value beyond inflation.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in net worth due to other changes
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in net worth arising from events other than transactions and holding gains/losses, such as write-offs, discoveries of resources, or reclassifications.

str:Code.com:Name: Remaining changes in net worth
str:Code.com:Description: The remaining portion of net worth changes not explained by other specific categories. This may capture statistical discrepancies or unclassified effects.

str:Code.com:Name: External balance of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between exports and imports of both goods and services. A positive value indicates a trade surplus; a negative value a trade deficit.

str:Code.com:Name: External balance of goods
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between exports and imports of goods only.

str:Code.com:Name: External balance of services
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between exports and imports of services only.

str:Code.com:Name: Current external balance
str:Code.com:Description: Balance of current transactions (transactions in goods and services, earned income and transfer income) between residents and non-residents. The term current account balance is used in the external accounts and is expressed from the perspective of resident units. The term current external balance is used in the national accounts and is expressed from the perspective of the non-resident units, and therefore with the opposite sign.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP): oil related transactions
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of GDP directly attributable to oil-related economic activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Potential gross domestic product
str:Code.com:Description: The level of GDP that an economy could produce at full capacity utilization without generating inflationary pressures.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP): United States (WEO)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross Domestic Product specifically for the United States, as reported by the World Economic Outlook (WEO) database.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP): excluding oil related transactions
str:Code.com:Description: GDP excluding the portion related to oil activities, providing a measure of non-oil economic performance.

str:Code.com:Name: Value added excluding FISIM
str:Code.com:Description: Gross Value Added less Financial Intermediation Services Indirectly Measured (FISIM). FISIM represents the value of financial services provided by financial institutions for which they do not explicitly charge.

str:Code.com:Name: Net domestic product at market prices
str:Code.com:Description: GDP less consumption of fixed capital (depreciation). This measures the net output of the economy after accounting for wear and tear on capital assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Operating surplus and mixed income, gross
str:Code.com:Description: The income earned by resident corporations from the use of their capital (operating surplus) and unincorporated enterprises from the use of the owner's labor and capital (mixed income) in the production of goods and services during an accounting period, before the deduction of depreciation and depletion

str:Code.com:Name: Operating surplus and mixed income, net
str:Code.com:Description: Operating surplus and mixed income after deducting consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Operating surplus, mixed and net property income
str:Code.com:Description: Combines operating surplus, mixed income, and net property income (receipts less payments).

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of primary incomes, gross / National income, gross
str:Code.com:Description: The total income accruing to resident units as a result of their involvement in production and ownership of assets, before deducting consumption of fixed capital. Equivalent to Gross National Income (GNI).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross national income (SNA93/ESA95) excluding FISIM allocation
str:Code.com:Description: Gross national income according to the older SNA93/ESA95 standards, excluding the allocation of FISIM.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of primary incomes, net / National income, net
str:Code.com:Description: Total value of the earned incomes receivable by an institutional unit or sector less the total of the earned incomes payable, less depreciation and depletion

str:Code.com:Name: Gross national disposable income
str:Code.com:Description: Total income available for final consumption expenditure or saving. It is equal to Gross National Income less current transfers (other than taxes, less subsidies, on production and imports) payable to non-resident units, plus the corresponding transfers receivable by resident units from the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Disposable income, gross, adjusted for the net change in pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: Gross disposable income adjusted to reflect changes in pension entitlements.

str:Code.com:Name: Disposable income, net
str:Code.com:Description: Income available for final consumption expenditure or saving, less deprecaition and depletion.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjusted disposable income, gross
str:Code.com:Description: Disposable income adjusted for certain items, such as changes in pension entitlements.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjusted disposable income, net
str:Code.com:Description: Adjusted disposable income after deducting consumption of fixed capital.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross savings
str:Code.com:Description: The part of disposable income (adjusted for the change in pension entitlements) that is not spent on final consumption, before the deduction of deprecation

str:Code.com:Name: Gross national savings
str:Code.com:Description: Gross national saving represents the total amount of income earned by residents of an economic territory (including households, nonprofit institutions serving housholds, corporations, government entities) that is not consumed during a specific period.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross national saving less capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Gross national saving minus gross capital formation. This approximates net lending/borrowing.

str:Code.com:Name: Net saving
str:Code.com:Description: Gross saving, less depreciation and depletion.

str:Code.com:Name: Net national savings
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of national disposable income that is not used for final consumption expenditure, after deducting consumption of fixed capital (depreciation). This is the net amount a nation saves.

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / net borrowing (-)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between changes in net worth due to saving and capital transfers and net acquisitions of non-financial assets (acquisitions less disposals of non-financial assets, less depreciation and depletion). A negative value represents borrowing; a positive value represendts lending. It is equal to net national income less final consumption expenditure.

str:Code.com:Name: Net worth
str:Code.com:Description: The value of an institutional unit's assets less its outstanding liabilities (including shares and other equity). This is the value of what an institutional unit owns minus what it owes.

str:Code.com:Name: Net financial transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Net acquisition of financial assets or the net incurrence of liabilities, for each type of financial instrument.

str:Code.com:Name: Discrepancy between the financial (B9F) and non-financial (B9) net lending/borrowing
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between net lending/borrowing calculated from financial accounts and non-financial accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / (-) net borrowing excluding interest payable (primary deficit or surplus)
str:Code.com:Description: Net lending/borrowing excluding interest payments, reflecting the underlying fiscal position.

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / net borrowing (-) excluding government assistance to the financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Net lending/borrowing excluding government financial support to financial corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Net current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between current transfers received and current transfers paid.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial net worth
str:Code.com:Description: The value of financial assets minus the value of financial liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial net worth adjusted for pension liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Financial net worth adjusted to include pension liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital stock
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of fixed assets available for production at a given point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital stock, unadjusted
str:Code.com:Description: This represents the total value of a country's accumulated assets that are used to produce goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Consumer durables
str:Code.com:Description: Goods purchased by households for final consumption that can be used repeatedly or continuously over a period of more than one year (e.g., cars, appliances, furniture).

str:Code.com:Name: CIF/FOB Adjustments
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to convert import values from cost, insurance, and freight (CIF) to free on board (FOB).

str:Code.com:Name: Hourly compensation
str:Code.com:Description: Compensation of employees per hour worked.

str:Code.com:Name: Compensation per employee
str:Code.com:Description: Compensation of employees per employee.

str:Code.com:Name: Distributive transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Transactions covering how value added generated by production is distributed to labour, capital and government, and transactions involving the redistribution of income and wealth through taxes and other transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Compensation of employees
str:Code.com:Description: Total remuneration, in cash or in kind, payable by an enterprise to an employee in return for work done by the latter during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Compensation of employees and other taxes excluding other subsidies on products
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of compensation of employees and other taxes on production, excluding subsidies on products.

str:Code.com:Name: Wages and salaries
str:Code.com:Description: Renumeration of employees payable in cash and/or in kind, except for social contributions payable by employers.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual social contributions (primary income)
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers to social security schemes on behalf of their employees. These are considered part of primary income.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual pension contributions (primary income)
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of employers' actual social contributions specifically designated for pension schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual non-pension contributions (primary income)
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of employers' actual social contributions covering benefits other than pensions (e.g., health, unemployment).

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' imputed social contributions (primary income)
str:Code.com:Description: Estimated social contributions that employers would have to pay if they operated funded social insurance schemes themselves.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes payable per unit of a good or service.

str:Code.com:Name: Value added type taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on goods or services collected in stages by enterprises but that are ultimately charged in full to the final purchasers.

str:Code.com:Name: Value added tax paid to the EU (deprecated)
str:Code.com:Description: Formerly, this represented VAT payments made to the European Union.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes and duties on imports excluding value added tax (VAT)
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes and duties levied on imported goods, excluding VAT.

str:Code.com:Name: Import duties
str:Code.com:Description: Customs duties or other import charges that are payable on goods of a particular type when entering the economic territory.

str:Code.com:Name: Levies on imported agricultural products
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes specifically imposed on imported agricultural goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary compensatory amounts on imports
str:Code.com:Description: Payments or charges applied to imports to offset exchange rate fluctuations (typically within the EU's Common Agricultural Policy).

str:Code.com:Name: Excise duties
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied as a product specific unit tax on a predefined limited range of goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on imports excluding value added tax and duties and excluding general sales taxes
str:Code.com:Description: A residual category of import taxes not captured by other specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Profits of import monopolies
str:Code.com:Description: Profits earned by government-controlled entities with exclusive rights to import certain goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Customs and other import duties
str:Code.com:Description: General duties levied on imported goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Export taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on goods or services that become payable to government when the goods leave the economic territory or when the services are delivered to non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Levies on exported goods and services, other than export duties and monetary comp. amounts on exports
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on exported goods and services, excluding specific export duties and monetary compensatory amounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Profits of export monopolies
str:Code.com:Description: Profits earned by government-controlled entities with exclusive rights to export certain goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes resulting from multiple exchange rate regimes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes arising from situations where different exchange rates are applied to different types of transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Export taxes, exchange taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes specifically levied on exports or foreign exchange transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products except value added tax and import and export taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on goods and services that become payable as a result of the production, sale, transfer, leasing or delivery of those goods or services, or as a result of their use for own consumption or own capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Excise duties and consumption taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on the production or consumption of specific goods (e.g., alcohol, tobacco).

str:Code.com:Name: Stamp taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on legal documents or transactions, often through the purchase of stamps.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on financial and capital transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the change in ownership of property, except those classified as gifts, inheritance, or estate transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Car registration taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes paid when registering a vehicle.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on entertainment
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on entertainment activities or events.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on insurance premiums
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on insurance premiums paid by policyholders.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on specific services
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on specific services not covered by other categories.

str:Code.com:Name: General sales or turnover taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Broad-based taxes on sales or turnover of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: General sales or turnover taxes: General sales taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the production, leasing, delivery, sale, purchase or other change of ownership of a wide range of goods and the provision of a wide range of services.

str:Code.com:Name: Turnover and other general taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Broad based taxes on sales or turnover of goods and services, including other general taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Profits of fiscal monopolies
str:Code.com:Description: Profits earned by government-controlled entities with exclusive rights to produce or sell certain goods or services.

str:Code.com:Name: Export duties and monetary comp. amounts on exports
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes specifically levied on exported goods, including monetary compensatory amounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on products n.e.c.
str:Code.com:Description: Other taxes on products not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products involving EU Institutions, traditional EU own resources (custom and agricultural duties)
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on products that form part of the EU's own resources, primarily customs duties and agricultural levies.

str:Code.com:Name: Total sales taxes
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all sales taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Total excises
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all excise duties.

str:Code.com:Name: General taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Total general taxes on goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products: taxes on specific services
str:Code.com:Description: Total taxes on products that are taxes on specific services.

str:Code.com:Name: Total taxes on exports
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all taxes on exports.

str:Code.com:Name: Profits of export or import monopolies
str:Code.com:Description: Total profits of government-controlled export or import monopolies.

str:Code.com:Name: Total exchange taxes
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all exchange taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes less subsidies on products
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between taxes on products and subsidies on products.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes less subsidies on products excluding value added tax (VAT)
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes payable less subsidies receivable on goods or services produced as outputs including other taxes or subsidies on production such as those payable on the labour, machinery, buildings or other assets used in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on land, buildings or other structures
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on ownership or use of real estate.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on the use of fixed assets
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on the use of fixed assets, such as machinery or equipment.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on the use of motor vehicles
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes related to the use of motor vehicles.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on the use of other machinery or equipment
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on the use of machinery and equipment other than motor vehicles.

str:Code.com:Name: Total wage bill and payroll taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on employers based on their wage bill or payroll.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on international transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on transactions that cross international borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Business and professional licenses
str:Code.com:Description: Fees or taxes paid for the right to operate a business or profession.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on pollution
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes designed to discourage environmentally harmful activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Under-compensation of value added tax (flat rate system)
str:Code.com:Description: An adjustment related to simplified VAT systems for small businesses.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production n.e.c.: recurrent taxes on net wealth
str:Code.com:Description: Recurrent taxes on an entity's net wealth.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production n.e.c.: other recurrent taxes on property
str:Code.com:Description: Other recurrent taxes on property not covered elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production n.e.c.: other taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Other taxes on goods and services not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production n.e.c.: other taxes on production
str:Code.com:Description: Other taxes on production not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Net taxes on production
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on production less subsidies on production.

str:Code.com:Name: Indirect taxes on energy
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on energy products.

str:Code.com:Name: Indirect taxes excluding VAT and indirect taxes on energy
str:Code.com:Description: Indirect taxes excluding VAT and taxes on energy products.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on production and imports other than for own-account capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on production and imports that are not related to own-account capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Total taxes on goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on international trade and transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Other taxes on international trade and transactions not covered elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on international trade and transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes that become payable when goods cross the national or customs frontiers of the economic territory or when transactions in services exchange between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on production and imports less subsidies
str:Code.com:Description: Total taxes less total subsidies on production and imports.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies
str:Code.com:Description: Current unrequited payments that government units, including non-resident government units, make to enterprises on the basis of the levels of their production activities or the quantities or values of the goods or services that they produce, sell, export or import.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on products
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies payable per unit of a good or service.

str:Code.com:Name: Import subsidies
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies that are receivable on goods when entering the economic territory, or on services being delivered to resident institutional units.

str:Code.com:Name: Export subsidies
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies on goods or services that become payable by government when the goods leave the economic territory or when the services are delivered to non-resident units.

str:Code.com:Name: Other subsidies on products
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies on goods or services produced by resident enterprises, or on imports, that become payable as a result of the production, sale, transfer, leasing or delivery of those goods or services, or as a result of their use for own consumption or own capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on products, payable (positive sign, for reporting in GFS presentation)
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies granted to producers based on the quantity or value of goods or services they produce.

str:Code.com:Name: Other subsidies on production
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies other than subsidies on products that resident enterprises may receive as a consequence of engaging in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Other subsidies on production, payable (positive sign, for reporting in GFS presentation)
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies granted to producers for reasons other than the quantity or value of output.

str:Code.com:Name: Other subsidies on production, receivable (positive sign, for reporting in GFS presentation)
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies receivable by producers for reasons other than the quantity or value of output.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies, payable (positive sign, for reporting in GFS presentation)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of subsidies paid.

str:Code.com:Name: Property income
str:Code.com:Description: The income receivable by the owner of a financial asset or the owner of a non-produced non-financial asset in return for providing funds to or putting the non-financial assets at the disposal of, another institutional unit.

str:Code.com:Name: Property income and other current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: A broader category encompassing property income and various current transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest
str:Code.com:Description: Form of income that is receivable by the owners of certain kinds of financial assets, namely SDRs, deposits, debt securities, loans and other accounts receivable for putting the financial asset at the disposal of another institutional unit.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest accrued (not yet paid)
str:Code.com:Description: Interest income or expense that has been earned or incurred but not yet paid.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest before FISIM allocation
str:Code.com:Description: Interest flows before the allocation of FISIM.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest from the European instrument for temporary Support to mitigate Unemployment Risks in an Emergency (SURE) and the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF) loans
str:Code.com:Description: Interest payments specifically related to loans from these EU programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Distributed income of corporations
str:Code.com:Description: 1. data.ecb.europa.eu

str:Code.com:Name: Dividends
str:Code.com:Description: Earnings distributed to the owners of corporate equity for placing funds at the disposal of corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Withdrawals from income of quasi-corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Part of distributable income that the owner withdraws from the quasi-corporation.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment income
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income other than interest, such as dividends.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income attributable to insurance policyholders
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income receivable from the investment of insurance technical reserves, which is attributed to the policy holders.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income payable on pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income received from the investment of assets accumulated for defined contribution schemes, and the unwinding of the discount of the entitlements for defined benefit schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income attributable to collective investment fund share holders
str:Code.com:Description: Dividends and retained earnings of collective investment funds, which are attributable to the shareholders.

str:Code.com:Name: Dividends distributed to collective investment fund shareholders (IF)
str:Code.com:Description: Dividends paid out to shareholders of collective investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Reinvested earnings attributable to collective investment fund shareholders (IF)
str:Code.com:Description: Earnings of collective investment funds that are retained within the fund rather than distributed as dividends.

str:Code.com:Name: Property income from investment income disbursements
str:Code.com:Description: Property income arising from the distribution of investment income.

str:Code.com:Name: Rent
str:Code.com:Description: Income receivable by the owner of a non-produced non-financial asset (the lessor or landlord) for putting the non-financial asset at the disposal of another institutional unit (a lessee or tenant).

str:Code.com:Name: Property income attributed to insurance policy holders and rent
str:Code.com:Description: Property income specifically attributed to policyholders from the investment of insurance reserves, combined with income from rent on non-produced assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest and rents
str:Code.com:Description: A combination of interest income (returns on financial assets) and rent income (returns on non-produced non-financial assets like land).

str:Code.com:Name: Property income other than interest
str:Code.com:Description: All forms of property income excluding interest, such as dividends, withdrawals from quasi-corporations, reinvested earnings on FDI, and rent.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income (D4 less D45) (BOP)
str:Code.com:Description: nvestment income is all property income (D4) minus rent (D45), specifically in the context of Balance of Payments (BOP) statistics.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income
str:Code.com:Description: Income receivable by the owner of a financial asset in return for providing funds to another institutional unit.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on income, wealth, etc.
str:Code.com:Description: Mainly consisting of taxes on the incomes of households or profits of corporations and of taxes on wealth that are payable regularly every tax period (as distinct from capital taxes levied infrequently).

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes payable on incomes, profits and capital gains.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on individual or household income excluding holding gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the income of individuals or households, excluding taxes on capital gains or other increases in asset values.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on the income or profits of corporations excluding holding gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the profits of corporations, excluding taxes on capital gains.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on holding gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes specifically levied on capital gains, or increases in the value of assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on individual or household holding gains
str:Code.com:Description: Personal income taxes including those deducted by employers (pay-as-you-earn taxes) and surtaxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on holding gains of corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on capital gains specifically for corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on holding gains n.e.c
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on holding gains not elsewhere classified, likely referring to those outside of corporations or individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on individuals' winnings from lottery or gambling
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on winnings from lotteries or gambling by individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on corporations' winnings from lottery or gambling
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on winnings from lotteries or gambling by corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on income n.e.c.
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on income not elsewhere classified, a residual category.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income payable by individuals
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on income specifically paid by individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income payable by corporations and other enterprises
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on income paid by corporations and other business entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on income, profits, and capital gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes payable on the actual or presumed incomes, profits and capital gains.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on individual or household income including holding gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on the income of individuals and households, including taxes on capital gains.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on the income or profits of corporations including holding gains
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on corporate income including capital gains.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on land, buildings and other structures
str:Code.com:Description: Recurrent taxes on the ownership or use of real estate.

str:Code.com:Name: Recurrent taxes on net wealth: other current taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Periodic taxes on the net wealth of individuals or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other recurrent taxes on property: other current taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Recurrent taxes on property not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on ownership of motor vehicles
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the ownership of vehicles.

str:Code.com:Name: Poll taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied as a fixed amount per person.

str:Code.com:Name: Expenditure taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on specific expenditures or purchases.

str:Code.com:Name: Payments by households for licenses
str:Code.com:Description: Fees paid by households for various licenses (e.g., driver's licenses, professional licenses).

str:Code.com:Name: Payments by households for licenses to use motor vehicle taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Payments for licenses specifically related to motor vehicle use.

str:Code.com:Name: Payments by households for radio and television licenses
str:Code.com:Description: Payments for licenses to operate radios and televisions.

str:Code.com:Name: Payments by households for other licenses and permits
str:Code.com:Description: Payments for other licenses and permits not specified.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current taxes on international transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on transactions crossing international borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current taxes on international transactions: exchange taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes specifically on foreign exchange transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current taxes on international transactions: other
str:Code.com:Description: Other taxes on international transactions not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Social contributions and social benefits
str:Code.com:Description: This encompasses both payments made into social security systems (contributions) and payments received from them (benefits).

str:Code.com:Name: Net social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between social contributions received and social benefits paid.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Actual payments made by employers into pension schemes for their employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security actual pension contributions by employers
str:Code.com:Description: Employer pension contributions specifically to social security systems.

str:Code.com:Name: Employment related schemes actual pension contributions by employers
str:Code.com:Description: Employer pension contributions to schemes outside of social security, linked to employment.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual non-pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Actual payments made by employers for social benefits other than pensions (e.g., health insurance, unemployment benefits).

str:Code.com:Name: Social security actual no pension contributions by employers
str:Code.com:Description: Employer non-pension contributions to social security.

str:Code.com:Name: Employment related schemes actual no pension contributions by employers
str:Code.com:Description: Employer non-pension contributions to employment-related schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Compulsory employers' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Required social contributions paid by employers.

str:Code.com:Name: Voluntary employers' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Optional social contributions paid by employers.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' imputed social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Estimated social contributions that employers would have to pay if they operated their own social insurance schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Households' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Actual social contributions made by households.

str:Code.com:Name: Households' actual pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Actual pension contributions made by households.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security actual pension contributions by employees
str:Code.com:Description: Employee pension contributions to social security.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security actual pension contributions by self-employed or nonemployed
str:Code.com:Description: Pension contributions to social security by those not formally employed.

str:Code.com:Name: Employment related schemes actual pension contributions by employees
str:Code.com:Description: Employee pension contributions to employment-related schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Households' actual non-pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Actual non-pension contributions made by households.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security actual no pension contributions by employees
str:Code.com:Description: Employee non-pension contributions to social security.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security actual no pension contributions by self-employed or nonemployed
str:Code.com:Description: Non-pension contributions to social security by those not formally employed.

str:Code.com:Name: Employment related schemes actual no pension contributions by employees
str:Code.com:Description: Employee non-pension contributions to employment-related schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Compulsory households' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Required social contributions paid by households.

str:Code.com:Name: Compulsory employees' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Required social contributions paid by employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Compulsory actual social contributions by the non-employed
str:Code.com:Description: Required social contributions paid by non-employed individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Compulsory actual social contributions by the self-employed
str:Code.com:Description: Required social contributions paid by self-employed individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Voluntary households' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Optional social contributions paid by households.

str:Code.com:Name: Households' social contribution supplements
str:Code.com:Description: Additional payments made by households to supplement their social contributions.

str:Code.com:Name: Households' pension contribution supplements
str:Code.com:Description: Supplements to household pension contributions.

str:Code.com:Name: Households' non-pension contribution supplements
str:Code.com:Description: Supplements to household non-pension contributions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other (actuarial) change of pension entitlements in social security pension schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in pension entitlements due to actuarial adjustments within social security pension schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Actual social contributions from employers and households
str:Code.com:Description: The total of actual social contributions from both employers and households.

str:Code.com:Name: Social insurance scheme service charges (-)
str:Code.com:Description: Fees charged for administering social insurance schemes (recorded as a negative value).

str:Code.com:Name: Social security benefits in cash
str:Code.com:Description: Cash benefits paid out by social security systems.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security pension benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Cash pension benefits paid by social security.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security non-pension benefits in cash
str:Code.com:Description: Cash benefits paid by social security systems other than pensions (e.g., unemployment benefits, sickness benefits, disability benefits).

str:Code.com:Name: Other social insurance benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Social benefits payable by social insurance schemes to households, because the beneficiary participates in a social insurance scheme, other than a social security scheme, and the social risk insured against has occurred.

str:Code.com:Name: Other social insurance pension benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Pension benefits paid by social insurance schemes other than social security (e.g., occupational pension schemes).

str:Code.com:Name: Other social insurance non-pension benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Non-pension benefits paid by social insurance schemes other than social security (e.g., private health insurance benefits).

str:Code.com:Name: Social assistance benefits in cash
str:Code.com:Description: Cash benefits provided to individuals or households in need, typically means-tested (e.g., welfare payments).

str:Code.com:Name: Other social allowances (payments of family, education, medical services and other allowances)
str:Code.com:Description: Cash payments for specific purposes such as family support (child allowances), education grants, and medical expenses.

str:Code.com:Name: Retirement and survivors' Pensions
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made to retired individuals and surviving family members of deceased individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Social transfers in kind
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services provided to households by government and NPISHs either free or at prices that are not economically significant.

str:Code.com:Name: Social transfers in kind: non-market production
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services provided to households for free or at significantly reduced prices by government or non-profit institutions (e.g., public education, healthcare provided directly by public hospitals).

str:Code.com:Name: Social transfers in kind: purchased market production
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services provided to households for free or at reduced prices by government or NPISHs, but purchased from market producers (e.g., government-funded vouchers for private healthcare).

str:Code.com:Name: Social transfers in kind: purchased market production, social security
str:Code.com:Description: Social transfers in kind purchased from market producers and related to social security schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Social transfers in kind: purchased market production, employment-related schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Social transfers in kind purchased from market producers and related to employment-related schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Social transfers in kind: purchased market production, social assistance
str:Code.com:Description: Social transfers in kind purchased from market producers and related to social assistance programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Social contribution and social benefits, other than social transfer in kind
str:Code.com:Description: The combined value of social contributions and all social benefits excluding transfers in kind.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits other than social transfers in kind and social transfers in kind - purchased market production
str:Code.com:Description: All cash social benefits plus social transfers in kind produced by non-market producers.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: All current transfers other than current taxes on income, wealth, etc., social contributions and benefits. In the external accounts, all current transfers other than personal transfers are included in other current transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Net non-life insurance premiums
str:Code.com:Description: Premiums paid for non-life insurance (e.g., car insurance, property insurance) minus the service charges of the insurance companies.

str:Code.com:Name: Net non-life direct insurance premiums
str:Code.com:Description: Net premiums for direct insurance contracts (between the insurer and the insured).

str:Code.com:Name: Net non-life reinsurance premiums
str:Code.com:Description: Net premiums for reinsurance contracts (between insurance companies).

str:Code.com:Name: Net premiums of nonlife insurance schemes other than standardized guarantee
str:Code.com:Description: Non-life insurance premiums excluding those related to standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fees for standardized guarantee schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Fees paid for standardized guarantees, which are mechanisms to mitigate risks (e.g., deposit insurance).

str:Code.com:Name: Non-life insurance claims
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts payable in settlement of injuries or damages that result from an event covered by a non-life insurance (or reinsurance) policy. In the case of major catastrapohic events, some proportion of the claims may be recorded as capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-life direct insurance claims
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by insurers to policyholders for claims under direct insurance contracts.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-life reinsurance claims
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by reinsurers to insurance companies for claims covered by reinsurance contracts.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers within general government
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers between different levels of government, such as frequently occur between central and state or local government, and between general government and social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Current grants to/from governments and international organizations
str:Code.com:Description: Unrequited current transfers between governments and international organizations (e.g., foreign aid).

str:Code.com:Name: Current international cooperation
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers in cash or in kind between the governments of different countries or between governments and international organisations.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers, in cash or in kind, other than current taxes on income, wealth, etc.; net social contributions; social benefits; net non-life insurance premiums and claims; current transfers within general government; and current international cooperation. In the external accounts, miscellaneous current transfers include all current transfers, in cash or in kind, other than personal transfers, current taxes on income, wealth, etc.; social contributions; social benefits; net nonlife insurance premiums and claims; and current international cooperation.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers to NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Unrequited transactions between two parties where one party provides a good, service, non-financial or financial asset to the other party, with no expectation of anything of economic value in exchange. Unlike capital transfers, they do not oblige one or both parties to acquire, or dispose of, an asset.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers between resident and non-resident households
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers of money between households in different countries (e.g., remittances).

str:Code.com:Name: Other miscellaneous current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Fines and penalties, payments (after deduction of service charge and donations) and receipts related to lotteries and gambling, and payments for compensation

str:Code.com:Name: Fines, penalties, and forfeits
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made as a result of legal or administrative actions (e.g., traffic fines, court penalties).

str:Code.com:Name: Other miscellaneous current transfers: other than fines, penalties, and forfeits
str:Code.com:Description: Other current transfers not elsewhere classified, excluding fines, penalties, and forfeits.

str:Code.com:Name: Value added tax and GNI - based EU own resources
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by EU member states to the EU budget, based on VAT revenue and Gross National Income (GNI).

str:Code.com:Name: VAT-based third EU own resource
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of EU own resources derived from VAT revenue.

str:Code.com:Name: GNI-based fourth EU own resource
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of EU own resources derived from GNI.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers of which UK rebate
str:Code.com:Description: Part of the current transfers related to the EU budget, specifically highlighting the UK rebate (now historically relevant).

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous non-tax contribution of the government to the institutions of the EU
str:Code.com:Description: Other contributions made by governments to EU institutions that are not taxes.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous non-tax contribution of the government to the institutions of the European Union (EU): of which plastics contribution - EU own resource
str:Code.com:Description: A specific non-tax contribution related to plastics, forming part of the EU's own resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous current transfers and VAT and GNI-based own resources [deprecated]
str:Code.com:Description: A previously used category encompassing various current transfers and EU own resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers within general government, current international cooperation and miscellaneous current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers between different levels of government, international cooperation transfers, and other miscellaneous current transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Current international cooperation, miscellaneous current transfers and VAT and GNI-based EU own resources
str:Code.com:Description: Combines international cooperation transfers, miscellaneous current transfers, and EU own resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers financed by the Recovery and Resilience Facility (RRF)
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers specifically funded by the EU's Recovery and Resilience Facility.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: all miscellaneous current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: A broad category encompassing all miscellaneous current transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjustment for the change in pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: An adjustment made to account for changes in the value of pension entitlements.

str:Code.com:Name: Transfer of pension entitlements between schemes
str:Code.com:Description: The value of pension entitlements transferred between different pension schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Change in entitlements due to negotiated changes in scheme structure
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in pension entitlements resulting from alterations to the rules or structure of pension schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Net capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Net unrequited transfers, either in cash or in kind, linked to the acquisition, disposal or transfer of an asset (other than cash or inventories); or where a liability is forgiven or assumed; or where the transfers are intended to address accumulated losses incurred over a multi-year period.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes charged at irregular and infrequent intervals on the values of the assets or net worth owned by institutional units or on the values of assets transferred between institutional units as a result of legacies, gifts inter vivos, or other transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes levied on the transfer of capital assets (e.g., inheritance taxes, gift taxes).

str:Code.com:Name: Capital levies
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on the values of the assets or net worth owned by institutional units levied at irregular, and very infrequent, intervals of time.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital taxes n.e.c.
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital taxes not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment grants
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers made by governments to other resident or non-resident institutional units to finance all or part of the costs of their acquiring fixed assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers other than investment grants, capital taxes, and debt forgiveness.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers from general government to relevant sectors representing taxes and social contributions assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers representing taxes and social contributions that the government expects not to receive.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on products that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Other taxes on production that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on income that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current taxes assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Other current taxes that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers actual social contributions assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions from employers that are assessed but not expected to be collected.
