str:Code.com:Name: Households' actual social contributions assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions from households that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Employees' actual social contributions assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions from employees that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Actual social contributions by non-employed persons assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions from non-employed persons that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Actual social contributions by self-employed persons assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions from self-employed persons that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital taxes assessed but unlikely to be collected
str:Code.com:Description: Capital taxes that are assessed but not expected to be collected.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonlife insurance capital claims
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers related to non-life insurance claims, often in cases of large-scale damage.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers that do not fit into other specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Calls on guarantees
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made when a guarantee is invoked, transferring a financial obligation.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital injections recorded as capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: It refers to capital injections (funds invested in a business) that are recorded as capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers recorded in the context of asset purchases
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers that occur as part of transactions involving the purchase of assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers and investment grants
str:Code.com:Description: A combination of other capital transfers and grants specifically for investment purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on use of goods and on permission to use goods and perform activities
str:Code.com:Description: Fees levied for the issuance of a license or permit that are not commensurate with the cost of the control function of government.

str:Code.com:Name: Motor vehicles taxes [IMF GFSM definition]
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on the use of motor vehicles or permission to use motor vehicles.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on use of goods and on permission to use goods or perform activities
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes paid by enterprises in order to obtain a license to carry on a particular kind of business or profession and taxes payable by individuals to perform certain activities .

str:Code.com:Name: Social security contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Amounts payable to a social security scheme in order for a designated beneficiary to be entitled to recieve the social benefits covered by the scheme.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers payable by social security funds to households, intended to provide for the needs that arise from certain events or circumstances, for example, sickness, unemployment, retirement, housing, education or family circumstances.

str:Code.com:Name: Social assistance benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Social benefits payable in cash by government units or non-profit institutions serving households to households to meet the same needs as social insurance benefits, but without any requirement to participate in a social insurance scheme through the payment of contributions.

str:Code.com:Name: Transfers not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: All transfers other than taxes, grants, social contributions, social benefits, and fines penalties, and forfeits. As a government expense then subsidies are also excluded, but within government revenue subsidies are treated within transfers not elsewhere classified.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP) Oil-based production
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to the portion of a country's GDP that comes from the extraction, processing, and production of crude oil and related petroleum products.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP) PPP weight
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to a country's GDP adjusted for purchasing power parity (PPP).

str:Code.com:Name: Non-oil GDP
str:Code.com:Description: This represents the portion of a country's GDP generated from all economic activities excluding the oil sector. This includes manufacturing, agriculture, services, tourism, and other industries.

str:Code.com:Name: Economic appearance of assets
str:Code.com:Description: Appearance of assets which is neither due to transactions nor due to holding gains and losses (revaluations). Examples relate to the recognition of produced assets, not previously recognised as assets; discoveries of natural resources; changes in the quality of land; the recognition of goodwill in the purchase of a corporation; and the initiation of contracts, leases and licenses.

str:Code.com:Name: Catastrophic losses
str:Code.com:Description: Losses which are the result of large scale, discrete and recognizable events that may destroy a significantly large number of assets within any of the asset categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Uncompensated seizures
str:Code.com:Description: Governments or other institutional units taking possession of assets of other institutional units without compensation, for reasons other than payment of taxes, fines, etc.

str:Code.com:Name: Other changes in volume not elsewhere classified
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the value of assets or liabilities that are neither due to transactions nor due to holding gains and losses (revaluations). They include, amongst others, economic appearance and disappearance of non-produced assets, catastrophic losses, changes due to cancellation and write-offs of debt, uncompensated seizures, reclassifications, and the changes in financial assets arising from entities changing their economy of residence. In the external accounts, the other changes in the volume of assets and liabilities are restricted to those relating to financial assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in classification
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in assets and liabilities resulting from changes in sector classification and structure (among which changes in assets and liabilities arising from entities changing their economy of residence), and those resulting from changes in the classification of assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Real holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: The amount by which the value of assets increases (decreases) beyond the neutral holding gains for the period, in the absence of transactions and other changes in the volume of assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Labour productivity (per hours worked)
str:Code.com:Description: Measure of output, or value added, per unit of input.

str:Code.com:Name: Savings to investment ratio
str:Code.com:Description: This ratio compares the portion of an economy's income that is saved to the portion that is invested.

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / net borrowing (-) under EDP
str:Code.com:Description: Net lending is defined as the difference between changes in net worth due to saving and capital transfers and net acquisitions of non-financial assets (acquisitions less disposals of non-financial assets, less depreciation). If the amount is negative, it represents net borrowing. In the external accounts, it represents the sum of the balances on the current and capital accounts. This is conceptually equal to the net balance of the financial account. If the balance is positive, it is called net lending, implying that in net terms, the economy supplies funds to the rest of the world. If the balance is negative, it is called net borrowing, implying that in net terms, the economy receives funds from the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Output
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services produced by (part of) an enterprise, excluding the value of any goods and services used in an activity for which the establishment does not assume the risk of using the products in production, and excluding the value of goods and services consumed by the same establishment except for goods and services used for capital formation (fixed capital or changes in inventories) or own final consumption.

str:Code.com:Name: Market output
str:Code.com:Description: Output intended for sale at economically significant prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial Intermediation Services Indirectly Measured (FISIM)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial intermediation services which are implicitly charged in the form of either the difference between a reference rate and the interest rate actually paid to depositors, or the difference between the interest rate charged to borrowers and a reference rate.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-market output
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and individual or collective services produced by government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households that are supplied free, or at prices that are not economically significant, to other institutional units or the community as a whole.

str:Code.com:Name: Output gap
str:Code.com:Description: The output gap is the difference between the actual level of output in an economy and its potential level of output.

str:Code.com:Name: Intermediate consumption
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services consumed as inputs by a process of production, excluding fixed assets whose consumption is recorded as depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Final consumption expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: The amount of expenditure on consumption goods and services, whether individual or collective. Final consumption expenditure can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households (NPISHs).

str:Code.com:Name: CO2 emissions embodied in Final Domestic Demand
str:Code.com:Description: Amount of CO2 emissions from fuel combustion embodied in domestic final demand. Environment, Climate Change, Mitigation, Trade-Related indicators.

str:Code.com:Name: Collective consumption expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditure on services provided simultaneously to all members of the community or to all members of a particular section of the community. Collective final consumption expenditure is only measured for general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households (NPISHs).

str:Code.com:Name: Actual final consumption
str:Code.com:Description: Goods or services that are acquired by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of human needs or wants, whether individual or collective. Actual final consumption can be measured for households, general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Actual individual consumption
str:Code.com:Description: Goods or services that are acquired by resident institutional units for the direct satisfaction of the needs or wants of individual households. Actual individual consumption is only measured for households.

str:Code.com:Name: Actual collective consumption
str:Code.com:Description: Services provided simultaneously to all members of the community or to all members of a particular section of the community. Actual collective consumption is only measured for general government, the central bank and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Acquisitions less disposals of produced assets for purposes of fixed capital formation, inventories or valuables.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets: machinery and equipment
str:Code.com:Description: Net investment in non-residential machinery and equipment (e.g. vehicles, computers).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross fixed capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Acquisitions less disposals of fixed assets during the accounting period, including certain specified expenditures on services that add to the value of non-produced assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net fixed capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Gross fixed capital formation, after deduction of depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in inventories
str:Code.com:Description: Value of entries into inventories less the value of withdrawals and less the value of any recurrent losses of goods held in inventories during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Net capital formation
str:Code.com:Description: Gross capital formation, after deduction of depreciation.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: Change in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Merchanting
str:Code.com:Description: Purchase of goods by a resident (of the compiling economy) from a nonresident combined with the subsequent resale of the same goods to another nonresident without the goods being physically moved in and out the compiling economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods sent abroad after processing (inward processing, i.e. your country processes)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustment required to reconcile the treatment of pensions as current transfers (social contributions and social benefits) with the treatment of pension entitlements as financial assets. After the adjustment, the saving (SNA) and current account balance (BPM) are the same as what they would have been if social contributions and benefits related to pensions are considered as pure financial transactions, and not recorded as current transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of services
str:Code.com:Description: Services provided by residents of the compiling economy to non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Re-exports
str:Code.com:Description: Goods produced in other economies, and previously imported, that are exported with no substantial transformation from the state in which they were previously imported.

str:Code.com:Name: CO2 emissions embodied in Gross Exports
str:Code.com:Description: Amount of CO2 emissions from fuel combustion embodied in exports. Environment, Climate Change, Mitigation, Trade-Related indicators.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: Change in the economic ownership of goods from non-residents to residents of the compiling economy, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of services
str:Code.com:Description: Services provided by non-residents to residents of the compiling economy.

str:Code.com:Name: CO2 emissions embodied in Gross Imports
str:Code.com:Description: Amount of CO2 emissions from fuel combustion embodied in imports. Environment, Climate Change, Mitigation, Trade-Related indicators.

str:Code.com:Name: Total population
str:Code.com:Description: All persons who are resident in the economic territory at a given point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: CO2 emissions embodied in Production
str:Code.com:Description: Amount of CO2 emissions from fuel combustion embodied in production. Environment, Climate Change, Mitigation, Trade-Related indicators.

str:Code.com:Name: Employees
str:Code.com:Description: Persons who, by agreement, work for a resident institutional unit and receive remuneration for their labour.

str:Code.com:Name: Self employed
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals working for their own account.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Economic flows that are an interaction between institutional units by mutual agreement or through the operation of the law, or actions within an institutional unit that are analytically useful to treat like transactions because the unit is operating in two different capacities.

str:Code.com:Name: Total final use
str:Code.com:Description: The total spending on final goods and services in the economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Terms of trade
str:Code.com:Description: Ratio of the price of exports to the price of imports.

str:Code.com:Name: Total resources production account
str:Code.com:Description: The total resources available to the economy from domestic production.

str:Code.com:Name: Total resources generation of income account
str:Code.com:Description: Resources available for the generation of income.

str:Code.com:Name: Total resources allocation of primary income account
str:Code.com:Description: Resources available for the allocation of primary income.

str:Code.com:Name: Total resources secondary distribution of income account
str:Code.com:Description: Resources available for secondary income distribution.

str:Code.com:Name: Total resources use of disposable income account
str:Code.com:Description: Resources available for the use of disposable income.

str:Code.com:Name: Total change in liabilities and net worth: change in net worth due to saving and capital transfers account
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in liabilities and net worth due to saving and capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Total change in liabilities and net worth: acquisitions of nonfinancial assets accounts
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in liabilities and net worth due to the acquisition of non-financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Total supply
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of goods and services available in the economy (domestic production plus imports).

str:Code.com:Name: Total use
str:Code.com:Description: The total use of goods and services in the economy (intermediate consumption plus final use).

str:Code.com:Name: Total uses production account
str:Code.com:Description: The uses of goods and services in the production process.

str:Code.com:Name: Total uses generation of income account
str:Code.com:Description: The uses of resources in the generation of income.

str:Code.com:Name: Total uses allocation of primary income account
str:Code.com:Description: The uses of resources in the allocation of primary income.

str:Code.com:Name: Total uses secondary distribution of income account
str:Code.com:Description: The uses of resources in secondary income distribution.

str:Code.com:Name: Total uses use of disposable income account
str:Code.com:Description: The uses of disposable income.

str:Code.com:Name: Total change in assets: change in net worth due to saving and capital, transfers account
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in assets and net worth due to saving and capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Total change in assets: acquisitions of non-financial assets accounts
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in assets due to the acquisition of non-financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Total intermediate consumption/final use
str:Code.com:Description: The combined value of intermediate consumption and final use.

str:Code.com:Name: Domestic use
str:Code.com:Description: Legal tender in the economy which is issued by the monetary authority for that economy. That is, either that of an individual economy or, in a currency union, that of the common currency area to which the economy belongs.

str:Code.com:Name: Unit labour cost (based on hours worked)
str:Code.com:Description: The cost of labor per unit of output, measured using hours worked.

str:Code.com:Name: Unit labour cost (based on persons)
str:Code.com:Description: The cost of labor per unit of output, measured using the number of persons employed.

str:Code.com:Name: Unemployed persons
str:Code.com:Description: Persons who are the sole or joint owners of the unincorporated enterprises in which they work, excluding those unincorporated enterprises that are classified as quasi-corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Unit profits
str:Code.com:Description: Profits per unit of output.

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical discrepancy (expenditure approach)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between GDP calculated using the expenditure approach and the headline measure of GDP (either by the production approach or an average of the three approaches).

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical discrepancies in stock flow adjustment
str:Code.com:Description: Discrepancies arising in the reconciliation of changes in stocks and flows.

str:Code.com:Name: Other statistical discrepancies (+/-) in stock flow adjustment
str:Code.com:Description: Other discrepancies in stock flow adjustment not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Transaction imbalance
str:Code.com:Description: Discrepancies arising from the recording of transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Budget imbalance
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between government revenue and expenditure.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic income
str:Code.com:Description: Total income earned through the production of goods and services in the economy, consisting of remuneration of employees plus gross operating surplus plus gross mixed incomes plus taxes less subsidies on both production and imports.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP) production approach
str:Code.com:Description: The value of total output less total intermediate consumption plus any taxes less subsidies on products not already included in the value of output.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product (GDP) in US Dollars
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product reflects the total income earned through the production of goods and services in an economic territory during an accounting period. It can be measured in three different ways: using either the expenditure approach, the income approach, or the production approach. This value has been converted into US Dollars.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross domestic product in purchasing power parity (PPP)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross domestic product in purchasing power parity (PPP).

str:Code.com:Name: Potential gross domestic product (fiscal year)
str:Code.com:Description: The level of GDP that an economy could produce in a fiscal year at full capacity utilization without generating inflationary pressures.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Seasonal Adjustment
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents a set of codes identifying the statistical techniques employed to remove seasonal patterns or fluctuations from time series data.

str:Code.com:Name: Seasonally adjusted (SA)
str:Code.com:Description: Time series that have been adjusted for seasonal variations, including trading-day (working-day) effects and other regular calendar variations if present.

str:Code.com:Name: Seasonally adjusted factors
str:Code.com:Description: The seasonally adjusted component is the result of the extraction of the seasonal component (including the calendar effects component) from a time series. If neither seasonal nor calendar influences are present in the original data, the seasonally adjusted series is given by the original data.

str:Code.com:Name: Not seasonally adjusted (NSA)
str:Code.com:Description: Time series that have not been adjusted for seasonal variations, including trading-day (working-day) effects and other regular calendar variations if present.

str:Code.com:Name: Trend cycle data
str:Code.com:Description: Trend estimates are derived from seasonally adjusted estimates via an averaging process which attempts to remove the irregular component of the time series. This allows the underlying direction of a time series to be identified.

str:Code.com:Name: Working day adjusted
str:Code.com:Description: Working-day adjustment is a part of the calendar adjustment which focusses on the changing number of working days (Monday - Friday) in the various months and their effect on statistical indicators (e.g. industrial production, production in construction) for these months. (source: Eurostat)

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Not applicable

str:Code.com:Name: Trend
str:Code.com:Description: Trend estimates are derived from seasonally adjusted estimates via an averaging process which attempts to remove the irregular component of the time series. This allows the underlying direction of a time series to be identified.

str:Code.com:Name: Trend-cycle data, calendar adjusted
str:Code.com:Description: Trend-cycle data, calendar adjusted estimates are derived from seasonally adjusced estimates via an averaging process which attempts to remove the irregular components of che time series. This allows The underlying direccion of a time series to be idencified. The data are adjusted for variations in the calendar.

str:Code.com:Name: Trend-cycle data, not calendar adjusted
str:Code.com:Description: Trend-cycle data, calendar adjusted estimates are derived from seasonally adjusced estimates via an averaging process which attempts to remove the irregular components of che time series. This allows The underlying direccion of a time series to be idencified. The data are not adjusted for variations in the calendar.

str:Code.com:Name: Calendar component
str:Code.com:Description: Calendar component

str:Code.com:Name: Seasonal component
str:Code.com:Description: Seasonal component

str:Code.com:Name: Seasonal and calendar components
str:Code.com:Description: Seasonal and calendar components

str:Code.com:Name: Irregular component
str:Code.com:Description: Irregular component

str:Code.com:Name: Neither seasonally adjusted nor calendar adjusted data
str:Code.com:Description: Neither seasonally adjusted nor calendar adjusted data

str:Code.com:Name: Seasonally adjusted data, not calendar adjusted
str:Code.com:Description: Seasonally adjusted time series are series that have been adjusted for seasonal variations, including trading-day (working-day) effects.

str:Code.com:Name: Calendar adjusted data, not seasonally adjusted
str:Code.com:Description: Calendar adjusted data, not seasonally adjusted

str:Code.com:Name: Calendar and seasonally adjusted data
str:Code.com:Description: Seasonally adjusted time series are series that have been adjusted for seasonal variations, including trading-day (working-day) effects and other regular calendar variations.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Currency
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist provides a standardized list of currency codes and names, based on the ISO 4217 standard, representing the various currencies used globally for economic and financial transactions.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Accounting Entry
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist identifies whether a transaction represents a use of resources, a source of funds, an asset, a liability, or a change in equity.

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all relevant components or categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Not allocated/unspecified
str:Code.com:Description: Not specified or detailed.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Not applicable or relevant in the given context.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets
str:Code.com:Description: Assets is store of value, over which ownership rights are enforced and from which their owners may derive economic benefits by holding them over a period of time (2016 MFSMCG glossary).

str:Code.com:Name: Assets, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Assets, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Assets, Claims on, Credit to
str:Code.com:Description: Assets, Claims on, Credit to

str:Code.com:Name: Assets, Claims on, Other claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Assets, Claims on, Other claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Gross sales of assets (decrease)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of asset sales, representing a decrease in asset holdings.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross assets refer to the total value of all assets owned or held by an individual, organization, or entity before accounting for any deductions, such as depreciation or liabilities

str:Code.com:Name: Gross acquisition of assets (increase)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of assets acquired, representing an increase in asset holdings.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets (net)
str:Code.com:Description: Assets is store of value, over which ownership rights are enforced and from which their owners may derive economic benefits by holding them over a period of time (2016 MFSMCG glossary).

str:Code.com:Name: Assets - short position
str:Code.com:Description: Store of value representing a benefit or series of benefits accruing to the economic owner by holding or using the entity over a period of time. It is a means of carrying forward value from one accounting period to another.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets (short or negative positions)
str:Code.com:Description: Assets that are either shorted or have negative positions due to borrowing or other financial obligations.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance (credits minus debits)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the total credits and debits in an account, representing net credit position.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit (Resources)
str:Code.com:Description: Credits related to resource inflows, including financial and non-financial resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit (Environment + Economy)
str:Code.com:Description: Credits accounted for within the context of both environmental and economic transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit/Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Credit entries relate to exports of goods and services, earned income receivable, transfer income receivable and disposals of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit).

str:Code.com:Name: Contingent liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities that may occur depending on the outcome of a future event, as defined in the European System of Accounts (ESA) and Government Finance Statistics (GFS).

str:Code.com:Name: Claims
str:Code.com:Description: Financial claims on other entities

str:Code.com:Name: Credit to
str:Code.com:Description: Credit to

str:Code.com:Name: Credit (excluding exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Credits that exclude any exceptional financing measures such as bailouts or special financial assistance.

str:Code.com:Name: Debit (Uses)
str:Code.com:Description: Debit entries relate to imports of goods and services, earned income payable, transfer income payable and acquisitions of non-produced non-financial assets (the financial account in the external accounts uses the terms net acquisition of financial assets and net incurrence of liabilities rather than credit and debit).

str:Code.com:Name: Debit (Environment + Economy)
str:Code.com:Description: Debits accounted for within the context of both environmental and economic transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Domestic (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to domestic accounts to reflect accurate financial positions.

str:Code.com:Name: Debit/Expenditure
str:Code.com:Description: Outflows or payments made, representing expenditures.

str:Code.com:Name: End use
str:Code.com:Description: The final consumption or utilization of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: End use (Environment + Economy)
str:Code.com:Description: The final consumption or utilization of goods and services within the context of both environmental and economic considerations.

str:Code.com:Name: End use, of which emissions relevant
str:Code.com:Description: End use of goods and services where emissions are a significant factor.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (domestic)
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Transformation use
str:Code.com:Description: Use of resources or funds for transformation activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Transformation use (Environment + Economy)
str:Code.com:Description: Use of resources or funds for transformation activities within the context of environmental and economic considerations.

str:Code.com:Name: Economy to environment
str:Code.com:Description: Financial flows or impacts from economic activities to environmental resources or impacts.

str:Code.com:Name: Environment to economy
str:Code.com:Description: Financial flows or impacts from environmental resources or activities to the economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Assets that are in the form of cash, stocks, bonds, or other financial instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to the value or reporting of financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets (domestic)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assets held within the domestic economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to foreign accounts to reflect accurate financial positions.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial assets (foreign)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial assets held in foreign entities or markets.

str:Code.com:Name: Inflows (reserves template)
str:Code.com:Description: Incoming funds or resources within the context of a reserves template.

str:Code.com:Name: Net flows (reserves template)
str:Code.com:Description: Net inflows or outflows within the context of a reserves template.

str:Code.com:Name: Outflows (reserves template)
str:Code.com:Description: Outgoing funds or resources within the context of a reserves template.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross
str:Code.com:Description: The total value before any deductions.

str:Code.com:Name: Net income on inward FDI
str:Code.com:Description: The net income generated from inward foreign direct investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Inflows
str:Code.com:Description: The incoming funds or resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Net income on outward FDI
str:Code.com:Description: The net income generated from outward foreign direct investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (adjustment)
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross decrease in liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total reduction in liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (foreign)
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (gross)
str:Code.com:Description: Gross liabilities refer to the total value of all obligations or debts owed by an individual, organization, or entity before accounting for any adjustments or offsets

str:Code.com:Name: Gross incurrence of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of liabilities incurred before any deductions.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities to, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities to, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities: Unofficial
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.. Liabilities that are not necessarily recognized by official records or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities (excluding exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (assets minus liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value obtained by subtracting total liabilities from total assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net acquisition of financial assets other than cash
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of acquiring financial assets excluding cash.

str:Code.com:Name: Net Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Financial claims on other entities less liabilities to the same entity

str:Code.com:Name: Net Claims on, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Net Claims on, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Net Claims on, Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Net Claims on, Liabilities to

str:Code.com:Name: Net Credit to
str:Code.com:Description: Net Credit to

str:Code.com:Name: Net Credit to, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Net Credit to, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Net Credit, Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Net Credit, Liabilities to

str:Code.com:Name: Net Liabilities (liabilities minus assets)
str:Code.com:Description: The net value obtained by subtracting total assets from total liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Negative Credit/Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: A situation where credits or revenues are negative, indicating a deficit or loss.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (acquisitions less disposals)
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of total acquisitions minus total disposals.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (assets less liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: Net refers to the value of assets less the value of liabilities

str:Code.com:Name: Net (credits less debits)
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of total credits minus total debits.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (liabilities less assets)
str:Code.com:Description: Net refers to the value of liabilities less the value of assets

str:Code.com:Name: Net (receivables less payables)
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of total receivables minus total payables.

str:Code.com:Name: Net foreign assets
str:Code.com:Description: Net foreign assets

str:Code.com:Name: Net Foreign Assets, Claims on
str:Code.com:Description: Net Foreign Assets, Claims on

str:Code.com:Name: Net Foreign Assets, Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Net Foreign Assets, Liabilities to

str:Code.com:Name: Balance on goods, services, and income
str:Code.com:Description: The net balance of trade in goods, services, and income flows between countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Net FDI inward
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of foreign direct investment inflows.

str:Code.com:Name: Net FDI outward
str:Code.com:Description: The net result of foreign direct investment outflows.

str:Code.com:Name: Other Liabilities to
str:Code.com:Description: Other Liabilities to

str:Code.com:Name: Provided by
str:Code.com:Description: Resources or funds provided by an entity.

str:Code.com:Name: Private liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations.. Liabilities with private entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Provided to
str:Code.com:Description: Resources or funds provided to an entity.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities: Official
str:Code.com:Description: Obligations where one unit (the debtor) is obliged, under specific circumstances, to provide funds or other resources to another unit (the creditor). These include shares and other equity in corporations. Liabilities that are not necessarily recognized by official records or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Net (net acquisition of financial assets less net incurrence of liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between net acquisition of financial assets and the net incurrence of liabilities in an accounting period.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Civil (or Marital) Status
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist identifies the legal status of an individual regarding marriage, such as single, married, widowed, or divorced.

str:Code.com:Name: شخص أعزب
Холостой/незамужняя
Pessoa solteira
独身者
Single person
Personne célibataire
Persona soltera
单身人士
str:Code.com:Description: يشمل هذا الرمز الأشخاص الذين لم يسبق لهم الزواج أو الدخول في شراكة مسجلة.
Этот код включает лиц, которые никогда не состояли в браке или зарегистрированном партнерстве.
Este código inclui pessoas que nunca foram casadas ou em união de facto registrada.
このコードには、結婚または登録パートナーシップをしたことがない人が含まれます。
This code includes persons who have never been married or in registered partnership.
Ce code inclut les personnes qui n'ont jamais été mariées ou en partenariat enregistré.
Este código incluye a las personas que nunca han estado casadas o en una pareja de hecho registrada.
此代码包括从未结婚或登记伴侣关系的人。

str:Code.com:Name: شخص متزوج
Женатый/замужняя
Pessoa casada
既婚者
Married person
Personne mariée
Persona casada
已婚人士
str:Code.com:Description: يشمل هذا الرمز الأشخاص الذين يعيشون في زواج من نفس الجنس وكذلك في زواج من جنسين مختلفين. يستثني هذا الرمز الأشخاص المنفصلين قانونًا.
Этот код охватывает как лиц, живущих в однополом браке, так и в разнополом браке. Этот код исключает лиц, находящихся в законном разводе.

Este código abrange tanto as pessoas que vivem em casamento entre pessoas do mesmo sexo como as que vivem em casamento entre pessoas de sexo diferente. Este código exclui as pessoas legalmente separadas.
このコードは、同性婚と異性婚の両方の当事者を対象としています。このコードは、法的に別居している人を除外します。
This code covers both persons living in a same-sex marriage as well as in an opposite-sex marriage. This code excludes legally separated persons.
Ce code couvre les personnes vivant dans un mariage homosexuel ainsi que dans un mariage hétérosexuel. Ce code exclut les personnes légalement séparées.

Este código cubre tanto a las personas que viven en un matrimonio entre personas del mismo sexo como a las que viven en un matrimonio entre personas de distinto sexo. Este código excluye a las personas legalmente separadas.
此代码涵盖同性婚姻和异性婚姻中的双方。此代码不包括合法分居的人。

str:Code.com:Name: شخص أرمل
Вдова/вдовец
Pessoa viúva
配偶者と死別した人
Widowed person
Personne veuve
Persona viuda
丧偶人士
str:Code.com:Description: يشمل هذا الرمز الأشخاص الذين انتهى زواجهم بوفاة الشريك ولم يتزوجوا مرة أخرى أو في شراكة مسجلة.
Этот код включает лиц, чей брак был расторгнут смертью партнера и которые не вступили в повторный брак или зарегистрированное партнерство.
Este código inclui pessoas cujo casamento terminou com a morte do parceiro e que não voltaram a casar ou em união de facto registrada.
このコードには、配偶者の死亡により婚姻が終了し、再婚または登録パートナーシップをしていない人が含まれます。
This code includes persons whose marriage ended with the death of the partner and who are not remarried or in a registered partnership.
Ce code inclut les personnes dont le mariage a pris fin par le décès du partenaire et qui ne sont pas remariées ou en partenariat enregistré.
Este código incluye a las personas cuyo matrimonio terminó con la muerte de la pareja y que no se han vuelto a casar ni están en una pareja de hecho registrada.
此代码包括因伴侣去世而婚姻终止且未再婚或登记伴侣关系的人。

str:Code.com:Name: شخص مطلق
Разведенный/разведенная
Pessoa divorciada
離婚者
Divorced person
Personne divorcée
Persona divorciada
离婚人士
str:Code.com:Description: يشمل هذا الرمز الأشخاص الذين انتهى زواجهم بإجراءات الطلاق ولم يتزوجوا مرة أخرى أو في شراكة مسجلة.
Этот код включает лиц, чей брак был расторгнут в результате процедуры развода и которые не вступили в повторный брак или зарегистрированное партнерство.
Este código inclui pessoas cujo casamento terminou com um processo de divórcio e que não voltaram a casar ou em união de facto registrada.
このコードには、離婚手続きにより婚姻が終了し、再婚または登録パートナーシップをしていない人が含まれます。
This code includes persons whose marriage ended with a divorce procedure and who are not remarried or in a registered partnership.
Ce code inclut les personnes dont le mariage a pris fin par une procédure de divorce et qui ne sont pas remariées ou en partenariat enregistré.
Este código incluye a las personas cuyo matrimonio terminó con un procedimiento de divorcio y que no se han vuelto a casar ni están en una pareja de hecho registrada.
此代码包括因离婚程序而婚姻终止且未再婚或登记伴侣关系的人。

str:Code.com:Name: شخص منفصل قانونيًا
Лицо, состоящее в законном раздельном проживании
Pessoa separada legalmente
法的に別居している人
Legally separated person
Personne séparée légalement
Persona separada legalmente
依法分居人士
str:Code.com:Description: يشمل هذا الرمز الأشخاص المنفصلين: فهم ما زالوا متزوجين، لكنهم منفصلون "رسميًا".
Этот код включает лиц, которые находятся в разводе: они все еще состоят в браке, но "официально" разлучены.
Este código inclui pessoas que estão separadas: ainda estão casadas, mas "oficialmente" separadas.
このコードには、別居している人が含まれます。彼らはまだ結婚していますが、「公式に」別居しています。
This code includes persons who are separated: they are still married, but "officially" separated.
Ce code inclut les personnes séparées : elles sont toujours mariées, mais séparées « officiellement ».
Este código incluye a las personas que están separadas: todavía están casadas, pero "oficialmente" separadas.
此代码包括分居的人：他们仍然结婚，但“正式”分居。

str:Code.com:Name: شخص في شراكة مسجلة
Лицо, состоящее в зарегистрированном партнерстве
Pessoa em união de facto/registada
登録されたパートナーシップの関係にある人
Person in registered partnership
Personne en partenariat enregistré
Persona en unión registrada
在册伴侣关系人士
str:Code.com:Description: يشمل هذا الرمز الأشخاص الذين يعيشون في شراكة مسجلة من جنسين مختلفين وكذلك في شراكة مسجلة من نفس الجنس. تشمل الشراكات المسجلة أيضًا الزيجات العرفية التي تعتبر قانونية وملزمة بموجب القانون الوطني. يستثني هذا الرمز الأشخاص الذين تم حل شراكتهم المسجلة بشكل قانوني.

Этот код охватывает как лиц, живущих в зарегистрированном партнерстве с лицом противоположного пола, так и в зарегистрированном партнерстве с лицом того же пола. Зарегистрированные партнерства также включают традиционные союзы, которые являются законными и обязательными в соответствии с национальным законодательством. Этот код исключает лиц, чье зарегистрированное партнерство было юридически расторгнуто.

Este código abrange tanto as pessoas que vivem em união de facto registrada entre pessoas de sexo diferente como as que vivem em união de facto registrada entre pessoas do mesmo sexo. As uniões de facto registradas também incluem as uniões consuetudinárias que são legais e vinculativas ao abrigo da lei nacional. Este código exclui as pessoas cuja união de facto registrada foi legalmente dissolvida.
このコードは、異性登録パートナーシップと、同性登録パートナーシップの両方の当事者を対象としています。登録パートナーシップには、国内法で合法かつ拘束力のある慣習的な組合も含まれます。このコードは、登録パートナーシップが法的に解消された人を除外します。

This code covers both persons living in an opposite-sex registered partnership as well as in a same-sex registered partnership. Registered partnerships also include customary unions that are legal and binding under national law. This code excludes persons whose registered partnership was legally dissolved.

Ce code couvre les personnes vivant dans un partenariat enregistré hétérosexuel ainsi que dans un partenariat enregistré homosexuel. Les partenariats enregistrés comprennent également les unions coutumières qui sont légales et contraignantes en vertu du droit national. Ce code exclut les personnes dont le partenariat enregistré a été légalement dissous.

Este código cubre tanto a las personas que viven en una pareja de hecho registrada entre personas de distinto sexo como a las que viven en una pareja de hecho registrada entre personas del mismo sexo. Las parejas de hecho registradas también incluyen las uniones consuetudinarias que son legales y vinculantes en virtud del derecho nacional. Este código excluye a las personas cuya pareja de hecho registrada se disolvió legalmente.
此代码涵盖异性登记伴侣关系和同性登记伴侣关系中的双方。登记伴侣关系还包括根据国家法律具有法律约束力的习惯结合。此代码不包括登记伴侣关系已合法解除的人。

str:Code.com:Name: شخص انتهت شراكته المسجلة بوفاة الشريك
Лицо, зарегистрированное партнерство которого прекратилось в связи со смертью партнера
Pessoa cuja união de facto/registada terminou com a morte do parceiro/a
登録されたパートナーシップがパートナーの死によって終了した人
Person whose registered partnership ended with the death of the partner
Personne dont le partenariat enregistré a pris fin par le décès du partenaire
Persona cuya unión registrada terminó con la muerte del compañero/a
登记伴侣关系因伴侣死亡而终止的人士
str:Code.com:Description: يشمل هذا الرمز الأشخاص الذين انتهت شراكتهم المسجلة بوفاة الشريك ولم يتزوجوا مرة أخرى أو في شراكة مسجلة.
Этот код включает лиц, чье зарегистрированное партнерство было расторгнуто смертью партнера и которые не вступили в повторный брак или зарегистрированное партнерство.
Este código inclui pessoas cuja união de facto registrada terminou com a morte do parceiro e que não voltaram a casar ou em união de facto registrada.
このコードには、パートナーの死亡により登録パートナーシップが終了し、再婚または登録パートナーシップをしていない人が含まれます。
This code includes persons whose registered partnership ended with the death of the partner and who are not remarried or in a registered partnership.
Ce code inclut les personnes dont le partenariat enregistré a pris fin par le décès du partenaire et qui ne sont pas remariées ou en partenariat enregistré.
Este código incluye a las personas cuya pareja de hecho registrada terminó con la muerte de la pareja y que no se han vuelto a casar ni están en una pareja de hecho registrada.
此代码包括因伴侣去世而登记伴侣关系终止且未再婚或登记伴侣关系的人。

str:Code.com:Name: شخص تم حل شراكته المسجلة قانونيًا
Лицо, зарегистрированное партнерство которого было расторгнуто в судебном порядке
Pessoa cuja união de facto/registada foi legalmente dissolvida
登録されたパートナーシップが法的に解消された人
Person whose registered partnership was legally dissolved
Personne dont le partenariat enregistré a été légalement dissous
Persona cuya unión registrada fue legalmente disuelta
登记伴侣关系依法解除的人士
str:Code.com:Description: يشمل هذا الرمز الأشخاص الذين انتهت شراكتهم المسجلة بإجراء قانوني ولم يتزوجوا مرة أخرى أو في شراكة مسجلة.
Этот код включает лиц, чье зарегистрированное партнерство было расторгнуто в результате судебного разбирательства и которые не вступили в повторный брак или зарегистрированное партнерство.
Este código inclui pessoas cuja união de facto registrada terminou com um processo legal e que não voltaram a casar ou em união de facto registrada.
このコードには、法的手続きにより登録パートナーシップが終了し、再婚または登録パートナーシップをしていない人が含まれます。
This code includes persons whose registered partnership ended with a legal procedure and who are not remarried or in a registered partnership.
Ce code inclut les personnes dont le partenariat enregistré a pris fin par une procédure légale et qui ne sont pas remariées ou en partenariat enregistré.
Este código incluye a las personas cuya pareja de hecho registrada terminó con un procedimiento legal y que no se han vuelto a casar ni están en una pareja de hecho registrada.
此代码包括因法律程序而登记伴侣关系终止且未再婚或登记伴侣关系的人。

str:Codelist.com:Name: COICOP 2018
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents an updated and expanded version of the COICOP classification system, designed to better reflect the evolving consumption patterns of the 21st century. It includes 14 divisions, 50 groups, 153 classes, and 449 subclasses.

str:Code.com:Name: Food and non-alcoholic beverages
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures on food items intended for human consumption, excluding alcoholic beverages.

str:Code.com:Name: Alcoholic beverages, tobacco and narcotics
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures on alcoholic beverages, tobacco products, and narcotic substances.

str:Code.com:Name: Clothing and footwear
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures on garments, footwear, and accessories for personal use.

str:Code.com:Name: Housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses related to housing, including rent, mortgage payments, utilities (water, electricity, gas), and fuels for heating.

str:Code.com:Name: Furnishings, household equipment and routine household maintenance
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures on furniture, appliances, household tools, and routine maintenance and repair services for the home.

str:Code.com:Name: Health
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses related to medical and paramedical services, therapeutic appliances, and pharmaceutical products.

str:Code.com:Name: Transport
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures on transportation services, including public transport fares, vehicle purchases, and fuel costs.

str:Code.com:Name: Information and communication
str:Code.com:Description: Spending on communication devices, internet access, postal services, and other information and communication technologies.

str:Code.com:Name: Recreation, sport and culture
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses on recreational activities, sports events, cultural events, hobbies, and leisure equipment.

str:Code.com:Name: Education services
str:Code.com:Description: Payments for educational services at all levels, including tuition fees.

str:Code.com:Name: Restaurants and accomodation services
str:Code.com:Description: Spending on meals and drinks at restaurants, cafes, and other food service establishments, as well as accommodation.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance and financial services
str:Code.com:Description: Expenses related to insurance premiums and financial services, such as bank charges and investment fees.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal care, social protection and miscellaneous goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: A wide range of expenditures, including personal care products, social services, and other miscellaneous items.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of non-profit institutions serving households (NPISHS)
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures by non-profit institutions on behalf of households.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of general government
str:Code.com:Description: Expenditures by the government on goods and services for individual consumption.

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The total sum of all individual consumption expenditures across all categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that involve the transfer of ownership of fixed assets, the forgiveness of liabilities, or the provision of funds linked to the acquisition or disposal of fixed assets. All institutional sectors except the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary incomes are incomes arising from the distribution of income between institutional units via current transfers. In the context of the external accounts, this account records current transfers between residents and non-residents. All institutional sectors except the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserves, entitlements, and provisions representing liabilities of the insurer, pension fund, or issuer of standardized guarantees, and a corresponding asset of the policyholders or beneficiaries.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserves, entitlements, and provisions representing liabilities of the insurer, pension fund, or issuer of standardized guarantees, and a corresponding asset of the policyholders or beneficiaries. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserves, entitlements, and provisions representing liabilities of the insurer, pension fund, or issuer of standardized guarantees, and a corresponding asset of the policyholders or beneficiaries. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that involve the transfer of ownership of fixed assets, the forgiveness of liabilities, or the provision of funds linked to the acquisition or disposal of fixed assets. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers: debt forgiveness, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers: debt forgiveness All institutional sectors except the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers other than debt forgiveness, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers related to the exchange of financial or physical assets, excluding those categorized as forgiveness of debt. All institutional sectors except the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current transfers excluding personal transfers, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Other current transfers excluding personal transfers All institutional sectors except the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Net credit and loans from the IMF (excluding reserve position)
str:Code.com:Description: The net amount of credit and loans received from the International Monetary Fund (IMF), excluding those held in reserve positions.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary incomes are incomes arising from the distribution of income between institutional units via current transfers. In the context of the external accounts, this account records current transfers between residents and non-residents. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes, Monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserves, entitlements, and provisions representing liabilities of the insurer, pension fund, or issuer of standardized guarantees, and a corresponding asset of the policyholders or beneficiaries. Institutions responsible for controlling a country's monetary policy, primarily central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserves, entitlements, and provisions representing liabilities of the insurer, pension fund, or issuer of standardized guarantees, and a corresponding asset of the policyholders or beneficiaries. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous current transfers, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Miscellaneous current transfers All institutional sectors except the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other equity
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Equity holdings that do not fall under the main categories of shares or stocks.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital taxes, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on income, wealth, etc., Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; business; acquisition of goods and services by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; business; acquisition of goods and services by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, Central bank, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Debt instruments, Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Debt instruments, Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)

str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods traded (exported and imported) recorded in a country's balance of payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Debt instruments, Accumulation of arrears on original interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. The build-up of overdue payments on the initially agreed interest or coupon payments, noted separately when related to extraordinary financing measures.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, Central bank, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, Central bank, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. str:Code.com:Name: Goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Goods are physical, produced objects, over which ownership rights can be established and whose ownership can be transferred from one institutional unit to another by engaging in transactions on markets. Services result from a production activity that changes the conditions of the consuming units, or facilitates the exchange of products or financial assets. Services are not generally separate items over which ownership rights can be established and cannot generally be separated from their production.

str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income excluding exceptional financing

str:Code.com:Name: Charges for the use of intellectual property n.i.e.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH

Reserves, entitlements, and provisions representing liabilities of the insurer, pension fund, or issuer of standardized guarantees, and a corresponding asset of the policyholders or beneficiaries. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial intermediation services indirectly measured (FISIM)
str:Code.com:Description: The value of services provided by financial intermediaries, such as banks, measured indirectly through the spread between interest rates on loans and deposits.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Debt instruments, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future, including SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, and other accounts receivable/payable. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Life insurance and annuities entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank, Short-term

str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Debt instruments, Cancellation of arrears on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. The formal elimination of overdue interest or coupon payments, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Debt instruments, Accumulation of arrears on penalty interest (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. The accumulation of overdue payments specifically on penalty interest charges, distinguished when associated with exceptional financing conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary incomes are incomes arising from the distribution of income between institutional units via current transfers. In the context of the external accounts, this account records current transfers between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / net borrowing (-)
str:Code.com:Description: Net lending is the difference between changes in net worth due to saving and capital transfers and net acquisitions of non-financial assets (acquisitions less disposals of non-financial assets, less depreciation). If the amount is negative, it represents net borrowing. In the external accounts, it represents the sum of the balances on the current and capital accounts. This is conceptually equal to the net balance of the financial account. If the balance is positive, it is called net lending, implying that in net terms, the economy supplies funds to the rest of the world. If the balance is negative, it is called net borrowing, implying that in net terms, the economy receives funds from the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: Current international cooperation, General government

Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Debt instruments, Accumulation of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)

str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. The total elimination of all types of overdue payments, distinguished when occurring under unique financing conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, General government, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)
