str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises that are both controlled by the same parent entity. Equity and investment fund shares/units excluding those acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity, Equity investment associated with debt reduction (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Ownership interest in a company or asset. Equity investments made in the context of reducing or restructuring a country's debt, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Total financial assets/liabilities, Investment income on other investment income
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. The total value of financial assets and liabilities. Investment income

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is nonresident, Debt instruments, Investment income on interest and income attributable to policy holders
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is not a resident of the country. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Interest and income attributable to policy holders

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident, Debt instruments, Investment income on interest and income attributable to policy holders
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is a resident of the country. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Interest and income attributable to policy holders

str:Code.com:Name: Current account balance (credit less debit)
str:Code.com:Description: The current account balance is the balance of current transactions (transactions in goods and services, earned income and transfer income) between residents and non-residents. The term current account balance is used in the external accounts and is expressed from the perspective of resident units. The term current external balance is used in the national accounts and is expressed from the perspective of the non-resident units, and therefore with the opposite sign.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial services
str:Code.com:Description: Services provided by the finance sector, including banking, insurance, investment services, and financial advice.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital account balance (credit less debit)
str:Code.com:Description: The capital account balance reflects the differences in capital transfers receivable and payable between residents and nonresidents and the acquisition and disposal of nonproduced, nonfinancial assets between residents and nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown, Debt instruments, Investment income on interest and income attributable to policy holders
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the status of the parent entity's residency is unknown. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Interest and income attributable to policy holders

str:Code.com:Name: Research and development services
str:Code.com:Description: Research and development services

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories, Total financial assets/liabilities, Investment income on other investment income
str:Code.com:Description: All categories of investment including direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives, employee stock options, reserve assets, and other investments. The total value of financial assets and liabilities. Investment income

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity, Equity investment associated with debt reduction (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), General government
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Ownership interest in a company or asset. Equity investments made in the context of reducing or restructuring a country's debt, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, Monetary authorities, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. Institutions responsible for controlling a country's monetary policy, primarily central banks. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Special Drawing Rights (SDRs): allocations, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: SDR allocations are reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and allocated to its member countries to supplement existing reserve assets. Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) are international reserve assets that the IMF allocates to its members as a form of supplementary liquidity. These allocations are based on each member country's IMF quota and are intended to provide additional liquidity and support to the global financial system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Equity and investment fund shares/units: other than reinvestment of earnings
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Equity and investment fund shares/units excluding those acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Total financial assets/liabilities, Investment income on other investment income
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The total value of financial assets and liabilities. Investment income

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investor in direct invesment enterprises, Equity and investment fund shares/units: other than reinvestment of earnings
str:Code.com:Description: An investor that makes direct investment in enterprises, having control or significant influence. Equity and investment fund shares/units excluding those acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Other modes of transport
str:Code.com:Description: Other modes of transport

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, Monetary authorities, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. Institutions responsible for controlling a country's monetary policy, primarily central banks. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity, Equity investment associated with debt reduction (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Ownership interest in a company or asset. Equity investments made in the context of reducing or restructuring a country's debt, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Services
str:Code.com:Description: Services results from a production activity that changes the conditions of the consuming units, or facilitates the exchange of products or financial assets. Services are not generally separate items over which ownership rights can be established and cannot generally be separated from their production.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: o/w exceptional financing (memo item)
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: o/w exceptional financing (memo item)

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions/Disposals (net) of nonproduced nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: The net value of purchases minus disposals of nonproduced assets, such as land and natural resources, during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Construction
str:Code.com:Description: The process of constructing buildings or infrastructure, including both the materials and labor involved.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Total financial assets/liabilities, Investment income on other investment income
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. The total value of financial assets and liabilities. Investment income

str:Code.com:Description: Total remittances and transfers (supplementary item) Non-profit institutions primarily serving households, including charities and educational institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity securities: excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Equity securities that exclude those related to exceptional financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Unlisted shares
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Unlisted shares.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Listed shares
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Listed shares.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods, services and primary income
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of a country's balance of trade in goods and services plus net income from abroad.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants received from IMF subsidy accounts (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial grants received from the International Monetary Fund, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Description: Travel; business; other than acquisition of goods and services by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers

str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. str:Code.com:Name: Air transport; passenger

Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, Other Sectors (BPM6), Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Goods for processing abroad
str:Code.com:Description: Goods sent from one country to another for processing and then returned to the original country, recorded in the balance of payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises, Debt instruments, Investment income on interest and income attributable to policy holders
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises that are both controlled by the same parent entity. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Interest and income attributable to policy holders

str:Code.com:Name: Direct insurance
str:Code.com:Description: Trade balance (exports less imports), oil

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on investment income attributable to shareholders, including income on if, including rie
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Investment income attributable to shareholders, including income on IF, including RIE

str:Code.com:Name: Travel services; goods
str:Code.com:Description: Travel services; goods

str:Code.com:Name: Total current, capital (credit less debit) and financial account balances (assets less liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: Total current, capital (credit less debit) and financial account balances (assets less liabilities)

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; personal; other
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; personal; other than heath-related and education-related

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Debt instruments, Investment income on interest and income attributable to policy holders
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable. Interest and income attributable to policy holders

str:Code.com:Name: Goods acquired under merchanting
str:Code.com:Description: Goods purchased by a resident of one country from a resident of another country and sold to a third country without entering the merchant's country.

str:Code.com:Name: Sea transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Sea transport; other than passenger and freight

str:Code.com:Name: Travel services, Both Business and Personal Travel, Other Services, of which health services
str:Code.com:Description: Travel services, Both Business and Personal Travel, Other Services, of which health services.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises, Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises that are both controlled by the same parent entity. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investor in direct invesment enterprises, Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: An investor that makes direct investment in enterprises, having control or significant influence. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Air transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Air transport services not directly related to carrying passengers or freight, such as aerial surveying, firefighting, and training flights.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Equity and investment fund shares/units: excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Equity and investment fund shares/units that exclude those related to exceptional financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on investment income attributable to shareholders, including income on if, including rie
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Investment income attributable to shareholders, including income on IF, including RIE

str:Code.com:Name: Information services
str:Code.com:Description: Services that provide data, news, research, and analysis to individuals, businesses, and organizations.

str:Code.com:Name: Reinsurance
str:Code.com:Description: Reinsurance

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Computer services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to the use of computers and information technology, including software development, IT consulting, and hardware maintenance.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Net incurrence liabilities (adjusted using Fund records), Central bank, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The net incurrance of liabilities represents the difference between taking on new liabilities less the retirement of liabilities. Country data adjusted using Fund records. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity and investment fund shares, Non financial corporations, households and NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Net incurrence liabilities (adjusted using Fund records), Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The net incurrance of liabilities represents the difference between taking on new liabilities less the retirement of liabilities. Country data adjusted using Fund records. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Credit and loans with the IMF: other than reserves, Central bank, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Credit and loans provided by the IMF that are not related to reserve assets. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity and investment fund shares, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment enterprises in direct investor, Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Enterprises in which direct investment is made by the investor. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable.

str:Code.com:Name: All functional categories, Total financial assets/liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: All categories of investment including direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives, employee stock options, reserve assets, and other investments. The total value of financial assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers to NPISH, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers directed towards Non-Profit Institutions Serving Households. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Investment fund shares/units: reinvestment of earnings
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Shares or units in investment funds acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: SDRs holdings, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: SDR Holdings refer to an interest-bearing reserve asset created by the IMF to supplement members’ reserve assets. It is a purely official asset and can only be held and used by members in the SDR department, the IMF, and certain designated official entities. SDR holdings can be exchanged with other members for freely usable currency. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on income, wealth, etc. of which: payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers (current taxes on income, wealth, etc.), General government
str:Code.com:Description: Income and wealth taxes specifically related to individuals working across borders on a seasonal or short-term basis. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Net incurrence liabilities (adjusted using Fund records), General government, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The net incurrance of liabilities represents the difference between taking on new liabilities less the retirement of liabilities. Country data adjusted using Fund records. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers to NPISH, Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers directed towards Non-Profit Institutions Serving Households, noted separately for accounting clarity. All institutional sectors except the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident, Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is a resident of the country. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is nonresident, Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is not a resident of the country. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown, Debt instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the status of the parent entity's residency is unknown. Debt instruments are financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future. Debt instruments consist of SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, pension and related entitlements, provision for calls under standardized guarantees, and other accounts receivable/payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Equity, Equity investment associated with debt reduction (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Ownership interest in a company or asset. Equity investments made in the context of reducing or restructuring a country's debt, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes, Investment income on income attributable to policy holders (insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes)
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserves, entitlements, and provisions representing liabilities of the insurer, pension fund, or issuer of standardized guarantees, and a corresponding asset of the policyholders or beneficiaries. Investment income attributable to policy holders (insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Other Sectors (BPM6), Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Other Sectors (BPM6), Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserves, entitlements, and provisions representing liabilities of the insurer, pension fund, or issuer of standardized guarantees, and a corresponding asset of the policyholders or beneficiaries. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, Other Sectors (BPM6), Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, Other Sectors (BPM6), Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, Other Sectors (BPM6), Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, Other Sectors (BPM6), Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown, Equity and investment fund shares/units: other than reinvestment of earnings
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the status of the parent entity's residency is unknown. Equity and investment fund shares/units excluding those acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident, Equity and investment fund shares/units: other than reinvestment of earnings
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is a resident of the country. Equity and investment fund shares/units excluding those acquired through the reinvestment of earnings.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Description: An investor that makes direct investment in enterprises, having control or significant influence. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the status of the parent entity's residency is unknown. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is a resident of the country. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment enterprises in direct investor, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Enterprises in which direct investment is made by the investor. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is nonresident, Equity and investment fund shares, Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is not a resident of the country. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds. Total income distributions, including dividends and withdrawals from quasi-corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders, excluding reverse investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposits: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Deposits that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans, Repo Loans (reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents)
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents, specifically those involved in repurchase agreements (repo loans).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt securities: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Debt instruments, Other sectors
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future, including SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, and other accounts receivable/payable. Broad classification for sectors not fitting traditional classifications.

str:Code.com:Name: Loans, Other (reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents)
str:Code.com:Description: Loans that are reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities related to reserve assets owed to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Other sectors
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. Broad classification for sectors not fitting traditional classifications.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, General government, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs): allocations, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. SDR allocations are reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and allocated to its member countries to supplement existing reserve assets. Special Drawing Rights (SDRs) are international reserve assets that the IMF allocates to its members as a form of supplementary liquidity. These allocations are based on each member country's IMF quota and are intended to provide additional liquidity and support to the global financial system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Central bank, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund and allocated to its members to supplement existing reserve assets. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Investment fund shares/units, Money market funds
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Shares or units in a fund that pools investor money to purchase securities. Investment funds that invest in short-term debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, credit, and loans with the IMF (other than reserves), Central bank, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments including loans, credit, and loans with the International Monetary Fund (IMF), excluding those held as reserves. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises that are both controlled by the same parent entity. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Other short-term foreign currency liabilities to nonresidents: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term foreign currency liabilities to nonresidents that are classified as reserve-related liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investor in direct invesment enterprises, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: An investor that makes direct investment in enterprises, having control or significant influence. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve Assets included in SDR basket
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve Assets included in SDR basket

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Investment fund shares/units
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Shares or units in a fund that pools investor money to purchase securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment enterprises in direct investor, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Enterprises in which direct investment is made by the investor. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Debt instruments, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments that require the payment of principal and/or interest at some point(s) in the future, including SDRs, currency and deposits, debt securities, loans, insurance technical reserves, and other accounts receivable/payable. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, credit, and loans with the IMF (other than reserves), General government, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial instruments including loans, credit, and loans with the International Monetary Fund (IMF), excluding those held as reserves. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is unknown, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the status of the parent entity's residency is unknown. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is resident, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is a resident of the country. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment between fellow enterprises if ultimate controlling parent is nonresident, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment between enterprises controlled by the same parent entity, where the parent entity is not a resident of the country. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit and loans from the IMF: reserve-related liabilities (RRL) to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve-related liabilities owed to nonresidents arising from credit and loans provided by the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other debt instruments, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Debt instruments not classified under specific categories like bonds or loans. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions (net): nonproduced assets
str:Code.com:Description: The purchases of nonproduced assets, such as land and natural resources, during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct investment, Primary income
str:Code.com:Description: Direct Investment represents cross-border investment associated with a resident in one economy having control or a significant degree of influence on the management of an enterprise that is resident in another economy. Ownership of 10 percent or more of the voting power is evidence of a direct investment relationship. Primary incomes are incomes earned by institutional units as a consequence of their involvement in processes of production or ownership of assets that may be needed for purposes of production.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial account balance (assets less liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: The financial account balance reflects the difference in transactions in financial assets and liabilities in the accounting period. In the external accounts, only transactions between residents and non-residents are included.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents total
str:Code.com:Description: The aggregate notional value of all foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, inter-company lending
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents arising from inter-company lending.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents: inter-company lending
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents arising from inter-company lending.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, inter-company lending, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents arising from inter-company lending. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, General government
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents: inter-company lending, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents arising from inter-company lending. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents total, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: The aggregate notional value of all foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Financial claims arising from the direct extension of credit by suppliers to buyers for the purchase of goods and services, and advances for work in progress.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other accounts receivable
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Accounts that are receivable but not classified under specific categories like trade receivables or loans.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other equity
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Equity holdings that do not fall under the main categories of shares or stocks.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other accounts payable
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Accounts that are payable but not classified under specific categories like trade payables or loans.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), Not sectorised
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Reserve assets created by the International Monetary Fund and allocated to its members to supplement existing reserve assets. Refers to entities or data that have not been assigned to any sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other accounts receivable, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Trade credits and advances, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Monetary gold
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Gold held as a reserve asset.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial derivatives (other than reserves) and employee stock options, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Negotiable financial instruments and employee stock options that are linked to other specific financial instruments, indicators, or commodities. Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial derivatives excluding those held as reserves and employee stock options. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF, Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs. Central bank

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Other accounts payable, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Accounts that are payable but not classified under specific categories like trade payables or loans. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Currency and deposits, Monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions. Institutions responsible for controlling a country's monetary policy, primarily central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Currency and deposits
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing) The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Financial derivatives
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Negotiable financial instruments linked to another specific financial instrument, indicator, or commodity, through which specific risks (e.g., interest rate risk, foreign exchange risk, equity and commodity price risk, credit risk) can be traded in their own right in financial markets. Financial instruments whose value is derived from the performance of underlying assets, indexes, or interest rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment, Debt securities, Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes.
