str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Loans
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Sums of money that are borrowed and expected to be paid back with interest.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Ownership interest in companies (equity) and shares in investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Debt securities, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Credit and loans with the IMF: other than reserves, General government
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Debt securities, Long-term
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The total sum of all overdue payments including principal, interest, and penalty interest, specifically highlighted when part of unique financing solutions. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Non financial corporations, households and NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The total sum of all overdue payments including principal, interest, and penalty interest, specifically highlighted when part of unique financing solutions. Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Credit and loans with the IMF: other than reserves, Monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. Credit and loans provided by the IMF that are not related to reserve assets. Institutions responsible for controlling a country's monetary policy, primarily central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on penalty interest (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The accumulation of overdue payments specifically on penalty interest charges, distinguished when associated with exceptional financing conditions. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The total amount of principal payments that have fallen due but remain unpaid, separately recorded in the context of unusual financial arrangements. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on original interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment represents a residual investment category that includes positions and transactions other than those included in direct investment, portfolio investment, financial derivatives and employee stock options, and reserve assets. The build-up of overdue payments on the initially agreed interest or coupon payments, noted separately when related to extraordinary financing measures. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Accumulation of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), Non financial corporations, households and NPISH

Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Tradable financial instruments representing a debt, such as bonds and notes. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in more than one year, or has no stated maturity (referred to as long-term maturity)

str:Code.com:Name: Portfolio investment
str:Code.com:Description: Portfolio investment reflects cross-border transactions and positions involving debt or equity securities, other than those included in direct investment or reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment, Debt instruments other than insurance, pension, and standardised schemes, Investment income, interest income

Debt instruments other than insurance, pension, and standardised schemes. The cost of borrowing money, expressed as a percentage of the amount borrowed, paid by the borrower to the lender for the use of the lender's money.

str:Code.com:Name: Current and capital account balance (credit less debit)

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Monetary gold: gold bullion
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Gold in the form of bars or ingots held as a reserve asset.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Unallocated gold accounts
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Unallocated gold accounts.

Central bank

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Special Drawing Rights (SDRs)

str:Code.com:Description: The aggregate notional value of all foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, General government
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Central, state, and local governments, including their agencies and departments.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other sectors: Other
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign currency derivatives by sector notional value of contracts with nonresidents, Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: The total notional value of foreign currency derivative contracts with nonresidents, categorized by sector. Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Currency and deposits, Claims on monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions, claims on monetary authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Currency and deposits, Claims on other entities
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Monetary assets held in the form of currency or in deposit accounts with financial institutions, claims on other entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserve assets, Reserve position in the IMF
str:Code.com:Description: External assets that are readily available to and controlled by monetary authorities for meeting balance of payments financing needs, for intervention in exchange markets to affect the currency exchange rate, and for other related purposes (such as maintaining confidence in the currency and the economy, and serving as a basis for foreign borrowing). Reserve assets must be denominated and settled in foreign currency. Reserve position in the IMF is the sum of a member’s reserve tranche position and any indebtedness of the IMF in the General Resources Account (GRA) that is readily available to the member to meet balance of payments financing needs.

str:Code.com:Name: Total liabilities, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of all financial obligations owed by an entity to other entities. These obligations represent claims held by others against the entity and may include loans, debt securities, accounts payable, and other forms of debt. This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Total assets, Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: Total assets This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Other Sectors (BPM6), Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt liabilities to nonresidents, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities owed to nonresidents in the form of debt. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Other Sectors (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Other Sectors (BPM6), Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Sectors excluding deposit-taking corporations and general government. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt claims on nonresidents, Short-term
str:Code.com:Description: Claims for debt owed by nonresidents. Original contract stipulates that payment is due in one year or less or payable on demand (referred to as short-term maturity).

str:Code.com:Name: Reseve Assets, Not in SDR basket
str:Code.com:Description: Reserve Assets, not included in the basket of currencies used to value Special Drawing Rights (SDRs).

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all relevant components or categories.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Statistical Measures
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist describe characteristics of a group of data. They summarize the distribution, central tendency, or spread of multiple values.

str:Code.com:Name: Maximum value
str:Code.com:Description: In a dataset or set of numbers, the maximum value refers to the largest or highest value present.

str:Code.com:Name: Mean
str:Code.com:Description: The mean is a measure of central tendency, calculated by summing all values in a dataset and dividing by the number of values.

str:Code.com:Name: Minimum value
str:Code.com:Description: The minimum value is the smallest or lowest value within a dataset. It represents the lower limit of the data range.

str:Code.com:Name: Standard deviation
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical measure that quantifies the amount of variation or dispersion of a set of values from their average (mean).

str:Code.com:Name: Individual forecast
str:Code.com:Description: An estimated future value or outcome of a random variable for a specific individual observation expressed as a conditional expectation or probability.

str:Code.com:Name: Median
str:Code.com:Description: The median is the middle value in a data set when the values are arranged in ascending or descending order.

str:Code.com:Name: Lag 1 (Opening)
str:Code.com:Description: A value for a variable that refers to a one-period delay of that variable relative to its current value.

str:Code.com:Name: 60-day moving average
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical measure that smooths time-series data by calculating the average of a variable over the most recent 60 days, updating the average each day.

str:Code.com:Name: 60-month moving average
str:Code.com:Description: An average of a variable over the most recent 60 months, updating the average each month to track long-term trends.

str:Code.com:Name: Rate
str:Code.com:Description: A measure that expresses the frequency of occurrence of an event or phenomenon in relation to another quantity.

str:Code.com:Name: Rate, 60-day moving average
str:Code.com:Description: The average of rates (e.g., stock prices, sales figures) over the past 60 days, updating the rate each day.

str:Code.com:Name: Rate, 60-month moving average
str:Code.com:Description: The average of rates (e.g., stock prices, sales figures) over the past 60 months, updating the rate each month.

str:Code.com:Name: 95 percent interval - lower bound
str:Code.com:Description: The lower bound of a 95 percent confidence interval, representing the lower limit within which a parameter is likely to fall.

str:Code.com:Name: 95 percent interval - upper bound
str:Code.com:Description: The upper bound of a 95 percent confidence interval, representing the upper limit within which a parameter is likely to fall.

str:Code.com:Name: Z-Score
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical measure that determines how many standard deviations a value is from the mean of a dataset

str:Code.com:Name: Sustainability Logit Metric
str:Code.com:Description: A metric that uses a logistic transformation to assess the sustainability of a particular system or behavior.

str:Code.com:Name: Null value
str:Code.com:Description: A variable that has no assigned value (either no data is available, not applicable or is unknown for a given field)

str:Code.com:Name: Aggregate Z-score
str:Code.com:Description: A combined Z-score, aggregating multiple Z-scores to evaluate overall performance or comparison.

str:Code.com:Name: Sustainability Logit Z-score
str:Code.com:Description: A Z-score derived from the Sustainability Logit Metric to assess sustainability on a comparable, unit-free scale.

str:Code.com:Name: Scaling factor
str:Code.com:Description: A constant number used to multiply or divide values in order to change their scale, magnitude, or units while preserving their relative relationships.

str:Code.com:Name: Signal
str:Code.com:Description: An early indicator of change or risk that refers to the magnitude of an effect

str:Code.com:Name: Rank
str:Code.com:Description: Ranking determines the position of a value within a set of values when they're arranged in order (either largest to smallest or smallest to largest).

str:Code.com:Name: Scores
str:Code.com:Description: Scores provided to a categorial variable indicates ranking relative to a benchmark or a distribution.

Еврозона
ユーロ圏
Euro Area (EA) – aggregate of member states
Zone euro
Zona Euro
欧元区
str:Code.com:Description: Economies that are members of the Euro Area – WEO aggregation.

str:Code.com:Name: الاتحاد الأوروبي
Европейский Союз (ЕС)
欧州連合（EU）
European Union (EU)
Union européenne (UE)
Unión Europea (UE)
欧洲联盟 (欧盟)
str:Code.com:Description: Economies that are members of the European Union. Umbrella term referring to a three "pillar" construction encompassing the European Community (EC), Common Foreign and Security Policy, and Justice and Home Affairs.

str:Code.com:Name: الدول الهشة والمتأثرة بالنزاعات
Нестабильные и затронутые конфликтом государства
脆弱で紛争の影響を受けている国家
Fragile and Conflict-affected States (FCS)
États fragiles et touchés par un conflit
Estados Frágiles y Afectados por Conflictos
脆弱和受冲突影响的国家
str:Code.com:Description: This is a country grouping established by the World Bank's classification of countries in fragile and conflict-affected situations. The list consists of (i) countries with high levels of institutional and social fragility and (ii) countries affected by violent conflict, using indicators and thresholds assessed by the World Bank. It is updated annually and available publicly. Under the new IMF strategy for Fragile and Conflicted States (FCS), the Fund has adopted the methodology, thresholds, and criteria of the World Bank's Fragility and Conflict Situations for the IMF Fragile and Conflicted States. This is explained in the FCS Strategy Board paper: The IMF Strategy for Fragile and Conflict-Affected States (March 14, 2022, p. 21-22).

str:Code.com:Name: مجموعة العشرين
Большая двадцатка
G20
G20
G20
G20
二十国集团
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of the G20 international forum, which consists of 19 individual countries and the European Union.

str:Code.com:Name: مجموعة السبع
Большая семерка
G7
G7
G7
G7
七国集团
str:Code.com:Description: Economies that are members of the G7 (Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States).

str:Code.com:Name: مجلس التعاون الخليجي
Совет сотрудничества стран Персидского залива (ССАГПЗ)
湾岸協力会議
Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC)
Conseil de coopération du Golfe (CCG)
Consejo de Cooperación del Golfo (CCG)
海湾合作委员会
str:Code.com:Description: Member countries of the regional intergovernmental political and economic alliance the Gulf Cooperation Council, which consists of all the Arab states of the Persian Gulf, except Iraq.

str:Code.com:Name: البلدان الفقيرة المثقلة بالديون
Бедные страны с высоким уровнем задолженности
重債務貧困国
Heavily Indebted Poor Countries (HIPC)
Pays pauvres très endettés (PPTE)
Países Pobres Muy Endeudados (PPME)
重债穷国
str:Code.com:Description: Heavily Indebted Poor Countries as named by IMF/WB. Includes AFR Heavily Indebted Poor Countries (712).

str:Code.com:Name: أمريكا اللاتينية ومنطقة البحر الكاريبي
Латинская Америка и Карибский бассейн
ラテンアメリカ・カリブ海地域
Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC)
Amérique latine et Caraïbes
América Latina y el Caribe
拉丁美洲和加勒比
str:Code.com:Description: EMDEs located in the Latin America and Caribbean region, including Mexico. This group includes Caribbean (1218) + Central America (121) + South America (2041) and Mexico.

str:Code.com:Name: البلدان النامية منخفضة الدخل
Страны с низким уровнем дохода
低所得開発途上国
Low-Income Developing Countries (LIDC)
Pays en développement à faible revenu
Países en Desarrollo de Bajos Ingresos
低收入发展中国家
str:Code.com:Description: Countries that have per capita income levels below a certain threshold (currently set at $2,700 in 2016 as measured by the World Bank's Atlas method), structural features consistent with limited development and structural transformation, and insufficiently close external financial linkages to be widely seen as emerging market economies.

str:Code.com:Name: الشرق الأوسط وآسيا الوسطى
Ближний Восток и Центральная Азия
中東・中央アジア
Middle East and Central Asia
Moyen-Orient et Asie centrale
Oriente Medio y Asia Central
中东和中亚
str:Code.com:Description: EMDEs located in the Middle East and North Africa region, Afghanistan, and Pakistan (MENAP) or Caucasus and Central Asia region (CCA).

str:Code.com:Name: الشرق الأوسط وآسيا الوسطى: مُصدرو النفط
Ближний Восток и Центральная Азия: экспортеры нефти
中東・中央アジア：石油輸出国
Middle East and Central Asia: Oil Exporters
Moyen-Orient et Asie centrale : Exportateurs de pétrole
Oriente Medio y Asia Central: Exportadores de petróleo
中东和中亚：石油出口国
str:Code.com:Description: EMDEs in MECA whose main source (>50%) of export earnings is Fuel.

str:Code.com:Name: الشرق الأوسط وآسيا الوسطى: مُستوردو النفط

Middle East and Central Asia: Oil Importers
Moyen-Orient et Asie centrale : Importateurs de pétrole
Oriente Medio y Asia Central: Importadores de petróleo
中东和中亚：石油进口国
str:Code.com:Description: EMDEs in MECA whose main source (>50%) of export earnings is not Fuel.

Ближний Восток и Северная Африка (БВСА)
中東・北アフリカ
Middle East and North Africa (MENA)
Moyen-Orient et Afrique du Nord (MENA)
Oriente Medio y Norte de África (MENA)
中东北非
str:Code.com:Description: EMDEs located in the Middle East and North Africa region.

str:Code.com:Name: أمريكا الشمالية
Северная Америка
北アメリカ
North America
Amérique du Nord
América del Norte
北美洲
str:Code.com:Description: North America refers to Canada, Mexico, Puerto Rico, and the United States.

str:Code.com:Name: الاتحاد الجمركي لجنوب إفريقيا
Южноафриканский таможенный союз
南部アフリカ関税同盟
Southern African Customs Union (SACU)
Union douanière d'Afrique australe (SACU)
Unión Aduanera de África Austral (SACU)
南部非洲关税同盟
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of Southern African Customs Union (consists of Botswana, Eswatini, Lesotho, Namibia, and South Africa).

str:Code.com:Name: أفريقيا جنوب الصحراء الكبرى
Страны Африки к югу от Сахары
サハラ以南アフリカ
Sub-Saharan Africa (SSA)
Afrique subsaharienne
África subsahariana
撒哈拉以南非洲
str:Code.com:Description: EMDEs located in the Sub-Sahara Africa region.

str:Code.com:Name: أفريقيا جنوب الصحراء الكبرى (SSA): الدخل المنخفض
Африка к югу от Сахары (SSA): Низкий доход
サハラ以南アフリカ (SSA)：低所得国
Sub-Saharan Africa (SSA): Low-Income
Afrique subsaharienne (ASS) : Faible revenu
África Subsahariana (SSA): Bajos Ingresos
撒哈拉以南非洲 (SSA)：低收入
str:Code.com:Description: Countries geographically located in Africa, fully or partially south of the Sahara desert with an average GNI per capita income of US$1025 or below for the period 2016-18.

str:Code.com:Name: أفريقيا جنوب الصحراء الكبرى (SSA): الدخل المتوسط
Африка к югу от Сахары (SSA): Средний доход
サハラ以南アフリカ (SSA)：中所得国
Sub-Saharan Africa (SSA): Middle-Income
Afrique subsaharienne (ASS) : Revenu intermédiaire
África Subsahariana (SSA): Ingresos Medios

str:Code.com:Description: Countries geographically located in Africa, fully or partially south of the Sahara desert with an average GNI per capita income of above US$1025 for the period 2016-18.

str:Code.com:Name: الاتحاد الاقتصادي والنقدي لغرب أفريقيا
Западноафриканский экономический и валютный союз
西アフリカ経済通貨同盟
West African Economic and Monetary Union (WAEMU)
Union économique et monétaire ouest-africaine (UEMOA)
Unión Económica y Monetaria de África Occidental (UEMOA)
西非经济货币联盟
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of the West African Economic and Monetary Union (WAEMU). Supersedes the West African Monetary Union.

str:Code.com:Name: العالم
Мир
世界
World
Monde
Mundo
世界
str:Code.com:Description: All economies covered by the dataset. Consult the dataset documentation for the specific composition of economies at each point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Euro Area (EA)
str:Code.com:Description: Economies that are members of the Euro Area. https://ec.europa.eu/info/business-economy-euro/euro-area/what-euro-area_en

str:Code.com:Name: ASEAN
str:Code.com:Description: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations is a political and economic union of 10 states in Southeast Asia. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Member_states_of_ASEAN)

str:Code.com:Name: Southern African Development Community (SADC)
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of the Southern African Development Community (SADC): https://www.sadc.int/

str:Code.com:Name: Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA)
str:Code.com:Description: Member states of the largest regional economic organization in Africa, the Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa (COMESA), which includes a free trade area and a customs union (launched in 2009): https://www.comesa.int/members/

str:Code.com:Name: Benelux
str:Code.com:Description: Grouping of Belgium, the Netherlands, and Luxembourg. The term "Benelux," formed from the first two letters of each country's name, originally referred to a customs union established in 1948. Today, the term is used as a generic grouping of the three countries. (Eurostat)

str:Code.com:Name: South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands

str:Codelist.com:Name: Interest Rate types, terms and conditions
str:Codelist.com:Description: A classification of interest rates based on their type (fixed or floating), terms (maturity), and other conditions (e.g., collateralization, reference rates).

str:Code.com:Name: 3- or 6-month London interbank offered interest rate (LIBOR)
str:Code.com:Description: The average interest rate, determined by a daily survey of major global banks, at which banks in London lend unsecured short-term funds to other banks in the London interbank market. The rate is quoted for various maturities, including 3- and 6-month periods.

str:Code.com:Name: 3- or 6-month London interbank offered interest rate (LIBOR), real
str:Code.com:Description: The 3- or 6-month LIBOR, adjusted for inflation, to reflect constant purchasing power. This is typically calculated by subtracting the expected inflation rate from the nominal LIBOR rate.

str:Code.com:Name: 3-month bond yield (or nearest equivalent)
str:Code.com:Description: The return on investment for a 3-month government bond or a bond of similar maturity.

str:Code.com:Name: 3-month Euro Interbank offered rate (EURIBOR)
str:Code.com:Description: The average unsecured interbank lending rate in the Euro area for a three-month maturity, based on daily bank submissions.

str:Code.com:Name: 3-Month interbank rate
str:Code.com:Description: The average interbank lending rate for a three-month maturity, based on daily bank submissions.

str:Code.com:Name: 3-month London Interbank offered rate (LIBOR)
str:Code.com:Description: The average unsecured interbank lending rate in London for a three-month maturity, based on daily submissions from major global banks.

str:Code.com:Name: 6-month London interbank offered interest rate (LIBOR), real
str:Code.com:Description: The 6-month LIBOR, adjusted for inflation, to reflect constant purchasing power. This is typically calculated by subtracting the expected inflation rate from the nominal LIBOR rate.

str:Code.com:Name: 6-month London interbank offered interest rate (LIBOR), real, Archive
str:Code.com:Description: The archived interest rate for real 6-month LIBOR.

str:Code.com:Name: Average cost of funds
str:Code.com:Description: The average interest rate that financial institutions pay to borrow money.

str:Code.com:Name: Bond yield
str:Code.com:Description: The rate of return implied by a bond’s price, coupon payments, and time to maturity.

str:Code.com:Name: Central bank borrowing facility
str:Code.com:Description: A facility through which financial institutions borrow funds from the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Central bank certificates
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities issued by central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Certificates of deposit
str:Code.com:Description: Time deposits issued by banks with a fixed maturity date and a specified interest rate.

str:Code.com:Name: Corporate bond yield spread
str:Code.com:Description: Yield difference between corporate and government bonds of similar maturity, expressed in standard deviation units.

str:Code.com:Name: Corporate paper rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate on corporate paper.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate paid by banks on funds deposited by account holders. This rate determines how much interest a depositor earns on their savings or deposit accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit rate, overnight
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate paid by banks on deposits that are held for a single night. This rate applies to very short-term deposits, typically from one banking day to the next.

str:Code.com:Name: Discount rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate charged by central banks on loans to commercial banks.

str:Code.com:Name: EMBI Global - stripped spread
str:Code.com:Description: The spread on emerging market bonds, adjusted for risk by stripping out risk-free components. One unit is equivalent to one standard deviation of standardized variable.

str:Code.com:Name: Emerging market stock returns
str:Code.com:Description: The returns on stocks from emerging markets. One unit is equivalent to one standard deviation of standardized variable.

str:Code.com:Name: Euro system deposit facility rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate paid on deposits held at the central bank of the Eurozone by commercial banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Government bond yields, medium term
str:Code.com:Description: The annualized rate of return for government bonds with medium-term maturities (typically 3-10 years).

str:Code.com:Name: Government bond yields
str:Code.com:Description: The annualized rate of return for long-term debt securities issued by a government.

str:Code.com:Name: Government securities: Treasury bills
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term debt securities issued by a government.

str:Code.com:Name: Government securities: Treasury bills, 3-month
str:Code.com:Description: Short-term debt securities issued by a government with a maturity of 3 months.

str:Code.com:Name: Government securities: Treasury bills, bond equivalent
str:Code.com:Description: Treasury bills with yields expressed on a bond-equivalent basis.

str:Code.com:Name: Government securities: Treasury bills, long-term
str:Code.com:Description: Long-term debt securities issued by a government.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New business: Deposits agreed maturity up to 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new deposits with agreed maturities up to 1 year in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New Business: Deposits, households
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new deposit accounts for households in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New Business: Deposits, non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new deposit accounts for non-financial corporations in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New business: Loans consumption floating rate and up to 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new household consumer loans with floating rates or initial rate fixation up to one year in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New business: Loans house purchase, over 5 years
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new loans to households for house purchase in the Euro area with an initial rate fixation period of more than five years.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New business: Loans other than bank overdrafts, over EUR 1 million, over 3 months and up to 1 year
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new loans other than bank overdrafts over EUR 1 million, with initial rate fixation over three months and up to one year, in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New Business: Loans, House Purchase, households
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new loan contracts to households for house purchase in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New Business: Loans, households
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new loan contracts to households in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, New Business: Loans, non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new loan contracts to non-financial corporations in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, Outstanding Amounts: Deposits agreed maturity up to 2 years
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on outstanding deposits with agreed maturities up to 2 years in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates, Outstanding Amounts: Deposits non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on outstanding deposit accounts for non-financial corporations in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Inverted term spread
str:Code.com:Description: A situation in which short-term interest rates exceed long-term interest rates. The indicator is expressed in standardized units, where one unit corresponds to one standard deviation from the historical mean.

str:Code.com:Name: Key repurchase agreement rate
str:Code.com:Description: The main interest rate applied to repurchase agreements in which a central bank purchases securities from financial institutions with a commitment to resell them at a later date.

str:Code.com:Name: Lending rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate charged by banks on loans to borrowers.

str:Code.com:Name: Lending rate: overnight
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate charged by banks on loans that are to be repaid the next day.

str:Code.com:Name: London interbank offered rate
str:Code.com:Description: Reference interest rate, calculated as a trimmed mean of daily submissions from a panel of major global banks, representing the rate at which those banks estimated they could borrow unsecured funds in the London interbank market for various maturities.

str:Code.com:Name: Long-term bond yield
str:Code.com:Description: The return on investment for long-term debt securities, typically with maturities of ten years or more.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary policy-related
str:Code.com:Description: Pertaining to the actions and policies implemented by a central bank to control the money supply and interest rates in an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Money market
str:Code.com:Description: The market for short-term debt securities and financial instruments, typically with maturities of less than one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Money market, maximum
str:Code.com:Description: The highest interest rate offered in the money market for short-term debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Money market, minimum
str:Code.com:Description: The lowest interest rate offered in the money market for short-term debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Monthly stock returns (measured as declines)
str:Code.com:Description: The performance of stocks on a monthly basis, specifically measured by declines in value. One unit is equivalent to one standard deviation of standardized variable.

str:Code.com:Name: Policy-related interest rate
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates set by central banks as part of their monetary policy to influence economic activity.

str:Code.com:Name: Policy-related interest rate, percentage point difference in scenario (data from Modeling unit)
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage point difference in the policy-related interest rate under various economic scenarios, based on data from a modeling unit.

str:Code.com:Name: Real long-term bond yield
str:Code.com:Description: The yield on long-term bonds adjusted for inflation.

str:Code.com:Name: Real short-term deposit rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate on short-term deposits adjusted for inflation.

str:Code.com:Name: Refinancing rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate at which existing debt can be replaced with new debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Repurchase agreement rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate charged on repurchase agreements.

str:Code.com:Name: Reverse repurchase agreement rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate charged on reverse repurchase agreements.

str:Code.com:Name: Risk-free interest rate
str:Code.com:Description: The theoretical rate of return on an investment with no credit risk, typically proxied by the yield on short-term government securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Savings rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate paid by banks on savings accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: SDR interest rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate applicable to Special Drawing Rights (SDRs), an international type of monetary reserve currency created by the International Monetary Fund (IMF).

str:Code.com:Name: Short-term interest rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate on debt securities or financial instruments with maturities of less than one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Three month interest rates
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rates applicable to debt securities or financial instruments with a maturity of three months.

str:Code.com:Name: Treasury bills, 3-months
str:Code.com:Description: The return on investment for short-term U.S. government debt securities with a maturity of three months.

str:Code.com:Name: Treasury-EuroDollar spread (TED) or interbank spread
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the interest rates on short-term U.S. government debt (Treasury bills) and Eurodollar deposits (LIBOR). One unit is equivalent to one standard deviation of standardized variable.

str:Code.com:Name: 6-month London interbank offered rate (LIBOR)
str:Code.com:Description: Historical benchmark interest rate reflecting the estimated cost of unsecured borrowing for a six-month maturity, based on submissions from major banks in the London interbank market.

str:Code.com:Name: Fixed rate
str:Code.com:Description: An interest rate that remains unchanged for the entire term of a loan or investment, regardless of movements in market interest rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Bilateral rates
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates agreed upon directly between two parties, typically in a large financial transaction or between a bank and a large corporation.

str:Code.com:Name: London Eurodollar deposit rate, three-month
str:Code.com:Description: A benchmark interest rate reflecting the rate at which banks are willing to lend U.S. dollar–denominated deposits (Eurodollars) to other banks in the London market for a three-month maturity.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro Area rates, new business: loans, consumer credit and other, households
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new loan contracts to households in the Euro area for consumer credit and other purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Government bond yields, minimum
str:Code.com:Description: The lowest observed yield among government bonds over a specified period or across a defined set of maturities.

str:Code.com:Name: Variable rate
str:Code.com:Description: An interest rate on a loan or security that varies over time in line with a specified benchmark interest rate or index.

str:Code.com:Name: Policy-related interest rate for specific countries
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates set by central banks as part of their monetary policy to influence economic activity for a specific group of countries.

str:Code.com:Name: US Federal Reserve Fund's target rate
str:Code.com:Description: The US Federal Reserve's target rate, also known as the federal funds rate, is the interest rate that commercial banks charge each other for overnight loans of reserves.

str:Code.com:Name: Repurchase agreement rate (Repo rate)
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate applied to repurchase agreements, under which securities are sold with a commitment to repurchase them at a later date.

str:Code.com:Name: 6-month London interbank offered interest rate (LIBOR), real
str:Code.com:Description: The 6-month LIBOR, adjusted for inflation, to reflect constant purchasing power. This is typically calculated by subtracting the expected inflation rate from the nominal LIBOR rate.

str:Code.com:Name: Short-term CD interest rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate applicable to certificates of deposit with short-term maturities, typically up to one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Government bonds yields medium and long term
str:Code.com:Description: The market rates of return on medium- and long-term debt securities issued by a government, expressing the annual income generated by a bond relative to its current market price.

str:Code.com:Name: Government bonds yields short-term
str:Code.com:Description: The market rates of return on short-term debt securities issued by a government, expressing the annual income generated by a bond relative to its current market price.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro Area Rates, New Business, Loans, Non-financial Corporations, Other Than Bank Overdrafts, Over EUR 1 Million, Floating Rate and up to 1 Year
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on new loan contracts to non-financial corporations in the Euro area, excluding bank overdrafts, with loan amounts over EUR 1 million and with floating rates or an initial rate fixation period of up to one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro Area Rates, Outstanding Amounts, Deposits, Non-Financial Corporations, Repos
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on outstanding deposit positions of non-financial corporations in the Euro area arising from repurchase agreements (repos).

str:Code.com:Name: 1-month London interbank offered rate (LIBOR)
str:Code.com:Description: Historical benchmark interest rate reflecting the estimated cost of unsecured borrowing for a one-month maturity, based on submissions from major banks in the London interbank market.

str:Code.com:Name: 1-year London interbank offered rate (LIBOR)
str:Code.com:Description: Historical benchmark interest rate reflecting the estimated cost of unsecured borrowing for a one-year maturity, based on submissions from major banks in the London interbank market.

str:Code.com:Name: 6-month London interbank offered rate (LIBOR)
str:Code.com:Description: Historical benchmark interest rate reflecting the estimated cost of unsecured borrowing for a six-month maturity, based on submissions from major banks in the London interbank market.

str:Code.com:Name: Overnight London interbank offered rate (LIBOR)
str:Code.com:Description: Historical benchmark interest rate reflecting the estimated cost of unsecured overnight borrowing, based on submissions from major banks in the London interbank market.

str:Code.com:Name: Rate of remuneration
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate paid on funds or balances held with an institution.

str:Code.com:Name: Treasury bills yield
str:Code.com:Description: The yield on short-term U.S. government debt securities with maturities of up to one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary credit rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate charged by a central bank on primary credit extended to eligible depository institutions, typically through its standing lending facility.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates: Deposits
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on deposit contracts with credit institutions in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates: Loans other than bank overdrafts, Over EUR 1 Million
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on loans other than bank overdrafts in the Euro area, with loan amounts exceeding EUR 1 million.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro Area rates: Loans, consumer credit and other
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on loans for consumer credit and other purposes in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates: Loans
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on loans in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro Area Rates: Deposits, Repos
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on deposit positions arising from repurchase agreements (repos) in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Euro area rates: Deposits agreed maturity
str:Code.com:Description: Interest rates on deposits with an agreed maturity in the Euro area.

str:Code.com:Name: Government securities: Treasury bills yields
str:Code.com:Description: The yields on short-term government debt securities (Treasury bills), typically with maturities of up to one year.

str:Code.com:Name: Money market rate
str:Code.com:Description: The market for short-term debt securities and financial instruments, typically with maturities of less than one year.

str:Code.com:Name: London Eurodollar deposit rate
str:Code.com:Description: A benchmark interest rate reflecting the rate at which banks are willing to lend U.S. dollar–denominated deposits (Eurodollars) to other banks in the London market.

str:Code.com:Name: Interbank rate
str:Code.com:Description: The interest rate at which banks lend funds to one another in the interbank market.

str:Code.com:Name: Government securities
str:Code.com:Description: Debt securities issued by a government to finance its operations and manage public debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Government bonds
str:Code.com:Description: Long-term government debt securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Ukraine National Definition, Refinancing Rate
str:Code.com:Description: The policy interest rate set by the National Bank of Ukraine, applied to its refinancing operations and serving as the key benchmark rate for monetary policy.

str:Code.com:Name: Ukraine Other Rates, Treasury Bills
str:Code.com:Description: Yields on short-term government debt securities (Treasury bills) issued by Ukraine.

str:Code.com:Name: Ukraine PFTS Index Oct 1, 1997 EOM
str:Code.com:Description: A Ukrainian equity market price index tracking the performance of selected listed shares, with a base value of 100 as of October 1, 1997, measured at end-of-month values.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Flow or stock entry
str:Codelist.com:Description: A classification that indicates whether a data point represents a flow (a quantity measured over a period of time) or a stock (a quantity measured at a specific point in time).

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: A term used when a particular concept or data item does not apply to the context in question.

str:Code.com:Name: Balancing items
str:Code.com:Description: Accounting constructs used to ensure that the accounts balance, representing the difference between total sources and uses.

str:Code.com:Name: Holding gains in assets and liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Categorization of gains in the value of assets and liabilities due to price changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Holding gains in assets
str:Code.com:Description: Categorization of gains in the value of assetsdue to price changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Holding gains in liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Categorization of gains in the value of liabilities due to price changes.

str:Code.com:Name: Other changes in the volume of assets and liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Categorization of changes in the volume of assets and liabilities due to factors other than transactions and revaluations.

str:Code.com:Name: Other changes in the volume of assets
str:Code.com:Description: Categorization of changes in the volume of assets due to factors other than transactions and revaluations.

str:Code.com:Name: Other changes in the volume of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Categorization of changes in the volume of liabilities due to factors other than transactions and revaluations.

str:Code.com:Name: Other economic flows (holding gains + other changes in volume) in assets and liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Organizing and categorizing all non-transactional changes in the value and volume of assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other economic flows (holding gains + other changes in volume) in assets
str:Code.com:Description: Organizing and categorizing all non-transactional changes in the value and volume of assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Other economic flows (holding gains + other changes in volume) in liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Organizing and categorizing all non-transactional changes in the value and volume of liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Expenditures by function of government
str:Code.com:Description: Categorizing government expenditures based on the purpose or function they serve, such as health, education, or defense.

str:Code.com:Name: Stocks of assets and liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Organizing and categorizing assets and liabilities based on their characteristics and economic functions.

str:Code.com:Name: Stocks of assets
str:Code.com:Description: Organizing and categorizing assets based on their characteristics and economic functions.

str:Code.com:Name: Stocks of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Organizing and categorizing liabilities based on their characteristics and economic functions.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions in assets and liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Categorizing transactions based on their impact on assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions in assets
str:Code.com:Description: Categorizing transactions based on their impact on assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions in liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Categorizing transactions based on their impact on liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Future flows, contingent
str:Code.com:Description: Expected future financial flows that depend on the occurrence of specific events or conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Stocks and future flows to be reported with standard periodicity and timeliness
str:Code.com:Description: The requirement to report stock positions and expected future financial flows at regular intervals and within specified timeframes.

str:Code.com:Name: Future flows, predetermined
str:Code.com:Description: Expected future financial flows that are certain and based on existing agreements or conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Integrated flow and stock positions
str:Code.com:Description: Combined presentation of economic flows (transactions) and stock positions (assets and liabilities) to provide a comprehensive view of financial status and activity.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in positions other than transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Variations in asset and liability positions due to factors other than transactions, such as revaluations or other changes in volume.

str:Code.com:Name: Revaluations / nominal holding gains and losses
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the value of assets and liabilities due to price changes, reflecting gains or losses in nominal terms.

str:Code.com:Name: Revaluations due to exchange rate changes
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments in the value of assets and liabilities resulting from fluctuations in exchange rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Revaluations due to other price changes
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments in the value of assets and liabilities resulting from changes in market prices other than exchange rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Other changes in the volume of Assets / liabilities = other changes excluding revaluations
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments to the volume of assets and liabilities that are not due to revaluations, such as write-offs or physical changes in assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Closing balance sheet / positions / stocks
str:Code.com:Description: The financial position of an entity at the end of an accounting period, showing the values of assets, liabilities, and equity.

str:Code.com:Name: Long positions
str:Code.com:Description: Assets held by an entity with the expectation of appreciation or income generation.

str:Code.com:Name: Opening balance sheet / positions / stocks
str:Code.com:Description: The financial position of an entity at the beginning of an accounting period, showing the values of assets, liabilities, and equity.

str:Code.com:Name: Changes in balance sheet / positions / stocks
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments or variations in the value or composition of assets, liabilities, and equity items on the balance sheet over a period.

str:Code.com:Name: Main aggregates
str:Code.com:Description: Key summary indicators or measures that provide an overview of economic activity, such as GDP, total revenue, or total expenditure.

str:Code.com:Name: Total other economic flows
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all changes in the value and volume of assets and liabilities that are not due to transactions, including holding gains and other changes in volume.

str:Code.com:Name: Positions
str:Code.com:Description: The financial holdings of assets, liabilities, and equity at a specific point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Short positions
str:Code.com:Description: Assets held by an entity through borrowing and selling, expecting a decrease in value.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock positions
str:Code.com:Description: The values of assets, liabilities, and equity held at a specific point in time.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock positions by counterpart sector
str:Code.com:Description: The values of assets and liabilities categorized by the sector of the counterpart entity involved in the transaction.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Economic flows that result from interactions between institutional units by mutual agreement, involving an exchange of value.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions by counterpart sector
str:Code.com:Description: The economic transactions categorized by the sector of the counterpart entity involved.

str:Code.com:Name: Total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all relevant components or categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Revenue
str:Code.com:Description: Revenue.

str:Code.com:Name: Expense
str:Code.com:Description: Expense.

str:Code.com:Name: Transactions (cash basis of recording)
str:Code.com:Description: Transactions (cash basis of recording).

str:Codelist.com:Name: Social Concepts
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents a broad category encompassing various social indicators, such as tourism and labor concepts.

str:Code.com:Name: Employed persons
str:Code.com:Description: Persons above the age of 15 who were in paid employment or self-employment during a specified reference period.

str:Code.com:Name: Employed persons index
str:Code.com:Description: An index that measures the level of employed persons relative to a base period, providing a quantitative measure of employment trends.

str:Code.com:Name: Labor force
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of employed and unemployed persons in an economy, actively participating in the labor market.

str:Code.com:Name: Tourist
str:Code.com:Description: Persons traveling to and staying in places outside their usual environment for not more than one consecutive year for leisure, business, and other purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Tourist visitors index
str:Code.com:Description: A numerical representation that measures the relative changes in the number of tourist visitors over time compared to a base period.

str:Code.com:Name: Tourist visitors
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals who engage in tourism activities, including travel and stay outside their usual environment for leisure, business, and other purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Unemployment rate
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage of the labor force that is unemployed and actively seeking employment. It is calculated as the number of unemployed persons divided by the total labor force.

str:Code.com:Name: Unemployed persons
str:Code.com:Description: Persons of working age who are without work, are currently available for work, and are actively seeking employment.

str:Code.com:Name: Wage rate
str:Code.com:Description: A measure of the monetary compensation for labor services, typically expressed in terms of a fixed amount per hour, day, month, or other unit of time.

str:Code.com:Name: Number of tourist visitors
str:Code.com:Description: The total count of individuals participating in tourism activities within a specified period.

str:Code.com:Name: Population
str:Code.com:Description: The total number of people living in a specific area or country.

str:Code.com:Name: Labor compensation
str:Code.com:Description: The total remuneration, in cash or in kind, payable by employers to employees in return for work done.

str:Code.com:Name: Employee compensation
str:Code.com:Description: All payments made to employees, including wages, salaries, and benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Employed persons, based on natural rate of unemployment
str:Code.com:Description: The number of people employed, adjusted to account for the natural rate of unemployment, which is the rate of unemployment expected in a healthy economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Hourly compensation
str:Code.com:Description: The amount of money paid to an employee for one hour of work.

str:Code.com:Name: Labor share of national income
str:Code.com:Description: The proportion of national income paid to labor, usually expressed as a percentage.

str:Code.com:Name: Population ages 16 to 64
str:Code.com:Description: The total number of people within the age range of 16 to 64, often considered the working-age population.

str:Code.com:Name: Population aggregation
str:Code.com:Description: The process of compiling data on the population from various sources to create a comprehensive dataset.

str:Code.com:Name: Labor productivity
str:Code.com:Description: A measure of economic performance that compares the amount of goods and services produced with the number of hours worked.

str:Code.com:Name: Unit labor cost
str:Code.com:Description: The average cost of labor per unit of output, calculated by dividing total labor costs by the number of units produced.

str:Code.com:Name: Unemployment rate
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage of the labor force that is unemployed and actively seeking employment.

str:Code.com:Name: Natural rate of unemployment
str:Code.com:Description: The rate of unemployment that is expected in a healthy economy due to normal labor market turnover.

str:Code.com:Name: Health-related personal travel services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to travel for health purposes, including medical tourism and travel for medical treatment.

str:Code.com:Name: Labor share of national income
str:Code.com:Description: The portion of national income that is allocated to labor compensation.

str:Code.com:Name: Average monthly earnings
str:Code.com:Description: The average amount of money earned by workers per month.

str:Code.com:Name: Average weekly earnings
str:Code.com:Description: The average amount of money earned by workers per week.

str:Code.com:Name: Wages
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made to workers for their labor or services, typically on an hourly, daily, or piecework basis.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized unemployment rate
str:Code.com:Description: A standardized measure of unemployment that allows for comparison across different countries or regions.

str:Code.com:Name: Hours worked per employee
str:Code.com:Description: The total number of hours worked by an employee within a specific period.

str:Code.com:Name: Poverty rate
str:Code.com:Description: The percentage of the population living below the poverty line, often defined by income thresholds.

str:Code.com:Name: Unit labor cost in US Dollars
str:Code.com:Description: The average cost of labor per unit of output, calculated by dividing total labor costs by the number of units produced in US Dollars.

str:Codelist.com:Name: REPORTING_PERIOD_TYPE
str:Codelist.com:Description: It is used to identify and distinguish between different types of time periods in economic and financial reporting. The specific start and end dates for a fiscal year (FY) can vary depending on the country, organization, or industry. When reporting data using this codelist, it is essential to specify the exact start and end dates of the fiscal year being referenced.

str:Code.com:Name: Calendar year
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting on January 1st and ending on December 31st.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period that a company or government uses for accounting purposes. The fiscal year may or may not align with the calendar year. For example, a company's fiscal year could start on July 1st and end on June 30th of the following year.

str:Code.com:Name: Base year
str:Code.com:Description: The Base Year is a specific year chosen as a reference point for constructing an index or comparing data over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Standard reference period (SRP)
str:Code.com:Description: Standard Reference Period (SRP) is a consistent timeframe used for comparing data over time.

str:Code.com:Name: To delete
str:Code.com:Description: To delete

str:Code.com:Name: Standard reference period (2010=100)
str:Code.com:Description: Standard Reference Period (SRP) is a consistent timeframe used for comparing data over time. This series is references to 2010=100.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in February
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in February 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in March
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in March 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in April
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in April 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in May
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in May 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in June
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in June 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in July
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in July 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in August
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in August 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in September
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in September 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in October
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in October 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in November
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in November 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year starting in December
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period starting in December 1st that a company or government uses for accounting purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Fiscal year (other definition)
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month period that a company or government uses for accounting purposes. The fiscal year may or may not align with the calendar year. For example, a company's fiscal year could start on July 1st and end on June 30th of the following year.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Transformation
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents a set of codes identifying the specific transformations or calculations applied to source data to derive new data elements or indicators. A transformation involves a computation with one or more input values (either base variables obtained by observation or derived variables from prior transformations) that results in a single output value (the value of a new derived variable).

str:Code.com:Name: Percent, 1 decimal
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to a percentage value that is expressed with one digit after the decimal point, providing a level of precision to the tenths place (e.g., 23.4%).

str:Code.com:Name: 12-month moving average
str:Code.com:Description: A moving average is a method for smoothing time series by calculating the average of the most recent 12 consecutive months of observations. Each new month, the oldest value is dropped and the newest value is added, producing a continuously updated average.

str:Code.com:Name: 12-month rolling beta
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-month rolling beta is a statistical measure that shows the volatility of an investment compared to a benchmark index (like the S&P 500) over a 12-month period. This calculation is done continuously, with each new month's data replacing the oldest month in the calculation, creating a "rolling" effect. One unit is equivalent to one standard deviation of the standardized variable.

str:Code.com:Name: 12-period cumulated sum
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-period cumulated sum is a transformation that calculates the sum of values over the most recent twelve consecutive periods. The calculation is updated each period by adding the newest observation and removing the oldest, producing a rolling cumulative total.

str:Code.com:Name: 12-period moving average
str:Code.com:Description: A 12-period moving average is a transformation that calculates the arithmetic mean of a variable over the most recent twelve consecutive periods. The calculation is updated each period by including the latest observation and excluding the oldest, resulting in a rolling average

str:Code.com:Name: 16-period cumulated sum
str:Code.com:Description: A 16-period cumulated sum is a transformation that calculates the sum of values over the most recent sixteen consecutive periods. The calculation is updated each period by adding the newest observation and removing the oldest, producing a rolling cumulative total.

str:Code.com:Name: 1st quartile
str:Code.com:Description: The first quartile (Q1) is the value below which 25 percent of the observations in a distribution fall. It represents the lower boundary of the second quarter of the ordered data and is a measure of dispersion and position.

str:Code.com:Name: Percent, 2 decimals
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to a percentage value that is expressed with two digits after the decimal point, providing a level of precision to the hundredths place (e.g., 56.78%).

str:Code.com:Name: 2nd quartile
str:Code.com:Description: The second quartile (Q2) or Median is the value below which 50 percent of the observations in a distribution fall. It corresponds to the median of the ordered data.

str:Code.com:Name: 3-period cumulated sum
str:Code.com:Description: A 3-period cumulated sum is a calculation that adds up a set of values over a consecutive 3-period window. This could be 3 months, 3 quarters, or any other relevant time frame.

str:Code.com:Name: 3-period moving average
str:Code.com:Description: A 3-period moving average is a transformation that calculates the arithmetic mean of a variable over the most recent three consecutive periods. The calculation is updated each period by including the latest observation and excluding the oldest, resulting in a rolling average

str:Code.com:Name: 3rd quartile
str:Code.com:Description: The third quartile (Q3) is the value below which 75 percent of the observations in a distribution fall. It represents the upper boundary of the third quarter of the ordered data

str:Code.com:Name: 4-period cumulated sum
str:Code.com:Description: A 4-period cumulated sum is a calculation that adds up a set of values over a consecutive 4-period window. This could be 4 months, 4 quarters.

str:Code.com:Name: 4-period moving average
str:Code.com:Description: A 4-period moving average is a transformation that calculates the arithmetic mean of a variable over the most recent four consecutive periods. The calculation is updated each period by including the latest observation and excluding the oldest, resulting in a rolling average

str:Code.com:Name: Weighted index, adjusted by relative consumer prices, 6-month rolling, monthly squared change.
str:Code.com:Description: Measures the volatility of a data series by calculating the squared difference between each month's value and the previous month's value, averaged over a rolling 6-month window. One unit is equivalent to one standard deviation of the standardized variable.

str:Code.com:Name: 6-period cumulated sum
str:Code.com:Description: A 6-period cumulated sum is a transformation that calculates the sum of values over the most recent six consecutive periods. The calculation is updated each period by adding the newest observation and removing the oldest, producing a rolling cumulative total.

str:Code.com:Name: 6-period moving average
str:Code.com:Description: A 6-period moving average is a transformation that calculates the arithmetic mean of a variable over the most recent six consecutive periods. The calculation is updated each period by including the latest observation and excluding the oldest, resulting in a rolling average

str:Code.com:Name: Battery Grade, 99%
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to a material or substance that has a purity level of 99% and is suitable for use in batteries. The term "Battery Grade" indicates that the material meets the specific quality and performance requirements for battery applications, such as electrochemical stability, high energy density, and long cycle life.

str:Code.com:Name: Coarse particle, 99.2%
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to a material with a specific particle size distribution where 99.2% of the particles are larger than a certain size threshold, indicating a coarse or granular material. The exact size threshold would depend on the context and the specific material being described.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to data that has been modified or corrected using the accounting records maintained by the International Monetary Fund (IMF). These adjustments are typically made to ensure consistency and accuracy in international financial statistics, particularly for balance of payments data and other macroeconomic indicators. The IMF's accounting records provide a standardized framework for recording and reporting economic transactions between countries, and using these records helps ensure comparability and reliability of data across different nations.

str:Code.com:Name: Aggregate
str:Code.com:Description: A transformation that combines multiple observations into a single value according to a specified aggregation rule, such as summation, averaging, or another defined statistical operation

str:Code.com:Name: Aggregate weighted by GDP
str:Code.com:Description: A transformation that aggregates multiple observations into a single value using gross domestic product (GDP) as weights. Each component contributes to the aggregated result in proportion to its GDP weight.

str:Code.com:Name: Aggregate weighted by GDP, percent change
str:Code.com:Description: A transformation that aggregates percentage changes across multiple observations using gross domestic product (GDP) as weights. Each component’s percentage change contributes to the aggregated result in proportion to its GDP weight.

str:Code.com:Name: Aggregated using market exchange rate based weights, percent change
str:Code.com:Description: A transformation that aggregates percentage changes across multiple observations using foreign exchange rates as conversion or weighting factors. The aggregation reflects changes after accounting for exchange rate effects.

str:Code.com:Name: Aggregation weight
str:Code.com:Description: A transformation that aggregates multiple observations into a single value using specified weights. Each component contributes to the aggregated result in proportion to its assigned weight.

str:Code.com:Name: Annual levels
str:Code.com:Description: Annual levels refer to data that is collected, analyzed, or reported on a yearly basis.

str:Code.com:Name: Annual rate of change corrected for the effect of methodological changes where applicable
str:Code.com:Description: This is a measure of the percentage change of a variable compared with the same period one year earlier.

str:Code.com:Name: Annualized
str:Code.com:Description: It indicates that a rate of change or flow has been converted to an annual rate, assuming the current-period pace continues for a full year.

str:Code.com:Name: Annualized percent change
str:Code.com:Description: It represents the period-to-period percent change, converted to an annual rate.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets to gross domestic product (GDP)
str:Code.com:Description: This ratio can refer to different types of assets, such as total financial assets, total assets of the banking sector, or assets of a specific company or sector. It measures the size of these assets relative to the country's GDP.

str:Code.com:Name: Assets to total financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: This ratio looks at the composition of a specific entity's assets (e.g., company, bank, individual) and measures the proportion that is made up of financial assets (such as cash, stocks, bonds, loans receivable).

str:Code.com:Name: Assets to total financial system assets
str:Code.com:Description: This ratio compares an entity's total assets to the aggregate assets of the entire financial system (including all banks, insurance companies, investment funds, etc.) in a country or region.

str:Code.com:Name: Average of percent change
str:Code.com:Description: It measures the average of period-to-period percent changes over a specified time span, most commonly over a year. Rather than using end-period values, it reflects the mean growth rate across periods

str:Code.com:Name: Average since 1999
str:Code.com:Description: The arithmetic mean (average) value of a specific data series or indicator calculated over the period from 1999 to the present time.
