str:Code.com:Description: Percent of rural population

str:Code.com:Name: Percent of total water withdrawal
str:Code.com:Description: Percent of total water withdrawal

str:Code.com:Name: Percent of emissions in the energy sector
str:Code.com:Description: Percent of emissions in the energy sector

str:Code.com:Name: Births per 1,000 women ages 15-19
str:Code.com:Description: Births Per 1,000 Women Ages 15-19.

str:Code.com:Name: Births per woman
str:Code.com:Description: The value is expressed per unit of births per woman.

str:Code.com:Name: Per 100,000 persons
str:Code.com:Description: The value is expressed per unit of 100,000 persons.

str:Code.com:Name: Per 100,000 live births
str:Code.com:Description: The value is expressed per unit of 100,000 live births

str:Code.com:Name: Births per 1,000 women
str:Code.com:Description: Births per 1,000 women

str:Code.com:Name: Categorical value
str:Code.com:Description: A categorical qualifier indicating that the observation represents a value-type measure, as opposed to a volume, price, index, rate, or ratio, within a classification or attribute dimension.

str:Code.com:Name: Percent of total labor force
str:Code.com:Description: Percent of total labor force.

str:Code.com:Name: Percent of total employment
str:Code.com:Description: Percent of total employment.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Monetary and Financial Instruments
str:Codelist.com:Description: A classification of financial instruments used in monetary and financial statistics, including currency, deposits, loans, securities, and derivatives.

str:Code.com:Name: Broad money
str:Code.com:Description: Broad money is the sum of all liquid financial instruments held by money-holding sectors that are widely accepted in an economy as a medium of exchange, plus those that can be converted into a medium of exchange at short notice at, or close to, their full nominal value (2016 MFSMCG).

str:Code.com:Name: Stock market index S&P500
str:Code.com:Description: The S&P 500 is a stock market index that tracks the performance of 500 of the largest publicly traded companies in the United States, serving as a benchmark for the overall U.S. equity market.

str:Code.com:Name: Benchmark stock market index
str:Code.com:Description: A benchmark stock market index is a standard measure that tracks the performance of a specific group of stocks, serving as a reference point for evaluating the returns of investment portfolios or the overall market.

str:Code.com:Name: Benchmark stock market index, US NASDAQ Stock Composite
str:Code.com:Description: The NASDAQ Composite is a benchmark U.S. stock market index that tracks the performance of over 3,000 companies listed on the NASDAQ Stock Exchange

str:Code.com:Name: Benchmark stock market index, US Dow Jones 30 Industrial Stocks
str:Code.com:Description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) is a benchmark U.S. stock market index that tracks the performance of 30 major publicly traded companies across various industries.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock market index Dow Jones Industrial Average
str:Code.com:Description: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) is a benchmark U.S. stock market index that tracks the performance of 30 major publicly traded companies across various industries.

str:Code.com:Name: Consumer confidence
str:Code.com:Description: Consumer confidence is an economic indicator that measures the degree of optimism consumers feel about the overall state of the economy and their personal financial situation.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit to the private sector
str:Code.com:Description: Credit to the private sector refers to the total amount of loans and financial resources provided by financial institutions to households and businesses, excluding government entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Money supply
str:Code.com:Description: Money supply is the total amount of money available in an economy at a specific time; there are different measures of it, in particular broad money defined above.

str:Code.com:Name: Money policy evaluation, probability of rate change at/before next FOMC meeting
str:Code.com:Description: Monetary policy evaluation regarding the probability of a rate change at or before the next FOMC meeting involves assessing economic indicators and market expectations to estimate whether the Federal Reserve will adjust interest rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Narrow money
str:Code.com:Description: The most liquid forms of money, including cash and assets that can quickly be converted to cash, such as demand deposits.

str:Code.com:Name: Use of IMF credit and loans (IMF records)
str:Code.com:Description: Utilization of credit and loans from the International Monetary Fund, recorded by the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial sector support
str:Code.com:Description: Financial sector support.

str:Code.com:Name: Use of Fund credit
str:Code.com:Description: Use of Fund credit.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodities Research Bureau's futures price, index
str:Code.com:Description: Commodities Research Bureau's futures price, index.

str:Code.com:Name: Benchmark stock market index 1
str:Code.com:Description: Benchmark stock market index 1.

str:Code.com:Name: Benchmark stock market index 2
str:Code.com:Description: Benchmark stock market index 2.

str:Code.com:Name: Income velocity of broad money (GDP in current prices / Broad money)
str:Code.com:Description: The rate at which broad money circulates in the economy.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Trade flow
str:Codelist.com:Description: Data related to the movement of goods and services between countries, including exports and imports.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods
str:Code.com:Description: Physical, tangible products that are traded internationally.

str:Code.com:Name: Total imports
str:Code.com:Description: Total imports reflects the change in the economic ownership of products from non-residents to residents of the compiling economy, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports
str:Code.com:Description: Imports of goods are recorded at the time when they enter the economic territory of a country.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of manufactures
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of manufactured goods sold by foreign sellers to domestic purchasers

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of non-oil related products
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of non-oil-related products bought by a country from foreign sellers.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of oil related products
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of oil-related products bought by a country from foreign sellers.

str:Code.com:Name: Re-imports
str:Code.com:Description: Re-imports are domestic goods imported in the same state as previously exported, without any substantial transformation occurring on the goods while they were outside the territory.

str:Code.com:Name: Import of goods for inward processing
str:Code.com:Description: Import of goods for the purpose of processing and re-exporting them.

str:Code.com:Name: Import of goods after outward processing
str:Code.com:Description: Import of goods that were sent abroad for processing and then imported back.

str:Code.com:Name: Intra-firm imports
str:Code.com:Description: Imports of goods or services between different branches of the same multinational company.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: Imports of goods are recorded at the time when they enter the economic territory of a country.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods (customs basis)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods bought by a country from foreign sellers, measured at the customs border.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Imports of goods and services reflect the change in the economic ownership of goods from non-residents to residents of the compiling economy, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. Imports of services reflect services provided by non-residents to residents

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of services
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of services bought by a country from foreign sellers.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade balance goods
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the value of a country's exports and imports of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Oil trade balance
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the value of a country's exports and imports of oil and oil-related products.

str:Code.com:Name: Three component-based goods trade imports
str:Code.com:Description: Three component-based goods trade imports

str:Code.com:Name: Three component-based goods trade exports
str:Code.com:Description: Three component-based goods trade exports

str:Code.com:Name: Total exports
str:Code.com:Description: Total exports reflects the change in the economic ownership of products from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of goods are recorded at the time when they leave the economic territory of a country.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of manufactures
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of manufactured goods sold by a country to foreign buyers.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports and imports
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods and services exported and imported by a country.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of non-oil related products
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of non-oil-related products sold by a country to foreign buyers.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of oil related products
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of oil-related products sold by a country to foreign buyers.

str:Code.com:Name: Re-exports
str:Code.com:Description: Re-exports are goods produced in other economies, and previously imported, that are exported with no substantial transformation from the state in which they were previously imported.

str:Code.com:Name: Re-exports (customs basis)
str:Code.com:Description: Re-exports are goods produced in other economies, and previously imported, that are exported with no substantial transformation from the state in which they were previously imported, measured on a customs basis.

str:Code.com:Name: Export of goods after inward processing
str:Code.com:Description: Export of goods that have been imported, processed, and then exported.

str:Code.com:Name: Export of goods for outward processing
str:Code.com:Description: Export of goods sent abroad for processing and then returned or further traded.

str:Code.com:Name: Intra-firm exports
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of goods or services between different branches of the same multinational company.

str:Code.com:Name: Crude oil exports
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of crude oil sold by a country to foreign buyers.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of goods are recorded at the time when they leave the economic territory of a country.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods (customs basis)
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of goods sold by a country to foreign buyers, measured at the customs border.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of goods and services reflect the export of goods reflect changes in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. Exports of services provided by residents to non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports and imports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: The combined total value of all goods exported and imported by a country.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports and imports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: The combined total value of all goods and services exported and imported by a country.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of services
str:Code.com:Description: The total value of services sold by a country to foreign buyers.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of environmental goods
str:Code.com:Description: Imports of environmental goods comprise all environmental goods entering the national territory. A relatively high share of environmental goods imports indicates that an economy purchases a significant share of environmental goods from other economies. Exports of environmental goods comprise all environmental goods leaving the national territory. A relatively high share of environmental goods exports indicates that an economy produces and sells a significant share of environmental goods to other economies. An economy’s environmental goods trade balance is the difference between its exports and imports of environmental goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Quality of imported goods
str:Code.com:Description: Quality of imported goods

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of low carbon technology products
str:Code.com:Description: Low carbon technology products produce less pollution than their traditional energy counterparts, and will play a vital role in the transition to a low carbon economy. Low carbon technologies include mechanics like wind turbines, solar panels, biomass systems and carbon capture equipment. Imports of low carbon technology products comprise all low carbon technology products entering the national territory.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade balance in environmental goods
str:Code.com:Description: Environmental goods include both goods connected to environmental protection—such as goods related to pollution management and resource management—and adapted goods—which are goods that have been specifically modified to be more “environmentally friendly” or “cleaner.” Imports of environmental goods comprise all environmental goods entering the national territory.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade balance in low carbon technology products
str:Code.com:Description: Low carbon technology products produce less pollution than their traditional energy counterparts, and will play a vital role in the transition to a low carbon economy. Low carbon technologies include mechanics like wind turbines, solar panels, biomass systems and carbon capture equipment. Imports of low carbon technology products comprise all low carbon technology products entering the national territory.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of industrial inputs
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of industrial inputs

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of refined petroleum products
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of refined petroleum products

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of environmental goods
str:Code.com:Description: Environmental goods include both goods connected to environmental protection—such as goods related to pollution management and resource management—and adapted goods—which are goods that have been specifically modified to be more “environmentally friendly” or “cleaner.”

str:Code.com:Name: Quality of exported goods
str:Code.com:Description: Quality of exported goods

str:Code.com:Name: Quality of exported goods index
str:Code.com:Description: Quality of exported goods index

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of low carbon technology products
str:Code.com:Description: Low carbon technology products produce less pollution than their traditional energy counterparts, and will play a vital role in the transition to a low carbon economy. Low carbon technologies include mechanics like wind turbines, solar panels, biomass systems and carbon capture equipment. Imports of low carbon technology products comprise all low carbon technology products entering the national territory.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade in environmental goods
str:Code.com:Description: Environmental goods include both goods connected to environmental protection—such as goods related to pollution management and resource management—and adapted goods—which are goods that have been specifically modified to be more “environmentally friendly” or “cleaner.” Imports of environmental goods comprise all environmental goods entering the national territory.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade in low carbon technology products
str:Code.com:Description: Low carbon technology products produce less pollution than their traditional energy counterparts, and will play a vital role in the transition to a low carbon economy. Low carbon technologies include mechanics like wind turbines, solar panels, biomass systems and carbon capture equipment. Imports of low carbon technology products comprise all low carbon technology products entering the national territory. A relatively high share of low carbon technology products imports indicates that an economy purchases a significant share of low carbon technology products from other economies. Exports of low carbon technology products comprise all low carbon technology products leaving the national territory. A relatively high share of low carbon technology products exports indicates that an economy produces and sells a significant share of low carbon technology products to other economies. An economy’s trade balance in low carbon technology products is the difference between its exports and imports of low carbon technology products.

str:Code.com:Name: Natural gas exports
str:Code.com:Description: Natural gas exports.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Gender
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents the spectrum of an individual's internal and individual experience of gender, which can be the same or different from their sex assigned at birth.

str:Code.com:Name: Men
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who defines themselves and lives as a male.

str:Code.com:Name: Women
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who defines themselves and lives as a female.

str:Code.com:Name: Transgender men
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who defines themselves and lives as a male, who was assigned female at birth.

str:Code.com:Name: Transgender women
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who defines themselves and lives as a female, who was assigned as male at birth.

str:Code.com:Name: Cis men
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who defines themselves and lives as a male, who was assigned male at birth.

str:Code.com:Name: Cis women
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who defines themselves and lives as a female, who was assigned female at birth.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-binary
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who defines themselves outside the binary of man or woman, and which may include various identities located at any point on the gender spectrum.

str:Code.com:Name: Gender fluid
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who does not have a fixed gender, and whose gender moves between different points on the gender spectrum. 3

str:Code.com:Name: Gender non-conforming
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who does not conform with the gender roles/norms/behaviours/expression expected from a given gender in a given society

str:Code.com:Name: Agender / Gender neutral
str:Code.com:Description: An individual who does not identify with any gender.

str:Code.com:Name: Gender queer
str:Code.com:Description: Genderqueer individuals typically reject notions of static categories of gender and embrace a fluidity of gender identity and often, though not always, sexual orientation. People who identify as "genderqueer" may see themselves as being both male and female, neither male nor female or as falling completely outside these categories.”

str:Code.com:Name: Indigenous gender-variant identities
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals from indigenous communities who define themselves in a range of identities on the gender continuum outside the western binary approach to gender. They often define their gender from a spiritual perspective and are seen/see themselves as being able to cross freely between male and female, or to exist in a state that is in between, or neither.

str:Code.com:Name: Two-spirit
str:Code.com:Description: Two-spirit spirit is also used to refer to the 2SLGBTQ+ community, as indigenous peoples reclaim their ancestral ways of respecting individuals who do not define themselves within the gender social construct of man and woman.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: For cases where the concept of gender is not applicable.

str:Code.com:Name: Other
str:Code.com:Description: For individuals who do not define themselves as any of the available options.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Institutional Sector
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents a classification of institutional units based on their economic function and role in the economy, such as households, non-financial corporations, financial corporations, or government.

str:Code.com:Name: Affiliated entities
str:Code.com:Description: Enterprises linked through a direct or indirect ownership or control relationships.

str:Code.com:Name: All resident sectors excluding financial intermediaries (CDIS)
str:Code.com:Description: All resident sectors excluding financial intermediaries as per Coordinated Direct Investment Survey guidelines.

str:Code.com:Name: All sectors other than households and NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: All institutional sectors except households and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit-taking corporations, except the central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations, excluding the central bank, that are principally engaged in financial intermediation and incur liabilities in the form of deposits and/or close substitutes for deposits.

str:Code.com:Name: Banks and other financial institutions headquartered in the reporting country or currency area
str:Code.com:Description: Financial institutions headquartered in the reporting country or currency area.

str:Code.com:Name: Banks and other financial institutions headquartered outside the reporting country or currency area
str:Code.com:Description: Financial institutions headquartered outside the reporting country or currency area.

str:Code.com:Name: Banks headquartered in the reporting country or currency area
str:Code.com:Description: Banks headquartered in the reporting country or currency area.

str:Code.com:Name: Banks headquartered outside the reporting country or currency area
str:Code.com:Description: Banks headquartered outside the reporting country or currency area.

str:Code.com:Name: Bond funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds primarily investing in bonds.

str:Code.com:Name: Budgetary central government
str:Code.com:Description: Central government operations financed through the general budget.

str:Code.com:Name: Budgetary units, central government
str:Code.com:Description: Units within the central government financed by budgetary resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Captive financial institutions and money lenders
str:Code.com:Description: Financial entities providing services within a group, often for tax or legal purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Captive financial institutions and money lenders (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Captive financial institutions and money lenders where the specific sub-sector is not identified.

str:Code.com:Name: Central and state government excluding social security
str:Code.com:Description: Combined central and state government operations excluding social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Central bank
str:Code.com:Description: The monetary authority of a country responsible for issuing currency and regulating the banking system.

str:Code.com:Name: Central banks and general government
str:Code.com:Description: Includes central banks and all general government sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Central clearing counterparties, which are part of other OFIs
str:Code.com:Description: Entities part of other financial intermediaries providing clearing and settlement services.

str:Code.com:Name: Central government
str:Code.com:Description: Central government including both budgetary and non-budgetary units.

str:Code.com:Name: Central government excluding social security
str:Code.com:Description: Central government operations excluding social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Central government including social security
str:Code.com:Description: Central government operations including social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Central government non profit institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Non-profit institutions operated by or closely linked to the central government.

str:Code.com:Name: Closed-end bond funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds issuing fixed shares and primarily investing in bonds.

str:Code.com:Name: Closed-end equity funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds issuing fixed shares and primarily investing in equities.

str:Code.com:Name: Closed-end hedge funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds issuing fixed shares and employing hedge fund strategies.

str:Code.com:Name: Closed-end mixed funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds issuing fixed shares and investing in a mix of asset classes.

str:Code.com:Name: Closed-end non MMFs
str:Code.com:Description: Closed-end funds that are not money market funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Closed-end real estate funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds issuing fixed shares and primarily investing in real estate.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit-taking corporations, except the central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations, excluding the central bank, that are principally engaged in financial intermediation and incur liabilities in the form of deposits and/or close substitutes for deposits.

str:Code.com:Name: Composite life and non-life insurance corporations excluding reinsurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance corporations providing both life and non-life insurance products.

str:Code.com:Name: Constant net asset value (NAV) MMFs
str:Code.com:Description: Money market funds maintaining a constant net asset value per share.

str:Code.com:Name: Corporate groups captive financial entities
str:Code.com:Description: Captive financial entities serving corporate groups.

str:Code.com:Name: Corporations
str:Code.com:Description: All corporations operating within the economy - including non-financial and financial corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Money Lenders
str:Code.com:Description: Financial institutions primarily engaged in providing credit.

str:Code.com:Name: Credit unions and credit cooperatives
str:Code.com:Description: Are financial institutions owned and controlled by their members, regardless of whether they do business exclusively with their members, and/or non-members. Credit unions accept deposits (technically these may be designated as shares) and make various types of loans

str:Code.com:Name: Defined benefit funds
str:Code.com:Description: Pension funds that promise a specified benefit to participants based on salary and years of service.

str:Code.com:Name: Defined contribution funds
str:Code.com:Description: Pension funds where contributions are defined, and benefits depend on investment returns.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit takers
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations primarily accepting deposits and lending.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit taking corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations that accept deposits, excluding central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations accepting deposits, except for the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Deposit-taking corporations with an unidentified sub-sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit taking microfinance institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Deposit taking microfinance institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Depository corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations engaged in accepting deposits and lending or investing funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit-taking corporations and other sectors
str:Code.com:Description: Includes deposit-taking corporations and other institutional sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Deposit-taking corporations except the central bank and excluding electronic money institutions principally engaged in financial intermediation
str:Code.com:Description: Excludes institutions primarily engaged in financial intermediation with e-money.

str:Code.com:Name: Domestic debtor
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to entities within the country owing debts.

str:Code.com:Name: Domestically controlled financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations controlled by residents of the reporting country.

str:Code.com:Name: Domestically controlled non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations controlled by residents of the reporting country.

str:Code.com:Name: Electronic money institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Financial institutions issuing electronic money.

str:Code.com:Name: Electronic money non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations issuing electronic money.

str:Code.com:Name: Employees
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals engaged in paid employment.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers
str:Code.com:Description: Institutional units that employ workers and provide remuneration.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds primarily investing in equities.

str:Code.com:Name: European institutions and bodies (EU accounts)
str:Code.com:Description: European Union institutions involved in managing EU accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: External creditors
str:Code.com:Description: Non-resident institutional units that are creditors to resident units. Counterparty concept; not an institutional sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Extrabudgetary central government
str:Code.com:Description: Units of central government funded outside the main budget.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial auxiliaries
str:Code.com:Description: Entities providing support services to financial corporations; and do not take control of the assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial auxiliaries (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial auxiliaries with unidentified sub-sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Corporations engaged in market financial services including financial intermediation or auxiliary services.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial corporations engaged in lending
str:Code.com:Description: Corporations primarily focused on providing loans or credit.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial corporations other than central banks
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations excluding central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial corporations other than central banks and credit institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Corporations other than central banks and credit institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial corporations other than MFIs
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations excluding monetary financial institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial corporations other than the central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations excluding the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial corporations, nonfinancial corporations, households, and NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: All institutional sectors except the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial corporations, SPEs
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations including special purpose entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial intermediaries (CDIS)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial intermediaries as per Coordinated Direct Investment Survey guidelines.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial vehicle corporations engaged in securitisation
str:Code.com:Description: Entities involved in securitisation by issuing securities backed by pooled assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled
str:Code.com:Description: Entities controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled captive financial institutions and money lenders
str:Code.com:Description: Captive financial institutions controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Deposit-taking corporations controlled by non-residents, excluding central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled financial auxiliaries
str:Code.com:Description: Financial auxiliaries controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled insurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance corporations controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled money market funds
str:Code.com:Description: Money market funds controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled non MMF investment funds
str:Code.com:Description: Non-MMF investment funds controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled other financial intermediaries, except insurance corporations and pension funds
str:Code.com:Description: Financial intermediaries other than insurance and pension funds, controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign controlled pension funds
str:Code.com:Description: Pension funds controlled by non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Foreign owned SPE-type captives
str:Code.com:Description: Captive financial institutions operating as special purpose entities, foreign-owned.

str:Code.com:Name: General government
str:Code.com:Description: The general government sector consists of such institutional units at central, state and local level together with social security funds and non-market producers controlled by them.

str:Code.com:Name: General government and institutions financed by the European Union (EU) budget (including the European Development Fund (EDF))
str:Code.com:Description: General government institutions financed by the EU budget, including the European Development Fund.

str:Code.com:Name: General government and institutions of the European Union (ESA GFS concept)
str:Code.com:Description: General government and EU institutions defined by ESA GFS concepts.

str:Code.com:Name: General government excluding social security
str:Code.com:Description: Central, state, and local governments, excluding social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: General government involved in monetary policy operations
str:Code.com:Description: General government units involved in monetary policy operations.

str:Code.com:Name: General government non profit institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Non-profit institutions controlled and mainly financed by general government.

str:Code.com:Name: General government, except central government
str:Code.com:Description: Includes state and local governments, excluding central government.

str:Code.com:Name: General government, except local government
str:Code.com:Description: Includes central and state governments, excluding local government.

str:Code.com:Name: General government, except social security funds
str:Code.com:Description: General government excluding social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: General government, except state government
str:Code.com:Description: Includes central and local governments, excluding state government.

str:Code.com:Name: General government, households and non profit institutions serving households
str:Code.com:Description: Includes general government, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Government, various levels
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to a combination or mix of different levels of government within a country or region, such as national (central), regional (state/province), and local (municipal/county) governments.

str:Code.com:Name: Head offices of financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Head office units overseeing the operations of financial corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Head offices of non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Head office units overseeing the operations of non-financial corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Hedge funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds engaging in diverse and complex strategies to maximize returns.

str:Code.com:Name: Holding companies
str:Code.com:Description: Companies whose primary purpose is to hold shares in other corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Households
str:Code.com:Description: Institutional units consisting of individuals or groups of persons who share living accommodation, pool income and wealth, and consume collectively

str:Code.com:Name: Households and non profit institutions serving households (NPISH)
str:Code.com:Description: Includes households and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Institutions financed by the European Union (EU) budget (including the European Development Fund (EDF))
str:Code.com:Description: Entities funded by the European Union budget, including the European Development Fund.

str:Code.com:Name: Institutions of the European Union (ESA GFS concept)
str:Code.com:Description: Institutions of the EU defined by ESA GFS concepts.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Are financial institutions whose principal function is to provide life, accident, sickness, fire, or other forms of coverage to individual institutional units, groups of units, or reinsurance services to other insurance corporations. For the purposes of the FAS, insurance corporations are disaggregated into life and non-life.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance corporations (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance corporations with unidentified sub-sector.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance corporations and Pension Funds
str:Code.com:Description: Includes insurance corporations and pension funds.

str:Code.com:Name: International organizations
str:Code.com:Description: Organizations established by treaties between sovereign states or other international agreements.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment funds (MMFs and non-MMFs)
str:Code.com:Description: Includes both money market funds and non-money market funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Issuer headquartered in the reporting country or currency area
str:Code.com:Description: Entities issuing financial instruments, headquartered in the reporting country or currency area.

str:Code.com:Name: Life insurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance corporations offering life insurance policies.

str:Code.com:Name: Life insurance corporations excluding reinsurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Life insurance corporations, excluding reinsurance entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Local government excluding social security
str:Code.com:Description: Local government units excluding social security schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Local government including social security
str:Code.com:Description: Local government units, including social security schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Local government non-profit institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Non-profit institutions controlled and mainly financed by local governments.

str:Code.com:Name: Local government, budgetary units
str:Code.com:Description: Budgetary units within local government.

str:Code.com:Name: Local government, extra-budgetary units
str:Code.com:Description: Extra-budgetary units within local government.

str:Code.com:Name: All Microfinance institutions
str:Code.com:Description: All Microfinance institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Mixed funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds combining various asset types such as bonds, equities, and other securities.

str:Code.com:Name: Mobile money service provider
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to a mobile network operator or other entity that partners with mobile network operators to offer mobile money services to its clients through agents independent of the traditional banking network

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Institutions responsible for controlling a country's monetary policy, primarily central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary authorities and international organizations
str:Code.com:Description: Includes central banks and international organizations.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary financial institutions (MFI)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations engaged in monetary intermediation, including central banks and deposit-taking corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary financial institutions (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: MFIs with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary financial institutions other than central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Monetary financial institutions excluding central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Money market funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds that invest in short-term debt instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Money market funds (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Money market funds with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: National private
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to entities owned or controlled by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private captive financial institutions and money lenders
str:Code.com:Description: Captive financial institutions owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private deposit taking corporations, except the Central Bank
str:Code.com:Description: Deposit-taking corporations owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private financial and non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Corporations owned by private domestic interests, spanning both financial and non-financial sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: National private financial auxiliaries
str:Code.com:Description: Financial auxiliaries owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private financial corporations which are part of domestic multinationals
str:Code.com:Description: Domestic financial corporations integrated into multinational groups.

str:Code.com:Name: National private insurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance corporations owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private money market funds
str:Code.com:Description: Money market funds owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private non MMF investment funds
str:Code.com:Description: Non-MMF investment funds owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private non-financial corporations, which are part of domestic multinationals
str:Code.com:Description: Domestic non-financial corporations integrated into multinational groups.

str:Code.com:Name: National private other financial intermediaries, except insurance corporations and pension funds
str:Code.com:Description: Financial intermediaries, excluding insurance and pension funds, owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: National private pension funds
str:Code.com:Description: Pension funds owned by private domestic interests.

str:Code.com:Name: Non financial corporations, households and NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Includes non-financial corporations, households, and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Non financial sectors, except central government
str:Code.com:Description: Includes all non-financial sectors excluding central government.

str:Code.com:Name: Non financial sectors, except households
str:Code.com:Description: Includes all non-financial sectors excluding households.

str:Code.com:Name: Non MMF investment funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds other than money market funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Non MMF investment funds (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Non-MMF investment funds with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Non profit institutions serving households
str:Code.com:Description: Non-profit institutions primarily serving households, including charities and educational institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-deposit taking microfinance institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Also called microcredit institutions, are financial institutions that provide microcredit and finance their activities with sources other than deposits. Microcredit is small-scale credit typically targeted to low-income individuals who are either self-employed or employed in the informal sector; and microenterprises.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations are institutional units whose principal activity is the production of market goods or non-financial services.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-financial corporations (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-financial corporations and general government
str:Code.com:Description: Includes non-financial corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-financial corporations, general government and households and NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Includes all these institutional sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-financial corporations, SPEs
str:Code.com:Description: Includes non-financial corporations and special purpose entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-life insurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance corporations primarily offering non-life insurance products like property or casualty insurance.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-life insurance corporations excluding reinsurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Non-life insurance corporations excluding those engaged in reinsurance.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-MMFs open end funds - bond funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds focusing on bond investments, excluding MMFs.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-MMFs open end funds - equity funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds focusing on equity investments, excluding MMFs.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-MMFs open end funds - hedge funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds classified as hedge funds, excluding MMFs.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-MMFs open end funds - mixed or balanced funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds investing in a mix of assets, excluding MMFs.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-MMFs open end funds - real estate funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds focusing on real estate investments, excluding MMFs.

str:Code.com:Name: Not affiliates
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to entities that are not affiliates of any other entity or corporation.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Indicates that the classification or category is not applicable.

str:Code.com:Name: Not sectorised
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to entities or data that have not been assigned to any sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Open-end bond funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds focusing on bond investments.

str:Code.com:Name: Open-end equity funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds focusing on equity investments.

str:Code.com:Name: Open-end hedge funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds classified as hedge funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Open-end investment funds
str:Code.com:Description: General category for open-end investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Open-end mixed funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds investing in a mix of assets such as equities and bonds.

str:Code.com:Name: Open-end non MMFs
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds excluding MMFs.

str:Code.com:Name: Open-end real estate funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds focusing on real estate investments.

str:Code.com:Name: Other captive finance companies and money lenders
str:Code.com:Description: Captive finance companies and money lenders not classified under specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other closed-end non MMFs
str:Code.com:Description: Closed-end investment funds excluding MMFs, not classified under specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other deposit takers
str:Code.com:Description: Include all remaining financial institutions other than the central bank, commercial banks, credit unions and credit cooperatives, and deposit taking microfinance institutions, that accept deposits or issue other types of liabilities that are included in the national definition of broad money. These institutions have varying names across countries, such as savings and loan associations, building societies, rural banks and agricultural banks, post office giro institutions, post office savings banks, savings banks, and money market funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other depository corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Depository corporations not classified as banks or credit unions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations not classified under other specific financial sub-sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Other financial institutions (Financial corporations other than MFIs, insurance corporations and pension funds)
str:Code.com:Description: Includes financial corporations other than MFIs, insurance corporations, and pension funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other financial institutions (Financial corporations other than MFIs, insurance corporations, pension funds and financial auxiliaries)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations excluding MFIs, insurance corporations, pension funds, and financial auxiliaries.

str:Code.com:Name: Other financial institutions (Financial corporations other than MFIs, insurance corporations, pension funds and non MMFs Investment Funds)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations excluding MFIs, insurance corporations, pension funds, and non-MMF investment funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other financial institutions (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Other financial institutions with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Other financial intermediaries, except insurance corporations and pension funds
str:Code.com:Description: Financial intermediaries excluding insurance corporations and pension funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other financial intermediaries, except insurance corporations and pension funds (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial intermediaries with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Other financial intermediaries, excluding financial vehicle corporations engaged in securitizations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial intermediaries excluding certain specialized entities engaged in securitization.

str:Code.com:Name: Other forms of institutional savings
str:Code.com:Description: Savings institutions not classified under traditional categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other funds (other than equity, bond, mixed, real estate and hedge funds)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds that do not fall under equity, bond, mixed, real estate, or hedge fund categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other general government
str:Code.com:Description: General government units with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Other households and non-profit institutions serving households (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Includes unidentified households and non-profit institutions serving households.

str:Code.com:Name: Other insurance corporations and pension funds (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance corporations and pension funds with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Other Non-MMFs open end funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds excluding MMFs, not classified under specific categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other OFIs
str:Code.com:Description: Other financial intermediaries excluding insurance corporations and pension funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other open-end funds
str:Code.com:Description: Open-end investment funds that are not classified under specific categories such as equity, bond, or mixed funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other private financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Private financial corporations not classified under specific financial sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Other public financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Public financial corporations not classified under specific financial sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Other public non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Public non-financial corporations not classified under specific sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors
str:Code.com:Description: Broad classification for sectors not fitting traditional classifications.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors including non-financial corporations, money market funds, non-MMF investment funds, OFIs, insurance corporations, pension funds, general government or a central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Comprehensive grouping of sectors beyond traditional categories.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding central banks.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than central bank and general government
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding central banks and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than central government
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding central government units.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than financial corporations and general government
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding financial corporations and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than local government
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding local government.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than MFIs
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding monetary financial institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than MFIs and central government
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding monetary financial institutions and central government.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than MFIs and general government
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding monetary financial institutions and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than monetary authorities
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding monetary authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than social security funds
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors than state government
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding state government.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors: Non-financial corporations, Households, NPISHs
str:Code.com:Description: Group of sectors including non-financial corporations, households, and NPISHs.

str:Code.com:Name: Other sectors: Other
str:Code.com:Description: Miscellaneous or residual sectors not classified elsewhere.

str:Code.com:Name: Other than general government, clearing and settlement organisations and other financial institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Broad classification excluding specified entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Own account workers
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals operating their own business or trade without employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Payment institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Financial institutions specializing in payment processing and related services.

str:Code.com:Name: Pension funds
str:Code.com:Description: Funds that collect, manage, and invest money on behalf of individuals for retirement income.

str:Code.com:Name: Pension funds (sub-sector not identified)
str:Code.com:Description: Pension funds with unidentified sub-sector classification.

str:Code.com:Name: Post office giro
str:Code.com:Description: Postal service-related financial institutions offering giro payment services.

str:Code.com:Name: Private corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Corporations owned by private individuals or entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Private deposit-taking corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Private sector corporations engaged in deposit-taking activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Private financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations owned by private entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Private non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations owned by private entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Private sector
str:Code.com:Description: Broad category encompassing privately owned corporations and individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Public captive financial institutions and money lenders
str:Code.com:Description: Captive financial institutions and money lenders under public ownership.

str:Code.com:Name: Public corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Corporations owned and operated by the government.

str:Code.com:Name: Public deposit-taking corporations, except the central bank
str:Code.com:Description: Deposit-taking corporations under public ownership, excluding the central bank.

str:Code.com:Name: Public financial auxiliaries
str:Code.com:Description: Financial auxiliaries owned and operated by public authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Public financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations under public ownership.

str:Code.com:Name: Public financial corporations - sovereign wealth fund
str:Code.com:Description: Public financial corporations managing sovereign wealth funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Public financial corporations controlled by central government
str:Code.com:Description: Public financial corporations under the control of the central government.

str:Code.com:Name: Public financial corporations, which are part of domestic multinationals
str:Code.com:Description: Public financial corporations affiliated with domestic multinational corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Public insurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance corporations owned and operated by public authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Public money market funds
str:Code.com:Description: Money market funds under public ownership.

str:Code.com:Name: Public non-MMF investment funds
str:Code.com:Description: Non-MMF investment funds under public ownership.

str:Code.com:Name: Public non-financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations owned and operated by public authorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Public non-financial corporations controlled by central government
str:Code.com:Description: Non-financial corporations under the control of the central government.

str:Code.com:Name: Public non-financial corporations, natural resources
str:Code.com:Description: Public non-financial corporations involved in natural resource sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Public non-financial corporations, which are part of domestic multinationals
str:Code.com:Description: Public non-financial corporations affiliated with domestic multinational corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Public other financial intermediaries, except insurance corporations and pension funds
str:Code.com:Description: Publicly owned financial intermediaries other than insurance corporations and pension funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Public pension funds
str:Code.com:Description: Pension funds under public ownership.

str:Code.com:Name: Public private partnership
str:Code.com:Description: Collaboration between public authorities and private entities to finance, build, and operate projects or services.

str:Code.com:Name: Public sector
str:Code.com:Description: All entities owned and operated by government, including financial and non-financial corporations.

str:Code.com:Name: Real estate funds
str:Code.com:Description: Investment funds specializing in real estate assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Real estate markets
str:Code.com:Description: Markets dealing with buying, selling, and leasing of real estate properties.

str:Code.com:Name: Recipients of other transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals or entities receiving transfers other than pensions or property income.

str:Code.com:Name: Recipients of pensions
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals receiving retirement or disability pensions.

str:Code.com:Name: Recipients of property and transfer income
str:Code.com:Description: Broad category encompassing recipients of property income and other transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Recipients of property income
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals or entities receiving income from property or investments.

str:Code.com:Name: Reinsurance corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Corporations providing reinsurance services, which involve insuring insurance companies.

str:Code.com:Name: Resident financial intermediaries
str:Code.com:Description: Resident financial intermediaries are resident financial corporations that incur liabilities on their own account for the purpose of acquiring financial assets by engaging in financial transactions on the market.

str:Code.com:Name: Rest of the world
str:Code.com:Description: All non-resident institutional units. The rest of the world is not part of the total economy and is shown separately.

str:Code.com:Name: Sectors other than central bank, credit institutions, and general government
str:Code.com:Description: Sectors excluding central bank, credit institutions, and general government.

str:Code.com:Name: Other Sectors (BPM6)

str:Code.com:Name: Security and derivative dealers

str:Code.com:Name: Social security funds
str:Code.com:Description: Funds established to manage contributions and disbursements for social security programs.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security funds, budgetary units
str:Code.com:Description: Social security funds operating within a government budget framework.

str:Code.com:Name: Social security funds, extra-budgetary units
str:Code.com:Description: Social security funds operating outside a government budget framework.

str:Code.com:Name: Special purpose entities
str:Code.com:Description: Entities created for specific purposes, such as securitization or risk management.

str:Code.com:Name: Specialized financial corporations
str:Code.com:Description: Financial corporations offering specialized services, such as clearing houses or derivative trading.

str:Code.com:Name: State and local government excluding social security
str:Code.com:Description: Government entities at state and local levels, excluding social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: State government excluding social security
str:Code.com:Description: State-level government entities excluding social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: State government including social security
str:Code.com:Description: State-level government entities including social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: State government non-profit institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Non-profit institutions operated by state-level governments.

str:Code.com:Name: State government, budgetary units
str:Code.com:Description: State government units operating within a formal budget framework.

str:Code.com:Name: State government, extra-budgetary units
str:Code.com:Description: State government units operating outside a formal budget framework.

str:Code.com:Name: Total economy
str:Code.com:Description: The entire set of resident institutional units in an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Total economy (including SPEs)
str:Code.com:Description: Entire set of resident institutional units, explicitly including special purpose entities (SPEs).

str:Code.com:Name: Trusts, estate and agency accounts
str:Code.com:Description: Legal arrangements for managing assets on behalf of others, including trusts, estates, and agency accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Variable NAV MMFs
str:Code.com:Description: Money market funds with variable net asset value, reflecting market fluctuations in asset prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonresidents
str:Code.com:Description: All institutional units that are not resident of the reporting economy

str:Codelist.com:Name: Frequency
str:Codelist.com:Description: This code list provides a set of values indicating the "frequency" of the data (e.g. weekly, monthly, quarterly). The concept “frequency” may refer to various stages in the production process, e.g. data collection or data dissemination. For example, a time series could be disseminated at annual frequency but the underlying data are compiled monthly. The code list is applicable for all different uses of "frequency". This code list was formally adopted on 4 December 2013. More information about and supporting material for this code list and SDMX code lists in general (e.g. list of generic codes for expressing general concepts like "Total", "Unknown", etc.; syntaxes for the creation of further codes; general guidelines for the creation of SDMX code lists) can be found at this address: https://sdmx.org/?page_id=4345.
