str:Code.com:Name: Biennial
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every two years.

str:Code.com:Name: Triennial
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every three years.

str:Code.com:Name: Quadrennial
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every four years.

str:Code.com:Name: Quinquennial
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every five years.

str:Code.com:Name: Decennial
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every ten years.

str:Code.com:Name: Bidecennial
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every twenty years.

str:Code.com:Name: Tridecennial
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every thirty years.

str:Code.com:Name: Three times a year
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated three times a year.

str:Code.com:Name: Bimonthly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every two months.

str:Code.com:Name: Semimonthly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated twice a month.

str:Code.com:Name: Three times a month
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated three times a month.

str:Code.com:Name: Biweekly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every two weeks.

str:Code.com:Name: Triweekly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every three weeks.

str:Code.com:Name: Four-weekly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every four weeks.

str:Code.com:Name: Semiweekly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated twice a week.

str:Code.com:Name: Three times a week
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated three times a week.

str:Code.com:Name: Twice a day
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated twice a day.

str:Code.com:Name: Hourly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every hour.

str:Code.com:Name: Bihourly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every two hours.

str:Code.com:Name: Trihourly
str:Code.com:Description: To be used for data collected or disseminated every three hours.

str:Code.com:Name: Daily – business week
str:Code.com:Description: Similar to "daily", however there are no observations for Saturdays and Sundays (so, neither “missing values” nor “numeric values” should be provided for Saturday and Sunday)

str:Code.com:Name: Minutely
str:Code.com:Description: While N denotes "minutely", usually, there may be no observations every minute (for several series the frequency is usually "irregular" within a day/days). And though observations may be sparse (not collected or disseminated every minute), missing values do not need to be given for the minutes when no observations exist: in any case the time stamp determines when an observation is observed.

str:Code.com:Name: Irregular
str:Code.com:Description: To be used with irregular time series that stores data for a sequence of arbitrary timepoints. Irregular time series are appropriate when the data arrives unpredictably, such as when the application records every stock trade or when random events are recorded (the interval between each element can be a different length)

str:Code.com:Name: Occasional annual
str:Code.com:Description: The event occurs occasionally with an infrequent update that could span from 1 year to several years between events. It implies a survey that experiences a gap for several years prior to the next survey update (this is commonly linked to funding available to run a specific survey (i.e. health surveys), whereas a regular annual survey refers typically to ‘programs’ that are funded regularly and fall under the Statistics Act, and therefore never experience a gap)

str:Code.com:Name: Occasional monthly
str:Code.com:Description: The event occurs occasionally with an infrequent update that could span from 1 month to several months between events. It implies a survey that experiences a gap for several months prior to the next survey update.

str:Code.com:Name: Other
str:Code.com:Description: To be used when the qualitative or quantitative values that a variable takes in a data set is associated to multiple occurrences with frequency other than the already defined ones (for example every 5 hours and 32 minutes etc.)

str:Code.com:Name: Unspecified
str:Code.com:Description: To be used when a set of values are reported within a time range but not associated to sub ranges. Often this could happen in case of missing or sparse information. (Let’s say we have two observations for 2020 but we do not know if they are part of a monthly reporting or quarterly reporting)

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: To be used when the qualitative or quantitative values that a variable takes in a data set is not associated to multiple occurrences (only single occurrence exists) one can use the _Z as frequency.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Confidentiality Status
str:Codelist.com:Description: This classification provides information about the sensitivity and confidentiality status of the data. (Source:SDMX)

str:Code.com:Name: Primary confidentiality due to small counts
Secondary confidentiality set by the sender, not for publication
Not for publication, restricted for internal use only (equivalent to the code N) until the embargo time elapses; Free for publication (equivalent to the code F) after the embargo time elapses.
Primary confidentiality due to dominance by one or two units
Primary confidentiality due to data declared confidential based on other measures of concentration
Not for publication, restricted for internal use only
Primary confidentiality due to dominance by one unit
Secondary confidentiality set and managed by the receiver, not for publication
Primary confidentiality due to dominance by two units

str:Codelist.com:Name: Derivation Type
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist identifies the specific method used to derive a new data element or indicator from source data, such as raw data, IMF staff calculations, IMF staff estimates, or IMF staff projections.

str:Code.com:Name: Staff estimates using group mean imputation methods
str:Code.com:Description: Staff estimates using group mean imputation methods.

str:Code.com:Name: Staff estimates using historical imputation methods
str:Code.com:Description: Staff estimates using historical imputation methods.

str:Code.com:Name: Raw data (no derivation)
str:Code.com:Description: Non-transformed data (also called original data) refers to data in its original form, before any mathematical or statistical operations have been applied to change its values or distribution.

str:Code.com:Name: IMF Staff calculations (derived value)
str:Code.com:Description: Data that results from direct calculations or transformations performed on raw data by IMF staff. These calculations are usually straightforward and transparent, with the published numbers easily traceable back to the source data.

str:Code.com:Name: IMF Staff calculations (derived from counterpart data)
str:Code.com:Description: Data resulting from direct application of counterpart data by IMF staff to the official information reported by other countries.

str:Code.com:Name: IMF Staff estimates
str:Code.com:Description: Data derived through calculations or transformations on available source data, where some necessary information might be missing. In these cases, IMF staff uses estimates to fill in the gaps and produce the published numbers.

str:Code.com:Name: IMF Staff projections
str:Code.com:Description: Forecasts of future values for a time series, constructed using various techniques such as statistical modeling, trend analysis, and expert judgment. These projections are based on historical data, current economic conditions, and other relevant factors.

str:Code.com:Name: Mixed-type data
str:Code.com:Description: Data that combines both raw and derived data. This could occur when some components of a dataset are directly reported while others are calculated or estimated.

str:Code.com:Name: Reported official data
str:Code.com:Description: Official data reported to the IMF by country authorities without any modifications or adjustments made by IMF staff.

str:Code.com:Name: Reported official data converted to US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Official data reported to the IMF by country authorities in a country's domestic currency, which IMF staff then converts to US dollars using appropriate exchange rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjusted using Fund records
str:Code.com:Description: Official data reported to the IMF by country authorities that has that have been adjusted using internal IMF records.

str:Code.com:Name: Third party data
str:Code.com:Description: Third party data.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Balance of Payments (BOP) Adjusted from Records

str:Code.com:Name: Adjusted using IMF accounting records
str:Code.com:Description: This indicator has been adjusted using IMF accounting records received from the IMF Finance Department.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Unit of Measure
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents the standard unit used to quantify a particular data element or indicator. It encompasses various units, including currency, physical units, percentages, and other standardized measures.

str:Code.com:Name: 1000 hectares
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of area equal to 1,000 hectares (10,000,000 square meters).

str:Code.com:Name: Barrel
str:Code.com:Description: Unit of volume for crude oil and other petroleum products. Equal to 42 US gallons.

str:Code.com:Name: Barrels per day
str:Code.com:Description: Unit of measurement used in the oil and gas industry to describe the rate of production or consumption of petroleum products.

str:Code.com:Name: Basis points
str:Code.com:Description: Basis points refers to hundredths of 1 percentage point.

str:Code.com:Name: Carat
str:Code.com:Description: The carat is a unit of mass equal to 200 mg, used for measuring gemstones and pearls. Also used to indicate the proportion of gold in an alloy out of 24 parts (e.g., 18K gold is 18/24 parts gold).

str:Code.com:Name: Centimeter
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of length in the metric system, equal to one hundredth of a meter.

str:Code.com:Name: Cubic meter
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of volume equal to a cube one meter long on each side.

str:Code.com:Name: Days
str:Code.com:Description: A period of twenty-four hours as a unit of time.

str:Code.com:Name: Degrees Celsius
str:Code.com:Description: The degree Celsius (symbol: °C) can refer to a specific point on the Celsius temperature scale or to a difference or range between two temperatures.

str:Code.com:Name: Gallon
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of liquid capacity equal to 3.79 liters.

str:Code.com:Name: Gigawatt hours
str:Code.com:Description: Gigawatt hours (GWh) is a unit of energy representing one billion watt hours.

str:Code.com:Name: Hectare
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of area equal to 10,000 square meters.

str:Code.com:Name: Hours
str:Code.com:Description: A period of time equal to a twenty-fourth part of a day and night.

str:Code.com:Name: US dollar index
str:Code.com:Description: A value expressed in US dollar that is derived from an index computation.

str:Code.com:Name: Index
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical measure that expresses the relative change in a variable in the current period compared to the index reference period.

str:Code.com:Name: Kilogram
str:Code.com:Description: The base unit of mass in the International System of Units (SI).

str:Code.com:Name: Kilometer
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of length in the metric system, equal to one thousand meters.

str:Code.com:Name: Kiloton of CO₂ equivalent
str:Code.com:Description: Measurement of carbon dioxide equivalent emissions.

str:Code.com:Name: Kilowatt hour
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of energy equal to one kilowatt of power expended for one hour.

str:Code.com:Name: Liter
str:Code.com:Description: A metric unit of capacity, equal to 1,000 cubic centimeters.

str:Code.com:Name: Long ton
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of weight equal to 2,240 pounds (1,016.0 kg).

str:Code.com:Name: Magnitude (dimensionless)
str:Code.com:Description: A general unit for quantities without specific dimensions, often used for indices, ratios, or proportions.

str:Code.com:Name: Megawatt
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of power equal to one million watts.

str:Code.com:Name: Meter
str:Code.com:Description: The base unit of length in the International System of Units (SI).

str:Code.com:Name: Metric ton
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of weight equal to 1,000 kilograms (2,204.6 pounds).

str:Code.com:Name: Metric ton of CO₂
str:Code.com:Description: Measurement of carbon dioxide emissions.

str:Code.com:Name: Metric ton of CO₂ equivalent
str:Code.com:Description: A metric measure used to compare the emissions from various greenhouse gases on the basis of their global-warming potential. 1

str:Code.com:Name: Millimeter
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of length in the metric system, equal to one thousandth of a meter.

str:Code.com:Name: Millions of metric tonnes
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of weight equal to one million metric tons.

str:Code.com:Name: Months
str:Code.com:Description: Each of the twelve named periods into which a year is divided.

str:Code.com:Name: Number
str:Code.com:Description: A mathematical object used to count, measure, and label.

str:Code.com:Name: Parts per million
str:Code.com:Description: A measure of concentration, indicating one part per million of a substance in a solution.

str:Code.com:Name: Per US dollar
str:Code.com:Description: Indicates a rate or quantity relative to one US dollar.

str:Code.com:Name: Percent
str:Code.com:Description: A ratio expressed as a fraction of 100.

str:Code.com:Name: Percentage points
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between two percentages.

str:Code.com:Name: Persons
str:Code.com:Description: Individuals.

str:Code.com:Name: Pound
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of weight equal to 16 ounces (0.4536 kg).

str:Code.com:Name: Short ton
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of weight equal to 2,000 pounds (907.18 kg).

str:Code.com:Name: Square kilometer
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of area equal to a square that is one kilometer long on each side.

str:Code.com:Name: Square meter
str:Code.com:Description: The unit of area in the International System of Units (SI).

str:Code.com:Name: Thousand cubic feet
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of volume equal to one thousand cubic feet of gas.

str:Code.com:Name: Thousand cubic meters
str:Code.com:Description: A unit of volume equal to one thousand cubic meters.

str:Code.com:Name: Number of tourist nights
str:Code.com:Description: The number of nights spent by tourists at a destination.

str:Code.com:Name: Visitors
str:Code.com:Description: People visiting a person, place, or event.

str:Code.com:Name: Fine troy ounces
str:Code.com:Description: Measurement of volume in millions of troy ounces of precious metals.

str:Code.com:Name: Fine troy ounces of gold bullion
str:Code.com:Description: Volume measurement specifically for gold bullion in millions of troy ounces.

str:Code.com:Name: Fine troy ounces of unallocated gold holdings
str:Code.com:Description: Volume measurement for unallocated gold holdings in millions of troy ounces. Unallocated gold accounts are held by individuals or institutions with a claim on a certain amount of gold, but not on specific bars or coins.

str:Code.com:Name: Weeks
str:Code.com:Description: A period of seven days.

str:Code.com:Name: Years
str:Code.com:Description: A period of 12 months or 365 (or 366) days.

str:Code.com:Name: CO₂ emissions per unit of output
str:Code.com:Description: A measure of the amount of carbon dioxide emissions produced per unit of economic output.

str:Code.com:Name: Euro per kilogram
str:Code.com:Description: Price or cost in Euros per kilogram.

str:Code.com:Name: US cents per gallon
str:Code.com:Description: Price or cost in US cents per gallon.

str:Code.com:Name: US cents per kilogram
str:Code.com:Description: Price or cost in US cents per kilogram.

str:Code.com:Name: US cents per pound
str:Code.com:Description: Price or cost in US cents per pound.

str:Code.com:Name: US dollar per barrel
str:Code.com:Description: Price or cost in US dollar per barrel.

str:Code.com:Name: US dollar per bushel
str:Code.com:Description: Price or cost in US dollar per bushel.

str:Code.com:Name: US dollar per ounce of gold
str:Code.com:Description: The price of gold in US dollar per ounce.

str:Code.com:Name: US dollar per cubic meter

str:Code.com:Description: Specific rare earth carbonate material with a defined range of Rare Earth Oxide (REO) content.

str:Code.com:Name: Coarse particle, 99.2%
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to a material with a specific particle size distribution where 99.2% of the particles are larger than a certain size threshold, indicating a coarse or granular material. The exact size threshold would depend on the context and the specific material being described.

str:Code.com:Name: Battery Grade, 99%
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to a material or substance that has a purity level of 99% and is suitable for use in batteries. The term "Battery Grade" indicates that the material meets the specific quality and performance requirements for battery applications, such as electrochemical stability, high energy density, and long cycle life.

str:Code.com:Name: Cost, insurance, and freight Northwest Europe
str:Code.com:Description: Cost, Insurance, and Freight Northwest Europe (CIF NWE) is a pricing term used in the oil and petroleum industry. It indicates that the seller is responsible for the costs, insurance, and freight of delivering the product to a port in Northwest Europe assumes responsibility for the goods once they are loaded onto the ship at the port of origin. NEW likely refers to a specific geographic region or port, but without further context, it's difficult to be certain.

str:Code.com:Description: The European Currency Unit (ECU) was a basket of European currencies used as the unit of account of the European Monetary System (EMS) from 1979 to 1998, prior to the introduction of the euro in 1999. The ECU was a composite currency, meaning its value was derived from a weighted average of the currencies of the participating countries.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Balance of Payments (BOP) Asset Liability Type

str:Code.com:Name: Net acquisition of financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Net acquisition of financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net incurrence of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Net incurrence of liabilities.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Methodology
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist identifies the specific statistical methodologies and conceptual frameworks used in the collection, processing, analysis, and presentation of data within a dataset.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of Payments Statistics (BOP) Statistics
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical framework for recording and classifying international transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of Payments and International Investment Position Manual, Sixth Edition (BPM6)
str:Code.com:Description: Updated in 2009, BPM6 incorporates changes in the global economy like globalization and financial innovation. It provides a more integrated approach to balance of payments and international investment position statistics.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of Payments and International Investment Position Manual 6th Edition (BPM6): Memorandum Items
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to additional items in BPM6 that provide supplementary information.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of Payments and International Investment Position Manual 6th Edition (BPM6): Standard Component
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to the core components of the BPM6 framework.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of Payments and International Investment Position Manual 6th Edition (BPM6): Supplementary Items
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to additional items in BPM6 that provide supplementary information.

str:Code.com:Name: International Investment Position (IIP)
str:Code.com:Description: Statistics that measure a country's stock of foreign assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: International Investment Position (IIP): Standard Component
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to the core components of the IIP framework.

str:Code.com:Name: International Investment Position (IIP): Supplementary Items
str:Code.com:Description: Refers to additional items in the IIP framework that provide supplementary information.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of Payments and International Investment Position Manual, Fifth Edition (BPM5)
str:Code.com:Description: A comprehensive guide to compiling balance of payments and international investment position statistics, published in 1993. It provides a framework for recording and classifying international transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Central Product Classification (CPC)
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/classifications/unsdclassifications/cpcv21.pdf

str:Code.com:Name: Classification by Broad Economic Categories (BEC)
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/trade/classifications/bec.asp

str:Code.com:Name: Consumer Price Statistics
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical method for measuring changes in the prices of consumer goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: COICOP 1999
str:Code.com:Description: Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (COICOP) 1999; https://unstats.un.org/unsd/classifications/Econ/Download/In%20Text/CPCprov_english.pdf

str:Code.com:Name: COICOP 2018
str:Code.com:Description: Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (COICOP) 2018; https://unstats.un.org/unsd/classifications/unsdclassifications/COICOP_2018_-_pre-edited_white_cover_version_-_2018-12-26.pdf

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity Statistics
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical framework for collecting and analyzing data on the production, trade, and consumption of commodities.

str:Code.com:Name: Coordinated Direct Investment Survey Guide 2015 (CDIS 2015)
str:Code.com:Description: Provides guidance on conducting surveys of foreign direct investment, covering data collection, processing, and analysis.

str:Code.com:Name: Coordinated Portfolio Investment Survey Guide 2017 (CPIS 2017)
str:Code.com:Description: Provides guidance on conducting surveys of portfolio investment, covering data collection, processing, and analysis.

str:Code.com:Name: European System of Accounts 1995 (ESA 1995)
str:Code.com:Description: The European Union's version of the System of National Accounts, providing a framework for macroeconomic accounting within the EU.

str:Code.com:Name: European System of Accounts 2010 (ESA 2010)
str:Code.com:Description: An update to ESA 1995, aligning with SNA 2008 and incorporating changes in the European economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Exchange Rate (ER)
str:Code.com:Description: Statistical methods for determining and analyzing the price of one currency in terms of another currency.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial Access Survey Guidelines and Manual 2019 (FAS 2019)
str:Code.com:Description: Provides guidance on measuring financial access and inclusion, covering access to financial institutions and usage of financial products.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial Soundness Indicators Compilation Guide 2019 (FSI 2019)
str:Code.com:Description: Offers guidance on compiling financial soundness indicators, which assess the stability and resilience of financial systems.

str:Code.com:Name: Fund Accounts (FA)
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical framework for recording and reporting the financial activities of international financial institutions like the IMF.

str:Code.com:Name: Government Finance Statistics (GFS)
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical framework for collecting and presenting data on government revenue, expenditure, and debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Government Finance Statistics Manual 2014 (GFSM 2014)
str:Code.com:Description: This edition builds on GFSM 2001, with updates to reflect changes in public sector accounting standards and practices. It also includes new guidance on public corporations and natural resource wealth.

str:Code.com:Name: Government Finance Statistics Manual 2001 (GFSM 2001)
str:Code.com:Description: A guide for compiling government finance statistics, focusing on the accrual accounting approach. It covers revenue, expense, assets, and liabilities of government units.

str:Code.com:Name: Government Finance Statistics Manual 1986 (GFSM 1986)
str:Code.com:Description: An earlier framework for compiling government finance statistics.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonized Commodity Description and Coding Systems (HS), Rev. 5
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/tradekb/Knowledgebase/50018/Harmonized-Commodity-Description-and-Coding-Systems-HS

str:Code.com:Name: Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC)
str:Code.com:Description: Statistical and scientific methods used by the IPCC to assess climate change.

str:Code.com:Name: International Classification of Status in Employment 1993 (ICSE 1993)
str:Code.com:Description: http://www.ilo.org/global/statistics-and-databases/statistics-overview-and-topics/status-in-employment/current-guidelines/lang--en/index.htm

str:Code.com:Name: International Labor Organization (ILO)
str:Code.com:Description: Statistical standards and guidelines related to labor issues, developed by the International Labor Organization.

str:Code.com:Name: International Merchandise Trade Statistics (IMTS)
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical framework for measuring the flow of goods across international borders.

str:Code.com:Name: International Merchandise Trade Statistics: Concepts and Definitions 2010 (IMTS 2010)
str:Code.com:Description: Provides a framework for compiling international merchandise trade statistics, covering concepts, definitions, and classifications.

str:Code.com:Name: International Recommendations for Tourism Statistics 2008 (IRTS 2008)
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/tradekb/Knowledgebase/50551/IRTS-2008

str:Code.com:Name: International Reserves and Foreign Currency Liquidity: Guidelines for a Data Template 2013 (IRFCL 2013)
str:Code.com:Description: Offers guidelines for compiling data on international reserves and foreign currency liquidity, focusing on data consistency and transparency.

str:Code.com:Name: International Standard Classification of Education 2011 (ISCED 2011)
str:Code.com:Description: http://www.uis.unesco.org/Education/Pages/international-standard-classification-of-education.aspx

str:Code.com:Name: International Standard Industrial Classification (ISIC)
str:Code.com:Description: A standard classification system used in statistical analysis to categorize economic activities.

str:Code.com:Name: International Standard Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities, Rev. 3 (ISIC Rev. 3)
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/classifications/Econ/ISIC.cshtml

str:Code.com:Name: International Standard Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities, Rev. 3.1 (ISIC Rev. 3.1) 2004
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/classifications/Econ/ISIC.cshtml

str:Code.com:Name: International Standard Industrial Classification of All Economic Activities, Rev. 4 (ISIC Rev. 4) 2008
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/classifications/Econ/ISIC.cshtml

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary and Financial Statistics (MFS)
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical framework for collecting and analyzing data on money supply, credit, and financial institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary and Financial Statistics Manual and Compilation Guide 2016 (MFS 2016)
str:Code.com:Description: Provides a framework for compiling monetary and financial statistics, covering financial institutions, instruments, and markets. It aims to promote consistency and cross-country comparability.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary and Financial Statistics Manual and Compilation Guide 2016 (MFS 2016), Concepts Based on Euro Area Wide Residency
str:Code.com:Description: A version of MFS16 adapted for the specific circumstances of the Euro Area.

str:Code.com:Name: National Accounts Statistics (SNA)
str:Code.com:Description: A statistical framework for macroeconomic accounting, providing a consistent system for measuring economic activity.

str:Code.com:Name: System of National Accounts 1993 (SNA 1993)
str:Code.com:Description: A comprehensive international framework for macroeconomic accounting, providing a consistent system for measuring economic activity. It covers production, income, consumption, accumulation, and capital formation.

str:Code.com:Name: System of National Accounts 2008 (SNA 2008)
str:Code.com:Description: An update to SNA 1993 that incorporates changes in economic activity and measurement. It provides expanded guidance on financial services, globalization, and environmental-economic accounting.

str:Code.com:Name: OECD Benchmark Definition of Foreign Direct Investment (BMD4)
str:Code.com:Description: The OECD's definition of foreign direct investment, providing guidance on measuring and classifying FDI.

str:Code.com:Name: Per National Definition
str:Code.com:Description: Indicates that the definition or methodology is determined by each country's own standards.

str:Code.com:Name: Population Statistics
str:Code.com:Description: Statistical methods for collecting and analyzing data on the size, distribution, and characteristics of populations.

str:Code.com:Name: Quality Assurance (QA)
str:Code.com:Description: Statistical standards and procedures used to ensure the quality of data and statistics.

str:Code.com:Name: Standard International Trade Classification, Rev. 2 (SITC Rev. 2) 1975
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/classifications/Family/Detail/1070

str:Code.com:Name: Standard International Trade Classification, Rev. 4 (SITC Rev. 4) 2007
str:Code.com:Description: https://unstats.un.org/unsd/publications/catalogue?selectID=104

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical Classification of Economic Activities in the European Community, Rev. 2 (NACE Rev. 2) 2008
str:Code.com:Description: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/documents/3859598/5902521/KS-RA-07-015-EN.PDF https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/ramon/nomenclatures/index.cfm?TargetUrl=LST_NOM_DTL&StrNom=NACE_REV2&StrLanguageCode=EN

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical classification of products by activity 2008 (CPA 2008)
str:Code.com:Description: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/ramon/nomenclatures/index.cfm?TargetUrl=LST_NOM_DTL&StrNom=CPA_2008&StrLanguageCode=EN&IntPcKey=&StrLayoutCode=HIERARCHIC

str:Code.com:Name: Producer Price Index Manual: Theory and Practice
str:Code.com:Description: https://www.imf.org/en/Publications/Manuals-Guides/Issues/2016/12/30/Producer-Price-Index-Manual-Theory-and-Practice-16966

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary and Financial Statistics Manual and Compilation Guide 2000 (MFS 2000)
str:Code.com:Description: An earlier version of the MGS manual, providing similar guidance for compiling monetary and financial statistics.

str:Code.com:Name: International Recommendations for the Index of Industrial Production 2010
str:Code.com:Description: A global guideline for countries to uniformly measure and report industrial output changes (Index of Industrial Production - IIP), ensuring international data comparability.

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary and Financial Statistics non-standard reporting
str:Code.com:Description: The Monetary and Financial Statistics (MFS) nonstandard dataset is compiled using country-specific methodologies and terminology, rather than the standard methodology outlined in different versions of Monetary and Financial Statistics Manual.

str:Code.com:Name: Export and Import Price Index Manual: Theory and Practice 2009
str:Code.com:Description: This manual provides guidance on how to construct a price index for exports and imports. It is relevant to statistical agencies involved in the compilation of export and import price indexes. This 2009 edition updates the 1999 edition and incorporates recent methodological advances. https://www.imf.org/-/media/files/data/manuals-guides/eipim_2009_book.ashx

str:Code.com:Name: Monetary and Financial Statistics Compilation Guide 2008
str:Code.com:Description: This guide provides a framework for compiling monetary and financial statistics, offering practical advice and examples to statistical compilers. It is designed to assist countries in producing high-quality monetary and financial statistics that are comparable internationally. https://www.imf.org/-/media/Files/Data/manuals-guides/mfs-guide-2008.ashx

str:Code.com:Name: A Guide to Money and Banking Statistics in IFS 1984
str:Code.com:Description: This guide explains the methodology and concepts used in compiling money and banking statistics for the International Financial Statistics (IFS). It is relevant to users and compilers of these statistics, providing context for the data presented in the IFS.

str:Code.com:Name: International Merchandise Trade Statistics 1998 (IMTS 1998)
str:Code.com:Description: This manual provides a framework of concepts and definitions for the compilation of international merchandise trade statistics. It is relevant to national statistical authorities involved in collecting, compiling, and disseminating trade data, promoting international comparability.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance of Payments Manual, fourth edition (BPM4)
str:Code.com:Description: This manual sets forth the conceptual framework for the compilation of balance of payments statistics. It provides guidelines for statistical agencies to ensure consistency and comparability of balance of payments data across countries.

str:Code.com:Name: External Debt Statistics Guide 2013 (EDS 2013)
str:Code.com:Description: This guide provides comprehensive methodological guidance for the compilation, dissemination, and use of external debt statistics. It is relevant to compilers in national statistical agencies, central banks, and ministries of finance, as well as to users of external debt data. https://www.imf.org/-/media/Files/Data/manuals-guides/eds_guide2013.ashx

str:Code.com:Name: External Debt Statistics Guide 2003 (EDS 2003)
str:Code.com:Description: This guide provides methodological guidance for the compilation, dissemination, and use of external debt statistics. It was a predecessor to the 2013 edition, offering a framework for data collection and presentation relevant to the period. https://www.imf.org/-/media/Files/Data/manuals-guides/eds_guide2003.ashx

str:Code.com:Name: Consumer Price Index Manual 2004 (CPI 2004)
str:Code.com:Description: This manual provides guidelines for statistical offices and other agencies responsible for constructing Consumer Price Indexes (CPIs), explaining methods, underlying economic and statistical concepts, and principles. https://www.elibrary.imf.org/display/book/9789221136996/9789221136996.xml

str:Code.com:Name: Producer Price Index Manual 2004 (PPI 2004)
str:Code.com:Description: This manual provides clear, up-to-date guidance on the concepts, uses, methods, and economic theory of the Producer Price Index (PPI), including information on classifications, sources, compilation techniques, and analytical uses. It supersedes previous international guidance on PPIs. https://www.elibrary.imf.org/display/book/9781589063044/9781589063044.xml

str:Code.com:Name: Consumer Price Index Manual 2020 (CPI 2020)
str:Code.com:Description: This manual, an update of the 2004 CPI Manual, provides comprehensive information and explanations on compiling a consumer price index (CPI). Its primary purpose is to assist countries in producing CPIs that reflect internationally recommended methods and practices. https://www.elibrary.imf.org/display/book/9781484354841/9781484354841.xml

str:Code.com:Name: System of Environmental-Economic Accounting (SEEA)
str:Code.com:Description: https://seea.un.org/

str:Code.com:Name: System of National Accounts 2025 (SNA 2025)
str:Code.com:Description: The latest update to the SNA framework, reflecting new developments in the global economy, digitalization, sustainability, and well-being. It enhances guidance on globalization, financial innovation, environmental and social dimensions, and emerging data sources.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Index type
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents different types of price indices and related measures.

str:Code.com:Name: Bank stock index
str:Code.com:Description: A financial index that tracks the performance of a basket of stocks issued by banking institutions. This index provides insights into the health and performance of the banking sector, reflecting changes in stock prices of banks in response to economic, regulatory, and market conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Core consumer price index (CPI)
str:Code.com:Description: A measure of the price level of consumer goods and services, excluding volatile items such as food and energy.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity price index, energy, crude oil (petroleum)
str:Code.com:Description: The Commodity Price Index for energy, crude oil (petroleum) is a measure that tracks the average price changes of crude oil over time.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity net export price index
str:Code.com:Description: Represents the difference in the trends of export and import price indices for commodities. It is calculated by subtracting the Commodity Import Price Index from the Commodity Export Price Index. A positive value indicates that the prices of exports are increasing at a faster rate than the prices of imports, potentially leading to a favorable trade balance and contributing to economic growth.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity export price index
str:Code.com:Description: A measure that tracks the changes over time in the prices of a basket of goods exported by a country. This index provides insight into the trends of export prices for commodities such as oil, metals, and agricultural products. An increase in the index suggests that the prices of exported commodities are rising, potentially leading to higher revenues for exporters.

str:Code.com:Name: Core HICP inflation forecast
str:Code.com:Description: The Core Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) Inflation Forecast predicts the future rate of inflation, excluding volatile items such as energy, food, alcohol, and tobacco. This forecast helps in assessing underlying inflation trends and is crucial for monetary policy decisions and economic planning.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity import price index
str:Code.com:Description: This index measures the changes in the prices of a basket of goods imported into a country over time. It reflects the cost trends of imported commodities, including energy sources, raw materials, and food products. A higher index indicates increasing import prices, which may affect domestic inflation rates and the purchasing power of consumers, as well as impact the costs for businesses relying on imported goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Core PCE Price Index
str:Code.com:Description: The Core Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Price Index is a measure of inflation that tracks changes in the prices of goods and services purchased by consumers, excluding food and energy prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Consumer price index (CPI)
str:Code.com:Description: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is an index of the prices of consumption goods and services, as compared to a certain reference period.

str:Code.com:Name: Relative consumer price index (CPI)
str:Code.com:Description: The CPI of the country compared to the trade-weighted CPI of the country’s trade partners

str:Code.com:Name: Consumer price index (Urban)
str:Code.com:Description: The Consumer Price Index (Urban), often abbreviated as CPI-U, is a key economic indicator that measures the average change over time in the prices paid by urban consumers for a fixed basket of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Commodity price index
str:Code.com:Description: A commodity price index is a measurement tool that tracks the average price changes of a basket of commodities over time. These commodities can include various raw materials or primary agricultural products

str:Code.com:Name: Deflator
str:Code.com:Description: A measure used to adjust nominal values for inflation, reflecting the real value.

str:Code.com:Name: Flexible exchange market pressure index
str:Code.com:Description: Measures the pressure on a country's exchange rate in a flexible exchange rate system, taking into account factors such as changes in exchange rates, interest rates, and international reserves. It helps in identifying periods of excessive upward or downward pressure on the currency, indicating potential stability or vulnerability in the foreign exchange market. Calculated as one unit equivalent to one standard deviation of standarized variable.

str:Code.com:Name: Export price index (XPI)
str:Code.com:Description: An index that measures the price changes of goods and services sold to foreign buyers.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial stress indicators
str:Code.com:Description: Quantitative measures that signal the level of stress in the financial system. These indicators can include volatility in stock and bond markets, widening credit spreads, and increased interbank lending rates. They are used to identify periods of financial turbulence that could impact economic stability and inform policy responses. Calculated as the sum of standardized subcomponents.

str:Code.com:Name: Herfindahl index
str:Code.com:Description: A measure of market concentration and competition, calculated as the sum of the squares of the market shares of all firms in the market.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP)
str:Code.com:Description: The Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) gives comparable measures of inflations for the countries and country groups for which it is produced. It is an economic indicator that measures the change over time of the prices of consumer goods and services acquired by households. In other words, it is a set of consumer price indexes (CPIs) calculated according to a harmonized approach and a set of definitions as laid down in Regulations and Recommendations. For example, the European Union's HICP provides the official measure of consumer price inflation in the euro area for the purposes of monetary policy and the assessment of inflation convergence as required under the Maastricht criteria for accession to the euro.

str:Code.com:Name: Industrial production index (IPI)
str:Code.com:Description: Industrial Production Index (IPI) is a monthly economic indicator that measures changes in the volume of output in the industrial sector, typically comprising manufacturing, mining, and utilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Chain linked volume
str:Code.com:Description: A volume measure that adjusts for changes in price levels over time by chaining together successive periods' volume measures.

str:Code.com:Name: Chain linked volume (rebased)
str:Code.com:Description: A chain linked volume measure that has been recalibrated to a new base period.

str:Code.com:Name: Import price index
str:Code.com:Description: An index that measures the price changes of goods and services purchased from foreign sellers.

str:Code.com:Name: Nominal effective exchange rate (NEER)
str:Code.com:Description: A nominal effective exchange rate (NEER) is a measure of the value of a currency against a weighted average of several foreign currencies. The weights are derived by taking a three-year average of the trade partners' combined value of imports and exports with the focal country as a share of the focal countries' total combined value of imports and exports.

str:Code.com:Name: Price deflator
str:Code.com:Description: An economic indicator used to adjust nominal economic data series for changes in relevant price levels. Price deflators can be explicit, such as producer or consumer price indices, or implicit, such as the GDP deflator derived by dividing nominal GDP by GDP in volume terms.

str:Code.com:Name: Producer price index (PPI)
str:Code.com:Description: The Producer Price Index (PPI) is a key economic indicator that measures the average change over time in the selling prices received by domestic producers for their output.

str:Code.com:Name: Real effective exchange rate (REER)
str:Code.com:Description: A real effective exchange rate (REER) is a measure of the value of a currency against a weighted average of several foreign currencies divided by a price deflator or index of costs. The weights are derived by taking a three-year average of the trade partners' combined value of imports and exports with the focal country as a share of the focal countries' total combined value of imports and exports. An increase in REER implies that exports become more expensive and imports become cheaper; therefore, an increase indicates a loss in trade competitiveness.

str:Code.com:Name: Retail sales index
str:Code.com:Description: The Retail Sales Index (RSI) measures the monthly performance of the retail sector by tracking changes in the value of sales at retail establishments. It is an important economic indicator that reflects consumer spending and retail sector health, providing insights into economic trends and consumer behavior.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial price index
str:Code.com:Description: A composite index that tracks the movement of financial asset prices, such as stocks, bonds, and other securities. It provides insights into the overall performance of financial markets and can signal shifts in investor sentiment, economic expectations, and market conditions. Calculated as one unit equivalent to one standard deviation of standarized variable.

str:Code.com:Name: Stock returns
str:Code.com:Description: Measures the profit or loss generated on an investment in a stock over a specific period. Stock returns can be calculated based on price appreciation (capital gains) and any dividends received, offering investors a comprehensive view of the performance and profitability of their stock investments.

str:Code.com:Name: Theil index
str:Code.com:Description: A Theil index can be interpreted as a measure of redundancy, lack of diversity, isolation, segregation, inequality, non-randomness, and compressibility. In the context of diversification, the higher the index the less diversification.

str:Code.com:Name: Terms of trade (TOT)
str:Code.com:Description: The ratio of export prices to import prices, indicating the relative value of imports in terms of exports.

str:Code.com:Name: Volume index
str:Code.com:Description: Index showing the average of the proportionate changes in quantities of a specified set of goods or services between two periods.

str:Code.com:Name: Wholesale price index (WPI)
str:Code.com:Description: The Wholesale Price Index (WPI) measures changes in the purchaser prices paid by non-household institutional units

str:Code.com:Name: Price deflator (Eurostat)
str:Code.com:Description: An economic indicator used to adjust nominal economic data series for changes in relevant price levels. Price deflators can be explicit, such as producer or consumer price indices, or implicit, such as the GDP deflator derived by dividing nominal GDP by GDP in volume terms.

str:Code.com:Name: Unit value deflator
str:Code.com:Description: A price index constructed by comparing the average price of a unit of goods or services in a given period to the average price of that same unit in a base period. UVDs are often used in international trade to analyze changes in export and import prices.

str:Code.com:Name: Core producer price index (Core PPI)
str:Code.com:Description: The Producer price index (PPI) is a key economic indicator that measures the average change over time in the selling prices received by domestic producers for their output.

str:Code.com:Name: Purchasing managers index (PMI)
str:Code.com:Description: The Purchasing Managers’ Index (PMI) is a monthly survey-based economic indicator measuring changes in key business conditions—such as production, new orders, and employment—across manufacturing and services, with readings above 50 indicating expansion and below 50 signaling contraction.

str:Code.com:Name: Harmonised index of producer prices (HIPP)
str:Code.com:Description: The Harmonised Index of Producer Prices (HICP) gives comparable measures of inflations for the countries and country groups for which it is produced. It is an economic indicator that measures the change over time of the prices of producer goods and services acquired by producers. In other words, it is a set of producer price indexes (CPIs) calculated according to a harmonized approach and a set of definitions as laid down in Regulations and Recommendations.

str:Code.com:Name: Morgan Stanley MSCI stock price index
Terms of trade (TOT) Goods
str:Code.com:Description: The ratio of export of goods prices to import of goods prices, indicating the relative value of imports of goods in terms of exports of goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Price deflator (EUR)
str:Code.com:Description: An economic indicator used to adjust nominal economic data series for changes in relevant price levels. Price deflators can be explicit, such as producer or consumer price indices, or implicit, such as the GDP deflator derived by dividing nominal GDP by GDP in volume terms. Derived by taking the current price series denominated in EUR divided by the constant price series denominated in EUR.

str:Code.com:Name: Price deflator (USD)
str:Code.com:Description: An economic indicator used to adjust nominal economic data series for changes in relevant price levels. Price deflators can be explicit, such as producer or consumer price indices, or implicit, such as the GDP deflator derived by dividing nominal GDP by GDP in volume terms. Derived by taking the current price series denominated in USD divided by the constant price series denominated in USD.

str:Code.com:Name: Price deflator (SDR)
str:Code.com:Description: An economic indicator used to adjust nominal economic data series for changes in relevant price levels. Price deflators can be explicit, such as producer or consumer price indices, or implicit, such as the GDP deflator derived by dividing nominal GDP by GDP in volume terms. Derived by taking the current price series denominated in SDR divided by the constant price series denominated in SDR.

str:Code.com:Name: Production index
str:Code.com:Description: Production Index is a indicator that measures changes in the volume of output in a sector, typically comprising manufacturing, mining, and utilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Core inflation excluding food and energy
str:Code.com:Description: Core inflation excluding food and energy.

str:Code.com:Name: Core inflation including housing
str:Code.com:Description: Core inflation including housing.

str:Code.com:Name: Consumer price index (CPI) common reference period
str:Code.com:Description: The Consumer Price Index (CPI) is an index of the prices of consumption goods and services, as compared to a certain reference period.

str:Codelist.com:Name: COICOP 1999
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist represents a standardized classification system developed by the United Nations Statistics Division to categorize household consumption expenditures by purpose. It includes 14 divisions, 47 groups, and 117 classes.

str:Code.com:Name: All Items
str:Code.com:Description: All items in the context of the consumer price index indicates that the index includes prices for all items included in the consumer price index basket. This includes prices related to food, beverages, clothing, housing, health, transport, communications, recreation, education, restaurants and hotels and other miscellanoues goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Food and non-alcoholic beverages
str:Code.com:Description: Food and non-alcoholic beverages consumer price index is produced using prices related to food items and non-alcoholic beverages such as fresh produce, packaged foods, and beverages excluding alcoholic drinks, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Alcoholic beverages, tobacco and narcotics
str:Code.com:Description: Alcoholic beverages, tobacco and narcotics consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as beer, wine, spirits, cigarettes, tobacco products, and illicit drugs, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Clothing and footwear
str:Code.com:Description: Clothing and footwear consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as apparel, footwear, accessories, and related products, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels
str:Code.com:Description: Housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as housing-related services, including rent or mortgage payments, utilities such as water, electricity, gas, and other fuels, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Furnishings, household equipment and routine household maintenance
str:Code.com:Description: Furnishings, household equipment and routine household maintenance consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as furniture, appliances, household supplies, and maintenance services, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Health
str:Code.com:Description: Health consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as health-related products, including medical services, pharmaceuticals and healthcare products, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Transport
str:Code.com:Description: Transport consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as transportation-related services, including vehicle purchases, fuel, public transportation fares, vehicle maintenance, and transportation services, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Communication
str:Code.com:Description: Communication consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as communication-related services, including telephone services, internet subscriptions, postal services, and communication equipment, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Recreation and culture
str:Code.com:Description: Recreation and culture consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as recreational and cultural activities, including leisure pursuits, entertainment, hobbies, cultural events, and recreational goods and services, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Education
str:Code.com:Description: Education consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as educational services, including tuition fees, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Restaurants and hotels
str:Code.com:Description: Restaurants and hotels consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as dining out at restaurants and expenditures on accommodation and related services provided by hotels and lodging establishments, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Miscellaneous goods and services consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as a diverse range of products, including personal care products, personal services, insurance premiums, financial services, and other miscellaneous products, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of non-profit institutions serving households (NPISHs)
str:Code.com:Description: Individual consumption expenditure of non-profit institutions serving households (NPISHs) consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as payments of consumers to non-profit institutions serving households (NPISHs) for individual consumption, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Code.com:Name: Individual consumption expenditure of general government
str:Code.com:Description: Individual consumption expenditure of general government consumer price index is produced using prices related to items such as payments by consumers to general government for individual consumption, aggregated by their respective consumer expenditure weights.

str:Codelist.com:Name: Language
str:Codelist.com:Description: This codelist identifies the natural languages in which a data resource is available or presented, using standardized codes based on the ISO 639-1 standard.

str:Codelist.com:Name: International accounts item
str:Codelist.com:Description: A classification of transactions in the balance of payments, such as trade in goods, services, income, and financial flows.

str:Code.com:Name: Not applicable
str:Code.com:Description: Not applicable.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisition of foreign assets
str:Code.com:Description: The purchase or obtaining of assets located outside the domestic economy, including investments, real estate, or business entities.

str:Code.com:Name: Scheduled amortization
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to the planned repayments of principal on outstanding loans during a specific period

str:Code.com:Name: Scheduled amortization, before adjustments for exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to the planned repayments of principal on outstanding loans during a specific period. It excludes any adjustments made for exceptional financing, such as debt rescheduling or forgiveness.

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisition of other nonreserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: Purchasing assets that do not qualify as reserve assets, such as non-financial assets or financial assets not held for transaction motives or international liquidity.

str:Code.com:Name: Balance on invisibles
str:Code.com:Description: The net balance of transactions that do not result in the transfer of tangible goods, including services, primary income, and secondary income flows.

str:Code.com:Name: Current account balance
str:Code.com:Description: The current account balance is the balance of current transactions (transactions in goods and services, earned income and transfer income) between residents and non-residents. The term current account balance is used in the external accounts and is expressed from the perspective of resident units. The term current external balance is used in the national accounts and is expressed from the perspective of the non-resident units, and therefore with the opposite sign.

str:Code.com:Name: Current account balance, excluding grants
str:Code.com:Description: The current account balance is the balance of current transactions (transactions in goods and services, earned income and transfer income) between residents and non-residents. The term current account balance is used in the external accounts and is expressed from the perspective of resident units. The term current external balance is used in the national accounts and is expressed from the perspective of the non-resident units, and therefore with the opposite sign. It excludes grants.

str:Code.com:Name: Classic comparative advantage
str:Code.com:Description: A country had comparative advantage in specific service exports in 2000-04 and maintains comparative advantage in 2008-13, on average.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital and financial account balance
str:Code.com:Description: The combined net balance of the capital account and the financial account, showing the net flow of capital and financial transactions between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital and financial account balance less reserve assets
str:Code.com:Description: This measures the net flow of capital and financial transactions between a country and the rest of the world, excluding changes in the country's official reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Current and capital account balance
str:Code.com:Description: The combined balance of the current and capital accounts, reflecting the net flow of goods, services, income, and transfers, as well as capital transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers (receivables less payables)
str:Code.com:Description: The net balance of current transfers into and out of a country, reflecting the difference between receivable and payable transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers (receivables less payables) (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Officially recorded net current transfers, emphasizing government or formal transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Compensation of employees
str:Code.com:Description: Total remuneration, in cash or in kind, payable by an enterprise to an employee in return for work done by the latter during the accounting period.

str:Code.com:Name: Wages and salaries excluding employers' social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Remuneration of employees payable in cash and/or in kind, except for social contributions payable by employers.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions payable by employers to social security funds and other social insurance schemes, to secure social benefits for their employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Total contributions made by employers for social benefits for their employees, including both pension and non-pension contributions.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers actual pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers into pension schemes on behalf of their employees for future retirement benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers actual non-pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers for benefits other than pensions, such as health insurance or unemployment insurance, that are not deferred to a future date.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers imputed social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions for social benefits attributed to employers, including both pension and non-pension, not directly paid out.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers imputed pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions to pension benefits attributed to employers but not directly paid out, often representing future pension liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers imputed non-pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions for non-pension benefits attributed to employers but not directly paid out, such as the value of in-kind benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on production and imports
str:Code.com:Description: Compulsory, unrequited payments levied by government in respect of production or import of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes payable per unit of a good or service.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes and duties on imports: customs duties, traditional own resources
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes and duties on imports: customs duties, traditional own resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes and duties on imports: agricultural levies, trade with non member countries under the CAP, traditional own resources
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes and duties on imports: agricultural levies, trade with non member countries under the CAP, traditional own resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products: sugar/isoglucose production levies, traditional own resources
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on products: sugar/isoglucose production levies, traditional own resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products: involving EU institutions, traditional own resources
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on products: involving EU institutions, traditional own resources.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products: other than those involving EU institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on products: other than those involving EU institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes on production other than taxes on products that enterprises incur as a result of engaging in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production: ECSC levy
str:Code.com:Description: Other taxes on production: ECSC levy.

str:Code.com:Name: Other taxes on production: other than those involving EU institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Other taxes on production: other than those involving EU institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on products and production
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes payable per unit of a good or service and in respect of production.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies
str:Code.com:Description: Current unrequited payments that government units, including nonresident government units, make to enterprises on the basis of the levels of their production activities or the quantities or values of the goods or services that they produce, sell, export or import.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on products
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies payable per unit of a good or service.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on products: EU institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies on products: EU institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on products: agriculture and fishery
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies on products: agriculture and fishery.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on products: other than those involving EU institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies on products: other than those involving EU institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other subsidies on production excluding subsidies on products
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies other than subsidies on products that resident enterprises may receive as a consequence of engaging in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Other subsidies on production excluding subsidies on products: EU institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Other subsidies on production excluding subsidies on products: EU institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on production: agriculture and fishery
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies on production: agriculture and fishery.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on production: grants for interest relief
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies on production: grants for interest relief.

str:Code.com:Name: Subsidies on production: other excluding agriculture, fishery and grants for interest of relief
str:Code.com:Description: Subsidies on production: other excluding agriculture, fishery and grants for interest of relief.

str:Code.com:Name: Other subsidies on production excluding subsidies on products: other than those involving EU institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Other subsidies on production excluding subsidies on products: other than those involving EU institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on property income
str:Code.com:Description: Property income.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income, interest income
str:Code.com:Description: The cost of borrowing money, expressed as a percentage of the amount borrowed, paid by the borrower to the lender for the use of the lender's money.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on interest before FISIM
str:Code.com:Description: Interest before FISIM.

str:Code.com:Name: Interest: investment income
str:Code.com:Description: Form of investment income or interest-like income that is receivable by the owners of certain kinds of financial assets, namely: deposits, debt securities, loans, and other accounts receivable, and some similar instruments in the case of Islamic finance, for putting the financial asset at the disposal of another institutional unit. Income on SDR holdings and allocations is also treated as interest and similar returns. By convention, lending fees on securities, gold loans, gold swaps, and crypto assets without a corresponding liability designed to act as a general medium of exchange are classified as interest and similar returns. In the external accounts, trade credit and advances are in a separate category from other accounts receivable/payable and are also included in the list of financial assets that may accrue interest and similar returns.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on interest (lending fees on equity securities, gold loans and gold swaps)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on interest (lending fees on equity securities, gold loans and gold swaps).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations (excluding RIE and excluding IF)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations (excluding RIE and excluding IF).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on dividends
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made out of a corporation's profits to its shareholders, typically on a regular basis.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on withdrawals from income of quasi corporations (investment income)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on withdrawals from income of quasi corporations (investment income).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations (including RIE, excluding IF)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations (including RIE, excluding IF).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on dividends and withdrawals from income of quasi corporations and income distributed to investment fund shareholders (excluding RIE).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on reinvested earnings on FDI (excluding IF)
str:Code.com:Description: Reinvested earnings on foreign direct investment (excluding investment funds)

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on reinvested earnings on FDI (including IF)
str:Code.com:Description: Reinvested earnings on foreign direct investment (including investment funds)

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on other investment income (ESA)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on other investment income (ESA).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on investment income attributable to insurance policy holders
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on investment income attributable to insurance policy holders.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on income payable on pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on income payable on pension entitlements.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on income attributable to investment fund shareholders (investment income)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on income attributable to investment fund shareholders (investment income).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income, income on investment income attributable to investment fund shareholders, dividends (investment income)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income, income on investment income attributable to investment fund shareholders, dividends (investment income).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on reinvested earnings (investment income)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on reinvested earnings (investment income).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on equity and investment fund shares
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on equity and investment fund shares.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on income attributable to policy holders (insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes)
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on income attributable to policy holders (insurance, pension and standardized guarantee schemes).

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on rent
str:Code.com:Description: Income receivable by the owner of a non-produced natural resource or another non-produced nonfinancial asset (the lessor or landlord) for putting the asset at the disposal of another institutional unit (a lessee or tenant) for use in production.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on other primary income
str:Code.com:Description: Primary incomes are incomes earned by institutional units as a consequence of their involvement in processes of production or ownership of assets that may be needed for purposes of production.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on other investment income
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on other investment income.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on interest and income attributable to policy holders
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on interest and income attributable to policy holders.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment income on investment income attributable to shareholders, including income on IF, including RIE
str:Code.com:Description: Investment income on investment income attributable to shareholders, including income on IF, including RIE.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on income, wealth, etc.
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes which mainly consist of taxes on the incomes of households or profits of corporations and of taxes on wealth that are payable regularly every tax period (as distinct from capital taxes levied infrequently). In the external accounts, current taxes on income, wealth, etc., consist mainly of taxes levied on the income earned by nonresidents from the provision of their labor or financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Taxes on income
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes payable on incomes, profits, and capital gains.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current taxes excluding on income
str:Code.com:Description: Current taxes on income, wealth, etc. excluding the taxes on income, profits, and capital gains.

str:Code.com:Name: Current taxes on income, wealth, etc. of which: payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers (current taxes on income, wealth, etc.)
str:Code.com:Description: Income and wealth taxes specifically related to individuals working across borders on a seasonal or short-term basis.

str:Code.com:Name: Social contributions and benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions are actual and imputed contributions payable to social insurance schemes in order for a designated beneficiary to be entitled to receive the social benefits covered by the scheme, and social benefits are current transfers receivable by households intended to provide for the needs that arise from certain events or circumstances, for example, sickness, unemployment, retirement, housing, education or family circumstances.

str:Code.com:Name: Social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Actual and imputed contributions payable to social insurance schemes in order for a designated beneficiary to be entitled to receive the social benefits covered by the scheme.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Actual contributions payable by employers to social security funds and other social insurance schemes, to obtain entitlement to social benefits for their employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers into pension schemes on behalf of their employees for future retirement benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' actual non-pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made by employers for benefits other than pensions, such as health insurance or unemployment insurance, that are not deferred to a future date.

str:Code.com:Name: Employers' imputed social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Imputed contributions payable by employers in order to deliver non-pension social benefits paid directly by employers to their employees, ex-employees and other eligible persons, and pension benefits under a defined benefit pension scheme. It is calculated as a residual after accounting for employers' and households' actual contributions.

str:Code.com:Name: Households' actual social contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions payable on their own behalf by employees or self-employed persons, which may include persons temporarily without employment, to social insurance schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Households actual pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by households into pension schemes for future retirement benefits.

str:Code.com:Name: Households actual non-pension contributions
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by households for non-pension benefits, such as health insurance or savings plans, that are not deferred to a future date.

str:Code.com:Name: Social contributions payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: Social contributions payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers.

str:Code.com:Name: Social benefits
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers receivable by households intended to provide for the needs that arise from certain events or circumstances, for example, sickness, unemployment, retirement, housing, education, or family circumstances.

str:Code.com:Name: Other social allowances (payments of family, education, medical services and other allowances) excluding retirement and survivors' pensions
str:Code.com:Description: Other social allowances (payments of family, education, medical services and other allowances) excluding retirement and survivors' pensions.

str:Code.com:Name: Retirement and survivors' pensions
str:Code.com:Description: Retirement and survivors' pensions.

str:Code.com:Name: Social transfers in kind
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services provided to households by government and NPISHs either free or at prices that are not economically significant.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: All current transfers other than current taxes on income, wealth, etc., social contributions and social benefits, and social transfers in kind. In the external accounts, all current transfers other than personal transfers are included in other current transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Net non-life insurance premiums
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of actual non-life insurance (or reinsurance) premiums payable by policyholders to obtain insurance cover during the accounting period (actual premiums earned) and the premium supplements payable out of the investment income attributed to insurance policyholders less the service charges payable to the insurance corporation.

str:Code.com:Name: Net non-life direct insurance premiums (net of claims)
str:Code.com:Description: Net non-life direct insurance premiums (net of claims).

str:Code.com:Name: Net non-life reinsurance premiums
str:Code.com:Description: Net non-life reinsurance premiums.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonlife insurance claims
str:Code.com:Description: The amounts payable in settlement of injuries or damages that result from an event covered by the nonlife insurance (or reinsurance) policy occurring in the period for which the policy is valid. In the case of major catastrophic events, some proportion of the claims may be recorded as capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-life direct insurance claims
str:Code.com:Description: Non-life direct insurance claims.

str:Code.com:Name: Non-life reinsurance claims
