str:Code.com:Description: Non-life reinsurance claims.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers within general government
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers between different government units, such as frequently occur between central and state or local government units, and between general government and social security funds.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: Current international cooperation
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers in cash or in kind between the governments of different countries or between governments and international organizations.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: o/w exceptional financing (memo item)
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: o/w exceptional financing (memo item).

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: grants received from IMF subsidy accounts (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), secondary income
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Grants received from IMF subsidy accounts (memo item: o/w exceptional financing), secondary income.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: other intergovernmental grants (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Other intergovernmental grants (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current international cooperation involving the EU institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Current international cooperation involving the EU institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current international cooperation - European development fund
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Current international cooperation - European development fund.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current international cooperation - European social fund
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Current international cooperation - European social fund.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current international cooperation - agriculture and fish (guidance and other)
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Current international cooperation - agriculture and fish (guidance and other).

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current international cooperation - European regional development fund
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Current international cooperation - European regional development fund.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current international cooperation - European community steel and coal transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Current international cooperation - European community steel and coal transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current international cooperation - other
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Current international cooperation - other.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income: current international cooperation other
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income: Current international cooperation other.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Miscellaneous current transfers include all current transfers, in cash or in kind, other than personal transfers, current taxes on income, wealth, etc.; social contributions less service charges and social benefits; nonlife insurance premiums less service charges and nonlife insurance claims; and current international cooperation.

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers to NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers directed towards Non-Profit Institutions Serving Households.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal transfers (current transfers between resident and nonresident households)
str:Code.com:Description: Personal transfers (current transfers between resident and nonresident households).

str:Code.com:Name: Personal transfers other than workers' remittances
str:Code.com:Description: Personal transfers other than workers' remittances.

str:Code.com:Name: Workers' remittances
str:Code.com:Description: Workers' remittances.

str:Code.com:Name: Other miscellaneous current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Fines and penalties, payments (after deduction of service charge and donations) and receipts related to lotteries and gambling, and payments for compensation

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers to NPISHs: miscellaneous current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Various current transfers to NPISHs, encompassing a range of activities and purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Value added tax and gross national income-based European Union own resources
str:Code.com:Description: Value added tax and gross national income-based European Union own resources.

str:Code.com:Name: VAT-based third EU own resource
str:Code.com:Description: VAT-based third EU own resource.

str:Code.com:Name: GNI-based fourth EU own resource
str:Code.com:Description: Contributions made by EU member states to the EU budget based on a percentage of their Gross National Income (GNI).

str:Code.com:Name: Current transfers of which UK rebate
str:Code.com:Description: Particular current transfers related to the UK's rebate from the EU budget.

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous non-tax contribution of the government to the institutions of the EU
str:Code.com:Description: Miscellaneous non-tax contribution of the government to the institutions of the EU.

str:Code.com:Name: Current international cooperation, miscellaneous current transfers (including in the EU VAT and GNI-based own resources)
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers related to international cooperation and miscellaneous activities, including contributions based on VAT and GNI in the EU context.

str:Code.com:Name: All miscellaneous current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Current transfers that do not fit into the regular categories of the current account, encompassing a variety of one-time or irregular transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current transfers: official
str:Code.com:Description: These are transfers between governments that are not related to trade or investment.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current transfers: private
str:Code.com:Description: These are transfers between individuals or private entities in different countries. This can include remittances (money sent by migrant workers to their home countries), gifts, and inheritances.

str:Code.com:Name: Adjustment for change in pension entitlements
str:Code.com:Description: Modifications made to the accounting records to reflect changes in the value or amount of pension benefits that individuals are entitled to receive.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Unrequited transfers, either in cash or in kind, linked to the acquisition, disposal or transfer of an asset (other than cash or inventories); or where a liability is forgiven or assumed; or where the transfers are intended to address accumulated losses incurred over a multi-year period.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital taxes
str:Code.com:Description: Taxes charged at irregular and infrequent intervals on the values of the assets or net worth owned by institutional units or on the values of assets transferred between institutional units as a result of legacies, gifts inter vivos, or other transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment grants
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers made by governments or international organizations to other institutional units to finance all or part of the costs of their acquiring nonfinancial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment grants involving EU Institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Investment grants involving EU Institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Agriculture and fishery grants (EAGGF guidance and others)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial support provided to the agriculture and fisheries sectors, including those from the European Agricultural Guidance and Guarantee Fund (EAGGF) and other sources.

str:Code.com:Name: European regional development fund - grants
str:Code.com:Description: Grants provided by the European Union aimed at strengthening economic and social cohesion by correcting imbalances between its regions.

str:Code.com:Name: European development fund: EU Commission capital transfers to third countries
str:Code.com:Description: Financial contributions from the EU Commission to non-EU countries for development purposes, funded by member states.

str:Code.com:Name: Cohesion funds
str:Code.com:Description: EU funds aimed at reducing economic and social disparities and promoting sustainable development across regions.

str:Code.com:Name: Investment grants: other
str:Code.com:Description: Investment grants: other.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers
str:Code.com:Description: All capital transfers except capital taxes, investment grants, and debt forgiveness.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers: debt forgiveness
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers: debt forgiveness.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt forgiveness (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The cancellation of debt, noted separately when associated with special financing conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt forgiveness granted by the IMF (other capital transfers)
str:Code.com:Description: Specific debt forgiveness actions taken by the International Monetary Fund as part of broader capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt forgiveness, net non-debt-creating inflows (other capital transfers)
str:Code.com:Description: Forgiveness of debt that does not create new debt obligations, categorized within other capital transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers: excluding debt forgiveness
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers: excluding debt forgiveness

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers involving EU Institutions
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers involving EU Institutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers: agriculture and fishery (EAGGF guidance and others)
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers: agriculture and fishery (EAGGF guidance and others).

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers: other
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers: other.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers: migrant transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers: migrant transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Other capital transfers: other
str:Code.com:Description: Other capital transfers: other.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers grants
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers that do not need to be repaid and are intended for financing specific capital projects or for capital assistance.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers between households
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers of capital among households, documented separately for detailed accounting.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers to NPISH
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers directed towards Non-Profit Institutions Serving Households, noted separately for accounting clarity.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers other than debt forgiveness
str:Code.com:Description: Transfers related to the exchange of financial or physical assets, excluding those categorized as forgiveness of debt.

str:Code.com:Name: Other investment grants (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Other investment grants (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Capital transfers (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Capital transfers noted separately when they are part of unusual or exceptional financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Grants received from IMF subsidy accounts (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Financial grants received from the International Monetary Fund, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Disappearing Comparative Advantage
str:Code.com:Description: Disappearing Comparative Advantage.

str:Code.com:Name: A generalized measure of diversity of service exporters
str:Code.com:Description: Average ubiquity of a given service export (service specific measure).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross external borrowing
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of funds borrowed from foreign sources by a country or its residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Emerging Comparative Advantage
str:Code.com:Description: Emerging Comparative Advantage.

str:Code.com:Name: Net errors and omissions
str:Code.com:Description: Stemming from imperfections in source data and compilation. In the balance of payments, it is used to show the difference between net lending/net borrowing derived from the financial account and net lending/net borrowing from the current and capital accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical discrepancy
str:Code.com:Description: Statistical imbalances stemming from imperfections in source data and compilation. In the balance of payments, it is used to show the difference between net lending/net borrowing derived from the financial account and net lending/net borrowing from the current and capital accounts.

str:Code.com:Name: Statistical discrepancy between changes and stocks of reserves
str:Code.com:Description: Statistical discrepancy between changes and stocks of reserves.

str:Code.com:Name: Exchange rates
str:Code.com:Description: The price of one currency in terms of another, used in converting from one currency to another in international transactions.

str:Code.com:Name: Estimated level of reserves
str:Code.com:Description: The projected amount of a country's international reserves, including foreign currency, gold, and other assets held by the central bank to back liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial account balance
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the net acquisition of financial assets and the net incurrence of liabilities resulting from transactions between residents and nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Change in foreign balances of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Change in foreign balances of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt service charges: amortization paid
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made to cover the amortization portion of debt service charges.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt service charges: amortization paid, official
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made to cover the amortization portion of debt service charges.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt service charges: interest paid
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made to cover the interest components of debt service charges.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt service charges: interest and principal paid
str:Code.com:Description: Payments made to cover both the interest and principal components of debt service charges.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt service charges: paid
str:Code.com:Description: Total payments made to cover all components of debt service charges.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt, Amortization paid
str:Code.com:Description: External debt, Amortization paid.

str:Code.com:Name: Total exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Total exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Equity investment associated with debt reduction (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Equity investments made in the context of reducing or restructuring a country's debt, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Drawings on new loans/Issues of new securities by authorities or by other sectors on behalf of authorities (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: New borrowings or securities issuances by authorities, or on their behalf by other sectors, noted separately in the context of special financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Prepayment/buyback (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Prepayment/buyback (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Rescheduling of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Rescheduling of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Rescheduling of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Rescheduling of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Rescheduling of arrears on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Rescheduling of arrears on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The total sum of all overdue payments including principal, interest, and penalty interest, specifically highlighted when part of unique financing solutions.

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of principal payments that have fallen due but remain unpaid, separately recorded in the context of unusual financial arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears on original interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The build-up of overdue payments on the initially agreed interest or coupon payments, noted separately when related to extraordinary financing measures.

str:Code.com:Name: Accumulation of arrears on penalty interest (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The accumulation of overdue payments specifically on penalty interest charges, distinguished when associated with exceptional financing conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Repayment of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Repayment of arrears: total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Repayment of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Repayment of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Repayment of arrears on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Repayment of arrears on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: Rescheduling of payments due in current reporting period, on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing).

str:Code.com:Name: Cancellation of arrears total (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The total elimination of all types of overdue payments, distinguished when occurring under unique financing conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Cancellation of arrears on principal (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The formal elimination of overdue principal payments, specifically identified in the context of extraordinary financial measures.

str:Code.com:Name: Cancellation of arrears on interest/coupon (memo item: o/w exceptional financing)
str:Code.com:Description: The formal elimination of overdue interest or coupon payments, noted separately when part of special financing arrangements.

str:Code.com:Name: Arrears not in exceptional financing (supplementary item: o/w)
str:Code.com:Description: Overdue payments that are not part of any extraordinary financing arrangements, listed separately for clarity.

str:Code.com:Name: Exceptional financing: arrears
str:Code.com:Description: Financing received or recorded when a country falls behind on its payment obligations, part of special arrangements to address payment difficulties.

str:Code.com:Name: Exceptional financing: other
str:Code.com:Description: Other forms of financing that do not fit standard categories and are provided under special conditions to address financial needs.

str:Code.com:Name: Exceptional financing: rescheduling
str:Code.com:Description: The process of renegotiating the terms of debt repayment, often involving extending payment periods, under special financing conditions.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial flows
str:Code.com:Description: Movements of capital across borders in the form of investments, loans, or other transactions affecting a country's balance of payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial flows (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Government-related financial transactions across borders, including aid, grants, and loans from official sources.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to nonresidents by residents of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt service charges reflect the amortization (interest and regular payments) and principle payments related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, amortization
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt amortization charges reflect the amortization (interest and regular payments) charges related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, amortization (official)
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of principal repayments (amortization) due on external debt, as recorded by official sources.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, amortization (private)
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of principal repayments (amortization) due on external debt, as recorded by private entities.

str:Code.com:Name: External debt: total debt service, interest
str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt interest charges reflect the interest payments related to gross external debt during the accounting period. Gross external debt reflects liabilities that require payment(s) of interest and/or principal by the debtor at some point(s) in the future and that are owed to non-residents by residents of an economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Factor income
str:Code.com:Description: Income earned from the factors of production, including wages for labor, interest for capital, rent for land, and profits for entrepreneurship.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods
str:Code.com:Description: Physical, produced objects, over which ownership rights can be established and whose ownership can be transferred from one institutional unit to another by engaging in transactions on markets.

str:Code.com:Name: General merchandise on a balance of payments basis
str:Code.com:Description: Goods whose economic ownership is changed between a resident and a nonresident and that are not included in the following specific categories: goods under merchanting, nonmonetary gold, and parts of travel, construction, and government goods and services n.i.e.

str:Code.com:Name: General merchandise on a balance of payments basis, re-exports
str:Code.com:Description: Goods that are imported into a country and then exported again without significant modification, recorded in the balance of payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Net exports of goods under merchanting
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between sales over purchases of goods for merchanting is shown as the item “net exports of goods under merchanting.” This item includes merchants’ margins, holding gains and losses, and changes in inventories of goods under merchanting. As a result of losses or increases in inventories, net exports of goods under merchanting may be negative in some cases.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods acquired under merchanting
str:Code.com:Description: Goods purchased by a resident of one country from a resident of another country and sold to a third country without entering the merchant's country.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods sold under merchanting
str:Code.com:Description: Goods purchased and then sold by a merchant without the goods entering the merchant's country.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonmonetary gold
str:Code.com:Description: Gold bullion not held as reserve assets. It is a nonfinancial asset. In the national accounts, stocks and flows of nonmonetary gold are recorded in the capital account. In the external accounts, cross-border transactions are recorded in the goods account under nonmonetary gold while stocks are not recorded.

str:Code.com:Name: Branding - quasi-transit trade adjustment
str:Code.com:Description: Adjustments made to trade figures to account for goods that pass through a country (transit trade) but are also branded or otherwise modified, attributing some economic value to the transit country.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods balance
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the value of a country's exports and imports of goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods balance: oil related products
str:Code.com:Description: This refers to the difference between the value of exports and imports of goods that are directly related to oil, such as crude oil, refined petroleum products, and petrochemicals.

str:Code.com:Name: Gas and condensates exports
str:Code.com:Description: The export of natural gas and condensates (light liquid hydrocarbons recovered from gas fields) to other countries.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross external financing
str:Code.com:Description: The total amount of financing a country receives from foreign sources, including loans, investments, and aid.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross external financing needs
str:Code.com:Description: Gross external financing needs.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross international reserves
str:Code.com:Description: The total holdings of a country's central bank in foreign currencies, gold, and other reserve assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross official reserves
str:Code.com:Description: The official holdings of a country's central bank in foreign currencies, gold, and other assets designated to back the country's liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Repair on goods
str:Code.com:Description: Repair on goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: Goods are physical, produced objects, over which ownership rights can be established and whose ownership can be transferred from one institutional unit to another by engaging in transactions on markets. Services result from a production activity that changes the conditions of the consuming units, or facilitates the exchange of products or financial assets. Services are not generally separate items over which ownership rights can be established and cannot generally be separated from their production.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods and services balance
str:Code.com:Description: The net balance of a country's exports and imports of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods, services and primary income
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of a country's balance of trade in goods and services plus net income from abroad.

str:Code.com:Name: International investment position
str:Code.com:Description: Statement that shows at a particular point in time the value and composition of financial assets of residents of an economy that are claims on nonresidents or are gold bullion held as reserve assets and of the liabilities of residents of an economy to nonresidents.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary plus secondary income
str:Code.com:Description: Primary incomes are incomes earned by institutional units as a consequence of their involvement in processes of production or ownership of assets that may be needed for purposes of production. Secondary incomes are incomes arising from the distribution of income between institutional units via current transfers. In the context of the external accounts, this account records current transfers between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary income
str:Code.com:Description: Primary incomes are incomes earned by institutional units as a consequence of their involvement in processes of production or ownership of assets that may be needed for purposes of production.

str:Code.com:Name: Primary income balance
str:Code.com:Description: Total value of the earned incomes receivable by an institutional unit or sector less the total of the earned incomes payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary incomes are incomes arising from the distribution of income between institutional units via current transfers. In the context of the external accounts, this account records current transfers between residents and non-residents.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income, current transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income, current transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Other current transfers excluding personal transfers
str:Code.com:Description: Other current transfers excluding personal transfers.

str:Code.com:Name: Secondary income balance
str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income balance.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital account balance
str:Code.com:Description: The difference between the sum of disposals of nonproduced nonfinancial assets and capital transfers receivable and the sum of acquisitions of nonproduced nonfinancial assets and capital transfers payable.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital account total
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of all transactions recorded in the capital account, reflecting capital transfers and acquisitions/disposals of non-produced, non-financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Capital account balance, excluding reserves and related items
str:Code.com:Description: Capital account balance, excluding reserves and related items.

str:Code.com:Name: Liabilities to official creditors
str:Code.com:Description: Liabilities to official creditors.

str:Code.com:Name: Marginal Comparative Advantage
str:Code.com:Description: A country never had comparative advantage in exporting the given service in the period 2000-13.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: Changes in the economic ownership of goods from nonresidents to residents of the compiling economy, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: The import (purchase or barter) of physical, produced objects (goods) and services over which ownership rights can be established and whose ownership can be transferred from one institutional unit to another by engaging in transactions on markets by residents in one territory to residents in another territory. Imports reflect a change in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Imports of services
str:Code.com:Description: Services provided by nonresidents to residents of the compiling economy.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external borrowing
str:Code.com:Description: Net external borrowing.

str:Code.com:Name: Net external financing
str:Code.com:Description: Net external financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Net acquisition of financial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Net acquisition of financial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Net acquisition of financial assets (Official)
str:Code.com:Description: Net acquisition of financial assets (Official).

str:Code.com:Name: Net acquisition of financial assets (Unofficial)
str:Code.com:Description: Net acquisition of financial assets (Unofficial).

str:Code.com:Name: Net international investment position (NIIP)
str:Code.com:Description: The net balance of a country's external financial assets and liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Net incurrence of liabilities
str:Code.com:Description: Net incurrence of liabilities.

str:Code.com:Name: Net incurrence of liabilities (nonofficial)
str:Code.com:Description: Net incurrence of liabilities (nonofficial).

str:Code.com:Name: Net incurrence of liabilities (official)
str:Code.com:Description: Net incurrence of liabilities (official).

str:Code.com:Name: Net lending (+) / net borrowing (-)
str:Code.com:Description: Net lending is the difference between changes in net worth due to saving and capital transfers and net acquisitions of non-financial assets (acquisitions less disposals of non-financial assets, less depreciation). If the amount is negative, it represents net borrowing. In the external accounts, it represents the sum of the balances on the current and capital accounts. This is conceptually equal to the net balance of the financial account. If the balance is positive, it is called net lending, implying that in net terms, the economy supplies funds to the rest of the world. If the balance is negative, it is called net borrowing, implying that in net terms, the economy receives funds from the rest of the world.

str:Code.com:Name: Number of observations per country
str:Code.com:Description: Number of Observations per country.

str:Code.com:Name: Nonproduced nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Nonproduced nonfinancial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: Observed level of diversification of a country service exports
str:Code.com:Description: Diversity of a country's service exports.

str:Code.com:Name: Observed number of countries exporting the service export sector
str:Code.com:Description: Average number of countries exporting a given service (service specific measure).

str:Code.com:Name: Other transactions on capital account
str:Code.com:Description: Other transactions on capital account.

str:Code.com:Name: Private current transfers (receivables less payables)
str:Code.com:Description: Private current transfers (receivables less payables).

str:Code.com:Name: Private financial flows
str:Code.com:Description: Private financial flows.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal remittances (supplementary item)
str:Code.com:Description: Personal remittances (supplementary item).

str:Code.com:Name: Supplementary item: workers remittances and compensation of employees
str:Code.com:Description: Supplementary item: workers remittances and compensation of employees.

str:Code.com:Name: Total remittances (supplementary item)
str:Code.com:Description: Total remittances (supplementary item).

str:Code.com:Name: Total remittances and current transfers to NPISHs (supplementary item)
str:Code.com:Description: Total remittances and current transfers to NPISHs (supplementary item).

str:Code.com:Name: Total remittances and transfers (supplementary item)
str:Code.com:Description: Total remittances and transfers (supplementary item).

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: principal and accrued interest/coupon
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: principal and accrued interest/coupon.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: instrument subject to margin
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: instrument subject to margin.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: principal
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: principal.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: accrued interest/coupon
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: accrued interest/coupon.

str:Code.com:Name: Accrued interest and amortization
str:Code.com:Description: Interest that has been incurred but not yet paid, and the gradual reduction of debt or the allocation of the cost of an intangible asset over a period.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: short/long positions
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: short/long positions.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: calls
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: calls.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: puts
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: puts.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: collateral guarantees
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: collateral guarantees.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: liabilities other than on collateral guarantees
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: liabilities other than on collateral guarantees.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: in-the-money options, at current exchange rates
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: in-the-money options, at current exchange rates.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: in-the-money options, + 5 % (depreciation of 5%)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: in-the-money options, + 5 % (depreciation of 5%).

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: in-the-money options, + 5 % (appreciation of 5%)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: in-the-money options, + 5 % (appreciation of 5%).

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: in-the-money options, + 10 % (depreciation of 10%)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: in-the-money options, + 10 % (depreciation of 10%).

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: in-the-money options, + 10 % (appreciation of 10%)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: in-the-money options, + 10 % (appreciation of 10%).

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: in-the-money options, other
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: in-the-money options, other.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: instrument indexed to the exchange rate
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: instrument indexed to the exchange rate.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: instrument denominated in foreign currency and settled by other means (e.g., in domestic currency)
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: instrument denominated in foreign currency and settled by other means (e.g., in domestic currency).

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: pledged instruments
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: pledged instruments.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: instrument lent and on repo
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: instrument lent and on repo.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: instrument lent or on repo and included in section I
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: instrument lent or on repo and included in section I.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: instrument lent or on repo but not included in section I
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: instrument lent or on repo but not included in section I.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: instrument borrowed or acquired and included in section I
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: instrument borrowed or acquired and included in Section I.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves template: instrument borrowed or acquired but not included in section I
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves template: instrument borrowed or acquired but not included in Section I.

str:Code.com:Name: Services
str:Code.com:Description: Results from a production activity that changes the conditions of the consuming units, or facilitates the exchange of products, nonproduced nonfinancial assets or financial assets. Services are not generally separate items over which ownership rights can be established and cannot generally be separated from their production.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: audio-visual transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Extended Balance of Payments Services classification for transactions related to audio-visual services.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: audio-visual transactions; of which: C.1.1 licenses to use audio-visual products
str:Code.com:Description: Specific category within EBOPS for transactions involving licenses to use audio-visual products.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: cultural transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Extended Balance of Payments Services classification for transactions related to cultural services and products.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: computer software transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Extended Balance of Payments Services classification for transactions related to computer software.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: computer software transactions; of which: C.3.1 licenses to use computer software products
str:Code.com:Description: Specific category within EBOPS for transactions involving licenses to use computer software products.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: call-center services
str:Code.com:Description: Extended Balance of Payments Services classification for transactions related to call-center services.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: total trade-related transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Sum of all transactions classified under trade-related services in the Extended Balance of Payments Services system.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: environmental transactions
str:Code.com:Description: Extended Balance of Payments Services classification for transactions related to environmental services and activities.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: total health services
str:Code.com:Description: Sum of all transactions classified under health services in the Extended Balance of Payments Services system.

str:Code.com:Name: EBOPS 2010 complementary groupings: total education services
str:Code.com:Description: Sum of all transactions classified under education services in the Extended Balance of Payments Services system.

str:Code.com:Name: Manufacturing services on physical inputs owned by others
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to processing, assembly, labelling, packing and so forth undertaken by enterprises that do not own the goods concerned.

str:Code.com:Name: Manufacturing services on physical inputs owned by others, other
str:Code.com:Description: Manufacturing services on physical inputs owned by others, other.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods for processing: goods returned (CR), goods received (DE)
str:Code.com:Description: General term for goods sent to or received from another country for processing, with "CR" indicating credits and "DE" indicating debits.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods for processing in reporting economy
str:Code.com:Description: Goods received by a country for processing and then returned to the original country, recorded in the balance of payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods for processing abroad
str:Code.com:Description: Goods sent from one country to another for processing and then returned to the original country, recorded in the balance of payments.

str:Code.com:Name: Maintenance and repair services n.i.e.
str:Code.com:Description: Maintenance and repair work by residents on goods that are owned by nonresidents (and vice versa)

str:Code.com:Name: Transport
str:Code.com:Description: The process of carriage of people and objects from one location to another as well as related supporting and auxiliary services. This includes postal and courier services.

str:Code.com:Name: Sea transport
str:Code.com:Description: Sea transport.

str:Code.com:Name: Sea transport; passenger
str:Code.com:Description: Sea transport; passenger.

str:Code.com:Name: Sea transport; Passenger; Of which: Payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: Sea transport; Passenger; Of which: Payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers.

str:Code.com:Name: Sea transport; freight
str:Code.com:Description: Sea transport; freight.

str:Code.com:Name: Sea transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Sea transport; other than passenger and freight.

str:Code.com:Name: Air transport
str:Code.com:Description: The conveyance of passengers, goods, or mail by aircraft.

str:Code.com:Name: Air transport; passenger
str:Code.com:Description: The segment of air transport services specifically focused on carrying passengers.

str:Code.com:Name: Air transport; Passenger; Of which: Payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: Air transport; Passenger; Of which: Payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers

str:Code.com:Name: Air transport; freight
str:Code.com:Description: The specific segment of air transport services dedicated to the movement of goods and cargo by air.

str:Code.com:Name: Air transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Air transport services not directly related to carrying passengers or freight, such as aerial surveying, firefighting, and training flights.

str:Code.com:Name: Other modes of transport
str:Code.com:Description: Other modes of transport.

str:Code.com:Name: Other modes of transport; passenger
str:Code.com:Description: Other modes of transport; passenger.

str:Code.com:Name: Other modes of transport; passenger; payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: Other modes of transport; passenger; payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers.

str:Code.com:Name: Other modes of transport; freight
str:Code.com:Description: Other modes of transport; freight.

str:Code.com:Name: Other modes of transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Other modes of transport; other than passenger and freight.

str:Code.com:Name: Space transport
str:Code.com:Description: Space transport.

str:Code.com:Name: Rail transport
str:Code.com:Description: Rail transport.

str:Code.com:Name: Rail transport; passenger
str:Code.com:Description: Rail transport; passenger.

str:Code.com:Name: Rail transport; freight
str:Code.com:Description: Rail transport; freight.

str:Code.com:Name: Rail transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Rail transport; other than passenger and freight.

str:Code.com:Name: Road transport
str:Code.com:Description: Road transport.

str:Code.com:Name: Road transport; passenger
str:Code.com:Description: Road transport; passenger.

str:Code.com:Name: Road transport; freight
str:Code.com:Description: Road transport; freight.

str:Code.com:Name: Road transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Road transport; other than passenger and freight.

str:Code.com:Name: Inland waterway transport
str:Code.com:Description: The movement of goods and passengers using rivers, lakes, and canals as transportation routes.

str:Code.com:Name: Inland waterway transport; passenger
str:Code.com:Description: The segment of inland waterway transport specifically focused on carrying passengers.

str:Code.com:Name: Inland waterway transport; freight
str:Code.com:Description: The specific segment of inland waterway transport dedicated to the movement of goods and cargo.

str:Code.com:Name: Inland waterway transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Inland waterway services not directly related to carrying passengers or freight, such as rental services or maintenance.

str:Code.com:Name: Pipeline transport
str:Code.com:Description: Pipeline transport.

str:Code.com:Name: Electricity transmission
str:Code.com:Description: The process of transferring electricity from power generation sites to substations and then to consumers through high-voltage power lines.

str:Code.com:Name: Other supporting and auxiliary transport services
str:Code.com:Description: Other supporting and auxiliary transport services.

str:Code.com:Name: Postal and courier services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to pick-up, transport, and delivery of letters, newspapers, periodicals, brochures, other printed matter, parcels, and packages. It also includes post office counter services such as sales of stamps and money orders, poste restante services, telegram services, and mailbox rental services.

str:Code.com:Name: Services: postal services (memo item)
str:Code.com:Description: Services: postal services (memo item).

str:Code.com:Name: Services: courier services (memo item)
str:Code.com:Description: Services: courier services (memo item).

str:Code.com:Name: All modes of transport; passenger
str:Code.com:Description: The conveyance of passengers using any form of transportation, including air, sea, rail, and road.

str:Code.com:Name: All modes of transport; Passenger; Of which: payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: All modes of transport; Passenger; Of which: payable by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers

str:Code.com:Name: All modes of transport; freight
str:Code.com:Description: The movement of goods and cargo using any available form of transportation, including air, sea, rail, and road.

str:Code.com:Name: All modes of transport; other than passenger and freight
str:Code.com:Description: Services provided by the transportation sector that do not involve the direct movement of passengers or goods, such as rental services or maintenance.

str:Code.com:Name: All modes of transport; other than passenger and freight; other than postal and courier services
str:Code.com:Description: Transportation services excluding the transport of passengers, goods, and also excluding postal and courier activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Transport, Other
str:Code.com:Description: Transport, Other.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel
str:Code.com:Description: Goods and services for own use or to give away acquired from an economy by nonresidents during visits to that economy, or acquired from other economies by residents during visits to these other economies.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel services; goods
str:Code.com:Description: Goods for own use or to give away acquired from an economy by nonresidents during visits to that economy, or acquired from other economies by residents during visits to these other economies.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; local transport services
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; local transport services.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; accommodation services
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; accommodation services.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; food-serving services
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; food-serving services.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel services, than goods, local transport services, accommodation services, and food-serving services
str:Code.com:Description: Travel services, than goods, local transport services, accommodation services, and food-serving services.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel services, Both Business and Personal Travel, Other Services, of which education services
str:Code.com:Description: Travel services, Both Business and Personal Travel, Other Services, of which education services

str:Code.com:Name: Travel services; health services
str:Code.com:Description: Travel services; health services.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; business
str:Code.com:Description: Business travel covers goods and services acquired for personal use by persons whose primary purpose of travel is for business.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; business; acquisition of goods and services by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; business; acquisition of goods and services by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; business; other than acquisition of goods and services by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; business; other than acquisition of goods and services by border, seasonal, and other short-term workers.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; personal
str:Code.com:Description: Personal travel covers goods and services acquired by persons going abroad for purposes other than business, such as vacations, shopping, participation in recreational and cultural activities, visits with friends and relatives, pilgrimage, and education- and health-related purposes.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; personal; health-related
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; personal; health-related.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; personal; education-related
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; personal; education-related.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel; personal; other than heath-related and education-related
str:Code.com:Description: Travel; personal; other than heath-related and education-related.

str:Code.com:Name: Tourism-related services in travel and passenger transport
str:Code.com:Description: Tourism-related services in travel and passenger transport.

str:Code.com:Name: Tourism receipts
str:Code.com:Description: Part of services in the current account.

str:Code.com:Name: Construction
str:Code.com:Description: Creation, renovation, repair, or extension of nonfinancial assets in the form of buildings, land improvements of an engineering nature, and other such engineering constructions as roads, bridges, dams, and so forth. It also includes related installation and assembly work; site preparation and general construction as well as specialized services such as painting, plumbing and demolition; and management of construction projects.

str:Code.com:Name: Construction abroad
str:Code.com:Description: Construction abroad (less than one year) consists of:(a) construction work for nonresidents by enterprises resident in the compiling economy (credits/revenues), and (b) the goods and services acquired from the economy in which the construction activity is being undertaken by these enterprises (debits/expenditures)

str:Code.com:Name: Construction in the reporting economy
str:Code.com:Description: Construction in the compiling economy (less than one year) consists of (a) construction work for residents of the compiling economy by nonresident construction enterprises (debits/expenditures), and (b) the goods and services acquired in the compiling economy from resident enterprises by these nonresident construction enterprises (credits/revenues)

str:Code.com:Name: Services balance
str:Code.com:Description: Services balance.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance and pension services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to providing life insurance and annuities, nonlife insurance, reinsurance, pensions, standardized guarantees and auxiliary services to insurance, pension schemes, and standardized guarantee schemes.

str:Code.com:Name: Direct insurance
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance where a policy is issued by an insurance corporation to another type of institutional unit, to be clearly distinguished from reinsurance. There are two main types of direct insurance: life insurance and nonlife insurance.

str:Code.com:Name: Life insurance
str:Code.com:Description: Activity whereby a policyholder makes regular payments to an insurer in return for which the insurer guarantees to provide the policyholder (or in some cases another nominated person) with an agreed sum, or an annuity, at a given date or earlier if the policyholder dies beforehand. It excludes term life insurance.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross life insurance premiums receivable (credits) and payable (debits)
str:Code.com:Description: Total premiums related to life insurance that are receivable (credits) or payable (debits).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross life insurance claims receivable (credits) and payable (debits)
str:Code.com:Description: Total claims related to life insurance that are receivable (credits) or payable (debits).

str:Code.com:Name: Freight insurance
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance coverage for the loss of or damage to goods while they are being transported.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross freight insurance premiums receivable (credits) and payable (debits)
str:Code.com:Description: Total premiums related to freight insurance that are receivable (credits) or payable (debits).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross freight insurance claims receivable (credits) and payable (debits)
str:Code.com:Description: Total claims related to freight insurance that are receivable (credits) or payable (debits).

str:Code.com:Name: Direct insurance other than life and freight insurance
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance services covering risks other than life and transportation of goods.

str:Code.com:Name: Gross direct insurance (other than life and freight insurance) premiums receivable (credits) and payable (debits)
str:Code.com:Description: Total premiums related to direct insurance, excluding life and freight insurance, that are receivable (credits) or payable (debits).

str:Code.com:Name: Gross direct insurance (other than life and freight insurance) claims receivable (credits) and payable (debits)
str:Code.com:Description: Total claims related to direct insurance, excluding life and freight insurance, that are receivable (credits) or payable (debits).

str:Code.com:Name: Reinsurance
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance between one insurance corporation and another with the aim to spread the insurance risk.

str:Code.com:Name: Auxiliary insurance services
str:Code.com:Description: Services that are closely related to insurance and pension scheme operations. Included are agents’ commissions, insurance brokering and agency services, insurance and pension consultancy services, evaluation and loss adjustment services, actuarial services, salvage administration services, and regulatory and monitoring services on indemnities and recovery services.

str:Code.com:Name: Pension and standardized guarantee services
str:Code.com:Description: Pension and standardized guarantee services.

str:Code.com:Name: Pension services
str:Code.com:Description: Pension services.

str:Code.com:Name: Standardized guarantee services
str:Code.com:Description: Standardized guarantee services.

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance and pension services, other
str:Code.com:Description: Insurance and pension services, other.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to financial intermediation, financial risk management, liquidity transformation, or auxiliary financial activities. It also includes insurance and pension scheme services.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial services explicitly charged and other financial services
str:Code.com:Description: Financial services for which a direct fee is charged, as well as other financial services provided as part of a broader transaction.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial intermediation services indirectly measured (FISIM)
str:Code.com:Description: The value of services provided by financial intermediaries, such as banks, measured indirectly through the spread between interest rates on loans and deposits.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial services, other
str:Code.com:Description: Financial services, other.

str:Code.com:Name: Charges for the use of intellectual property n.i.e.
str:Code.com:Description: Charges for licenses to use proprietary rights from research and development (such as patents, copyrights, industrial processes and designs, and trade secrets) as well as from marketing (such as franchises, trademarks, and brand names); and charges for licenses to reproduce or distribute (or both) intellectual property embodied in produced originals or prototypes (such as copyrights on books and manuscripts, computer software, data and databases, cinematographic works, and sound recordings) and related rights (such as for live performances and television, cable, or satellite broadcast).

str:Code.com:Name: Franchises and trademarks licensing fees
str:Code.com:Description: Fees paid for the right to use franchises (business models) and trademarks (brand names and logos) owned by another party.

str:Code.com:Name: Licenses for the use of outcomes of research and development
str:Code.com:Description: Licenses for the use of outcomes of research and development.

str:Code.com:Name: Licenses to reproduce and/or distribute computer software
str:Code.com:Description: Licenses to reproduce and/or distribute computer software.

str:Code.com:Name: Licenses to reproduce and/or distribute audio-visual and related products
str:Code.com:Description: Licenses to reproduce and/or distribute audio-visual and related products.

str:Code.com:Name: Licenses to reproduce and/or distribute audio-visual products
str:Code.com:Description: Licenses to reproduce and/or distribute audio-visual products.

str:Code.com:Name: Licenses to reproduce and/or distribute other than audio-visual products
str:Code.com:Description: Licenses to reproduce and/or distribute other than audio-visual products.

str:Code.com:Name: Telecommunications, computer, and information services
str:Code.com:Description: Telecommunications, computer, and information services.

str:Code.com:Name: Telecommunications services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to the broadcast or transmission of sound, images, data, or other information by telephone, radio and television cable transmission, radio and television satellite, electronic mail, and so forth, including business network services, teleconferencing, and support services.

str:Code.com:Name: Computer services
str:Code.com:Description: Hardware- and software-related services.

str:Code.com:Name: Computer software
str:Code.com:Description: Programs and other operating information used by a computer, including both system software and application software.

str:Code.com:Name: Computer software; of which: software originals
str:Code.com:Description: Original versions of computer software developed for sale or licensing to users.

str:Code.com:Name: Computer services other than computer software
str:Code.com:Description: IT-related services excluding those directly related to software, such as hardware support and IT consulting.

str:Code.com:Name: Information services
str:Code.com:Description: News agency services such as the provision of news, photographs, and feature articles to the media.

str:Code.com:Name: News agency services
str:Code.com:Description: News agency services.

str:Code.com:Name: Information services other than news agency services
str:Code.com:Description: Information services that provide data and analysis excluding those offered by news agencies, such as market research or financial data services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other business services
str:Code.com:Description: Distribution services related to water, steam, gas, and other petroleum products and air-conditioning supply, where these are identified separately from transmission services; placement of personnel, security, and investigative services; translation and interpretation; photographic services; publishing; building cleaning; and real estate services. Also included are forfeited down payments not able to be specified to any other service.

str:Code.com:Name: Research and development services
str:Code.com:Description: Services associated with basic research, applied research, and experimental development of new products and processes.

str:Code.com:Name: Services: work undertaken on a systematic basis to increase the stock of knowledge
str:Code.com:Description: Services: Work undertaken on a systematic basis to increase the stock of knowledge.

str:Code.com:Name: Provision of customized and non-customized research and development services
str:Code.com:Description: Provision of customized and non-customized research and development services.

str:Code.com:Name: Sale of proprietary rights arising from research and development
str:Code.com:Description: Sale of proprietary rights arising from research and development.

str:Code.com:Name: Patents
str:Code.com:Description: Identifiable, non-monetary assets without physical substance,

str:Code.com:Name: Copyrights arising from research and development
str:Code.com:Description: Copyrights arising from research and development

str:Code.com:Name: Industrial processes and designs
str:Code.com:Description: The methods and plans used in industrial production, as well as the design of products, machinery, and plants.

str:Code.com:Name: Sales of proprietary rights arising from research and development other than patents, copyrights arising from research and development and industrial processes and designs
str:Code.com:Description: Sales of proprietary rights arising from research and development other than patents, copyrights arising from research and development and industrial processes and designs

str:Code.com:Name: Research and development services other than work undertaken on a systematic basis to increase the stock of knowledge
str:Code.com:Description: Research and development services other than work undertaken on a systematic basis to increase the stock of knowledge

str:Code.com:Name: Professional and management consulting services
str:Code.com:Description: Legal services, accounting, management consulting, managerial services and public relations services; and advertising, market research and public opinion polling services.

str:Code.com:Name: Legal, accounting, management consulting, and public relations services
str:Code.com:Description: Legal, accounting, management consulting, and public relations services.

str:Code.com:Name: Legal services
str:Code.com:Description: Legal services.

str:Code.com:Name: Accounting, auditing, bookkeeping, and tax consulting services
str:Code.com:Description: Accounting, auditing, bookkeeping, and tax consulting services.

str:Code.com:Name: Business and management consulting and public relations services
str:Code.com:Description: Professional services that advise businesses and organizations on management, strategy, operations, and public relations to improve performance and image.

str:Code.com:Name: Advertising, market research, and public opinion polling services
str:Code.com:Description: Services aimed at promoting products or services, gathering data on consumer preferences, and analyzing public opinion through surveys and studies.

str:Code.com:Name: Advertising, market research, and public opinion polling services; of which: convention, trade-fair and exhibition organization services
str:Code.com:Description: A subset of the broader advertising and market research services specifically focused on organizing events such as conventions, trade-fairs, and exhibitions.

str:Code.com:Name: Technical, trade-related, and other business services
str:Code.com:Description: Technical, trade-related, and other business services.

str:Code.com:Name: Architectural, engineering, scientific, and other technical services
str:Code.com:Description: A broad category of services encompassing specialized professional activities in architecture, engineering, science, and other technical fields.

str:Code.com:Name: Architectural services
str:Code.com:Description: Professional services related to the design and planning of buildings and structures, including consultation, drafting, and project management.

str:Code.com:Name: Engineering services
str:Code.com:Description: Professional services involving the application of engineering principles to design, develop, and utilize machines, materials, instruments, structures, processes, and systems.

str:Code.com:Name: Scientific and other technical services
str:Code.com:Description: Scientific and other technical services.

str:Code.com:Name: Waste treatment and de-pollution, agricultural and mining services
str:Code.com:Description: Waste treatment and de-pollution, agricultural and mining services.

str:Code.com:Name: Waste treatment and de-pollution
str:Code.com:Description: Waste treatment and de-pollution.

str:Code.com:Name: Services incidental to agriculture, forestry and fishing
str:Code.com:Description: Services incidental to agriculture, forestry and fishing.

str:Code.com:Name: Services incidental to mining, and oil and gas extraction
str:Code.com:Description: Services incidental to mining, and oil and gas extraction.

str:Code.com:Name: Memo grouping: services, services incidental to agriculture and mining
str:Code.com:Description: Memo grouping: services, services incidental to agriculture and mining.

str:Code.com:Name: Operating leasing services
str:Code.com:Description: Services associated with renting out produced assets under arrangements that provide use of a tangible asset to the lessee, but do not involve the transfer of the bulk of risks and rewards of ownership to the lessee.

str:Code.com:Name: Trade-related services
str:Code.com:Description: Trade-related services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other business services n.i.e.
str:Code.com:Description: Other business services n.i.e.

str:Code.com:Name: Other business services n.i.e.; of which: employment services, i.e., search, placement and supply services of personnel
str:Code.com:Description: Other business services n.i.e.; of which: employment services, i.e., search, placement and supply services of personnel.

str:Code.com:Name: Technical trade and related other business services
str:Code.com:Description: Technical trade and related other business services.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal, cultural, and recreational services
str:Code.com:Description: Audiovisual and related services; and other personal, cultural and recreational services.

str:Code.com:Name: Audiovisual and related services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to the production of motion pictures (on film, videotape, disk or transmitted electronically, etc.), radio and television programs (live or on tape), and musical recordings.

str:Code.com:Name: Audio-visual services
str:Code.com:Description: The provision of services involving both sound and visual components, typically in the entertainment, education, and communication sectors.

str:Code.com:Name: Audio-visual services; of which: audio-visual originals
str:Code.com:Description: A subset of audio-visual services specifically focused on the creation and production of original audio-visual content.

str:Code.com:Name: Artistic related services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to the creation, performance, or exhibition of artistic works including visual arts, performing arts, and literary arts.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services
str:Code.com:Description: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services; health services
str:Code.com:Description: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services; health services.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services; education services
str:Code.com:Description: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services; education services.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services; heritage and recreational services
str:Code.com:Description: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services; heritage and recreational services.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services; personal services other than health, education and heritage and recreational services
str:Code.com:Description: Personal, cultural, and recreational services other than audiovisual and related services; personal services other than health, education and heritage and recreational services.

str:Code.com:Name: Personal cultural and recreational services
str:Code.com:Description: Personal cultural and recreational services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other personal, cultural, and recreational services
str:Code.com:Description: Health services, education services and other services. This excludes the health and educational services included under travel.

str:Code.com:Name: Government goods and services n.i.e.
str:Code.com:Description: The sum of (a) goods and services supplied by and to enclaves, such as embassies, military bases, and international organizations; (b) goods and services acquired from the host economy by diplomats, consular staff, and military personnel located abroad and their dependents; and (c) services supplied by and to governments and not included in other categories of services

str:Code.com:Name: Embassies and consulates
str:Code.com:Description: Diplomatic offices of a government in a foreign country, representing the home country, managing diplomatic relations, and providing consular services.

str:Code.com:Name: Military units and agencies
str:Code.com:Description: Military units and agencies.

str:Code.com:Name: Government goods and services n.i.e other than embassies and consulates and military units and agencies
str:Code.com:Description: Government-provided goods and services not classified elsewhere, excluding those provided by embassies, consulates, and military units.

str:Code.com:Name: Services, government services: reimbursement of MSs' cost of collecting own resources (memo item)
str:Code.com:Description: Services, government services: reimbursement of MSs' cost of collecting own resources (memo item).

str:Code.com:Name: Services, government services: building rentals (memo item)
str:Code.com:Description: Services, government services: building rentals (memo item).

str:Code.com:Name: Services, government services: other government services n.i.e. (memo item)
str:Code.com:Description: Services, government services: other government services n.i.e. (memo item).

str:Code.com:Name: Government lending funds
str:Code.com:Description: Funds provided by a government for lending purposes, often to support economic development, infrastructure projects, or other government priorities.

str:Code.com:Name: Services not allocated
str:Code.com:Description: Services not allocated.

str:Code.com:Name: Memo grouping: services, commercial services
str:Code.com:Description: Memo grouping: services, commercial services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other commercial services: other services excluding government goods and services n.i.e
str:Code.com:Description: Other commercial services: other services excluding government goods and services n.i.e.

str:Code.com:Name: Other services
str:Code.com:Description: Other services.

str:Code.com:Name: Other services: total services excluding manufacturing services on physical inputs owned by others, maintenance and repair services, transport and travel
str:Code.com:Description: Other services: total services excluding manufacturing services on physical inputs owned by others, maintenance and repair services, transport and travel

str:Code.com:Name: Miscellaneous services: services: manufacturing services on physical inputs owned by others + services: maintenance and repair services n.i.e. + services: charges for the use of intellectual property n.i.e + services: personal, cultural, and recreational services + services: government goods and services n.i.e. + services: services not allocated
str:Code.com:Description: Miscellaneous services: services: manufacturing services on physical inputs owned by others + services: maintenance and repair services n.i.e. + services: charges for the use of intellectual property n.i.e + services: personal, cultural, and recreational services + services: government goods and services n.i.e. + services: services not allocated

str:Code.com:Name: Insurance and pension services + financial services
str:Code.com:Description: The combined offering of insurance, pension, and financial services, including banking, investment, and advisory services.

str:Code.com:Name: Goods related services: manufacturing services on physical inputs owned by others and maintenance and repair services
str:Code.com:Description: Services related to the manufacturing of goods owned by another party and the provision of maintenance and repair services.

str:Code.com:Description: Services Value.

str:Code.com:Description: Trade balance.

str:Code.com:Description: Total current and capital account balances.

str:Code.com:Description: Trade of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Description: This refers to the total value of a country's exports and imports of goods and services.

str:Code.com:Name: A generalized measure of ubiquity of country services exports
str:Code.com:Description: Ubiquity of a country's service exports.

str:Code.com:Name: Workers' remittances
str:Code.com:Description: Current cross-border transfers by migrants who are employed in new economies and considered residents there.

str:Code.com:Description: Workers' remittances inflows.

str:Code.com:Description: Workers' remittances inflows: Africa.

str:Code.com:Description: Workers' remittances inflows: Euro Area.

str:Code.com:Description: Workers' remittances inflows: GCC.

str:Code.com:Description: Workers' remittances inflows: Other regions.
