str:Code.com:Name: World service exports
str:Code.com:Description: World Service Exports.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods
str:Code.com:Description: The export (sale or barter) of physical, produced objects (goods) over which ownership rights can be established and whose ownership can be transferred from one institutional unit to another by engaging in transactions on markets by residents in one territory to residents in another territory. Exports reflect a change in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of goods and services
str:Code.com:Description: The export (sale or barter) of physical, produced objects (goods) and services over which ownership rights can be established and whose ownership can be transferred from one institutional unit to another by engaging in transactions on markets by residents in one territory to residents in another territory. Exports reflect a change in the economic ownership of goods from residents of the compiling economy to non-residents, irrespective of physical movement of goods across national borders. The f.o.b. price (free on board price) of exports and imports of goods is the market value of the goods at the point of uniform valuation, (the customs frontier of the economy from which they are exported). It is equal to the c.i.f. price less the costs of transportation and insurance charges, between the customs frontier of the exporting (importing) country and that of the importing (exporting) country. This indicator appears in other datasets published by the IMF. While every effort is made to ensure that estimates for the same indicator are consistent across different datasets, differences can occur due to differences in compilation methodology, reference year, splicing series to preserve time-series continuity, and when the data were collected, processed, and published.

str:Code.com:Name: Oil exports
str:Code.com:Description: Exports of oil related products (including crude petroleum, gasoline and other fuels).

str:Code.com:Description: Total external debt, Statistical discrepancy, Total debt.

str:Code.com:Description: Current account balance excluding exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Description: Financial account balance excluding exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Exports of services
str:Code.com:Description: Services results from a production activity that changes the conditions of the consuming units, or facilitates the exchange of products or financial assets. Services are not generally separate items over which ownership rights can be established and cannot generally be separated from their production. Exports of services are exchanges between the resident in one economic territory and a resident in another economic territory.

str:Code.com:Name: Debt service charges: amortization paid (principal only)
str:Code.com:Description: Debt service charges: amortization paid (principal only).

str:Code.com:Name: Debt service charges: paid (cash basis)
str:Code.com:Description: Debt service charges: paid (cash basis).

str:Code.com:Description: Secondary income excluding exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Net incurrence of liabilities excluding exceptional financing
str:Code.com:Description: Net incurrence of liabilities excluding exceptional financing.

str:Code.com:Name: Supplementary items, Financial account balance (assets less liabilities)
str:Code.com:Description: The financial account balance reflects the difference in transactions in financial assets and liabilities in the accounting period. In the external accounts, only transactions between residents and non-residents are included.

str:Code.com:Name: Transport, Passenger, Of which: Payable by Border, Seasonal, and Other S-T Workers
str:Code.com:Description: Transport, Passenger, Of which: Payable by Border, Seasonal, and Other S-T Workers

str:Code.com:Name: Transport passenger services of which payable by border seasonal and other short-term workers
str:Code.com:Description: Transport passenger services of which payable by border seasonal and other short-term workers

str:Code.com:Name: Acquisitions nonproduced nonfinancial assets
str:Code.com:Description: Acquisitions nonproduced nonfinancial assets.

str:Code.com:Name: All modes of transport; other than passenger and freight; including postal and courier services
str:Code.com:Description: Transportation services excluding the transport of passengers, goods, and also including postal and courier activities.

str:Code.com:Name: Travel services, Both Business and Personal Travel, Other Services
str:Code.com:Description: Travel services, Both Business and Personal Travel, Other Services.

str:Code.com:Name: Reserves and Related items
str:Code.com:Description: Reserves and Related items.

str:Code.com:Name: Financial account balance, excluding reserves and related items
str:Code.com:Description: Financial account balance, excluding reserves and related items.

str:Concept.com:Name: Country
str:Concept.com:Description: Country

str:Concept.com:Name: Indicator
str:Concept.com:Description: A variable that sheds light on some phenomenon or situation.

str:Concept.com:Name: BOP/IIP Accounting Entry
str:Concept.com:Description: BOP/IIP Accounting Entry

str:Concept.com:Name: BOP Type
str:Concept.com:Description: Balance of Payment Type

str:Concept.com:Name: Asset/Liabilities Type
str:Concept.com:Description: Asset/Liabilities Type

str:Concept.com:Name: Adjustments Using IMF Accounting Records
str:Concept.com:Description: Adjustments Using IMF Accounting Records

str:Concept.com:Name: BPM6 Basis Start Date
str:Concept.com:Description: BPM6 Basis Start Date for Balance of Payments (BOP) or International Investment Position (IIP) depends on the dataflow

str:Concept.com:Name: Currency
str:Concept.com:Description: This codelist provides a standardized list of currency codes and names, based on the ISO 4217 standard, representing the various currencies used globally for economic and financial transactions.

str:ConceptScheme.com:Name: STA: Master Concepts
str:ConceptScheme.com:Description: A standardized set of concepts and definitions that classify and describe the data elements used within STA.

str:Concept.com:Name: IFS flag
str:Concept.com:Description: A key indicator flag is used to identify and mark indicators that were part of the IFS data product.

str:ConceptScheme.com:Name: Master Metadata Concepts
str:ConceptScheme.com:Description: Standardized definitions for common data elements used across multiple datasets. These elements include attributes like identification and provenance, access controls, methodology and other general descriptive characteristics.

str:Concept.com:Name: DOI
str:Concept.com:Description: A public identifier (unique alphanumeric string) assigned to a digital object, typically a publication or dataset, to provide a persistent link to its location on the internet.Users use it for citing the resource.

str:Concept.com:Name: Series Name
str:Concept.com:Description: A label given to a specific series. This name helps to distinguish it from other series data and provides context for what the data represents.

str:Concept.com:Name: Author
str:Concept.com:Description: The person or organization responsible for resource’s substantive and intellectual content.

str:Concept.com:Name: Publisher
str:Concept.com:Description: The organization or institution responsible for making a resource available to the public or a specific audience. For datasets, the publisher is responsible for preparing and releasing the dataset for public or private use. This includes ensuring the dataset's quality, accuracy, and accessibility.

str:Concept.com:Name: Department
str:Concept.com:Description: The specific division or unit within an organization (e.g., the IMF) that is ultimately accountable for the creation, maintenance, and dissemination of a resource.

str:Concept.com:Name: Creator
str:Concept.com:Description: The creator is the entity primarily responsible for creating the resource. Institution responsible for producing official statistics.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data Owner
str:Concept.com:Description: Institution that holds the legal ownership and accountability for the data, ensuring it aligns with national or international policies.

str:Concept.com:Name: Data Steward
str:Concept.com:Description: Institution that ensures the quality and metadata of the data, and that it adheres to relevant standards.

str:Concept.com:Name: Publication Date
str:Concept.com:Description: The date when a resource was formally issued or made available to the public or a specific audience. This applies to datasets that have major releases, but the data may have minor updates throughout the year. In most cases where the data is regularly updated, the publication date is the same as the update date.

str:Concept.com:Name: Update Date
str:Concept.com:Description: The most recent date on which a resource was modified or updated, including changes to data or metadata. For a dataset, the update date is the time when the data within a dataset was last modified, or the metadata was modified.

str:Concept.com:Name: Source Organization
str:Concept.com:Description: The entity responsible for producing or collecting the original data used in a resource. This could be a government agency, a research institution, a private company, or any other entity that generates data. When citing or referencing information, it is important to identify the source organization to ensure credibility and accuracy.

str:Concept.com:Name: Short Source Citation
str:Concept.com:Description: A brief reference to the source of data or information used in a resource, typically including the author, title, and year of publication.

str:Concept.com:Name: Full Source Citation
str:Concept.com:Description: A complete reference to the source of data or information used in a resource, providing all necessary details to locate and verify the original source.

str:Concept.com:Name: Suggested Citation
str:Concept.com:Description: A recommended format for citing a resource in academic or professional works, ensuring proper attribution and acknowledgement of the source.

str:Concept.com:Name: Full Description
str:Concept.com:Description: A comprehensive description of the dataset, encompassing its content, scope, underlying concepts, coverage, classifications, and statistical techniques employed in its creation and analysis. This may include references to detailed methodological documents or papers that provide further elaboration on these aspects.

str:Concept.com:Name: Topic
str:Concept.com:Description: A topic of the resource. A high-level categorization of the resource's subject matter, indicating the main area or domain to which the data pertains.

str:Concept.com:Name: Topic Dataset
str:Concept.com:Description: A topic of the dataset. A high-level categorization of the dataset's subject matter, indicating the main area or domain to which the data pertains.

str:Concept.com:Name: Keywords
str:Concept.com:Description: A set of words or phrases that describe the content and subject matter of a resource.

str:Concept.com:Name: Keywords Dataset
str:Concept.com:Description: A set of words or phrases that describe the content and subject matter of a dataset.

str:Concept.com:Name: Key Indicator
str:Concept.com:Description: A flag or indicator that identifies a data element or indicator as being of particular importance or significance for a specific analysis or purpose.

str:Concept.com:Name: Language
str:Concept.com:Description: It refers to the language of the intellectual content of the resource. It can be applied to any type of resource, including documents, datasets, images, audio recordings, etc.

str:Concept.com:Name: Temporal Coverage
str:Concept.com:Description: This field represents the temporal coverage of the data, expressed using the ISO 8601 standard. The data spans the time interval from 1997 to 2009 (inclusive), indicated as 1997/2009.

str:Concept.com:Name: Frequency Coverage
str:Concept.com:Description: Frequency Coverage in a dataset like annual or monthly refers to the range of time periods for which data is available.

str:Concept.com:Name: Geographical Coverage
str:Concept.com:Description: This field specifies the spatial extent of the data, detailing the geographic areas or regions included in the dataset. It may be represented by countries, continents, coordinates, or specific boundaries such as cities, regions, or global coverage.

str:Concept.com:Name: Methodology
str:Concept.com:Description: Reference to the conceptual statistical framework and underlying statistical methodology used for the dataset (e.g. country specific).

str:Concept.com:Name: Methodology Notes
str:Concept.com:Description: Additional details or explanations regarding the methodology used for a specific dataset or indicator. These notes outline the conceptual frameworks and statistical methods used to collect, process, and analyze the data, and can vary in level of detail depending on the data's complexity. For example: a) At the country level, methodology notes describe the specific methods used by each country to calculate particular indicators. b) At the time series level, methodology notes provide a detailed explanation of how an indicator is generated for a specific country.

str:Concept.com:Name: Contact Point
str:Concept.com:Description: The primary individual or group responsible for handling inquiries and providing information about a resource. For datasets published on the IMF data portal, the point of contact shown to public users is standardized as a common help email address.

str:Concept.com:Name: Security Classification
str:Concept.com:Description: A data classification assigned to data based on its sensitivity, value, and the potential impact of unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction, resulting in the assignment of a tag that designates the data's sensitivity level. This attribute is assigned at lower levels of data when a dataset contains a mix of data access levels, with some series designated as public and others as confidential.

str:Concept.com:Name: Access and Sharing Level
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification that specifies the extent to which a resource can be shared with others, including restrictions on access, use, and distribution. It specifies who can access the data (e.g., public, specific groups, authorized personnel) and what actions they can take with it. The appropriate sharing level depends on the sensitivity and intended use of the data, ranging from open access for public data to restricted access for confidential information. This attribute is assigned at lower levels of data when a dataset contains a mix of data access levels, with some series designated as public and others as restricted.

str:Concept.com:Name: Access and Sharing Notes
str:Concept.com:Description: Additional notes involves documenting any нюances or complexities beyond the standard access and sharing levels.

str:Concept.com:Name: Access Instructions
str:Concept.com:Description: Detailed guidance on how to obtain or access a resource, particularly relevant for datasets.

str:Concept.com:Name: Usage Rights Notes
str:Concept.com:Description: Additional notes or explanations regarding the licensing terms for the source data used in a dataset.

str:Concept.com:Name: License
str:Concept.com:Description: Information on the licence for distribution / legal agreement that defines the terms under which a resource can be used, shared, or distributed.

str:Concept.com:Name: URL
str:Concept.com:Description: Direct link to the dataset on the external source's platform.

str:Concept.com:Name: Standard Classification Type
str:Concept.com:Description: Identifies the standard classifications used for the data.

str:ConceptScheme.com:Name: Master Data Concepts
str:ConceptScheme.com:Description: Standardized concepts and definitions for describing data elements across datasets, covering cross-cutting data attributes such as temporal information, country, unit of measure, transformation.

str:Concept.com:Name: Comments
str:Concept.com:Description: Additional notes, explanations, or remarks regarding the data, methodology, or other aspects of an observation.

str:Concept.com:Name: Observation Status
str:Concept.com:Description: Indicates the status of an observation within a dataset, such as whether it is a provisional value, an estimated value, a final figure, or a value where a time series break occurs. (Source: SDMX)

str:Concept.com:Name: Derivation Type
str:Concept.com:Description: Identifies the specific method used to derive a new data element or indicator from source data.

str:Concept.com:Name: IMF Data Overlap
str:Concept.com:Description: Indicates the potential for overlap within IMF data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Confidentiality Status
str:Concept.com:Description: This classification provides information about the sensitivity and confidentiality status of the data. (Source:SDMX)

str:Concept.com:Name: Reporting Period Type
str:Concept.com:Description: It is used to identify and distinguish between different types of time periods in economic and financial reporting. The specific start and end dates for a fiscal year (FY) can vary depending on the country, organization, or industry. When reporting data using this codelist, it is essential to specify the exact start and end dates of the fiscal year being referenced.

str:Concept.com:Name: Reference Period
str:Concept.com:Description: A time interval defined by a start date and an end date, used to constrain and specify the timeframe for reporting data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Base year
str:Concept.com:Description: The reference year used for comparison in the calculation of index numbers or other statistical measures. Changes in values over time are typically expressed as percentage deviations from the base year.

str:Concept.com:Name: Calendar year
str:Concept.com:Description: A period of twelve consecutive months starting on January 1st and ending on December 31st, as per the Gregorian calendar.

str:Concept.com:Name: Fiscal year
str:Concept.com:Description: A twelve-month period used by governments and businesses for accounting and financial reporting purposes. The start and end dates of a fiscal year can vary depending on the organization and country but often differ from the calendar year.

str:Concept.com:Name: Country
str:Concept.com:Description: The country or region for which the data or statistics in a resource are collected or reported.

str:Concept.com:Name: Counterpart Country
str:Concept.com:Description: The country or region with which a transaction or interaction occurs in the context of international statistics or data.

str:Concept.com:Name: Unit of Measure
str:Concept.com:Description: The standard unit used to quantify a particular data element or indicator.I It encompasses various units, including currency, physical units, percentages, and other standardized measures.

str:Concept.com:Name: Currency
str:Concept.com:Description: Denominated in Currency specifies the currency in which a financial instrument is valued and settled.

str:Concept.com:Name: Transformation
str:Concept.com:Description: Represents a set of codes identifying the specific transformations or calculations applied to source data to derive new data elements or indicators. A transformation involves a computation with one or more input values (either base variables obtained by observation or derived variables from prior transformations) that results in a single output value (the value of a new derived variable).

str:Concept.com:Name: Seasonal Adjustment
str:Concept.com:Description: Specific adjustment for the time series (e.g. seasonally adjusted).

str:Concept.com:Name: Gender
str:Concept.com:Description: Gender identity refers to each person’s internal and individual experience of gender. One's gender identity can be the same or different from their sex assigned at birth. (Source: UNECE 2023 Gender and salutation code lists)

str:Concept.com:Name: Sex
str:Concept.com:Description: An individual’s sex may be defined by one’s assigned sex at birth and/or one’s biological attributes which includes a combination of hormones, chromosomes, physiology, and anatomy. It includes both binary options as well as X and intersex. (Source: UNECE 2023 Gender and salutation code lists)

str:Concept.com:Name: Civil (or Marital) Status
str:Concept.com:Description: The legal status of an individual regarding marriage, such as single, married, widowed, or divorced.

str:ConceptScheme.com:Name: Master Domain Concepts
str:ConceptScheme.com:Description: Standardized definitions for data elements specific to particular domains or subject areas. This includes concepts and attributes related to macroeconomics, finance, socio-demographics, and other specialized fields.

str:Concept.com:Name: Accounting Entry
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification that identifies whether a transaction represents a use of resources, a source of funds, an asset, a liability, or a change in equity.

str:Concept.com:Name: Commodity
str:Concept.com:Description: Comprehensive list of all the different commodity codes used in IMF.

str:Concept.com:Name: Sector
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification of economic entities based on their primary function and economic behavior, such as households, corporations, government, or non-profit institutions.

str:Concept.com:Name: Counterpart Sector
str:Concept.com:Description: In the context of a specific transaction or economic flow, the counterpart sector refers to the institutional sector on the opposite side of the transaction.

str:Concept.com:Name: Exchange Rate
str:Concept.com:Description: Represents a set of codes identifying the exchange rate between two currencies. An exchange rate indicates how much of one currency is needed to buy one unit of another currency. It is derived by comparing the value of one currency to another, often influenced by economic factors like interest rates, inflation, and government policy. This codelist includes various exchange rate calculations, such as period averages, pre-Euro rates, and those involving Special Drawing Rights (SDRs).

str:Concept.com:Name: Flow or stock entry
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification that indicates whether a data point represents a flow (a quantity measured over a period of time) or a stock (a quantity measured at a specific point in time).

str:Concept.com:Name: Functional category
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification used to categorize investments based on their purpose or function, such as equity investments, debt investments, or other investments.

str:Concept.com:Name: GFS Stocks, Transactions, Other Flows
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification of data items used in the Government Finance Statistics (GFS) framework, distinguishing between stocks (levels at a point in time), transactions (changes over a period), and other flows.

str:Concept.com:Name: Index type
str:Concept.com:Description: Type of index prices.

str:Concept.com:Name: Interest Rate types, Terms and Conditions
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification of interest rates based on their type (fixed or floating), terms (maturity), and other conditions (e.g., collateralization, reference rates).

str:Concept.com:Name: Instrument and Assets Classification
str:Concept.com:Description: Because of the symmetry of financial assets and liabilities, the term ‘instrument’ is used to relate to both the asset and the liability aspect of financial transactions, other flows and financial balance sheets. (Source: Handbook of National Accounting, 2013)

str:Concept.com:Name: International Accounts Item
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification of transactions in the balance of payments, such as trade in goods, services, income, and financial flows.

str:Concept.com:Name: Macroeconomic and Financial Accounts
str:Concept.com:Description: A framework for organizing macroeconomic and financial data into a set of integrated accounts and tables that provide a comprehensive overview of an economy.

str:Concept.com:Name: Maturity of Financial Instrument
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification of financial instruments based on their remaining time to maturity or expiration.

str:Concept.com:Name: Monetary and Financial Instruments
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification of financial instruments used in monetary and financial statistics, including currency, deposits, loans, securities, and derivatives.

str:Concept.com:Name: Stocks, Transactions, Other Flows
str:Concept.com:Description: A classification of data items used in the System of National Accounts (SNA) framework, distinguishing between stocks (levels at a point in time), transactions (changes over a period), and other flows.

str:Concept.com:Name: Prices
str:Concept.com:Description: Represents different types of prices or price adjustments, including those that have been adjusted for inflation (real or constant prices) and those that have not (nominal or current prices). It also includes various methods for adjusting prices for inflation, such as using deflators or converting to a constant base period.

str:Concept.com:Name: Statistical Measures
str:Concept.com:Description: Describe characteristics of a group of data. They summarize the distribution, central tendency, or spread of multiple values.

str:Concept.com:Name: Trade flow
str:Concept.com:Description: Data related to the movement of goods and services between countries, including exports and imports.

str:Concept.com:Name: Valuation
str:Concept.com:Description: Valuation refers to the rules and conventions used for recording monetary flows and stocks in macroeconomic statistics. Standard national accounts valuations include the basic price (what the seller receives) and the purchaser's price (what the purchaser pays). The purchaser's price is the basic price, plus taxes less subsidies on products, plus invoiced transportation and insurance services, plus distribution margin. International trade in goods considers the free on board (fob) price and cost-insurance-freight price, among others. Other valuation types include prices used in financial markets (e.g., buy and sell prices).

str:Concept.com:Name: Social Concepts
str:Concept.com:Description: A broad category encompassing various social indicators, such as tourism and labor concepts.

str:Concept.com:Name: COFOG
str:Concept.com:Description: The Classification of the Functions of Government, a standardized system for classifying government expenditures by purpose.

str:Concept.com:Name: Expenditure Category
str:Concept.com:Description: The Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (COICOP) 1999 is a standardized classification system developed by the United Nations Statistics Division to categorize household consumption expenditures by purpose. It includes 14 divisions, 47 groups, and 117 classes.

str:Concept.com:Name: Expenditure Category (COICOP 2018)
str:Concept.com:Description: The Classification of Individual Consumption According to Purpose (COICOP) 2018 is an updated and expanded version of the COICOP classification system, designed to better reflect the evolving consumption patterns of the 21st century. It includes 14 divisions, 50 groups, 153 classes, and 449 subclasses.

str:ConceptScheme.com:Name: SDMX Concept Scheme for Concept Roles
str:ConceptScheme.com:Description: A concept role gives a particular context to a concept for easy and systematic interpretation by machine processing and visualization tools. For example, the concepts REPORTING_AREA and COUNTERPART_AREA are different concepts but they are both geographical characteristics, therefore they can be associated with the same concept role ID: “GEO”. This allows visualization systems to interpret these concepts as geographical data in order to generate maps. For any question, comment or correction, feel free to contact the SDMX Statistical Working Group (SWG) at the following address: swg@sdmx.org.

str:Concept.com:Name: Geographical
str:Concept.com:Description: Geographic area to which the measured statistical phenomenon relates.

str:Concept.com:Name: Flag
str:Concept.com:Description: Coded attribute in a data set that represents qualitative information for the cell or partial key (e.g. series) value.

str:Concept.com:Name: Frequency
str:Concept.com:Description: Time interval at which the source data are collected.

str:Concept.com:Name: Entity
str:Concept.com:Description: Describes the subject of the data set (e.g. a country).

str:Concept.com:Name: Comment
str:Concept.com:Description: Descriptive text which can be attached to data or metadata.

str:Concept.com:Name: Variable
str:Concept.com:Description: Characteristic of a unit being observed that may assume more than one of a set of values to which a numerical measure or a category from a classification can be assigned.

str:Concept.com:Name: Statistical operation
str:Concept.com:Description: Signifies statistical operations have been done on the observations.
